model_requirements.description: Advertising and Marketing is a dynamic industry delivering creative campaigns, media planning and buying, public relations, data-driven analytics, and brand strategy to help organizations reach and engage their target audiences.

_vibe_session_metadata.summary: Model quality: 83/100 (12 warnings, 1 errors). 95 tables across 10 domains.

model.name: Advertising
model.description: Advertising and Marketing is a dynamic industry delivering creative campaigns, media planning and buying, public relations, data-driven analytics, and brand strategy to help organizations reach and engage their target audiences.
model.core_business_processes: Campaign Strategy and Planning, Media Planning and Buying, Creative Development and Production, Account Management and Client Services, Data Analytics and Audience Insights, Brand Strategy and Positioning, Public Relations and Communications, Digital Marketing and Performance Optimization, Budget Management and Financial Reconciliation, Proposal Development and Pitching
model.data_domains: Campaign, Client, Media, Creative, Audience, Finance, Analytics, Talent, Channel, Contract
model.common_business_jargons: CPM (Cost Per Mille), CPC (Cost Per Click), CTR (Click-Through Rate), CPA (Cost Per Acquisition), ROAS (Return on Ad Spend), ROI (Return on Investment), GRP (Gross Rating Point), TRP (Target Rating Point), SOV (Share of Voice), SOW (Statement of Work), RFP (Request for Proposal), KPI (Key Performance Indicator), CRM (Customer Relationship Management), DSP (Demand-Side Platform), SSP (Supply-Side Platform), DMP (Data Management Platform), CDP (Customer Data Platform), RTB (Real-Time Bidding), PPC (Pay Per Click), SEO (Search Engine Optimization), SEM (Search Engine Marketing), OOH (Out-of-Home), DOOH (Digital Out-of-Home), OTT (Over-the-Top), CTV (Connected TV), VTR (View-Through Rate), CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value), NPS (Net Promoter Score), IO (Insertion Order), RFI (Request for Information), ABM (Account-Based Marketing), MQL (Marketing Qualified Lead), SQL (Sales Qualified Lead), DAM (Digital Asset Management), WIP (Work in Progress), FTE (Full-Time Equivalent), P&L (Profit and Loss), EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization), QBR (Quarterly Business Review), UAT (User Acceptance Testing), API (Application Programming Interface), ETL (Extract Transform Load), GA (Google Analytics), GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation), CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act), IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau), MRC (Media Rating Council), TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group), VAST (Video Ad Serving Template), VPAID (Video Player-Ad Interface Definition)
model.operational_systems_of_records: Salesforce CRM (Sales Cloud; Service Cloud; Marketing Cloud), SAP S/4HANA (FI; CO; MM; PS), Google Campaign Manager 360 (Ad Serving; Trafficking; Reporting), The Trade Desk (Programmatic Buying; Audience Targeting; Bid Management), Adobe Creative Cloud (Photoshop; Illustrator; InDesign; Premiere Pro), Workfront (Project Management; Resource Planning; Proofing; DAM), Nielsen Ad Intel (Competitive Monitoring; Audience Measurement; GRP Tracking), Comscore (Digital Audience Measurement; Cross-Platform Analytics), Mediaocean Prisma (Media Planning; Buying; Billing; Reconciliation), Workday HCM (Core HR; Payroll; Talent Management; Time Tracking)
model.industry_governing_body: Interactive Advertising Bureau (IAB), Media Rating Council (MRC), Advertising Standards Authority (ASA), Federal Trade Commission (FTC) - Advertising Practices Division, European Advertising Standards Alliance (EASA), American Association of Advertising Agencies (4As), Association of National Advertisers (ANA), Trustworthy Accountability Group (TAG), Network Advertising Initiative (NAI), International Chamber of Commerce (ICC) - Advertising and Marketing Communications Code

domains.name: campaign
domains.description: Core operational domain governing the end-to-end advertising campaign lifecycle — from strategy and planning through trafficking, activation, and delivery. Serves as the SSOT for campaign identity, flight dates, objectives, KPIs, targeting parameters, budget allocation, pacing rules, and execution status across all channels and formats.

products.name: campaign
products.description: Master record for an advertising campaign — the authoritative SSOT for campaign identity, objectives, KPIs, flight dates, status, and high-level strategy. Captures campaign name, advertiser/client reference, campaign type (brand awareness, direct response, performance, retention, seasonal, always-on), campaign status (draft, approved, active, paused, completed, cancelled), start and end dates, campaign objective, primary KPI, secondary KPIs, target ROAS, target CPA, target CPM, target CTR, overall budget ceiling, currency, campaign tier, IAB category, campaign brief reference, approval status, approved by, approved date, go-live date, and audit timestamps. Serves as the root entity for all downstream campaign execution, trafficking, and performance data.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the advertising campaign. Primary key.

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertiser or client who owns this campaign.

attributes.name: agency_relationship_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.agency_relationship. Business justification: Campaigns are executed under a specific agency-client relationship governing commission rates, scope of services, and contractual terms. Campaign billing, agency commission calculation, and AOR scope

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Campaigns execute under a Master Service Agreement. Direct FK enables compliance audits ('which MSA governs this campaign?') and contract-level campaign reporting without traversing through insertion

attributes.name: attribution_model_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.attribution_model. Business justification: Campaigns are configured with a specific attribution model that governs how conversions are credited. Campaign setup requires selecting an attribution model (last-touch, data-driven, etc.); the curren

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Campaigns are executed for specific client brands—fundamental relationship for brand-level performance reporting, budget tracking, share-of-voice analysis, and brand portfolio management. Domain exper

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Campaigns are assigned to cost centers for internal P&L reporting and budget ownership. Advertising agencies and advertisers routinely map campaigns to cost centers to track departmental spend. Enable

attributes.name: persona_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.persona. Business justification: Campaign strategy and creative briefing are built around target personas. Advertising planners select a persona during campaign setup to guide messaging, channel selection, and creative development. A

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: A SOW authorizes campaign scope, media budget, and deliverables. Direct FK enables SOW-level budget utilization reporting and scope compliance checks — 'is this campaign executing within its authorize

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Campaigns often contract directly with suppliers (data providers, verification vendors, measurement platforms) for campaign-level services. Essential for vendor cost allocation, performance tracking,

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Current approval status of the campaign in the review workflow: pending, approved, rejected, or revision requested.

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the person who approved the campaign for activation.

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: Date when the campaign was officially approved for execution.

attributes.name: brand_safety_level
attributes.description: Brand safety filtering level applied to the campaign: standard, moderate, strict, or custom. Determines content exclusions.

attributes.name: budget_amount
attributes.description: Total budget allocated for the campaign. Overall spending ceiling across all channels and placements.

attributes.name: campaign_code
attributes.description: External business identifier or code for the campaign, often used in trafficking systems and insertion orders.

attributes.name: campaign_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the advertising campaign used for identification and reporting.

attributes.name: campaign_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the campaign: draft (planning), approved (ready), active (running), paused (temporarily stopped), completed (finished), or cancelled (terminated).

attributes.name: campaign_tier
attributes.description: Strategic importance or priority tier of the campaign within the advertisers portfolio.

attributes.name: campaign_type
attributes.description: Classification of the campaign based on its strategic objective: brand awareness, direct response, performance, retention, seasonal, or always-on.

attributes.name: conversion_window_days
attributes.description: Number of days after an ad interaction during which a conversion can be attributed to the campaign.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the campaign record was first created in the system. Audit field.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the campaign budget and financial metrics.

attributes.name: device_targeting
attributes.description: Device types targeted by the campaign. Comma-separated list such as desktop, mobile, tablet, CTV, OTT.

attributes.name: end_date
attributes.description: The date when the campaign is scheduled to end. Flight end date. Nullable for always-on campaigns.

attributes.name: frequency_cap
attributes.description: Maximum number of times an ad should be shown to the same user within the frequency cap window.

attributes.name: frequency_cap_window
attributes.description: Time window for the frequency cap: per hour, day, week, month, or entire campaign duration.

attributes.name: geo_targeting
attributes.description: Geographic targeting parameters for the campaign. Comma-separated list of countries, regions, cities, or postal codes.

attributes.name: go_live_date
attributes.description: Actual date when the campaign went live and started serving ads. May differ from the planned start date.

attributes.name: iab_category
attributes.description: IAB content taxonomy category for the campaign, used for brand safety and contextual targeting.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the campaign record was last modified. Audit field for tracking changes.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-form notes or comments about the campaign for internal reference and collaboration.

attributes.name: objective
attributes.description: Primary business objective or goal of the campaign, such as increase brand awareness, drive conversions, generate leads, or boost engagement.

attributes.name: pacing_strategy
attributes.description: Budget pacing strategy for the campaign: even (spread evenly), ASAP (spend as fast as possible), custom (user-defined), or dayparting (time-of-day based).

attributes.name: primary_kpi
attributes.description: The main metric used to measure campaign success, such as CTR, CPA, ROAS, impressions, conversions, or reach.

attributes.name: start_date
attributes.description: The date when the campaign is scheduled to begin running. Flight start date.

attributes.name: target_audience_description
attributes.description: Textual description of the target audience for the campaign, including demographic, psychographic, and behavioral characteristics.

attributes.name: target_cpa
attributes.description: Target cost per acquisition or conversion for the campaign. Maximum acceptable cost to acquire one customer or conversion.

attributes.name: target_cpm
attributes.description: Target cost per thousand impressions for the campaign. Used for brand awareness and reach campaigns.

attributes.name: target_ctr
attributes.description: Target click-through rate percentage for the campaign. Represents the expected ratio of clicks to impressions.

attributes.name: target_roas
attributes.description: Target return on ad spend ratio for the campaign. Represents revenue generated per dollar spent on advertising.

attributes.name: viewability_standard
attributes.description: Viewability measurement standard applied to the campaign: MRC (Media Rating Council), GroupM, or custom threshold.

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: User or system identifier of who created the campaign record. Audit field.

products.name: flight
products.description: Represents a discrete time-bounded execution window within a campaign — a campaign flight (also called a campaign burst or wave). Captures flight name, parent campaign reference, flight start date, flight end date, flight status, flight budget allocation, flight objective override, pacing type (even, front-loaded, back-loaded, custom), pacing goal, daypart restrictions, flight priority, channel mix for this flight, and notes. Enables multi-flight campaign structures where a single campaign runs in sequential or overlapping bursts with distinct budgets and targeting parameters. Aligns with Mediaocean Prisma flight-level planning constructs.

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the campaign flight. Primary key.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent campaign under which this flight executes. Links this flight to its overarching campaign strategy.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Individual flights within a campaign can be funded by different cost centers (e.g., different business units or regions). Advertising agencies routinely assign flights to cost centers for sub-campaign

attributes.name: media_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.media_insertion_order. Business justification: A flight's authorized spend and delivery parameters are governed by a specific Media Insertion Order. Billing reconciliation, pacing oversight, and delivery reporting all require tracing which IO fund

attributes.name: milestone_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.project_milestone. Business justification: Campaign flight launch and completion dates are contractual project milestones (often billing triggers). Linking flight to project_milestone enables integrated timeline management where campaign opera

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Flights may contract flight-specific suppliers for verification, brand safety, or measurement services. Required for flight-level vendor cost allocation, reconciliation, and performance evaluation in

attributes.name: audience_segments
attributes.description: Comma-separated list or structured description of audience segments targeted in this flight (e.g., demographic, behavioral, contextual, or custom segments from DMP/CDP).

attributes.name: budget_allocated
attributes.description: The total budget allocated to this flight, representing the financial envelope for all media buys, creative production, and execution costs within this burst period.

attributes.name: budget_currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the flight budget (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

attributes.name: channel_mix
attributes.description: Comma-separated list or structured description of the media channels included in this flight (e.g., 'Display, Video, Social, OOH'). Defines the multi-channel execution strategy for this burst.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this flight record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.

attributes.name: creative_rotation
attributes.description: The strategy for rotating multiple creatives within this flight (even = equal distribution, weighted = custom weights, optimized = algorithm-driven, sequential = fixed order).

attributes.name: daypart_restrictions
attributes.description: Time-of-day or day-of-week restrictions for ad delivery within this flight (e.g., 'Weekdays 6AM-10PM', 'Prime Time Only'). Enables dayparting strategies to optimize audience reach.

attributes.name: device_targeting
attributes.description: Device types targeted for ad delivery in this flight (e.g., 'Desktop, Mobile, Tablet, CTV'). Enables cross-device or device-specific strategies.

attributes.name: end_date
attributes.description: The date when this flight concludes execution. Marks the end of the time-bounded burst window.

attributes.name: flight_code
attributes.description: Externally-known unique code or identifier for this flight, used in trafficking systems, insertion orders, and billing reconciliation.

attributes.name: flight_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name or label for this flight, often indicating the burst period, theme, or seasonal focus (e.g., 'Holiday Wave 1', 'Back-to-School Burst').

attributes.name: flight_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the flight. Indicates whether the flight is in planning, actively running, paused, or concluded.

attributes.name: frequency_cap
attributes.description: Maximum number of times a single user should see an ad from this flight within a defined time window. Prevents ad fatigue and optimizes budget efficiency.

attributes.name: frequency_cap_period
attributes.description: The time window over which the frequency cap is enforced (e.g., per day, per week, for the entire flight duration).

attributes.name: geo_targeting
attributes.description: Geographic targeting parameters for this flight, specifying countries, regions, DMAs, postal codes, or custom geo-fences where ads should be delivered.

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: Username or identifier of the user who last modified this flight record. Supports accountability and audit requirements.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this flight record was last modified. Audit trail for record updates.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or comments about this flight, capturing special instructions, strategic rationale, or operational considerations.

attributes.name: objective
attributes.description: The primary marketing objective for this flight. May override or refine the parent campaign objective for this specific burst period.

attributes.name: pacing_goal
attributes.description: The metric by which pacing is measured and controlled (e.g., impressions delivered, clicks generated, conversions achieved, unique reach, or spend amount).

attributes.name: pacing_type
attributes.description: The budget pacing strategy for this flight. Determines how spend is distributed across the flight duration (even = uniform daily spend, front_loaded = higher spend at start, back_loaded = higher spend at end, custom = user-defined schedule).

attributes.name: priority
attributes.description: Numeric priority ranking for this flight relative to other flights in the same campaign or portfolio. Higher values indicate higher priority for budget allocation and inventory access.

attributes.name: start_date
attributes.description: The date when this flight begins execution. Marks the start of the time-bounded burst window.

attributes.name: target_frequency
attributes.description: The planned average number of times each unique user should be exposed to the ad during this flight. Used to balance reach and repetition.

attributes.name: target_grp
attributes.description: The planned Gross Rating Points for this flight, representing the sum of all rating points delivered. Standard metric for traditional media planning.

attributes.name: target_impressions
attributes.description: The planned number of ad impressions to be delivered during this flight. Used for pacing and performance tracking.

attributes.name: target_reach
attributes.description: The planned number of unique individuals or households to be reached during this flight. Key metric for awareness campaigns.

attributes.name: target_trp
attributes.description: The planned Target Rating Points for this flight, representing GRP adjusted for the specific target audience. Used for audience-specific media planning.

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: Username or identifier of the user who created this flight record. Supports accountability and audit requirements.

products.name: line_item
products.description: Granular execution unit within a campaign flight representing a single buyable unit of media — the atomic record of what is being bought, at what price, on which channel, for which audience. Captures line item name, parent flight reference, campaign reference, channel type (display, video, search, social, OOH, CTV, audio), ad format, placement type, targeting summary, start date, end date, booked impressions, booked clicks, booked views, rate type (CPM, CPC, CPA, CPV, flat), rate amount, net cost, gross cost, currency, IO reference, vendor/publisher reference, trafficking status, delivery status, pacing status, and creative assignment status. The primary unit of media planning and buying execution. Aligns with Google Campaign Manager 360 placement and The Trade Desk ad group constructs.

attributes.name: line_item_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the line item. Primary key for the line item execution unit within a campaign flight.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Video Ad Serving Template (VAST)

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent campaign under which this line item executes. Enables rollup reporting and budget tracking at campaign level.

attributes.name: contract_rate_card_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_card. Business justification: Line items are priced per contracted rate cards. Direct FK enables rate compliance reporting — verifying line_item rate_amount matches the contracted rate card — and supports billing audits. Advertisi

attributes.name: creative_brief_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.creative_brief. Business justification: Media planners link line items to creative briefs to ensure each placement's creative requirements (format, messaging, channel) are fulfilled. This traceability is essential for creative-to-media alig

attributes.name: creative_deliverable_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.creative_deliverable. Business justification: Line items require specific creative deliverables (banner sizes, video lengths) to be produced and trafficked. Linking line items to deliverables enables production-to-media readiness tracking — a nam

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent campaign flight that contains this line item. Establishes the hierarchical relationship between flight and line item.

attributes.name: media_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.media_insertion_order. Business justification: A line item's booked spend is authorized by a Media Insertion Order. Finance reconciliation, billing, and delivery reporting require tracing each line item to its governing IO. The existing contract_i

attributes.name: plan_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.plan_line. Business justification: A line item is the execution-layer realization of a media plan line. Plan-vs-actual delivery reporting — a core media accountability process — requires linking each executed line item back to the plan

attributes.name: programmatic_deal_id
attributes.description: The unique identifier for a programmatic deal (PMP, preferred deal, or programmatic guaranteed) associated with this line item. Nullable for open exchange buys.

attributes.name: spec_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.spec. Business justification: Line items define placement requirements (ad format, dimensions, IAB unit) that must match a creative spec. Trafficking teams validate assigned creatives against the line item's spec before go-live. T

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Line items frequently use suppliers beyond publishers (ad verification, measurement partners, data providers). Critical for line-item-level vendor reconciliation, cost attribution, and compliance trac

attributes.name: trafficking_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.trafficking_order. Business justification: Line items are trafficked to ad servers via trafficking orders. Currently no FK exists to track which trafficking order activated a specific line item. This is essential for operational tracking and a

attributes.name: vendor_publisher_id
attributes.description: Reference to the media vendor or publisher delivering this line item inventory. Links to vendor master for billing and performance tracking.

attributes.name: work_package_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.work_package. Business justification: In advertising agencies, each line item's production and trafficking execution is managed as a work package. Linking enables project managers to track effort, cost, and status per media buy — essentia

attributes.name: ad_format
attributes.description: The creative format specification for this line item (e.g., 300x250 banner, 15-second pre-roll, sponsored post). Defines the deliverable unit.

attributes.name: agency_commission_rate
attributes.description: The commission percentage applied to this line item. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15%). Used to calculate net from gross cost.

attributes.name: booked_clicks
attributes.description: The contracted number of clicks to be delivered for this line item. Primary delivery goal for click-based buys.

attributes.name: booked_conversions
attributes.description: The contracted number of conversions or acquisitions to be delivered for this line item. Primary delivery goal for performance-based buys.

attributes.name: booked_impressions
attributes.description: The contracted number of impressions to be delivered for this line item. Primary delivery goal for impression-based buys.

attributes.name: booked_views
attributes.description: The contracted number of video views or viewable impressions to be delivered for this line item. Primary delivery goal for video and viewability-based buys.

attributes.name: brand_safety_tier
attributes.description: The level of brand safety filtering applied to this line item. Determines which content categories and contexts are excluded.

attributes.name: channel_type
attributes.description: The media channel through which this line item will be executed. Determines platform, format options, and measurement methodology. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: display|video|search|social|ooh|dooh|ctv|ott|audio|native|email|affiliate — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this line item record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and lifecycle tracking.

attributes.name: creative_assignment_status
attributes.description: The status of creative asset assignment and approval for this line item. Indicates whether creatives are ready for serving.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values on this line item. Enables multi-currency campaign management.

attributes.name: delivery_status
attributes.description: The current delivery performance status of the line item. Indicates whether the line item is meeting, exceeding, or falling short of delivery goals. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|delivering|under_delivering|over_delivering|completed|paused|stopped — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: The Trade Desk Delivery Monitoring Standard

attributes.name: end_date
attributes.description: The date when this line item stops serving. Defines the end of the delivery window. Nullable for evergreen line items.

attributes.name: frequency_cap_impressions
attributes.description: The maximum number of impressions a unique user can receive for this line item within the frequency cap period. Used to control ad exposure.

attributes.name: frequency_cap_period
attributes.description: The time period over which the frequency cap is applied. Defines the window for impression counting.

attributes.name: gross_cost
attributes.description: The gross media cost for this line item before agency commission. Represents the total amount billed to the client.

attributes.name: is_programmatic
attributes.description: Indicates whether this line item is executed through programmatic buying platforms (DSP) or through direct/manual insertion orders.
attributes.references: IAB Programmatic Supply Chain Transparency Standard

attributes.name: line_item_code
attributes.description: External business identifier or SKU for the line item. Used for trafficking, billing reconciliation, and cross-system reference.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Line Item Reference Standard

attributes.name: line_item_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name for the line item. Typically includes targeting, format, and channel descriptors for operational clarity.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 Placement Naming Convention

attributes.name: line_sequence
attributes.description: Ordinal position of this line item within the parent flight. Used for display ordering and operational sequencing.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this line item record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trail.

attributes.name: net_cost
attributes.description: The net media cost for this line item after agency commission and discounts. Used for internal P&L and margin analysis.

attributes.name: pacing_status
attributes.description: The current pacing health of the line item relative to its delivery goal and timeline. Used for proactive delivery management.
attributes.references: The Trade Desk Delivery Monitoring Standard

attributes.name: pacing_strategy
attributes.description: The delivery pacing strategy for this line item. Determines how impressions are distributed across the flight period.
attributes.references: The Trade Desk Bid Management Specification

attributes.name: placement_type
attributes.description: The buying model or inventory type for this line item. Indicates whether inventory is reserved, auction-based, or programmatic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: guaranteed|non-guaranteed|programmatic_guaranteed|preferred_deal|private_auction|open_exchange|direct — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Programmatic Supply Chain Transparency Standard

attributes.name: priority
attributes.description: The delivery priority of this line item relative to other line items competing for the same inventory. Higher values indicate higher priority.

attributes.name: rate_amount
attributes.description: The unit rate for this line item based on the rate type. For CPM, this is cost per thousand impressions; for CPC, cost per click; for flat, total cost.

attributes.name: rate_type
attributes.description: The pricing model for this line item. Determines how cost is calculated based on delivery metrics (Cost Per Mille, Cost Per Click, Cost Per Acquisition, Cost Per View, flat fee). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cpm|cpc|cpa|cpv|cpe|flat|cpcv|vcpm — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: start_date
attributes.description: The date when this line item begins serving. Defines the start of the delivery window.

attributes.name: targeting_summary
attributes.description: High-level summary of audience targeting parameters applied to this line item (demographics, geography, behavior, context). Detailed targeting stored in related tables.
attributes.references: The Trade Desk Audience Targeting Specification

attributes.name: trafficking_status
attributes.description: The operational status of line item setup and creative trafficking. Indicates readiness for delivery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_started|in_progress|ready_to_serve|live|paused|completed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: viewability_target_percentage
attributes.description: The target percentage of impressions that must meet viewability standards (MRC definition: 50% of pixels in view for 1 second for display, 2 seconds for video).

products.name: ad
products.description: Individual ad unit record representing a specific creative execution trafficked and served within a campaign line item. Captures ad name, parent line item reference, campaign reference, ad type (banner, video, native, rich media, audio, DOOH), ad format dimensions (width x height), VAST/VPAID tag reference, click-through URL, impression tracker URL, view tracker URL, creative asset reference, ad status (draft, pending review, approved, rejected, live, paused, archived), trafficking status, ad server ad ID (CM360), go-live date, end date, frequency cap, frequency cap period, and audit timestamps. Bridges the campaign execution layer to the creative asset layer. Aligns with Google Campaign Manager 360 ad trafficking entities.

attributes.name: ad_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the ad unit. Primary key.

attributes.name: asset_id
attributes.description: Reference to the creative asset (image, video, HTML5, audio) used in this ad execution.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent campaign to which this ad belongs.

attributes.name: creative_assignment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.creative_assignment. Business justification: An ad unit is the trafficked execution of a creative assignment — it represents the specific creative asset assigned to a line item being served. Adding creative_assignment_id to ad creates a direct,

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.flight. Business justification: An ad is executed within a flight's time window and targeting context. While flight can be derived via ad → line_item → flight, a direct flight_id on ad is operationally valuable for ad-server pacing,

attributes.name: line_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent campaign line item under which this ad is trafficked.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 Ad Trafficking Model

attributes.name: media_placement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.media_placement. Business justification: An ad runs within a specific contracted media placement (inventory slot on a publisher property). Delivery verification, viewability measurement, and brand safety compliance reporting all require know

attributes.name: spec_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.spec. Business justification: Each ad unit must conform to a technical spec (file format, dimensions, duration, VAST requirements) for its placement. Ad servers validate ads against specs before trafficking go-live. This link enab

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Individual ads may use specific suppliers for dynamic creative optimization, ad serving, or verification services. Required for ad-level vendor cost tracking, performance attribution, and troubleshoot

attributes.name: tech_partner_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.tech_partner. Business justification: Ad serving and third-party tracking assignment: each ad is delivered via a specific tech partner (ad server/verification vendor). ad carries vast_tag_url, vpaid_enabled, third_party_tracking_enabled,

attributes.name: trafficking_instruction_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.trafficking_instruction. Business justification: Each ad unit is governed by a trafficking instruction specifying ad-server tags, pixels, VAST URLs, and targeting directives. attributes.name: ad_type

attributes.name: weight

attributes.name: created_by

products.name: targeting_rule
products.description: Defines the audience, contextual, and brand safety targeting parameters applied to a campaign, flight, or line item. Captures targeting rule name, parent entity type (campaign/flight/line_item), parent entity reference, targeting dimension (geography, demographic, behavioral, contextual, keyword, device, daypart, audience segment, retargeting, lookalike, brand_safety_content_exclusion, brand_safety_keyword_block, brand_safety_domain_block, brand_safety_category_exclusion), targeting operator (include/exclude), targeting value, targeting value type, data source (first-party, second-party, third-party, DMP, CDP), audience segment reference, geo level (country, region, DMA, city, postal code), device type, OS type, browser type, connection type, language, daypart schedule, frequency cap limit, frequency cap window, frequency cap unit, brand safety tier (TAG certified, GARM standard), viewability standard, fraud prevention threshold, GDPR consent required flag, CCPA opt-out flag, recency window, and is_active flag. Enables precise audience targeting governance and brand safety enforcement across programmatic and direct channels.

attributes.name: targeting_rule_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the targeting rule. Primary key.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: The identifier of the parent entity (campaign, flight, or line_item) to which this targeting rule applies.

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.flight. Business justification: targeting_rule has parent_entity_type attribute indicating it can apply to campaign, flight, or line_item. Currently only has campaign_id FK. Adding flight_id (nullable) to support flight-level target

attributes.name: line_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.line_item. Business justification: targeting_rule has parent_entity_type attribute indicating it can apply to campaign, flight, or line_item. Currently only has campaign_id FK. Adding line_item_id (nullable) to support line_item-level

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Reference to the audience segment when targeting dimension is audience_segment, retargeting, or lookalike.

attributes.name: brand_safety_tier
attributes.description: The brand safety standard applied: Trustworthy Accountability Group (TAG) certified, Global Alliance for Responsible Media (GARM) standard, or custom.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certification; GARM Brand Safety Framework

attributes.name: browser_type
attributes.description: The web browser targeted (e.g., Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge).

attributes.name: ccpa_opt_out_flag
attributes.business_glossary_term: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) Opt-Out Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this targeting rule respects CCPA opt-out requests (Do Not Sell My Personal Information).
attributes.references: CCPA Section 1798.120; IAB CCPA Compliance Framework

attributes.name: connection_type
attributes.description: The type of network connection targeted: WiFi, cellular, ethernet, or unknown.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this targeting rule record was first created in the system.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Data Transparency Standard

attributes.name: data_source
attributes.description: The origin of the targeting data: first-party (owned), second-party (partner), third-party (purchased), Data Management Platform (DMP), or Customer Data Platform (CDP).
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Data Transparency Standard

attributes.name: daypart_schedule
attributes.description: The time-of-day or day-of-week schedule for ad delivery, specified in a structured format (e.g., JSON or cron-like expression).

attributes.name: device_type
attributes.description: The type of device targeted: desktop, mobile, tablet, Connected TV (CTV), Over-the-Top (OTT), or wearable.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date when this targeting rule expires and is no longer applied. Null indicates no expiration.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date when this targeting rule becomes active and begins to be applied.

attributes.name: fraud_prevention_threshold
attributes.description: The minimum fraud detection score (0-100) required for ad delivery, used to filter out suspected invalid traffic.

attributes.name: frequency_cap_limit
attributes.description: The maximum number of times an ad can be shown to a user within the specified frequency cap window.

attributes.name: frequency_cap_unit
attributes.description: The time unit for the frequency cap window: hour, day, week, month, or lifetime.

attributes.name: frequency_cap_window
attributes.description: The time window (in the unit specified by frequency_cap_unit) over which the frequency cap limit applies.

attributes.name: gdpr_consent_required
attributes.business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Consent Required
attributes.description: Indicates whether GDPR user consent is required for this targeting rule to be applied.

attributes.name: geo_level
attributes.description: The granularity of geographic targeting: country, region/state, Designated Market Area (DMA), city, or postal code.

attributes.name: is_active
attributes.description: Indicates whether this targeting rule is currently active and being applied to ad delivery.

attributes.name: language
attributes.description: The language targeted, specified as ISO 639-1 two-letter language code (e.g., en, es, fr).

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: The user or system identifier that last modified this targeting rule.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Data Transparency Standard

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this targeting rule record was last modified.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Data Transparency Standard

attributes.name: os_type
attributes.description: The operating system targeted (e.g., iOS, Android, Windows, macOS, tvOS).

attributes.name: parent_entity_type
attributes.description: The type of entity to which this targeting rule is applied: campaign, flight, or line_item.

attributes.name: priority
attributes.description: The priority or precedence of this targeting rule when multiple rules apply to the same entity. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.

attributes.name: recency_window
attributes.description: The time window (in days) for behavioral or retargeting data recency, specifying how recent user activity must be to qualify for targeting.

attributes.name: rule_name

attributes.name: targeting_dimension
attributes.description: The dimension or category of targeting applied by this rule. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: geography|demographic|behavioral|contextual|keyword|device|daypart|audience_segment|retargeting|lookalike|brand_safety_content_exclusion|brand_safety_keyword_block|brand_safety_domain_block|brand_safety_category_exclusion|frequency_cap|viewability|fraud_prevention — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: targeting_operator

attributes.name: targeting_value

attributes.name: targeting_value_type

products.name: budget_allocation
products.description: Unified financial and delivery control record governing how campaign budget is distributed AND how delivery is paced across flights, line items, channels, and time periods. Captures allocation reference, parent campaign reference, parent flight reference, line item reference, channel type, budget period (monthly, weekly, flight), planned budget amount, revised budget amount, committed spend, actual spend to date, remaining budget, currency, budget source (client-approved, agency-estimated), allocation method (percentage, fixed, dynamic), pacing rule name, pacing type (even, accelerated, front-loaded, back-loaded, custom curve), daily impression goal, daily spend cap, weekly impression goal, weekly spend cap, pacing check frequency (hourly, daily), over-delivery action (pause, throttle, alert), under-delivery action (boost, alert), pacing alert threshold percentage, over-delivery tolerance percentage, under-delivery tolerance percentage, auto-optimization enabled flag, optimization algorithm reference, effective date range, last reconciliation date, and reconciliation status. Serves as the single SSOT for budget allocation decisions, delivery pacing strategy, and pacing guardrails — practitioners manage budget and pacing as a unified workflow because pacing cannot be set independently of budget constraints. Subsumes all pacing rule definitions previously modeled separately.

attributes.name: budget_allocation_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the budget allocation record. Primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent campaign for which budget is being allocated.

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_insertion_order. Business justification: Budget allocations must reconcile against IO-authorized spend (total_authorized_spend). Direct FK enables IO-level budget utilization reports — comparing allocated/actual spend against IO commitments

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Campaign budget allocations must be charged to specific cost centers for internal accounting. In advertising agencies, each budget allocation (by channel, flight, or line item) maps to a cost center f

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent flight within the campaign for which budget is allocated.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Budget allocations in advertising are posted to GL accounts (e.g., media spend, production costs, agency fees) for financial reporting and audit compliance. A gl_account_id FK enables proper GL postin

attributes.name: line_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific line item receiving this budget allocation.

attributes.name: pacing_rule_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.pacing_rule. Business justification: budget_allocation currently has pacing_rule_name (STRING) which is a denormalized reference. Should normalize to FK relationship with pacing_rule.pacing_rule_id. This enables joining to get full pacin

attributes.name: project_budget_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.project_budget. Business justification: Finance reconciliation in advertising agencies requires matching campaign media spend allocations against project budgets (which include labor, production, and media). This link enables the CFO-level

attributes.name: actual_spend
attributes.description: The actual amount spent to date against this budget allocation, based on delivered impressions and clicks.

attributes.name: allocation_method
attributes.description: The method used to distribute budget across periods or channels (e.g., percentage-based, fixed amount, dynamic optimization).

attributes.name: allocation_reference
attributes.description: Business-facing reference number or code for this budget allocation, used for tracking and reconciliation.

attributes.name: allocation_status
attributes.description: The current lifecycle status of this budget allocation (e.g., draft, active, paused, completed, cancelled).

attributes.name: auto_optimization_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether automated optimization algorithms are enabled to adjust bids and budgets dynamically based on performance.

attributes.name: budget_period_type
attributes.description: The time period granularity for which this budget allocation applies (e.g., monthly, weekly, flight-level).

attributes.name: budget_source
attributes.description: The origin or authority for this budget allocation (e.g., client-approved, agency-estimated, internal forecast).

attributes.name: channel_type
attributes.description: The advertising channel or media type for which this budget is allocated. CTV (Connected TV), OTT (Over-the-Top), DOOH (Digital Out-of-Home), OOH (Out-of-Home). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: display|video|social|search|audio|native|ctv|ott|dooh|ooh|print|radio|tv — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: committed_spend
attributes.description: The amount of budget that has been committed through insertion orders or programmatic deals but not yet spent.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this budget allocation record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this allocation (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

attributes.name: daily_impression_goal
attributes.description: The target number of impressions to be delivered per day under the pacing rule.

attributes.name: daily_spend_cap
attributes.description: The maximum amount that can be spent per day for this allocation, enforced by the pacing rule.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date when this budget allocation expires and delivery must stop. Null for open-ended allocations.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date when this budget allocation becomes active and delivery can begin.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this budget allocation record was last updated or modified.

attributes.name: last_reconciliation_date
attributes.description: The date when this budget allocation was last reconciled against actual spend and delivery data from ad servers and billing systems.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or comments about this budget allocation, including rationale for revisions, special instructions, or reconciliation findings.

attributes.name: optimization_algorithm_reference
attributes.description: Reference to the specific optimization algorithm or strategy applied to this allocation (e.g., CPA optimization, ROAS maximization).

attributes.name: over_delivery_action
attributes.description: The action to take when delivery exceeds the pacing goal or budget cap (e.g., pause campaign, throttle bids, send alert).

attributes.name: over_delivery_tolerance_pct
attributes.description: The acceptable percentage over-delivery before corrective action is taken (e.g., 5.00 means 5% over-delivery is tolerated).

attributes.name: pacing_alert_threshold_pct
attributes.description: The percentage deviation from the pacing goal that triggers an alert to campaign managers (e.g., 10.00 means alert at 10% deviation).

attributes.name: pacing_check_frequency
attributes.description: How frequently the pacing engine evaluates delivery performance and adjusts bid strategies (e.g., real-time, hourly, daily).

attributes.name: pacing_type
attributes.description: The pacing strategy governing how budget and impressions are delivered over time (e.g., even, accelerated, front-loaded, back-loaded, custom curve).

attributes.name: planned_budget_amount
attributes.description: The original planned budget amount allocated for this period and channel, as approved in the initial media plan.

attributes.name: reconciliation_status
attributes.description: The current status of the budget reconciliation process for this allocation (e.g., pending, reconciled, discrepancy found, approved).

attributes.name: remaining_budget
attributes.description: The remaining unspent budget available for this allocation, calculated as revised (or planned) budget minus actual spend and committed spend.

attributes.name: revised_budget_amount
attributes.description: The revised budget amount after any adjustments, reallocations, or change orders. Null if no revision has occurred.

attributes.name: under_delivery_action
attributes.description: The action to take when delivery falls short of the pacing goal (e.g., boost bids, increase budget allocation, send alert).

attributes.name: under_delivery_tolerance_pct
attributes.description: The acceptable percentage under-delivery before corrective action is taken (e.g., 5.00 means 5% under-delivery is tolerated).

attributes.name: weekly_impression_goal
attributes.description: The target number of impressions to be delivered per week under the pacing rule.

attributes.name: weekly_spend_cap
attributes.description: The maximum amount that can be spent per week for this allocation, enforced by the pacing rule.

products.name: pacing_rule
products.description: Defines the delivery pacing strategy and guardrails for a campaign, flight, or line item. Captures pacing rule name, parent entity type, parent entity reference, pacing type (even, accelerated, front-loaded, back-loaded, custom curve), daily impression goal, daily spend cap, weekly impression goal, weekly spend cap, pacing check frequency (hourly, daily), over-delivery action (pause, throttle, alert), under-delivery action (boost, alert), pacing alert threshold percentage, auto-optimization enabled flag, optimization algorithm reference, and effective date range. Governs how campaign delivery is distributed over time to meet KPIs and avoid over/under-spend.
products.reference: The Trade Desk Bid Management Pacing

attributes.name: pacing_rule_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the pacing rule record. Primary key.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent campaign entity to which this pacing rule applies.

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent flight entity to which this pacing rule applies, if applicable.

attributes.name: line_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent line item entity to which this pacing rule applies, if applicable.

attributes.name: auto_optimization_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether automated optimization algorithms are enabled to adjust pacing dynamically based on performance data.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this pacing rule record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this pacing rule (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

attributes.name: custom_curve_definition
attributes.description: JSON or structured definition of a custom pacing curve when pacing_type is set to custom_curve. Defines delivery distribution over time.

attributes.name: daily_impression_goal
attributes.description: Target number of impressions to be delivered per day under this pacing rule.

attributes.name: daily_spend_cap
attributes.description: Maximum spend allowed per day under this pacing rule, expressed in the campaign currency.

attributes.name: dayparting_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether dayparting (time-of-day targeting) is applied as part of the pacing strategy.

attributes.name: dayparting_schedule
attributes.description: Structured definition of the dayparting schedule (e.g., JSON or cron-like format) specifying hours and days when delivery is allowed or prioritized.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which this pacing rule ceases to be active. Null indicates an open-ended rule.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date from which this pacing rule becomes active and begins governing delivery.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this pacing rule record was last updated.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or comments about this pacing rule, used for documentation and operational context.

attributes.name: over_delivery_action
attributes.description: The action to be taken when delivery exceeds the pacing goal or cap (e.g., pause delivery, throttle impressions, send alert).

attributes.name: pacing_alert_threshold_pct
attributes.description: The percentage deviation from the pacing goal that triggers an alert to campaign managers (e.g., 10.00 means alert if delivery is 10% off target).

attributes.name: pacing_check_frequency
attributes.description: The frequency at which the pacing engine evaluates delivery progress against goals and applies throttling or boosting actions.

attributes.name: pacing_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the pacing rule indicating whether it is actively governing delivery.

attributes.name: pacing_type
attributes.description: The delivery pacing strategy applied to the campaign or line item. Defines how impressions or spend are distributed over the flight period.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) Digital Ad Operations Standard

attributes.name: parent_entity_type
attributes.description: The type of parent entity to which this pacing rule is attached (e.g., campaign, flight, line item, insertion order).

attributes.name: priority_level
attributes.description: Numeric priority level for this pacing rule when multiple rules apply to the same entity. Higher values indicate higher priority.

attributes.name: rule_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name or label for the pacing rule, used for identification and reporting purposes.

attributes.name: timezone
attributes.description: IANA timezone identifier (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London) used for interpreting pacing schedules and dayparting rules.

attributes.name: under_delivery_action
attributes.description: The action to be taken when delivery falls short of the pacing goal (e.g., boost bid, send alert, reallocate budget).

attributes.name: weekly_impression_goal
attributes.description: Target number of impressions to be delivered per week under this pacing rule.

attributes.name: weekly_spend_cap
attributes.description: Maximum spend allowed per week under this pacing rule, expressed in the campaign currency.

products.name: trafficking_order
products.description: Represents the formal trafficking instruction set issued to an ad server or DSP to activate campaign line items and ads. Captures trafficking order number, parent campaign reference, parent flight reference, ad server platform (CM360, The Trade Desk, etc.), trafficking status (pending, submitted, in-progress, live, completed, rejected), submitted by, submitted date, go-live date, trafficking notes, QA status, QA completed by, QA completed date, number of line items trafficked, number of ads trafficked, VAST tag validation status, pixel firing validation status, and audit timestamps. Represents the operational handoff from planning to execution — a distinct business workflow from the campaign plan itself.

attributes.name: trafficking_order_id

attributes.name: campaign_id

attributes.references: IAB Digital Ad Operations Best Practices

attributes.name: contract_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_insertion_order. Business justification: Trafficking orders execute the media buys authorized by insertion orders. Direct FK enables IO-level trafficking status reporting and billing reconciliation — 'which IO does this trafficking order ful

attributes.description: Reference to the parent flight or media schedule within the campaign that this trafficking order activates.

attributes.name: parent_trafficking_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the original trafficking order if this is a revision or amendment, enabling tracking of the full order history and change lineage.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Trafficking orders involve third-party ad servers or trafficking platforms (suppliers). Essential for tracking which ad server/platform was used, critical for troubleshooting, billing, and vendor perf

attributes.name: task_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.task. Business justification: Trafficking orders are executed as project tasks with SLA commitments. Linking enables project managers to track trafficking task completion against campaign go-live dates, measure SLA adherence, and

attributes.name: tech_partner_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.tech_partner. Business justification: Trafficking orders are submitted to a specific ad server platform (tech partner). trafficking_order.ad_server_platform is a denormalized string identifying the tech partner; replacing it with a proper

attributes.name: trafficking_instruction_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.trafficking_instruction. Business justification: A trafficking order (internal workflow) generates trafficking instructions (ad-server directives). Linking them enables end-to-end trafficking workflow tracking: from internal order creation through a

attributes.name: actual_live_timestamp
attributes.description: Actual date and time when the trafficking order went live and ads began serving, as confirmed by the ad server platform.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Ad Operations Best Practices

attributes.name: ad_server_order_code
attributes.description: External order or campaign identifier assigned by the ad server platform (e.g., CM360 order ID, Trade Desk campaign ID) for cross-system reconciliation.

attributes.name: ads_trafficked_count
attributes.description: Total number of individual ad creatives that were trafficked and uploaded to the ad server as part of this trafficking order.

attributes.name: go_live_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date when the trafficked campaign line items and ads are expected to begin serving live impressions.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp

attributes.name: line_items_trafficked_count

attributes.name: pixel_firing_validation_status

attributes.references: IAB Digital Ad Operations Best Practices

attributes.name: sla_target_hours
attributes.description: Target number of hours within which the trafficking order should be completed from submission to go-live, as defined by internal SLA policies.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Ad Operations Best Practices

attributes.name: submitted_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the trafficking order was submitted to the ad server or DSP for processing and activation.

attributes.name: trafficking_notes
attributes.description: Free-text operational notes and instructions provided by the trafficking team regarding special setup requirements, targeting parameters, creative specifications, or platform-specific configurations.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Ad Operations Best Practices

attributes.name: trafficking_order_number
attributes.description: Business-facing unique trafficking order number used for operational reference and communication with ad ops teams and external platforms.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Ad Operations Best Practices

attributes.name: trafficking_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the trafficking order indicating its progress through the ad operations workflow from submission to go-live. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|submitted|in_progress|live|completed|rejected|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Digital Ad Operations Best Practices

attributes.name: trafficking_type
attributes.description: Classification of the trafficking order based on the campaign buying model and activation method (standard direct IO, programmatic guaranteed, sponsorship, etc.).
attributes.references: IAB Digital Ad Operations Best Practices

attributes.name: vast_tag_validation_status
attributes.business_glossary_term: Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) Tag Validation Status
attributes.description: Validation status of VAST tags for video ad creatives, indicating whether the tags conform to IAB VAST specifications and are ready for serving.

products.name: campaign_kpi_target
products.description: Defines the specific, measurable KPI targets set for a campaign, flight, or line item at the time of planning and as revised during execution. Captures KPI target ID, parent entity type (campaign/flight/line_item), parent entity reference, KPI type (impressions, clicks, CTR, conversions, CPA, CPM, CPC, ROAS, VTR, GRP, TRP, SOV, reach, frequency, NPS), target value, target unit, target period (total, daily, weekly, monthly), baseline value, stretch target value, measurement methodology, measurement source (Nielsen, Comscore, CM360, GA, custom), reporting cadence, is_primary_kpi flag, and effective date range. Enables structured KPI governance and performance benchmarking across all campaign types.

attributes.name: campaign_kpi_target_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Campaign Key Performance Indicator (KPI) Target Identifier
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the campaign KPI target record. Primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the campaign when parent_entity_type is 'campaign'. Null for flight or line_item parent types.

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Reference to the flight when parent_entity_type is 'flight'. Null for campaign or line_item parent types.

attributes.name: line_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the line item when parent_entity_type is 'line_item'. Null for campaign or flight parent types.

attributes.name: sla_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sla. Business justification: Campaign KPI targets must be traceable to contractual SLA commitments (e.g., viewability thresholds, delivery rates). Direct FK enables SLA compliance reporting — 'did campaign performance meet contra

attributes.name: target_audience_segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: KPI targets are frequently set for specific audience segments (e.g., 'achieve 8% conversion rate among in-market auto intenders segment'). Performance reporting and optimization decisions require meas

attributes.name: alert_threshold_percentage
attributes.description: The percentage deviation from target that triggers a performance alert. Used for automated monitoring and optimization workflows.

attributes.name: baseline_value
attributes.description: The baseline or minimum acceptable value for this KPI. Used to establish performance thresholds below which the campaign is considered underperforming.

attributes.name: benchmark_source
attributes.description: The source of industry benchmark data used to establish this target (e.g., Nielsen norms, IAB benchmarks, historical campaign data).

attributes.name: benchmark_value
attributes.description: The industry or historical benchmark value for this KPI type. Used for comparative performance analysis.

attributes.name: channel_scope
attributes.description: The media channel or platform for which this KPI target applies (e.g., display, video, social, search). Allows for channel-specific targets within multi-channel campaigns.

attributes.name: confidence_level
attributes.description: The level of confidence in achieving this target based on historical performance, market conditions, and campaign parameters.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this KPI target record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for monetary KPI targets (CPA, CPM, CPC, ROAS). Required when target_unit is 'currency'.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date through which this KPI target remains active. Null indicates the target is active through the end of the parent entity's duration.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date from which this KPI target becomes active and should be measured. Allows for phased KPI introduction or mid-campaign target changes.

attributes.name: geographic_scope
attributes.description: The geographic region or market for which this KPI target applies. Enables market-specific performance targets.

attributes.name: is_primary_kpi
attributes.business_glossary_term: Is Primary Key Performance Indicator (KPI) Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this is the primary KPI for the parent entity. True if this is the main success metric; false for secondary or supporting KPIs.

attributes.name: kpi_type
attributes.description: The specific KPI metric being targeted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: impressions|clicks|ctr|conversions|cpa|cpm|cpc|roas|vtr|grp|trp|sov|reach|frequency|nps|viewability|completion_rate|engagement_rate — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: measurement_methodology
attributes.description: Description of the methodology used to measure and calculate this KPI. Includes attribution model, measurement window, deduplication rules, and any custom calculation logic.
attributes.references: MRC Measurement Standards; IAB Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: measurement_source
attributes.description: The system or platform that will provide the actual measurement data for this KPI. Determines the source of truth for performance reporting.

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: The user or system that last modified this KPI target record. Supports change tracking and accountability.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this KPI target record was last modified in the system.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional notes, context, or special instructions related to this KPI target. Captures nuances not covered by structured fields.

attributes.name: optimization_priority
attributes.description: The priority level for optimization efforts when this KPI is underperforming. Guides resource allocation for campaign management.

attributes.name: parent_entity_type
attributes.description: The type of parent entity to which this KPI target is attached. Determines the scope of the KPI measurement (campaign-level, flight-level, or line-item-level).

attributes.name: reporting_cadence
attributes.description: The frequency at which this KPI should be measured and reported. Determines the monitoring and optimization cycle for this metric.

attributes.name: revision_reason
attributes.description: Business justification for the most recent revision to this KPI target. Captures why the target was changed mid-campaign.

attributes.name: stretch_target_value
attributes.description: The aspirational or stretch goal value for this KPI. Represents exceptional performance beyond the standard target.

attributes.name: target_audience_segment
attributes.description: The specific audience segment for which this KPI target applies. Allows for segment-specific performance targets within a single campaign.

attributes.name: target_period
attributes.description: The time period over which the target should be achieved. 'Total' indicates the target is for the entire campaign/flight/line_item duration.

attributes.name: target_revision_number
attributes.description: Version number tracking revisions to this KPI target. Increments each time the target is modified during campaign execution.

attributes.name: target_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the KPI target. Tracks whether the target is in planning, actively being measured, or has been completed or cancelled.

attributes.name: target_unit
attributes.description: The unit of measure for the target_value. Defines how the target should be interpreted (absolute count, percentage, currency amount, or rating point).

attributes.name: target_value
attributes.description: The numeric target value for the KPI. Interpretation depends on kpi_type and target_unit (e.g., 1000000 impressions, 2.5% CTR, $50 CPA).

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: The user or system that created this KPI target record. Supports audit trail and accountability.

products.name: creative_assignment
products.description: This association product represents the assignment of creative assets to line items for media execution. It captures the operational relationship between what is being bought (line item) and what creative content is served (creative asset). Each record links one line item to one creative asset with attributes that govern rotation, scheduling, trafficking, and performance tracking for that specific pairing.. Existence Justification: In advertising operations, line items routinely serve multiple creative assets simultaneously through rotation strategies (A/B testing, dynamic creative optimization, sequential messaging). Each creative asset is reused across multiple line items spanning different campaigns, flights, and targeting strategies. The business actively manages creative assignments as operational records with rotation weights, scheduling windows, trafficking status, and performance metrics tracked per line-item-asset pairing.

attributes.name: creative_assignment_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for this creative assignment record. Primary key.

attributes.name: asset_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to the creative asset assigned to this line item

attributes.name: deliverable_tracker_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.deliverable_tracker. Business justification: A creative assignment (assigning a produced asset to a line item) is the downstream outcome of a creative production deliverable. Linking enables agencies to trace from project deliverable through to

attributes.name: line_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to the line item that will serve this creative asset

attributes.name: media_placement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.media_placement. Business justification: A creative assignment maps a creative asset to a specific media placement for delivery. Placement-level creative performance reporting and brand safety verification per placement require this direct l

attributes.name: assignment_created_date
attributes.description: Timestamp when this creative assignment was created in the system. Audit trail for trafficking workflow.

attributes.name: click_count
attributes.description: The number of clicks generated by this specific creative asset on this specific line item. Performance metric tracked at the assignment level.

attributes.name: conversion_count
attributes.description: The number of conversions attributed to this specific creative asset on this specific line item. Performance metric for conversion-based campaigns.

attributes.name: ctr
attributes.description: Click-through rate for this creative-line item pairing, calculated as (clicks / impressions). Performance metric for creative effectiveness.

attributes.name: end_date
attributes.description: The date when this creative asset stops serving on this line item. Supports flight-based creative strategies and seasonal messaging.

attributes.name: impression_count
attributes.description: The number of impressions delivered for this specific creative asset on this specific line item. Performance metric tracked at the assignment level.

attributes.name: last_modified_date
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this assignment record. Supports change tracking and audit.

attributes.name: priority_rank
attributes.description: The priority or sequence order of this creative within the line item's rotation. Used when rotation is not purely weight-based but follows a priority waterfall.

attributes.name: rotation_weight
attributes.description: The percentage or weight assigned to this creative asset within the line item's creative rotation. Used for A/B testing and dynamic creative optimization. Sum of weights across all assignments for a line item typically equals 100.

attributes.name: start_date
attributes.description: The date when this creative asset begins serving on this line item. Enables sequential messaging and time-based creative rotation strategies.

attributes.name: trafficking_status
attributes.description: The operational status of this specific creative-to-line-item assignment in the ad serving platform. Indicates whether the creative has been successfully trafficked and is ready to serve.

attributes.name: view_count
attributes.description: The number of video views or viewable impressions for this specific creative asset on this specific line item. Applicable to video and viewability-measured formats.

domains.name: client
domains.description: Authoritative SSOT for all advertiser identity, account hierarchy, and relationship data. Manages client profiles, brand portfolios, account teams, contact information, business segments, relationship history, and CRM records for both direct advertisers and agency-managed accounts. Supports Account Management and Client Services.
domains.references: 4As Client Management Standards / Salesforce CRM Account Model

products.name: advertiser
products.description: Master record for every advertiser (direct client or agency-managed brand) that the agency serves. Captures authoritative identity including legal entity name, trading name, industry vertical (IAB taxonomy), business segment, holding company affiliation, CRM account ID (Salesforce), ERP customer number (SAP), tax identifiers (VAT/EIN), incorporation jurisdiction, credit terms, payment terms, preferred currency, account status (prospect, active, dormant, churned), account tier, and all engagement preferences (preferred communication channels, reporting cadence, invoice delivery method, language preference, time zone, approval workflow preferences, GDPR/CCPA data sharing consent flags). Also stores structured address records (registered office, billing address, shipping address, regional offices) with address type, full postal details, country (ISO 3166), geocoordinates, and validation status. This is the SSOT for advertiser identity, commercial configuration, engagement preferences, and physical/mailing addresses across the entire data model.

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the advertiser record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the advertiser master data product. Role: MASTER_PARTY.

attributes.name: iab_taxonomy_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.taxonomy. Business justification: DSP and DMP onboarding requires advertisers to be classified against IAB audience taxonomy for targeting eligibility and privacy compliance. 'iab_industry_vertical' is a denormalized text representati

attributes.name: account_status
