attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the advertiser account. Drives workflow routing, reporting segmentation, and CRM pipeline management. 'prospect' = pre-contract; 'active' = live campaigns; 'dormant' = no recent activity; 'churned' = relationship ended; 'suspended' = temporarily on hold.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; IAB Tech Lab

attributes.name: account_tier
attributes.description: Commercial tier classification of the advertiser based on revenue contribution, strategic importance, and engagement level. Determines service level, dedicated account team assignment, and reporting cadence. Aligns with agency tiering models used in account management.

attributes.name: address_validation_status
attributes.description: Indicates the validation status of the advertiser's billing address against a postal address verification service. 'validated' = confirmed deliverable; 'unvalidated' = not yet checked; 'failed' = address could not be verified; 'pending' = verification in progress. Used in SAP S/4HANA and Mediaocean Prisma billing workflows.
attributes.references: Universal Postal Union; SAP S/4HANA FI; ISO 19160

attributes.name: approval_workflow_type
attributes.description: Defines the creative and campaign approval workflow configuration for this advertiser. 'single_approver' = one designated approver; 'multi_approver' = sequential or parallel approval chain; 'auto_approve' = pre-authorized for specific asset types. Configured in Workfront proofing module.

attributes.name: ccpa_consent_flag
attributes.business_glossary_term: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) Data Sharing Consent Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the advertiser has opted in to data sharing under CCPA regulations. True = data sharing permitted; False = opt-out or not applicable. Applicable for California-based advertisers and campaigns targeting California consumers. Drives NAI and TAG compliance controls.
attributes.references: CCPA; NAI Code of Conduct; TAG; FTC

attributes.name: churn_date
attributes.description: The date on which the advertiser's account was marked as churned (relationship ended). Populated only when account_status = 'churned'. Used for churn analysis, win-back campaign targeting, and P&L impact reporting.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when the advertiser master record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Represents the audit trail creation event. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for data lineage, audit compliance, and record lifecycle tracking.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5(2); IAB Tech Lab; Databricks Lakehouse

attributes.name: credit_terms
attributes.description: The agreed payment credit terms for this advertiser, defining the number of days after invoice date by which payment is due. Configured in SAP S/4HANA AR and Mediaocean Prisma for billing workflows. Drives cash flow forecasting and P&L management.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI; Mediaocean Prisma; GAAP

attributes.name: crm_account_code
attributes.description: The unique account identifier assigned to this advertiser in Salesforce CRM (Sales Cloud). Serves as the cross-system join key between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the CRM system of record for account management and client services.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; IAB Tech Lab

attributes.name: ein
attributes.description: The US federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) assigned by the IRS to the advertiser. Used for US tax reporting, W-9 compliance, and financial reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA. Applicable for US-incorporated entities.
attributes.references: IRS EIN Standards; FTC Advertising Practices; SAP S/4HANA FI

attributes.name: erp_customer_number
attributes.description: The customer master number assigned to this advertiser in SAP S/4HANA (FI/AR module). Used for billing, accounts receivable, and financial reconciliation processes. Links advertiser identity to financial transactions.

attributes.name: gdpr_consent_flag
attributes.business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Data Sharing Consent Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the advertiser has provided explicit consent for data sharing and processing under GDPR. True = consent granted; False = consent not granted or withdrawn. Mandatory for EU-based advertisers and any data processing involving EU data subjects. Drives data governance controls.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 6; GDPR Article 7; EASA; IAB TCF 2.0

attributes.name: holding_company_name
attributes.description: The name of the parent holding company or corporate group to which this advertiser belongs (e.g., WPP, Publicis, Omnicom client-side equivalent). Used for consolidated billing, conflict-of-interest checks, and group-level revenue reporting.

attributes.name: incorporation_jurisdiction
attributes.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 or alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction in which the advertiser's legal entity is incorporated. Used for regulatory compliance, tax treatment, and contract law determination. Examples: 'US', 'GB', 'DEU'.

attributes.name: invoice_delivery_method
attributes.description: The preferred method by which invoices are delivered to the advertiser. Drives automated invoice dispatch workflows in SAP S/4HANA and Mediaocean Prisma. 'edi' = Electronic Data Interchange for enterprise clients with automated AP systems.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma; SAP S/4HANA FI; IAB Tech Lab

attributes.name: is_agency_managed
attributes.description: Indicates whether this advertiser is managed through an intermediary agency relationship (True) or is a direct client of the agency (False). Determines billing structure, IO (Insertion Order) routing, and account team assignment in Mediaocean Prisma and Salesforce CRM.

attributes.name: language_preference
attributes.description: The advertiser's preferred language for communications, reports, and invoices, expressed as an IETF BCP 47 language tag (e.g., 'en-US', 'fr-FR', 'de-DE'). Drives localization in Salesforce Marketing Cloud and report generation workflows.
attributes.references: IETF BCP 47; GDPR Article 12; Salesforce CRM

attributes.name: legal_entity_name
attributes.description: The full registered legal name of the advertiser as recorded with the relevant incorporation authority. Used for contracts, invoices, and regulatory filings. Maps to Salesforce CRM Account Name (legal) and SAP S/4HANA customer master legal name.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab; FTC Advertising Practices; GDPR Article 4

attributes.name: managing_agency_name
attributes.description: Name of the intermediary agency managing this advertiser's account, if is_agency_managed is True. Used for IO routing, commission calculations, and agency-of-record (AOR) tracking in Mediaocean Prisma and Salesforce CRM.

attributes.name: onboarding_date
attributes.description: The date on which the advertiser was formally onboarded as a client of the agency. Marks the start of the commercial relationship. Used for tenure calculations, anniversary-based QBR scheduling, and cohort analysis in analytics workflows.

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: The agreed payment method/instrument for this advertiser's invoices. Distinct from credit_terms (which defines timing); this defines the payment channel/instrument. Configured in SAP S/4HANA and Mediaocean Prisma for financial reconciliation.

attributes.name: preferred_comm_channel
attributes.description: The advertiser's preferred channel for day-to-day account communications and notifications. Used to route alerts, campaign updates, and approval requests in Salesforce CRM and Workfront project management workflows.

attributes.name: preferred_currency
attributes.description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the advertiser's preferred billing and reporting currency (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP'). Used in Mediaocean Prisma for media billing and SAP S/4HANA for invoicing and financial reconciliation.
attributes.references: ISO 4217; Mediaocean Prisma; SAP S/4HANA FI

attributes.name: primary_contact_email
attributes.description: The primary email address of the advertiser's main point of contact at the client organization. Used for campaign communications, reporting delivery, and account management correspondence. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Contact object.

attributes.name: primary_contact_name
attributes.description: Full name of the advertiser's primary point of contact at the client organization. Used in account management, QBR preparation, and client services workflows. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Contact object.

attributes.name: primary_contact_phone
attributes.description: Primary telephone number for the advertiser's main point of contact. Used for urgent campaign communications and account management. Stored in E.164 international format. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Contact object.

attributes.name: registered_office_country
attributes.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country where the advertiser's registered office is located. May differ from billing address country. Used for regulatory compliance, GDPR/CCPA applicability determination, and legal jurisdiction.

attributes.name: reporting_cadence
attributes.description: The agreed frequency at which performance reports and analytics dashboards are delivered to the advertiser. Drives automated report scheduling in Google Campaign Manager 360 and analytics workflows. Aligns with QBR (Quarterly Business Review) planning.
attributes.references: 4As Account Management Standards; IAB Tech Lab

attributes.name: time_zone
attributes.description: The advertiser's primary operational time zone expressed as an IANA Time Zone Database identifier (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London'). Used for scheduling campaign flights, reporting cadence, and meeting coordination in Workfront and Salesforce CRM.
attributes.references: IANA Time Zone Database; IAB Tech Lab

attributes.name: trading_name
attributes.description: The brand or 'doing business as' (DBA) name under which the advertiser operates commercially. May differ from the legal entity name. Used in campaign documentation, media plans, and client-facing materials.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when the advertiser master record was most recently modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5(2); IAB Tech Lab; Databricks Lakehouse

attributes.name: vat_number
attributes.description: The advertiser's Value Added Tax (VAT) registration number issued by the relevant tax authority. Required for EU/UK invoicing compliance and cross-border tax treatment. Stored for financial reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA FI module.
attributes.references: EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; EASA; GDPR Article 4

products.name: advertiser_hierarchy
products.description: Defines the parent-child organizational hierarchy among advertiser entities — holding company, subsidiary, brand, and sub-brand relationships. Supports multi-level account trees for large conglomerates (e.g., WPP, Publicis clients) where billing, reporting, and campaign attribution must roll up through corporate structures. Tracks hierarchy type (holding, subsidiary, brand portfolio), effective dates, and consolidation flags.

attributes.name: advertiser_hierarchy_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each advertiser hierarchy relationship record in the silver layer. Serves as the primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: child_advertiser_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the child advertiser entity in the hierarchy (e.g., subsidiary, brand, or sub-brand). References the advertiser entity that sits one level below the parent in the corporate tree.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Data Standards; Salesforce CRM Account Hierarchy

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Holding company and advertiser hierarchy nodes map to consolidated billing cost centers for group-level P&L rollup and consolidated invoice reporting. Finance teams require this link to perform hierar

attributes.name: parent_advertiser_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the parent advertiser entity in the hierarchy (e.g., holding company or parent subsidiary). References the advertiser entity that sits one level above the child in the corporate tree.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Data Standards; Salesforce CRM Account Hierarchy

attributes.name: primary_root_advertiser_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the ultimate holding company or top-level entity in the advertiser corporate tree. Enables direct roll-up to the conglomerate level for consolidated billing, SOV analysis, and enterprise reporting without traversing the full hierarchy path.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Data Standards; Salesforce CRM Account Hierarchy

attributes.name: agency_commission_rate
attributes.description: The negotiated agency commission rate (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.1500 for 15%) applicable to media buys placed on behalf of the child entity within this hierarchy. Stored at the hierarchy level to support tiered commission structures for holding company clients in Mediaocean Prisma.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma; 4As Commission Standards; SAP S/4HANA CO

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the account management or finance authority who approved the establishment or modification of this hierarchy relationship. Required for governance and SOW compliance in client services workflows.
attributes.references: 4As Account Management Standards; Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: The date on which this hierarchy relationship was formally approved by the designated account management or finance authority. Distinct from the effective start date — approval may precede the operational effective date.
attributes.references: 4As Account Management Standards; Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud

attributes.name: billing_consolidation_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether invoices and media spend for the child entity are consolidated and billed through the parent entity. Distinct from the general consolidation flag — specifically governs Mediaocean Prisma billing workflows and Insertion Order (IO) aggregation.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma; SAP S/4HANA FI; IAB Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: change_reason
attributes.description: Categorized reason code explaining why this hierarchy relationship record was created or last modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change management reporting for account management teams during corporate restructuring events. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: acquisition|divestiture|rebranding|restructuring|correction|initial_setup|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: 4As Account Management Standards; SAP S/4HANA CO

attributes.name: consolidation_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the child entity's spend, impressions, and campaign performance metrics should be financially consolidated into the parent entity's reporting and P&L. Critical for holding company clients where subsidiary budgets roll up to a master account in SAP S/4HANA CO.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO; Mediaocean Prisma; ANA Financial Reporting Standards

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the primary operating jurisdiction for this hierarchy relationship. Used to apply correct regulatory reporting requirements (GDPR, CCPA, FTC), tax treatment in SAP S/4HANA, and local media buying rules.
attributes.references: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; GDPR; CCPA; FTC Advertising Practices Division

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this advertiser hierarchy relationship record was first created in the system. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit trail creation anchor for data governance and compliance reporting.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5; Databricks Lakehouse Delta Lake Audit Standards

attributes.name: crm_hierarchy_code
attributes.description: The native account hierarchy relationship identifier from Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud. Enables bi-directional synchronization between the lakehouse silver layer and the CRM system of record for account management and client services workflows.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the billing and financial consolidation currency applicable to this hierarchy relationship (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP'). Governs how media spend and agency fees are consolidated in Mediaocean Prisma and SAP S/4HANA FI.
attributes.references: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: data_sharing_consent_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the child advertiser entity has provided consent for audience data and campaign performance data to be shared with the parent entity in this hierarchy. Critical for GDPR and CCPA compliance when consolidating audience insights across corporate structures.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 6; CCPA; IAB Transparency and Consent Framework (TCF) 2.0

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which this parent-child hierarchy relationship ceases to be operationally effective. Nullable for open-ended relationships. Supports temporal hierarchy versioning when corporate structures change due to divestitures or rebranding.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Data Standards; SAP S/4HANA FI; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date on which this parent-child hierarchy relationship became or becomes operationally effective. Used to enforce temporally accurate billing roll-ups and campaign attribution when corporate structures change (e.g., acquisition effective date).
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Data Standards; SAP S/4HANA FI; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: hierarchy_code
attributes.description: Externally-known alphanumeric business code uniquely identifying this parent-child hierarchy relationship. Used in Mediaocean Prisma and Salesforce CRM for cross-system reconciliation and billing roll-up references.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma; Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud

attributes.name: hierarchy_description
attributes.description: Free-text narrative describing the business context, rationale, or special conditions of this parent-child hierarchy relationship. Captures nuances such as regional carve-outs, brand licensing arrangements, or joint venture structures not fully expressed by the type and role fields.

attributes.name: hierarchy_level
attributes.description: Numeric depth of the child advertiser entity within the corporate account tree, where level 1 represents the ultimate holding company. Enables multi-level roll-up queries for consolidated P&L and campaign reporting across conglomerates such as WPP or Publicis.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Data Standards; Salesforce CRM Account Hierarchy

attributes.name: hierarchy_name
attributes.description: Human-readable label describing this specific parent-child hierarchy relationship (e.g., 'Unilever > Dove Brand Portfolio'). Used in account management dashboards, QBR presentations, and client services reporting for clear identification of the relationship.
attributes.references: 4As Account Management Standards; Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud

attributes.name: hierarchy_path
attributes.description: Materialized slash-delimited path string representing the full ancestry chain from the root holding company to the current child node (e.g., '/HoldCo/SubsidiaryA/BrandX'). Supports efficient subtree queries and lineage tracing in Databricks without recursive CTEs.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Data Standards; Databricks Lakehouse Best Practices

attributes.name: hierarchy_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the parent-child hierarchy relationship. Drives whether the relationship is used for active campaign attribution, billing consolidation, and reporting roll-ups in Mediaocean Prisma and Google Campaign Manager 360.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Data Standards; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: hierarchy_type
attributes.description: Classifies the nature of the parent-child relationship within the advertiser corporate structure. Determines how billing, reporting, and campaign attribution roll up through the account tree. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: holding_company|subsidiary|brand_portfolio|brand|sub_brand|division — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Data Standards; 4As Account Management Standards

attributes.name: industry_sector
attributes.description: The IAB Content Taxonomy industry sector classification of the child advertiser entity within this hierarchy (e.g., 'Consumer Packaged Goods', 'Automotive', 'Financial Services'). Supports audience segmentation, competitive SOV analysis, and Nielsen Ad Intel competitive monitoring.
attributes.references: IAB Content Taxonomy 3.0; Nielsen Ad Intel

attributes.name: is_primary_hierarchy
attributes.description: Indicates whether this relationship represents the primary (canonical) hierarchy path for the child advertiser entity. When an entity belongs to multiple hierarchy trees (e.g., matrix structures), this flag identifies the authoritative roll-up path for billing and consolidated reporting.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: market_region
attributes.description: The geographic market region in which this hierarchy relationship is operationally active (e.g., 'North America', 'EMEA', 'APAC'). Determines applicable regulatory frameworks (GDPR, CCPA), media buying practices, and regional account team assignments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: North America|EMEA|APAC|LATAM|MEA|Global — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: ownership_percentage
attributes.description: The percentage of ownership the parent entity holds in the child entity (0.00–100.00). Used to determine proportional financial consolidation thresholds and to classify the relationship as majority-owned, minority-owned, or affiliated for P&L reporting in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI; ANA Financial Reporting Standards; IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements

attributes.name: prisma_hierarchy_code
attributes.description: The native account hierarchy identifier from Mediaocean Prisma used for media planning, buying, and billing roll-up. Supports reconciliation between the lakehouse and Prisma for consolidated media spend reporting across the advertiser corporate tree.

attributes.name: relationship_role
attributes.description: Describes the commercial or legal role of the child entity relative to the parent within the hierarchy (e.g., wholly-owned subsidiary vs. franchise vs. joint venture). Informs contract and billing treatment in SAP S/4HANA and Mediaocean Prisma.
attributes.references: ANA Account Governance Standards; SAP S/4HANA FI

attributes.name: reporting_consolidation_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether campaign performance data (impressions, clicks, CTR, ROAS) for the child entity rolls up into the parent entity's consolidated reporting dashboards in Google Campaign Manager 360 and The Trade Desk. May differ from billing consolidation in matrix account structures.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360; The Trade Desk; MRC Measurement Standards

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this hierarchy relationship record was originally sourced or is mastered. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution when the same hierarchy is maintained across multiple systems.
attributes.references: Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage Standards; IAB Tech Lab Data Standards

attributes.name: sov_rollup_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the child entity's media spend and impressions should be included in the parent entity's Share of Voice (SOV) calculations for competitive analysis in Nielsen Ad Intel and Comscore. Enables accurate conglomerate-level SOV reporting.
attributes.references: Nielsen Ad Intel; Comscore; MRC Measurement Standards

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this advertiser hierarchy relationship record was most recently modified. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks silver layer.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5; Databricks Lakehouse Delta Lake Audit Standards

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Monotonically incrementing version counter for this hierarchy relationship record. Incremented each time the hierarchy definition is modified (e.g., ownership percentage change, status update). Supports optimistic concurrency control and audit trail reconstruction.
attributes.references: Databricks Lakehouse Delta Lake; 4As Data Governance Standards

products.name: brand
products.description: Represents an individual brand owned by an advertiser. A single advertiser may manage multiple brands (e.g., Unilever owns Dove, Axe, Lipton). Captures brand name, brand code, category, sub-category, brand tier, launch date, brand status, and the owning advertiser. Serves as the SSOT for brand identity referenced by campaigns, creative assets, and media plans. Distinct from the brand domain's brand strategy and positioning entities.

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Self-referencing identifier for the parent brand when this brand is a sub-brand or brand extension (e.g., Dove Men+Care is a sub-brand of Dove). Enables brand hierarchy navigation for portfolio reporting and consolidated media planning.
attributes.references: ANA Brand Management Guidelines; IAB Data Standards

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Brand-level P&L reporting and internal cost allocation require each client brand to map to a finance cost center. Agency finance teams run brand profitability reports by cost center; this link is stan

attributes.name: primary_client_brand_advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent advertiser (client) who owns this brand. A single advertiser may own multiple brands (e.g., Unilever owns Dove, Axe, Lipton).
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM - Account Object; IAB Data Standards

attributes.name: annual_media_budget
attributes.description: The total approved annual media spend budget allocated to this brand in the base currency. Used for budget pacing, media plan authorization, and Profit & Loss (P&L) reporting in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA - CO Budget Management; 4As Financial Management Standards; ANA Budget Benchmarking

attributes.name: brand_category
attributes.description: Primary product or service category the brand competes in (e.g., Personal Care, Food & Beverage, Automotive, Financial Services). Aligns with IAB Content Taxonomy for audience targeting and competitive analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Personal Care|Food & Beverage|Automotive|Financial Services|Technology|Retail|Healthcare|Entertainment|Travel|Other — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Content Taxonomy 3.0; Nielsen Ad Intel - Category Classification

attributes.name: brand_code
attributes.description: Externally-known alphanumeric short code uniquely identifying the brand across operational systems including Google Campaign Manager 360, Mediaocean Prisma, and The Trade Desk. Used for trafficking, billing, and reconciliation.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Advertiser/Brand Trafficking; Mediaocean Prisma - Brand Code

attributes.name: brand_description
attributes.description: Free-text narrative describing the brand's identity, value proposition, target audience, and key differentiators. Used by account teams, creative teams, and media planners to ensure brand-consistent execution.
attributes.references: 4As Account Management Standards; ANA Brand Management Guidelines

attributes.name: brand_name
attributes.description: The official commercial name of the brand as recognized in the market (e.g., Dove, Axe, Lipton). Used as the primary human-readable identifier across campaigns, creative assets, and media plans.
attributes.references: IAB Data Standards; ANA Brand Management Guidelines

attributes.name: brand_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the brand record. Controls whether the brand is eligible for campaign assignment, media planning, and creative production. 'pending_launch' indicates a brand approved but not yet publicly launched.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM - Account Status; IAB Data Standards

attributes.name: brand_tier
attributes.description: Strategic positioning tier of the brand within the advertiser's portfolio, indicating relative market positioning and investment priority. Drives media budget allocation, creative production standards, and account team resourcing.
attributes.references: ANA Brand Management Guidelines; 4As Account Management Standards

attributes.name: budget_currency
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the brand's annual media budget (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency financial reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA and Mediaocean Prisma.
attributes.references: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA - FI Currency Configuration

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when the brand record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for data governance and lineage tracking.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity; Internal Data Governance Policy

attributes.name: crm_brand_code
attributes.description: The brand's unique identifier in Salesforce CRM (Sales Cloud / Marketing Cloud). Enables cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational CRM system of record.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM - Account/Brand Object; Internal System Integration Standards

attributes.name: dam_folder_code
attributes.description: The folder or collection identifier in Workfront DAM (Digital Asset Management) where all creative assets for this brand are stored. Enables direct navigation from brand record to associated creative library.
attributes.references: Workfront DAM - Digital Asset Management; IAB VAST/VPAID Creative Standards

attributes.name: data_privacy_classification
attributes.description: Privacy classification governing data collection and audience targeting practices for this brand. 'children' applies COPPA/GDPR-K restrictions. 'sensitive' applies enhanced consent requirements under GDPR Article 9 and CCPA. Drives audience data usage policies in the DMP/CDP.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 9; CCPA Section 1798.100; COPPA; FTC Advertising Practices Division; NAI Code of Conduct

attributes.name: digital_advertising_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether the brand is approved and configured for digital advertising including programmatic, paid search (PPC/SEM), and social media. Controls eligibility for The Trade Desk DSP activation and Google Campaign Manager 360 trafficking.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Advertising Standards; TAG Certified Against Fraud

attributes.name: discontinuation_date
attributes.description: The date on which the brand was officially discontinued or retired from the market. Null for active brands. Used to enforce data governance rules preventing new campaign assignments to discontinued brands.
attributes.references: ANA Brand Management Guidelines; Internal Data Governance Policy

attributes.name: guidelines_url
attributes.description: URL pointing to the brand's official style guide and creative guidelines document stored in Workfront DAM or Adobe Creative Cloud. Used by creative teams to ensure brand-consistent asset production.
attributes.references: Workfront DAM - Asset Management; Adobe Creative Cloud - Brand Guidelines; 4As Creative Standards

attributes.name: is_house_brand
attributes.description: Indicates whether this brand is an agency house brand or proprietary brand (True) versus a client-owned advertiser brand (False). Affects revenue recognition, conflict-of-interest checks, and P&L attribution in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Financial Standards; SAP S/4HANA - CO Profit Center Accounting

attributes.name: launch_date
attributes.description: The date on which the brand was officially launched in the market. Used for brand age calculations, anniversary campaign planning, and historical performance benchmarking.

attributes.name: nielsen_brand_code
attributes.description: The brand identifier assigned by Nielsen Ad Intel for competitive monitoring, audience measurement, and Gross Rating Point (GRP) tracking. Enables competitive spend benchmarking and Share of Voice (SOV) analysis.
attributes.references: Nielsen Ad Intel - Brand Classification; MRC Audience Measurement Standards

attributes.name: operating_markets
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes representing all markets where the brand is actively sold and advertised. Used for multi-market campaign planning, regulatory compliance scoping, and global media buying via The Trade Desk.
attributes.references: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; IAB Data Standards; EASA Cross-Border Advertising Standards

attributes.name: portfolio_rank
attributes.description: Numeric rank of the brand within the advertiser's portfolio based on strategic importance and revenue contribution (1 = highest priority). Used for resource allocation, account team staffing, and Quarterly Business Review (QBR) prioritization.
attributes.references: ANA Brand Management Guidelines; 4As Account Management Standards

attributes.name: primary_channel
attributes.description: The primary media channel through which the brand predominantly advertises. Drives default media planning templates in Mediaocean Prisma and channel-specific KPI frameworks. OOH = Out-of-Home.
attributes.references: IAB Channel Classification; MRC Cross-Platform Measurement Standards

attributes.name: primary_market_country
attributes.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the brand's primary operating market (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Drives regulatory compliance requirements (GDPR, CCPA), media planning geography, and reporting currency selection.
attributes.references: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; GDPR Article 3; CCPA Section 1798

attributes.name: prisma_brand_code
attributes.description: The brand identifier used in Mediaocean Prisma for media planning, buying, billing, and financial reconciliation. Ensures accurate budget allocation and invoice matching at the brand level.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma - Brand/Product Code; 4As Media Billing Standards

attributes.name: programmatic_eligible
attributes.description: Indicates whether the brand is approved for Real-Time Bidding (RTB) and programmatic media buying via Demand-Side Platforms (DSPs) such as The Trade Desk. Brands may be excluded due to regulatory restrictions, brand safety policies, or client preference.
attributes.references: IAB RTB Standards; TAG Certified Against Fraud; The Trade Desk - Advertiser Eligibility

attributes.name: regulatory_restrictions
attributes.description: Free-text description of any regulatory or legal advertising restrictions applicable to the brand (e.g., alcohol advertising watershed rules, pharmaceutical fair balance requirements, gambling advertising restrictions). Informs creative compliance review and media placement approvals.
attributes.references: ASA Advertising Standards; FTC Advertising Practices Division; EASA Cross-Border Standards; ICC Advertising and Marketing Communications Code

attributes.name: safety_level
attributes.description: The brand safety classification tier governing content adjacency restrictions for programmatic and digital media buying. Determines keyword blocklists, content category exclusions, and inventory quality thresholds applied in The Trade Desk and Google Campaign Manager 360.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Standards; IAB Brand Safety Guidelines; GARM Brand Safety Floor

attributes.name: sov_target_pct
attributes.description: The client-agreed target Share of Voice (SOV) percentage for this brand within its competitive category. Used as a KPI benchmark for media planning, campaign performance evaluation, and Nielsen Ad Intel competitive monitoring.
attributes.references: Nielsen Ad Intel - SOV Measurement; MRC Audience Measurement Standards; ANA Media Accountability Standards

attributes.name: sub_category
attributes.description: Secondary classification within the brand category providing finer segmentation (e.g., within Personal Care: Skin Care, Hair Care, Deodorants). Supports audience insights, competitive benchmarking via Nielsen Ad Intel, and media planning.
attributes.references: IAB Content Taxonomy 3.0; Nielsen Ad Intel - Sub-Category Classification

attributes.name: tagline
attributes.description: The official marketing tagline or slogan associated with the brand (e.g., 'Just Do It', 'Real Beauty'). Stored as a reference for creative consistency checks and brand compliance reviews.
attributes.references: ASA Advertising Standards; FTC Advertising Practices Division

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when the brand record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit compliance.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity; Internal Data Governance Policy

attributes.name: website_url
attributes.description: The official public-facing website URL for the brand. Used for digital campaign trafficking, click-through destination validation in Google Campaign Manager 360, and SEO/SEM performance tracking.
attributes.references: IAB VAST/VPAID Standards; Google Campaign Manager 360 - Landing Page Validation

products.name: account_team
products.description: Maps agency-side personnel (account directors, strategists, planners, media buyers, creatives) to specific advertiser accounts. Captures assigned team member reference, role on account (Account Director, Strategic Planner, Media Lead, Creative Director), assignment start and end dates, FTE allocation percentage, primary contact flag, backup coverage, and team status. Enables resource planning, utilization tracking, conflict-of-interest checks, QBR preparation, and client-facing org chart generation.
products.reference: Salesforce CRM Account Team / Workfront Resource Assignment

attributes.name: account_team_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each account team assignment record in the advertising agency. Serves as the primary key for this entity.
attributes.references: IAB Data Standards; 4As Agency Operations Guidelines

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertiser or agency-managed client account to which this team member is assigned. Anchors the assignment within the client domain hierarchy.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud Account Object; 4As Account Management Standards

attributes.name: agency_relationship_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.agency_relationship. Business justification: Account team assignments exist within the context of a formal agency-client relationship. Linking account_team to agency_relationship via agency_relationship_id allows the model to scope team assignme

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Account team members are assigned to cost centers for overhead allocation, utilization tracking, and P&L reporting. Essential for agency financial management to track labor costs by client account and

attributes.name: managed_supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Account team members often have vendor relationship management responsibilities—e.g., media buyer assigned to manage Google/Meta relationships for specific clients. Tracks vendor stewardship assignmen

attributes.name: account_access_level
attributes.description: The level of access this team member has to the client account record in Salesforce CRM (read, edit, or all). Mirrors the Salesforce AccountTeamMember AccountAccessLevel field and governs data visibility for compliance and conflict-of-interest checks.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud AccountTeamMember Object; GDPR Article 25 Data Protection by Design; FTC Advertising Practices

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: The date on which this account team assignment was formally approved by the designated approver. Used for governance audit trails and staffing compliance reporting.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Governance Standards; Workday HCM; Workfront Approval Workflows

attributes.name: assignment_code
attributes.description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this account team assignment, used in Workfront project management and Salesforce CRM for cross-system reference and QBR (Quarterly Business Review) reporting.
attributes.references: Workfront Project Management; Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; 4As Account Management Standards

attributes.name: assignment_notes
attributes.description: Free-text field capturing additional context about the team member's assignment, such as special responsibilities, client preferences, transition notes, or coverage arrangements. Supports account management continuity.
attributes.references: 4As Account Management Standards; Workfront Project Management

attributes.name: assignment_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the team member's assignment to the account. 'active' indicates currently serving; 'on_leave' indicates temporary absence with backup coverage; 'transitioning' indicates handover in progress; 'inactive' indicates assignment ended; 'pending' indicates assignment approved but not yet started.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Operations Guidelines; Workday HCM Talent Management; Workfront Resource Planning

attributes.name: assignment_type
attributes.description: Classifies the nature of the team member's assignment to the account: 'dedicated' = exclusively assigned; 'shared' = split across multiple accounts; 'project_based' = assigned for a specific campaign or project; 'interim' = temporary coverage assignment.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Staffing Standards; Workfront Resource Planning; ANA Agency Relationship Standards

attributes.name: billing_rate_type
attributes.description: Specifies the time unit basis for the billing rate applied to this team member's account assignment (hourly, daily, monthly, or fixed fee). Determines how FTE allocation and time tracking translate to client invoicing.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI; Mediaocean Prisma Billing; 4As Agency Compensation Guidelines

attributes.name: billing_rate_usd
attributes.description: The hourly or daily billing rate in USD applied to this team member's time on the client account, as defined in the Statement of Work (SOW) or Master Service Agreement. Used for financial reconciliation and P&L reporting in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Financial Accounting; Mediaocean Prisma Billing; 4As Agency Compensation Guidelines; ANA Agency Financial Management Standards

attributes.name: client_facing_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this team member has direct client-facing responsibilities and appears on the client-facing org chart. True = client-facing; False = internal/support role only. Governs inclusion in client communications and org chart generation.
attributes.references: 4As Account Management Standards; Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud AccountTeamMember Object

attributes.name: conflict_of_interest_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether a potential conflict of interest has been identified for this team member's assignment to the account (e.g., simultaneous assignment to a competing advertiser). True = conflict identified; False = no conflict. Triggers compliance review workflow.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Ethics Guidelines; ANA Agency Relationship Standards; FTC Advertising Practices Division; TAG Trustworthy Accountability Group

attributes.name: conflict_review_date
attributes.description: The date on which the most recent conflict-of-interest review was conducted for this team member's account assignment. Supports compliance audit trails and periodic review scheduling.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Ethics Guidelines; ANA Agency Relationship Standards; FTC Advertising Practices Division

attributes.name: conflict_review_outcome
attributes.description: The outcome of the most recent conflict-of-interest review for this assignment. 'cleared' = no conflict found; 'waived' = conflict acknowledged and waived by client; 'escalated' = referred to senior management; 'pending' = review in progress.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Ethics Guidelines; ANA Agency Relationship Standards; FTC Advertising Practices Division

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this account team assignment record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit trail creation anchor for data lineage and compliance reporting.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity; IAB Data Standards; 4As Data Governance Guidelines

attributes.name: end_date
attributes.description: The date on which the agency personnel member's assignment to this client account is scheduled to end or actually ended. Null for open-ended assignments. Used for resource planning and transition management.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Staffing Standards; Workday HCM Time Tracking; Workfront Resource Planning

attributes.name: fte_allocation_pct
attributes.description: The percentage of the team member's full-time equivalent (FTE) capacity allocated to this specific client account, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 50.00 = 50%). Supports resource utilization tracking, capacity planning, and P&L cost allocation.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Resource Management Standards; Workday HCM FTE Tracking; SAP S/4HANA PS Project Staffing

attributes.name: is_backup_contact
attributes.description: Indicates whether this team member serves as the backup coverage contact for the client account when the primary contact is unavailable. True = designated backup; False = not a backup. Supports continuity planning and leave coverage.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Operations Guidelines; Workfront Resource Planning

attributes.name: is_primary_contact
attributes.description: Indicates whether this team member is the designated primary point of contact for the client account. True = primary contact; False = supporting team member. Used for client-facing org chart generation and escalation routing.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud Contact Roles; 4As Account Management Standards; ANA Agency Relationship Standards

attributes.name: last_review_date
attributes.description: The date of the most recent formal review of this team member's account assignment, typically conducted during QBR cycles or annual staffing reviews. Supports proactive account management and client satisfaction monitoring.
attributes.references: 4As Account Management Standards; ANA QBR Best Practices

attributes.name: next_review_date
attributes.description: The scheduled date for the next formal review of this team member's account assignment. Enables proactive scheduling of staffing reviews and QBR preparation cycles.
attributes.references: 4As Account Management Standards; ANA QBR Best Practices

attributes.name: org_chart_display_order
attributes.description: Numeric sequence controlling the display order of this team member within the client-facing org chart for the account. Lower values appear first. Enables structured presentation of the account team hierarchy to clients.
attributes.references: 4As Account Management Standards; Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud

attributes.name: planned_hours_per_month
attributes.description: The planned number of hours per month this team member is expected to dedicate to this client account, as agreed in the Statement of Work (SOW) or resource plan. Used for utilization tracking and capacity planning in Workfront.
attributes.references: Workfront Resource Planning; SAP S/4HANA PS Project Staffing; 4As Agency Resource Management Standards

attributes.name: qbr_presenter_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this team member is designated to present at the client's Quarterly Business Review (QBR). True = QBR presenter; False = not a QBR presenter. Used for QBR preparation and client-facing agenda planning.
attributes.references: 4As Account Management Standards; ANA QBR Best Practices; Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud

attributes.name: role
attributes.description: The functional role this agency personnel member holds on the client account (e.g., Account Director, Strategic Planner, Media Lead, Creative Director, Account Manager, Media Buyer). Drives org chart generation and responsibility mapping. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Account Director|Strategic Planner|Media Lead|Creative Director|Account Manager|Media Buyer|Digital Strategist|Data Analyst|PR Lead|Brand Strategist — promote to reference product]
attributes.value_regex: Account Director|Strategic Planner|Media Lead|Creative Director|Account Manager|Media Buyer
attributes.references: 4As Agency Role Taxonomy; ANA Agency Relationship Standards; Workfront Resource Planning

attributes.name: salesforce_team_member_code
attributes.description: The unique identifier of the corresponding AccountTeamMember record in Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud. Supports CRM synchronization, client relationship management, and QBR preparation workflows.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud AccountTeamMember Object; 4As CRM Standards

attributes.name: seniority_level
attributes.description: The seniority classification of the team member within their assigned role on this account (e.g., junior, mid, senior, lead, executive). Used for billing rate determination, resource planning, and QBR staffing reviews.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Staffing Standards; Workday HCM Talent Management; ANA Agency Relationship Standards

attributes.name: start_date
attributes.description: The date on which the agency personnel member formally began their assignment to this client account. Used for tenure tracking, utilization reporting, and conflict-of-interest checks.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Staffing Standards; Workday HCM Time Tracking; Workfront Project Management

attributes.name: team_function
attributes.description: The agency functional discipline this team member represents on the account (account management, media, creative, strategy, analytics, or PR). Enables cross-functional team composition reporting and resource planning by discipline.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Discipline Taxonomy; ANA Agency Relationship Standards; IAB Agency Structure Guidelines

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this account team assignment record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, data lineage, and Silver layer incremental processing.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity; IAB Data Standards; 4As Data Governance Guidelines

products.name: agency_relationship
products.description: Captures the formal relationship between the agency and an advertiser, including relationship type (AOR — Agency of Record, project-based, retainer, roster agency), relationship status, scope of services (media, creative, PR, digital, social), start and end dates, exclusivity flags, governing contract reference, and full relationship state-change history stored as effective-dated event records (AOR wins/losses, scope expansions, renewals, escalations, status transitions with effective dates, triggering events, previous state, new state, and narrative notes). This is the SSOT for both current relationship state and historical relationship lifecycle — no separate history table exists. Supports AOR tracking, pitch history, competitive conflict management, audit trail, and executive relationship lifecycle reporting.

attributes.name: agency_relationship_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the agency-advertiser relationship record. Primary key for this entity. Role classification: MASTER_AGREEMENT — represents a long-running formal binding relationship between the agency and an advertiser.
attributes.references: IAB Data Standards; 4As Agency Management Best Practices

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertiser or client organisation that is party to this agency relationship. Links to the client master record in the client domain.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Account Object; IAB Data Standards

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the governing contract or Statement of Work (SOW) that formalises the terms of this agency relationship. Links to the contract master record.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA — Contract Management; ANA Agency Contract Guidelines

attributes.name: primary_supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Agency relationships often designate primary media suppliers or tech platforms used for that client account. Tracks which DSPs, SSPs, or publishers are approved/preferred under specific agency-client

attributes.name: annual_fee_amount
attributes.description: The contracted annual agency fee or retainer amount in the agreed currency for this relationship. Used for P&L reporting, budget management, and financial reconciliation. Applicable for retainer and project-fee structures.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA — FI/CO Module; ANA Agency Fee Transparency Standards; 4As Compensation Guidelines

attributes.name: aor_designation
attributes.description: Specifies the specific Agency of Record (AOR) designation held by the agency for this client, if applicable. Distinguishes between full-service AOR and discipline-specific AOR designations (media, creative, digital, PR, social). Used for AOR tracking and executive relationship reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: full_service_aor|media_aor|creative_aor|digital_aor|pr_aor|social_aor|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: 4As Agency of Record (AOR) Classification; ANA AOR Management Guidelines

attributes.name: brand_scope
attributes.description: Comma-delimited list of the client's brand names or brand portfolio segments covered under this agency relationship. Distinguishes relationships that cover the full client portfolio from those scoped to specific brands or sub-brands.
attributes.references: 4As Scope of Services Framework; ANA Brand Management Guidelines

attributes.name: client_contact_email
attributes.description: The business email address of the primary client contact for this relationship. Used for direct communication, meeting scheduling, and CRM record maintenance. Subject to GDPR and CCPA data protection requirements.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Contact Object; GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.100

attributes.name: commission_rate_pct
attributes.description: The agreed agency commission rate expressed as a percentage of gross media spend, applicable when the fee structure type is commission_based or hybrid. Used for financial reconciliation in Mediaocean Prisma and SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Compensation Guidelines; ANA Media Transparency Report; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: competitive_conflict_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this agency-client relationship involves a known competitive conflict with another client in the agency's portfolio (i.e., the agency serves two competing advertisers in the same category). Critical for conflict management and new business governance.
attributes.references: 4As Conflict of Interest Guidelines; ANA Agency Conflict Management Standards

attributes.name: conflict_category
attributes.description: The product or industry category in which a competitive conflict exists, if the competitive conflict flag is true (e.g., 'Automotive', 'Financial Services', 'FMCG — Beverages'). Used for conflict management reporting and new business screening.
attributes.references: 4As Conflict of Interest Guidelines; IAB Content Taxonomy

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp at which this agency relationship record was first created in the system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and compliance reporting.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity; FTC Record Keeping Requirements; IAB Data Standards

attributes.name: crm_opportunity_code
attributes.description: The external identifier of the corresponding Salesforce CRM Opportunity record that originated or tracks this agency-client relationship. Enables bi-directional traceability between the data lakehouse and the CRM system of record.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Opportunity Object; Salesforce Sales Cloud

attributes.name: current_event_type
attributes.description: The type of the most recent state-change event recorded against this relationship. Supports AOR win/loss tracking, scope change management, and executive relationship lifecycle reporting. Full event history is stored as effective-dated event records within this product. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: aor_win|aor_loss|scope_expansion|scope_reduction|renewal|escalation|status_transition|termination|suspension|reinstatement|pitch_initiated — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: 4As Relationship Lifecycle Standards; ANA Agency Management Guidelines

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which the agency-client relationship is scheduled to end or was terminated. Nullable for open-ended relationships. Used for AOR tracking, renewal management, and competitive conflict management. Serves as EFFECTIVE_UNTIL for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity.
attributes.references: IAB Data Standards; SAP S/4HANA — Contract Validity End

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date on which the agency-client relationship formally commenced and became binding. Used for AOR tracking, tenure calculations, and relationship lifecycle reporting. Serves as EFFECTIVE_FROM for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity.
attributes.references: IAB Data Standards; SAP S/4HANA — Contract Validity Start

attributes.name: estimated_annual_billings
attributes.description: The estimated total annual media billings or revenue associated with this agency-client relationship, in the fee currency. Used for revenue forecasting, P&L reporting, and strategic account prioritisation. Distinct from the contracted annual fee amount.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Financial Reporting Standards; SAP S/4HANA — CO/FI Module; ANA Agency Compensation Guidelines

attributes.name: event_effective_date
attributes.description: The business-effective date of the most recent state-change event (e.g., the date an AOR win took effect, the date a scope expansion was agreed). Distinct from the record audit timestamp; used for effective-dated relationship lifecycle reporting.
attributes.references: 4As Relationship Lifecycle Standards; SAP S/4HANA — Effective Dating

attributes.name: event_notes
attributes.description: Free-text narrative notes providing additional context for the most recent relationship state-change event. Captures qualitative detail such as client rationale, negotiation outcomes, or escalation context. Supports audit trail and executive relationship lifecycle reporting.
attributes.references: 4As Audit Trail Standards; Salesforce CRM — Activity Notes

attributes.name: event_trigger
attributes.description: A brief description of the business event or circumstance that triggered the most recent relationship state change (e.g., 'Client initiated competitive review', 'Scope expanded to include social media', 'Contract renewed for FY2025'). Supports narrative audit trail and executive reporting.
attributes.references: 4As Audit Trail Standards; ANA Agency Management Guidelines

attributes.name: fee_currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the annual fee amount and any financial values associated with this relationship (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency financial reconciliation.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA — FI Module

attributes.name: fee_structure_type
attributes.description: The commercial fee model governing agency compensation under this relationship. Retainer_fee is a fixed recurring payment; commission_based is a percentage of media spend; project_fee is a one-time scoped payment; performance_based ties compensation to KPI outcomes; hybrid combines multiple models.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Compensation Guidelines; ANA Agency Fee Transparency Standards; SAP S/4HANA — CO Module

attributes.name: geographic_scope
attributes.description: The geographic markets or territories covered by this agency-client relationship (e.g., 'USA', 'GBR,DEU,FRA', 'Global'). Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where applicable. Supports multi-market AOR tracking and competitive conflict management.
attributes.references: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; IAB Geographic Standards; 4As Global Agency Guidelines

attributes.name: incumbent_agency_name
attributes.description: The name of the previous incumbent agency that held the account prior to this relationship being established, if applicable. Used for competitive intelligence, pitch history analysis, and AOR win/loss reporting.
attributes.references: 4As Competitive Intelligence Standards; Nielsen Ad Intel

attributes.name: is_exclusive
attributes.description: Indicates whether the agency holds an exclusive mandate for the defined scope of services with this advertiser, preventing the client from engaging competing agencies for the same services. Critical for competitive conflict management and AOR governance.
attributes.references: 4As Agency of Record (AOR) Guidelines; ANA Exclusivity Standards

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp at which this agency relationship record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking, data synchronisation with Salesforce CRM, and audit trail compliance.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity; FTC Record Keeping Requirements; Salesforce CRM — System Modified Date

attributes.name: lead_account_director
attributes.description: The name of the senior agency account director who holds primary responsibility for managing this client relationship. Used for account team assignment, escalation routing, and executive relationship reporting.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Account Team; Workfront — Resource Planning

attributes.name: mediaocean_client_code
attributes.description: The client code assigned to this advertiser in Mediaocean Prisma, the media planning and buying system of record. Used for reconciliation of media plans, insertion orders (IOs), and billing records against this relationship.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Client Setup; IAB Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: next_review_date
attributes.description: The scheduled date of the next formal relationship review or Quarterly Business Review (QBR). Used for proactive account management, renewal planning, and executive calendar management.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Performance Review Standards; Salesforce CRM — Activity Management

attributes.name: notice_period_days
attributes.description: The number of calendar days of advance written notice required by either party to terminate or materially modify this agency-client relationship, as specified in the governing contract or Statement of Work (SOW).
attributes.references: ANA Agency Contract Guidelines; SAP S/4HANA — Contract Management

attributes.name: pitch_type
attributes.description: The type of pitch or selection process through which this agency-client relationship was established. Competitive_review is a formal multi-agency pitch; direct_award is a non-competitive appointment; incumbent_retention is a renewal without a formal pitch; rfi_rfp is a formal Request for Information (RFI) or Request for Proposal (RFP) process.
attributes.references: 4As Pitch Process Standards; ANA Agency Search Guidelines

attributes.name: pitch_win_date
attributes.description: The date on which the agency formally won the pitch or competitive review that established this relationship. Used for pitch history tracking, new business reporting, and AOR win/loss analytics.
attributes.references: 4As New Business Reporting Standards; Salesforce CRM — Opportunity Close Date

attributes.name: previous_relationship_status
attributes.description: The relationship status immediately prior to the most recent state-change event. Stored alongside the current status to support audit trail requirements and state-transition reporting without requiring a separate history table.
attributes.references: 4As Audit Trail Standards; FTC Advertising Practices — Record Keeping

attributes.name: primary_client_contact
attributes.description: The name of the primary contact person on the client/advertiser side for this relationship (e.g., CMO, Marketing Director, Brand Manager). Used for account management, communication routing, and CRM record maintenance.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Contact Object; GDPR Article 4

attributes.name: relationship_code
attributes.description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for this agency-client relationship, used in Salesforce CRM, Mediaocean Prisma, and client-facing correspondence (e.g., REL-2024-00123). Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Opportunity/Account Relationship; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: relationship_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the agency-client relationship. Active indicates a live, operational engagement; pending indicates a relationship that has been agreed but not yet commenced; suspended indicates a temporary hold; terminated indicates the relationship has ended; under_review indicates a formal review or pitch process is underway. Serves as LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM — Opportunity Stage; 4As Agency Management Best Practices

attributes.name: relationship_tier
attributes.description: The strategic importance tier assigned to this agency-client relationship based on revenue, strategic value, and growth potential. Strategic denotes top-tier accounts receiving dedicated senior resources; key_account denotes significant accounts; standard denotes routine engagements; emerging denotes new or high-growth potential accounts.
attributes.references: 4As Account Segmentation Framework; ANA Client Tiering Guidelines

attributes.name: relationship_type
attributes.description: Classification of the formal relationship structure between the agency and the advertiser. AOR (Agency of Record) denotes full-service exclusive mandate; project_based is scoped to a single campaign or deliverable; retainer is an ongoing fee-based engagement; roster_agency is a non-exclusive approved supplier; preferred_vendor is a qualified but non-exclusive partner. Serves as CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Relationship Classification; ANA Agency Roster Management Guidelines

attributes.name: renewal_date
attributes.description: The date on which the agency-client relationship contract is due for renewal or renegotiation. Distinct from the effective end date; used for proactive renewal pipeline management and AOR retention tracking.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA — Contract Management; Salesforce CRM — Renewal Opportunity

attributes.name: review_cycle
attributes.description: The agreed frequency at which the agency-client relationship is formally reviewed, including performance evaluation, scope assessment, and fee renegotiation. Supports Quarterly Business Review (QBR) scheduling and executive relationship lifecycle reporting.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Performance Review Standards; ANA QBR Guidelines

attributes.name: scope_of_services
attributes.description: Comma-delimited list of service disciplines covered under this relationship (e.g., media, creative, pr, digital, social, ooh, programmatic, sem, seo). Defines the breadth of the agency mandate and is used for competitive conflict management and scope expansion tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: media|creative|pr|digital|social|ooh|programmatic|sem|seo|analytics|content|influencer — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: 4As Scope of Services Framework; ANA Agency Relationship Guidelines; IAB Service Taxonomy

products.name: client_brief
products.description: Formal creative or campaign brief submitted by the client to initiate a new campaign, project, or creative deliverable. Captures brief title, brief type (campaign, creative, media, PR), submission date, requesting contact, target audience description, key message, mandatory inclusions, budget indication, desired KPIs, timeline requirements, and brief status (draft, approved, in-progress, closed). The SSOT for client-originated work requests.
products.reference: Workfront Project Brief / Salesforce CRM Case

attributes.name: client_brief_id
attributes.description: Unique system-generated identifier for the client brief record. Primary key for the client_brief data product.

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the client (advertiser or agency) who submitted this brief. Links to the client master record.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM - Sales Cloud Account Object

attributes.name: agency_relationship_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.agency_relationship. Business justification: A client brief is submitted and executed within the scope of a formal agency-client relationship. Linking client_brief to agency_relationship via agency_relationship_id allows the agency to track whic

attributes.name: attribution_model_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.attribution_model. Business justification: Client briefs specify attribution requirements (e.g., 'use data-driven attribution, 30-day window'). Media planners reference the brief-specified attribution model when configuring campaigns. This lin

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: A client brief is typically scoped to a specific brand within the advertiser's portfolio. Adding client_brand_id to client_brief enables brand-level campaign brief tracking, allowing the agency to man

attributes.name: initiative_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.initiative. Business justification: Client briefs initiate projects/initiatives that are tracked in project management systems. Currently workfront_project_id is stored as an unlinked string. Normalizing to initiative_id FK enables join

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Campaign briefs specify target audience segments for media planning and activation. This is the primary mechanism linking client requirements to targetable audiences in advertising operations. Essenti

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Briefs frequently specify preferred suppliers/publishers for campaign execution. Media planners reference approved vendor lists when briefing campaigns. Essential for RFP routing and vendor selection

attributes.name: target_persona_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.persona. Business justification: Briefs reference personas to guide creative strategy, messaging, and channel selection. Standard practice in advertising where personas inform campaign development beyond just segment targeting. Suppo

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Calendar date on which the brief was formally approved by the agency account team and accepted for execution. Null if not yet approved.
attributes.references: Workfront - Approval Date; 4As Agency Operations Standards

attributes.name: brand_guidelines_version
attributes.description: Version identifier of the client brand guidelines document that must be adhered to for all creative executions under this brief (e.g., 'v3.2', '2024-Q1'). Ensures creative compliance with current brand standards.
attributes.references: Adobe Creative Cloud DAM - Asset Version Control; Workfront DAM

attributes.name: brief_number
attributes.description: Externally-visible, human-readable reference number assigned to the brief at submission (e.g., BRF-2024-000123). Used in client communications, Workfront project creation, and Mediaocean Prisma work orders.
attributes.references: Workfront Project Management - Work Request Numbering; IAB Data Standards

attributes.name: brief_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the client brief. Drives workflow routing: draft (being authored), submitted (awaiting review), approved (accepted for execution), in_progress (active work underway), on_hold (paused), closed (completed or cancelled).
attributes.references: Workfront - Work Request Status; 4As Agency Operations Standards

attributes.name: brief_type
attributes.description: Classification of the brief by the type of work being requested. Determines the downstream workflow and team assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: campaign|creative|media|pr|digital|brand_strategy — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: 4As Agency Operations Standards; Workfront Work Request Type

attributes.name: budget_currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the budget indication amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency client engagements.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Configuration

attributes.name: budget_indication_amount
attributes.description: Client-indicated total budget for the campaign or project described in this brief, expressed in the operating currency. This is a directional figure provided at brief stage, not a committed spend. Used for resource planning and scoping.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA - PS Project Budget; ANA Budget Management Guidelines

attributes.name: campaign_end_date
attributes.description: Client-requested end date for the campaign or deliverable described in the brief. Defines the desired flight window.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma - Campaign Flight Dates; IAB Campaign Standards

attributes.name: campaign_objective
attributes.description: Primary marketing objective the client wants to achieve with this campaign or project. Drives KPI selection and channel strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: brand_awareness|lead_generation|sales_conversion|retention|consideration|reach|engagement|app_install — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Digital Advertising Standards; ANA Marketing Objectives Framework

attributes.name: campaign_start_date
attributes.description: Client-requested start date for the campaign or deliverable described in the brief. Represents the desired go-live date as specified by the client.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma - Campaign Flight Dates; IAB Campaign Standards

attributes.name: channel_requirements
attributes.description: Client-specified media channels or platforms required or preferred for this campaign (e.g., CTV, DOOH, OTT, social, search, OOH, programmatic). Free-text to capture complex multi-channel requirements.
attributes.references: IAB Channel Taxonomy; Mediaocean Prisma Media Planning Standards

attributes.name: closed_date
attributes.description: Date on which the brief was formally closed, either upon successful completion of all deliverables or cancellation. Null if the brief is still active. Supports lifecycle reporting and pipeline analytics.
attributes.references: Workfront - Project Completion Date; 4As Agency Operations Standards

attributes.name: closure_reason
attributes.description: Reason code explaining why the brief was closed. Populated when brief_status transitions to 'closed'. Supports win/loss analysis and client relationship management.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM - Opportunity Close Reason; 4As Agency Operations Standards

attributes.name: competitive_context
attributes.description: Client-provided description of the competitive landscape relevant to this campaign, including key competitors, market positioning, and Share of Voice (SOV) considerations. Confidential business intelligence.
attributes.references: Nielsen Ad Intel Competitive Monitoring; ANA Competitive Intelligence Guidelines

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: System timestamp recording when the client brief record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field aligned with Silver Layer ingestion standards.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity; IAB Data Standards

attributes.name: creative_format_requirements
attributes.description: Specific creative formats, sizes, or specifications required by the client (e.g., IAB standard banner sizes, VAST video specs, print dimensions). Informs creative production scope.
attributes.references: IAB Ad Unit Guidelines; VAST/VPAID Standards; ASA Creative Standards

attributes.name: deliverable_due_date
attributes.description: Client-specified deadline by which the primary deliverable (creative, media plan, strategy deck, etc.) must be completed and delivered. May differ from campaign start date.
attributes.references: Workfront - Task Due Date; 4As Agency Operations Standards

attributes.name: geographic_scope
attributes.description: Description of the geographic markets or regions targeted by this campaign (e.g., national, specific DMAs, countries). Free-text to support complex geo-targeting requirements.
attributes.references: IAB Geo-Targeting Standards; Mediaocean Prisma Market Configuration

attributes.name: is_agency_managed
attributes.description: Indicates whether this brief is submitted through an intermediary agency on behalf of the end advertiser (True) or directly by the advertiser (False). Affects billing, reporting, and account team assignment.
attributes.references: 4As Agency-Client Relationship Standards; Salesforce CRM Account Type

attributes.name: is_confidential
