attributes.description: Indicates whether the client has designated this brief as confidential, restricting access to named account team members only. Drives data access controls and document handling procedures.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5; CCPA Section 1798; 4As Confidentiality Standards

attributes.name: key_message
attributes.description: The primary communication message or value proposition the client wants to convey to the target audience. Core creative direction provided in the brief.
attributes.references: 4As Creative Brief Standards; ASA Advertising Standards

attributes.name: mandatory_exclusions
attributes.description: Client-specified elements, themes, competitors, or content that must NOT appear in any deliverable. Includes brand safety restrictions and competitive separation requirements.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Standards; ASA Advertising Standards; IAB Brand Safety Guidelines

attributes.name: mandatory_inclusions
attributes.description: Client-specified elements that must appear in all creative executions or deliverables (e.g., legal disclaimers, brand taglines, product claims, regulatory copy, logo usage requirements).
attributes.references: ASA Advertising Standards; FTC Advertising Practices Division Guidelines; ICC Advertising and Marketing Communications Code

attributes.name: primary_kpi
attributes.description: The single most important Key Performance Indicator (KPI) the client will use to measure campaign success. Drives optimization strategy and reporting requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ctr|cpm|cpc|cpa|roas|vtr|grp|trp|reach|frequency|engagement_rate|conversion_rate — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Digital Advertising Standards; MRC Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: primary_kpi_target_value
attributes.description: Numeric target value the client expects to achieve for the primary KPI (e.g., a CTR of 0.0250 means 2.5%, a CPM of 5.00 means $5.00 per thousand impressions). Precision to 4 decimal places supports rate-based KPIs.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Advertising Standards; MRC Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: priority_level
attributes.description: Client or account team-assigned priority level for this brief, used to triage work intake and allocate agency resources. Drives scheduling in Workfront project management.
attributes.references: Workfront - Work Request Priority; 4As Resource Management Standards

attributes.name: regulatory_compliance_notes
attributes.description: Client-specified regulatory, legal, or compliance requirements that must be observed in all deliverables (e.g., GDPR consent requirements, CCPA disclosures, FTC endorsement guidelines, industry-specific advertising restrictions).
attributes.references: FTC Advertising Practices Division; GDPR Article 6; CCPA Section 1798; ASA CAP Code; EASA Best Practice Guidelines

attributes.name: revision_reason
attributes.description: Narrative explanation of why the brief was revised from a prior version. Populated when brief_version > 1. Supports change management and audit trail.
attributes.references: Workfront - Change Request Notes; 4As Brief Management Standards

attributes.name: salesforce_opportunity_code
attributes.description: Reference identifier of the associated Salesforce CRM Opportunity record, linking the brief to the revenue pipeline and account management context.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM - Sales Cloud Opportunity Object

attributes.name: submission_date
attributes.description: Calendar date on which the client formally submitted the brief to the agency. The principal business event date for this record.
attributes.references: Workfront - Work Request Submission Date; IAB Data Standards

attributes.name: title
attributes.description: Short, descriptive title of the client brief as provided by the requesting client contact (e.g., 'Q3 2024 Summer Campaign Launch Brief').

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: System timestamp recording when the client brief record was most recently modified. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity; IAB Data Standards

attributes.name: version
attributes.description: Incrementing version number of the brief document, starting at 1. Incremented each time the client submits a revised brief. Supports brief revision history and change management.
attributes.references: Workfront - Document Version Control; 4As Brief Management Standards

products.name: revenue_target
products.description: Annual and quarterly revenue targets set for each advertiser account, broken down by service line (media, creative, digital, PR). Captures target period, target amount, currency, service line, target type (gross revenue, net revenue, fee income), target owner (account director), and target status. Supports P&L management, QBR preparation, and account growth planning. Distinct from actual billed revenue owned by the finance domain.
products.reference: Salesforce CRM Forecast / SAP S/4HANA CO-PA

attributes.name: revenue_target_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each client revenue target record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertiser account for which this revenue target is set. Links to the client.advertiser master record.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud — Account Object

attributes.name: agency_relationship_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.agency_relationship. Business justification: Revenue targets are set in the context of a specific agency-client relationship. Linking revenue_target to agency_relationship via agency_relationship_id allows finance and account management teams to

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Revenue targets are set at the master agreement level in advertising — account teams forecast billings against contracted agreements. Agreement-level revenue attainment reporting and pipeline-to-contr

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Revenue targets in advertising agencies are frequently set at brand level within advertiser accounts, not just advertiser-level. Enables brand-specific revenue forecasting, pipeline management, accoun

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Revenue targets are managed and reported by cost center for management accounting and variance analysis. `cost_centre_code` is a denormalized text reference to the cost_center entity; replacing it wit

attributes.name: finance_budget_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Revenue targets drive budget allocation decisions and resource planning. Account directors set client revenue targets that inform budget approvals, headcount planning, and investment decisions for cli

attributes.name: initiative_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.initiative. Business justification: Agency revenue targets are set against specific initiatives (pitch wins, project scopes). Linking enables tracking of actual project revenue vs. targets — a core agency financial management and accoun

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Revenue targets in advertising are set against specific contracted SOWs. SOW-level revenue attainment tracking — comparing target vs. actual billings against contracted scope — is a core account manag

attributes.name: account_growth_rate_pct
attributes.description: The year-over-year or period-over-period percentage growth rate embedded in this target relative to the prior comparable period's target. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 = 15%). Used in account growth planning and QBR target-setting rationale.
attributes.references: ANA Agency Compensation Guidelines; Internal Account Growth Planning Framework

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: The date on which the revenue target was formally approved by the designated approver. Marks the transition from pending_approval to approved status and is used in audit trails for P&L governance.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO Approval Workflow; Internal P&L Governance

attributes.name: confidence_level
attributes.description: Account team's assessed confidence in achieving this revenue target: high (strong pipeline, contracted SOW coverage), medium (pipeline in progress, partial SOW coverage), low (speculative, dependent on new business wins). Used in risk-adjusted P&L forecasting.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud Forecast Category; Internal P&L Governance

attributes.name: contracted_sow_coverage_pct
attributes.business_glossary_term: Contracted Statement of Work (SOW) Coverage Percentage
attributes.description: The proportion of the revenue target already covered by signed Statements of Work (SOW) or Insertion Orders (IO) at the time of target setting or last update. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.6500 = 65%). Supports pipeline risk assessment and P&L confidence scoring.
attributes.references: IAB Insertion Order Standards; Mediaocean Prisma SOW Management; ANA Agency Compensation Guidelines

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this revenue target record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit trail creation anchor for data lineage and governance.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability); Internal Data Governance Standard

attributes.name: crm_opportunity_code
attributes.description: The Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud Opportunity record ID linked to this revenue target, where the target originates from or is tracked against a specific sales opportunity. Enables traceability from target to pipeline to closed-won revenue.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud — Opportunity Object

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the target_amount is denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency advertiser portfolios and cross-market P&L consolidation.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Multi-Currency Configuration

attributes.name: fiscal_quarter
attributes.description: The fiscal quarter to which this target applies (Q1–Q4). Populated when target_period_type is 'quarterly'; null for annual targets. Used in QBR preparation and quarterly pacing analysis.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Period; Internal QBR Governance

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: The four-digit fiscal year to which this revenue target applies (e.g., 2025). Aligns with the SAP S/4HANA FI fiscal year calendar for P&L management and budget reconciliation.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Year Variant; GAAP/IFRS Reporting Period

attributes.name: fx_rate_applied
attributes.description: The exchange rate used to convert target_amount to target_amount_usd at the time of target creation or last revision. Stored for auditability and variance analysis when FX rates fluctuate during the target period.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Exchange Rate Table; ISO 4217

attributes.name: is_stretch_target
attributes.description: Indicates whether this target is designated as a stretch (aspirational) target above the base committed target. True = stretch target used for incentive compensation modelling; False = base committed target used for P&L planning. Supports Workday HCM talent performance review and bonus calculation.
attributes.references: Workday HCM Talent Management — Performance Goals; ANA Agency Compensation Guidelines

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for account team commentary on the revenue target, including assumptions, dependencies, client commitments, or QBR talking points. Not used for structured data; supports qualitative context in account growth planning.
attributes.references: 4As Account Management Best Practices; Internal QBR Governance

attributes.name: original_target_amount
attributes.description: The initial revenue target amount set at the start of the period before any revisions. Preserved for variance analysis comparing original plan vs. revised plan vs. actual billed revenue. Populated only when revision_number > 0.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO Budget Version Management; ANA Agency Compensation Guidelines

attributes.name: period_end_date
attributes.description: The calendar date on which the revenue target period ends. Defines the boundary for target attainment measurement and QBR reporting cycles.
attributes.references: GAAP/IFRS Revenue Recognition (ASC 606 / IFRS 15); SAP S/4HANA FI

attributes.name: period_start_date
attributes.description: The calendar date on which the revenue target period begins. Used to align target pacing with actual billed revenue from the finance domain and to support time-series analytics.
attributes.references: GAAP/IFRS Revenue Recognition (ASC 606 / IFRS 15); SAP S/4HANA FI

attributes.name: pipeline_amount
attributes.description: The total value of identified but not yet contracted revenue opportunities in the Salesforce CRM pipeline that are expected to contribute to this target. Distinct from target_amount (the committed plan) and actual billed revenue. Supports gap-to-target analysis.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud — Opportunity Pipeline; Internal P&L Governance

attributes.name: profit_centre_code
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA CO profit centre code associated with this revenue target, enabling segment-level P&L reporting by business unit, geography, or service line. Supports EBITDA reporting and management accounting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO Profit Centre Accounting; GAAP/IFRS Segment Reporting (IFRS 8)

attributes.name: revision_number
attributes.description: Sequential integer indicating how many times this revenue target has been revised (0 = original). Enables version tracking of target changes throughout the fiscal period, supporting variance analysis between original and revised targets.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO Budget Version Management; Internal P&L Governance

attributes.name: revision_reason
attributes.description: Free-text explanation for why the revenue target was revised (e.g., client budget reduction, scope expansion, new service line added, market conditions). Supports QBR narrative and P&L variance commentary.
attributes.references: Internal P&L Governance; 4As Account Management Best Practices

attributes.name: roas_target
attributes.description: The target Return on Ad Spend (ROAS) ratio associated with this revenue target, where applicable (primarily for performance-based media service lines). Expressed as a multiplier (e.g., 4.0000 = $4 revenue per $1 ad spend). Used in digital performance optimization and KPI alignment with The Trade Desk programmatic buying.
attributes.references: IAB Performance Marketing Standards; The Trade Desk Bid Management KPIs

attributes.name: service_line
attributes.description: The agency service line to which this revenue target is attributed: media (Media Planning and Buying), creative (Creative Development and Production), digital (Digital Marketing and Performance Optimization), pr (Public Relations and Communications), analytics (Data Analytics and Audience Insights), strategy (Brand Strategy and Positioning). Enables service-line-level P&L reporting.
attributes.references: 4As Service Line Classification; ANA Agency Compensation Guidelines

attributes.name: target_amount
attributes.description: The gross or net revenue target value set for the advertiser account for the specified period and service line, expressed in the currency defined by currency_code. This is the planned figure; actual billed revenue is owned by the finance domain. Core input for P&L management and account growth planning.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO Profit Center Planning; ANA Agency Compensation Guidelines; GAAP/IFRS ASC 606

attributes.name: target_amount_usd
attributes.description: The revenue target amount converted to US Dollars (USD) using the exchange rate applied at the time of target setting. Enables cross-currency portfolio aggregation and global P&L reporting without requiring runtime FX conversion.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Translation; ISO 4217

attributes.name: target_code
attributes.description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for this revenue target record, used in Quarterly Business Review (QBR) reporting, P&L management, and cross-system reconciliation with SAP S/4HANA CO module.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO — Profit Center Planning; Internal Naming Convention

attributes.name: target_name
attributes.description: Human-readable label for this revenue target, typically combining the advertiser name, service line, and target period (e.g., 'Acme Corp — Media — FY2025 Q1'). Used in QBR decks and account growth planning documents.

attributes.name: target_owner_name
attributes.description: Display name of the account director or account manager who owns this revenue target. Stored denormalized for reporting convenience in QBR decks and P&L dashboards without requiring a join to the talent domain.
attributes.references: 4As Account Management Best Practices; Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud

attributes.name: target_period_type
attributes.description: Granularity of the target period: annual (full fiscal year), quarterly (fiscal quarter), or monthly (calendar month). Determines how the target_amount is interpreted and how actuals are paced against it in QBR reporting.
attributes.references: ANA Agency Compensation Guidelines; Internal P&L Governance

attributes.name: target_setting_method
attributes.description: The methodology used to derive this revenue target: top_down (allocated from agency-level P&L plan), bottom_up (built from individual campaign and SOW estimates), negotiated (agreed bilaterally with client), market_indexed (benchmarked against market growth indices such as Nielsen Ad Intel competitive data).
attributes.references: ANA Agency Compensation Guidelines; Nielsen Ad Intel Market Intelligence

attributes.name: target_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the revenue target record. draft: being authored; pending_approval: submitted for sign-off; approved: signed off by account director and finance; active: in-period and being tracked; revised: superseded by a revised target; closed: period ended; cancelled: target withdrawn. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|revised|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud Opportunity Stage; Internal P&L Governance

attributes.name: target_type
attributes.description: Classification of the revenue target by income recognition method: gross_revenue (total billings before agency commission deduction), net_revenue (billings net of media costs), fee_income (project or retainer fees), commission_income (media commission earned), retainer_fee (recurring fixed-fee arrangement). Drives P&L line allocation in SAP S/4HANA FI.
attributes.references: IAB Revenue Recognition Guidelines; SAP S/4HANA FI Revenue Accounting

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp of the most recent modification to this revenue target record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change detection in ETL pipelines and audit trail completeness.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability); Internal Data Governance Standard

domains.name: media
domains.description: Governs all media and channel operations — including channel taxonomy, publisher properties, placements, media planning, buying, trafficking, programmatic deals, rate cards, reconciliation, and delivery scheduling across all paid media channels (digital, broadcast, OOH, CTV/OTT, print).
domains.references: IAB / MRC Media Buying and Trafficking Standards

products.name: media_insertion_order
products.description: Authoritative master record for all Insertion Orders (IOs) issued to media vendors and publishers. Captures IO number, advertiser, agency, vendor, flight dates, total authorized spend, payment terms, cancellation policy, IO status (draft, executed, cancelled), and client authorization details (authorizing party, authorization date, authorization method). Sourced from Mediaocean Prisma and serves as the contractual backbone for all media buys. Links to media plans, placements, and vendor records.

attributes.name: media_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the Insertion Order record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — this is a discrete contractual business event with a clear lifecycle (draft → executed → cancelled), carrying authorized spend, flight dates, and vendor/advertiser party references.

attributes.name: ad_channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.ad_channel. Business justification: media_insertion_order currently has channel_type as a STRING attribute. IOs are issued to media vendors for specific advertising channels (e.g., IO for Display advertising, IO for CTV). This FK normal

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertiser (client brand) on whose behalf this IO is issued. Links to the client.advertiser master record. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE category.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Advertiser; Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud — Account

attributes.name: agency_relationship_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.agency_relationship. Business justification: IOs are issued under a specific agency-client relationship that determines commission rates, billing terms, and notice periods applied on the IO. IO-level agency relationship tracking is standard in m

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the overarching master service agreement or framework contract under which this IO is issued. Supports contract hierarchy and legal compliance tracking.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchase Contract; IAB Standard Terms and Conditions

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: IOs authorize spend for a specific brand. Brand-level IO tracking is required for brand budget reconciliation, brand safety enforcement, and publisher-facing brand identification. Media buyers and fin

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertising campaign this IO supports. Enables campaign-level spend aggregation, KPI tracking, and ROAS reporting.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 — Campaign; Mediaocean Prisma — Campaign

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Insertion orders require cost center assignment for budget allocation, financial reporting, and expense tracking - fundamental financial control in advertising operations.

attributes.name: finance_budget_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Insertion orders must reference approved budgets for spend authorization, compliance checking, and budget consumption tracking in financial controls.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: IOs are the primary financial commitment document in agency media operations. GL account coding at the IO level is required for accounts payable posting, accrual accounting, and financial statement cl

attributes.name: initiative_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.initiative. Business justification: Insertion orders execute within strategic initiatives. Linking enables portfolio-level budget management, cross-campaign spend analysis, and strategic alignment reporting required for client business

attributes.name: plan_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent media plan from which this IO was generated. Enables traceability from strategic plan through execution to billing.

attributes.name: publisher_id
attributes.description: Reference to the media vendor or publisher receiving this IO. Links to the vendor master record for payment, reconciliation, and compliance checks.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Vendor; TAG Certified Against Fraud

attributes.name: publisher_property_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.publisher_property. Business justification: media_insertion_order currently has FK to publisher (parent entity). Many IOs are property-specific (e.g., IO for NYTimes.com, IO for WSJ Print Edition). While media_insertion_order → publisher → publ

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: An IO is issued under a specific SOW that authorizes the media spend — more granular than the master agreement already linked. Agencies track IO spend against SOW budgets for client billing and scope

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the internal agency entity (buying unit) issuing this IO on behalf of the advertiser. Supports multi-agency holding company structures and commission calculations.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Agency; 4As Agency Identification Standards

attributes.name: vendor_rate_card_id
attributes.description: Reference to the vendor rate card governing the pricing of placements under this IO. Links to the media rate card product for CPM, CPC, CPA, and flat-fee rate validation.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Rate Card; IAB Standard Terms and Conditions

attributes.name: agency_commission_amount
attributes.description: Agency commission deducted from the gross authorized spend, typically calculated as a percentage of gross billings (standard 15% of gross or negotiated rate). Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET (adjustment) category.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Commission Amount; 4As Agency Commission Standards; SAP S/4HANA CO

attributes.name: authorized_spend_amount
attributes.description: Total gross media spend authorized under this IO, expressed in the IO currency. Represents the maximum contractual commitment to the vendor before agency commission and fees. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET (gross base amount) category.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Authorized Amount; IAB Standard Terms and Conditions; SAP S/4HANA FI

attributes.name: billing_cycle
attributes.description: Frequency at which the vendor invoices against this IO. Drives accounts payable scheduling and cash flow forecasting in SAP S/4HANA FI.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Billing Cycle; SAP S/4HANA FI-AP

attributes.name: brand_safety_requirements
attributes.description: Free-text or structured description of brand safety and suitability requirements contractually mandated for this IO (e.g., IAB content category exclusions, GARM brand safety floor). Enforced during trafficking and programmatic bid configuration.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certification; GARM Brand Safety Floor; IAB Tech Lab Content Taxonomy

attributes.name: buying_method
attributes.description: The transactional mechanism used to purchase media under this IO. Distinguishes direct buys from Real-Time Bidding (RTB), programmatic guaranteed, and Private Marketplace (PMP) deals. Informs DSP/SSP configuration in The Trade Desk.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab — OpenRTB Specification; The Trade Desk — Deal Types; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: cancellation_notice_days
attributes.description: Number of business days advance notice required to cancel this IO without incurring the full cancellation penalty. Operationalizes the cancellation policy for workflow and legal compliance.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Cancellation Notice; IAB Standard Terms and Conditions

attributes.name: cancellation_policy
attributes.description: Contractual cancellation terms governing the IO. Specifies whether the IO can be cancelled, the required notice period, and any financial penalties. Critical for financial liability management and vendor negotiations.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Cancellation Policy; IAB Standard Terms and Conditions for Internet Advertising

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this IO record was first created in the source system (Mediaocean Prisma). Used for audit trail and data lineage. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Created Date; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this IO (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET (currency code) category.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; Mediaocean Prisma — Currency

attributes.name: execution_date
attributes.description: The date on which the IO was formally executed (signed/authorized) by all parties. Represents the principal real-world business event timestamp at day granularity. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Execution Date; IAB Standard Terms and Conditions

attributes.name: flight_end_date
attributes.description: The last date on which media placements under this IO are authorized to run. Defines the end of the contracted delivery window. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Flight End Date; IAB Standard Terms and Conditions

attributes.name: flight_start_date
attributes.description: The first date on which media placements under this IO are authorized to run. Defines the beginning of the contracted delivery window. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER EFFECTIVE_FROM category.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Flight Start Date; IAB Standard Terms and Conditions

attributes.name: guaranteed_impressions
attributes.description: Total number of ad impressions contractually guaranteed by the vendor under this IO. Applicable for CPM and guaranteed programmatic buys. Used for delivery pacing and reconciliation against actuals.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Guaranteed Impressions; MRC Impression Measurement Guidelines; IAB

attributes.name: io_name
attributes.description: Human-readable descriptive name for the IO, typically combining advertiser, campaign, channel, and flight period (e.g., 'BrandX_Q3_2024_Display_IO'). Used in Mediaocean Prisma and trafficking systems for quick identification.

attributes.name: io_number
attributes.description: Externally-known, human-readable Insertion Order number assigned by Mediaocean Prisma at IO creation. Used on all vendor invoices, trafficking instructions, and reconciliation documents. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — IO Number; IAB Standard Terms and Conditions for Internet Advertising

attributes.name: io_status
attributes.description: Current workflow state of the Insertion Order. Drives downstream trafficking, billing, and reconciliation eligibility. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — IO Status; IAB Standard Terms and Conditions for Internet Advertising

attributes.name: io_type
attributes.description: Classification of the IO by buying method. Distinguishes direct buys from programmatic guaranteed, preferred deals, and private marketplace (PMP) deals. Informs DSP/SSP routing and billing treatment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|programmatic_guaranteed|preferred_deal|private_marketplace|direct_buy|sponsorship|barter — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab — Programmatic Supply Chain; Mediaocean Prisma — IO Type

attributes.name: is_programmatic
attributes.description: Indicates whether this IO governs programmatic media buying (True) or a direct/traditional buy (False). Used to route IO data to DSP/SSP systems such as The Trade Desk and to apply appropriate measurement and brand safety standards.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab — Programmatic Supply Chain; The Trade Desk; TAG Certified Against Fraud

attributes.name: makegood_policy
attributes.description: Contractual make-good policy specifying how the vendor compensates for under-delivery against guaranteed impressions or GRPs. Options include credit against future buys, re-run of spots, or cash refund.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Make-Good Policy; IAB Standard Terms and Conditions; 4As

attributes.name: net_spend_amount
attributes.description: Net media cost payable to the vendor after deducting agency commission from the gross authorized spend. Used for vendor payment and reconciliation. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET (net total) category.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Net Amount; IAB Standard Terms and Conditions; SAP S/4HANA FI-AP

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional contractual terms, special instructions, or operational notes associated with this IO that are not captured in structured fields. Sourced from Mediaocean Prisma IO comments.

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: Contractual payment terms agreed with the vendor, specifying the number of days after invoice date by which payment is due (e.g., Net 30, Net 45). Drives accounts payable scheduling in SAP S/4HANA FI-AP.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Payment Terms; SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; IAB Standard Terms and Conditions

attributes.name: po_number
attributes.description: Client-issued Purchase Order number associated with this IO. Required by many advertisers for invoice matching and financial controls in SAP S/4HANA MM. Links the IO to the client's internal procurement system.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchase Order; Mediaocean Prisma — PO Number

attributes.name: pricing_model
attributes.description: The cost pricing model agreed with the vendor for this IO. Determines how media delivery is measured and billed: Cost Per Mille (CPM), Cost Per Click (CPC), Cost Per Acquisition (CPA), Cost Per Completed View (CPCV), flat fee, or Cost Per View (CPV).
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab — Pricing Models; Mediaocean Prisma — Pricing Model; MRC Viewability Standards

attributes.name: prisma_io_code
attributes.description: Native system identifier for this IO in Mediaocean Prisma. Enables direct cross-reference and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the source system of record.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this IO record was last modified in the source system. Tracks amendments, status changes, and spend revisions. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Modified Date; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Sequential version number incremented each time the IO is formally amended (e.g., spend increase, flight date change). Enables change history tracking and audit compliance.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — IO Version; IAB Standard Terms and Conditions

products.name: plan
products.description: Master record representing a structured media plan developed during the media planning phase. Captures plan name, client, campaign reference, planning period, total planned budget, target audience, channel mix allocation, GRP/TRP delivery targets, reach and frequency objectives, target markets/DMAs, demographic targets, approval status, and planner ownership. Sourced from Mediaocean Prisma. Serves as the SSOT for planned media strategy before buying execution begins.

attributes.name: plan_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the media plan record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertiser/client for whom this media plan is developed. Aligns with the Salesforce CRM account record.

attributes.name: agency_relationship_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.agency_relationship. Business justification: A media plan is executed under a specific agency-client AOR relationship, which governs commission rates, scope of services, and approval workflows applied to the plan. Planners must reference the AOR

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Media plans are developed at the brand level — brand guidelines, safety requirements, SOV targets, and budget allocations are brand-specific. Brand-level media planning is a core advertising workflow;

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent campaign this media plan supports. Links the media plan to its overarching campaign strategy and objectives.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Media plans must be assigned to cost centers for budget planning, approval workflows, and financial accountability in campaign planning processes.

attributes.name: creative_brief_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.creative_brief. Business justification: Media plans are built from creative briefs that define messaging, audience, deliverables, and mandatories. Planners reference the brief throughout planning to ensure media strategy aligns with creativ

attributes.name: finance_budget_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Media plans tied to specific budgets for planning, approval workflows, and budget allocation - fundamental financial planning relationship.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Media plans are budget authorization documents requiring GL account coding for revenue recognition and cost allocation reporting. Agency finance teams code plans to GL accounts to support P&L reportin

attributes.name: initiative_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.initiative. Business justification: Media plans are tracked as projects/initiatives in project management systems. Currently workfront_project_id is stored as an unlinked string. Normalizing to initiative_id FK enables joining to projec

attributes.name: persona_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.persona. Business justification: Persona-based media planning: media plans are strategically built around target personas (channel mix, daypart, content preferences). A domain expert expects the plan to reference the persona driving

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Media plans define primary target audience segments for campaign planning, budget allocation, and channel mix decisions. Core planning workflow requires linking plan to specific audience segment for t

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Media plans are executed under SOWs that authorize budget and scope. Planners reference the governing SOW for budget authority validation and scope compliance checks. Essential for financial controls

attributes.name: agency_commission_pct
attributes.description: The percentage of the gross media budget retained by the agency as commission. Standard industry rate is typically 15%. Used in financial reconciliation and P&L reporting.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Compensation Guidelines; ANA Media Transparency Standards

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: The date on which the media plan received formal client or internal approval. Marks the transition from planning to buying execution phase.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Management Standards; Mediaocean Prisma Workflow Standards

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: The client or internal approval status of the media plan. Tracks whether the plan has been formally approved by the client before buying execution commences. Managed via Workfront approval workflow.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Management Standards; Workfront Project Management Standards

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the client contact or internal stakeholder who formally approved this media plan. Captured for audit trail and accountability purposes.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Management Standards; FTC Advertising Practices Guidelines

attributes.name: buying_approach
attributes.description: The media buying methodology planned for this plan — direct IO-based buying, programmatic Real-Time Bidding (RTB) via DSP, or a hybrid approach. Determines execution path in Mediaocean Prisma and The Trade Desk.
attributes.references: IAB Programmatic Buying Standards; TAG Trustworthy Supply Chain Guidelines; Mediaocean Prisma Buying Standards

attributes.name: channel_mix
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of media channels included in this plan (e.g., 'digital_display, paid_social, ctv, ooh, broadcast_tv'). Defines the cross-channel strategy. Detailed channel-level budget splits are captured in child media plan line records.
attributes.references: IAB Channel Taxonomy; Mediaocean Prisma Media Planning Standards

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this media plan record was first created in the system. Audit trail field aligned with GDPR data processing records requirements.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the media plan budget and financial targets are denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IAB Financial Standards

attributes.name: daypart_strategy
attributes.description: The planned daypart scheduling strategy for broadcast and digital media placements. Defines when within the day media will be concentrated to maximize target audience reach.
attributes.references: IAB Daypart Definitions; Nielsen Ad Intel Audience Measurement Standards

attributes.name: flight_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which the planned media activity concludes in-market. Defines the end of the planning period. Nullable for open-ended or evergreen plans.
attributes.references: IAB Insertion Order Guidelines; Mediaocean Prisma Media Planning Standards

attributes.name: flight_start_date
attributes.description: The date on which the planned media activity begins running in-market. Defines the start of the planning period and aligns with insertion order (IO) effective dates in Mediaocean Prisma.
attributes.references: IAB Insertion Order Guidelines; Mediaocean Prisma Media Planning Standards

attributes.name: frequency_target
attributes.description: The planned average number of times a member of the target audience is exposed to the advertising during the flight period. Balances reach objectives against budget constraints.
attributes.references: IAB Measurement Guidelines; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines; Nielsen Ad Intel Standards

attributes.name: geographic_scope
attributes.description: The geographic coverage level of the media plan, indicating whether the campaign runs nationally, regionally, locally, or internationally. Drives media buying strategy and publisher selection.
attributes.references: IAB Geographic Targeting Standards; Nielsen DMA Definitions

attributes.name: grp_target
attributes.description: The planned Gross Rating Point (GRP) delivery target for this media plan, representing the total weight of advertising exposure across the total population. Tracked via Nielsen Ad Intel.
attributes.references: Nielsen Ad Intel GRP Tracking Standards; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines; IAB Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: is_addressable
attributes.description: Indicates whether this media plan includes addressable media components (e.g., addressable TV, CTV/OTT targeting at household level). Triggers additional data governance and privacy compliance requirements under GDPR/CCPA.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5; CCPA Section 1798.100; IAB Addressable Media Standards; NAI Code of Conduct

attributes.name: is_programmatic
attributes.description: Indicates whether this media plan includes programmatic buying components executed via a Demand-Side Platform (DSP) such as The Trade Desk. Drives workflow routing and technology fee accounting.
attributes.references: IAB Programmatic Buying Standards; TAG Trustworthy Supply Chain Guidelines

attributes.name: net_budget
attributes.description: The net media budget after deducting agency commission and applicable fees from the gross planned budget. Represents the actual spend allocated to media vendors and publishers.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Financial Accounting; ANA Media Transparency Standards; Mediaocean Prisma Billing Standards

attributes.name: plan_name
attributes.description: Human-readable descriptive name of the media plan, typically including client name, campaign flight, and planning period (e.g., 'Acme Q3 2025 Brand Awareness Plan'). Used in Mediaocean Prisma and client presentations.

attributes.name: plan_number
attributes.description: Externally-known unique business identifier for the media plan as assigned by Mediaocean Prisma. Used in client-facing documents, insertion orders, and cross-system references.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Media Planning Standards; IAB Insertion Order Guidelines

attributes.name: plan_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the media plan within the planning and buying workflow. Drives downstream buying execution in Mediaocean Prisma. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|paused|completed|cancelled — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Workflow Standards; IAB Media Planning Guidelines

attributes.name: plan_type
attributes.description: Classification of the media plan by strategic objective and buying approach. Determines the KPI framework and channel mix strategy applied. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: brand_awareness|direct_response|performance|sponsorship|integrated|programmatic — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines; 4As Media Planning Standards

attributes.name: primary_kpi
attributes.description: The primary Key Performance Indicator (KPI) against which this media plan's success will be measured. Drives optimization strategy and reporting framework. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cpm|cpc|cpa|ctr|roas|grp|trp|vtr|reach|frequency — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines; MRC Measurement Standards; 4As KPI Framework

attributes.name: prisma_plan_code
attributes.description: The native plan identifier assigned by Mediaocean Prisma, the system of record for media planning and buying. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse and Prisma.

attributes.name: reach_target_pct
attributes.description: The planned percentage of the target audience to be reached at least once during the flight period. A core media planning objective used to evaluate channel mix effectiveness.
attributes.references: IAB Measurement Guidelines; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines; Comscore Cross-Platform Analytics Standards

attributes.name: sfdc_opportunity_code
attributes.description: The Salesforce CRM Opportunity ID associated with this media plan. Links the media plan to the sales pipeline record for revenue tracking and account management reporting.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud Standards; 4As CRM Integration Guidelines

attributes.name: target_age_range
attributes.description: The primary demographic age range targeted by this media plan (e.g., '25-54', '18-34'). Used for GRP/TRP calculation and audience buying parameters in Nielsen Ad Intel and The Trade Desk.
attributes.references: Nielsen Ad Intel GRP Tracking Standards; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: target_cpm
attributes.description: The planned Cost Per Mille (CPM) — cost per 1,000 impressions — used as the benchmark rate for this media plan. Informs rate card negotiations and programmatic bid strategies in The Trade Desk.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines; MRC Measurement Standards; Mediaocean Prisma Rate Card Standards

attributes.name: target_gender
attributes.description: Gender targeting parameter for the media plan audience. Informs GRP/TRP delivery targets and audience buying configurations in Nielsen Ad Intel and The Trade Desk.
attributes.references: Nielsen Ad Intel GRP Tracking Standards; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: target_markets
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of target geographic markets or Designated Market Areas (DMAs) where media will run (e.g., 'New York DMA, Los Angeles DMA, Chicago DMA'). Used for geo-targeting and local market buying.
attributes.references: Nielsen DMA Definitions; IAB Geographic Targeting Standards

attributes.name: total_planned_budget
attributes.description: The gross total budget allocated to this media plan across all channels and placements for the full flight period. Expressed in the plan currency. Sourced from Mediaocean Prisma budget module.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO Budget Management; Mediaocean Prisma Billing Standards; ANA Budget Management Guidelines

attributes.name: trp_target
attributes.description: The planned Target Rating Point (TRP) delivery target for this media plan, representing the total weight of advertising exposure within the defined target audience demographic. Tracked via Nielsen Ad Intel.
attributes.references: Nielsen Ad Intel GRP Tracking Standards; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this media plan record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.

attributes.name: version
attributes.description: Version identifier of the media plan (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Tracks iterative revisions submitted for client review or internal approval. Mediaocean Prisma supports versioned plan management.

products.name: plan_line
products.description: Individual line-item within a media plan representing a specific channel, vehicle, and flight combination. Captures line number, media type (TV, digital, OOH, print, OTT/CTV, DOOH), vehicle name, market, start/end dates, planned units, planned GRPs/TRPs, planned impressions, planned CPM/CPC/CPA, planned spend, and approval status. Enables granular budget allocation and channel-level planning visibility.
products.reference: Media Plan Line Item — Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: plan_line_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each individual line item within a media plan. Primary key for this entity. TRANSACTION_LINE role — each line represents one channel/vehicle/flight combination within a parent media plan.

attributes.name: ad_channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.ad_channel. Business justification: plan_line currently has media_type and media_subtype as STRING attributes. Each plan line represents a specific channel, vehicle, and flight combination. While plan_line → media_placement → ad_channel

attributes.name: ad_format_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.ad_format. Business justification: plan_line currently has ad_format as a STRING attribute. Each plan line specifies the ad format for the planned media (e.g., 300x250 Banner, Pre-roll Video). This FK normalizes the plan_line-to-format

attributes.name: asset_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.creative_asset. Business justification: Plan lines specify which creative assets will run in each placement. Essential for trafficking planning, publisher spec validation, and ensuring creative is ready before media flights begin.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertising campaign this media plan line supports. Links the line item to the overarching campaign strategy and objectives for cross-campaign reporting and budget tracking.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 — Campaign Object; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: contract_rate_card_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_card. Business justification: Media planners validate planned CPMs/rates against negotiated contract rate cards during plan line creation. This link enables rate compliance reporting — confirming planned spend aligns with contract

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Plan lines often allocated to specific cost centers for granular budget tracking and cross-functional cost allocation in matrix organizations.

attributes.name: creative_brief_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.creative_brief. Business justification: Plan lines are built to fulfill creative brief requirements (channel, format, audience). In multi-brand or multi-brief campaigns, individual plan lines may reference different briefs than the parent p

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Plan lines represent individual media cost commitments by channel/format. GL account coding at the line level enables granular cost classification for financial reporting, supporting channel-level P&L

attributes.name: media_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.media_insertion_order. Business justification: A plan line is executed via a media insertion order — the IO is the contractual instrument that authorizes the buy for that plan line. Adding media_insertion_order_id to plan_line provides direct trac

attributes.name: media_placement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific media placement or ad unit associated with this line item. Links to the placement master record defining the ad environment, size, and position. RESOURCE_REFERENCE per TRANSACTION_LINE canonical role.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 — Placement Object; IAB Ad Unit Guidelines

attributes.name: plan_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent media plan that contains this line item. Establishes the header/detail relationship between the media plan and its individual line items. HEADER_REFERENCE per TRANSACTION_LINE canonical role.

attributes.name: programmatic_deal_id
attributes.description: Unique deal identifier for programmatic private marketplace (PMP) or programmatic guaranteed (PG) transactions associated with this line item. Used in The Trade Desk and DSP platforms for deal activation and reporting.
attributes.references: IAB OpenRTB Specification; The Trade Desk Deal Management; TAG Programmatic Standards

attributes.name: publisher_id
attributes.description: FK to vendor.publisher

attributes.name: publisher_property_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.publisher_property. Business justification: A plan line specifies the exact publisher property (vehicle) where the ad will run — e.g., CNN.com, ESPN App, NBC broadcast. plan_line currently has vehicle_name (STRING) which is a denormalized refer

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Plan lines specify granular audience targeting for individual media vehicles and tactics. Buyers need explicit segment linkage for rate negotiation, inventory availability checks, and delivery forecas

attributes.name: agency_commission_pct
attributes.description: Percentage of gross media spend retained by the agency as commission for this line item. Standard advertising industry compensation model (historically 15%). Used for net spend calculation and client billing transparency.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Compensation Guidelines; ANA Agency Compensation Standards; SAP S/4HANA CO

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Current workflow approval status of this media plan line item. Governs whether the line is authorized for buying and trafficking. LIFECYCLE_STATUS per TRANSACTION_LINE canonical role. Tracks progression from draft through client and internal approval gates.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Line Status; Workfront Approval Workflow; 4As Media Plan Approval Standards

attributes.name: buying_status
attributes.description: Operational buying and trafficking status of this line item, tracking progression from plan through insertion order issuance, confirmation, trafficking, and delivery. Distinct from approval_status which governs internal authorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_ordered|io_sent|io_confirmed|trafficking|live|completed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Buying Status; Google Campaign Manager 360 — Trafficking Status; IAB IO Standards

attributes.name: buying_type
attributes.description: The pricing and buying model for this line item, determining how media is purchased and measured. CPM (Cost Per Mille), CPC (Cost Per Click), CPA (Cost Per Acquisition), CPV (Cost Per View), flat_rate, GRP (Gross Rating Point), or TRP (Target Rating Point). Drives rate card application and performance benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cpm|cpc|cpa|cpv|flat_rate|grp|trp — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Measurement Guidelines; MRC Viewability Standards; 4As Media Buying Standards

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the geographic territory where this media line item will be delivered (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN). Required for international campaign management and regulatory compliance.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this media plan line item record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, version history, and compliance with data governance requirements. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED per TRANSACTION_LINE canonical role.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity; SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values on this line item (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multi-currency campaign management and financial consolidation.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Multi-Currency; EASA International Standards

attributes.name: daypart
attributes.description: Broadcast or digital daypart specification for this line item, defining the time-of-day window for ad delivery (e.g., primetime, daytime, late_night). Critical for broadcast TV/radio buying and audience composition planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: primetime|daytime|late_night|early_morning|weekend|all_day|access — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Nielsen Daypart Definitions; 4As Broadcast Buying Standards; MRC Audience Measurement

attributes.name: flight_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which ad delivery for this media plan line item is scheduled to end. Defines the close of the flight period. Used for pacing, reconciliation, and campaign wrap-up reporting. EFFECTIVE_UNTIL per TRANSACTION_LINE canonical role.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Flight End; IAB Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: flight_start_date
attributes.description: The date on which ad delivery for this media plan line item is scheduled to begin. Defines the start of the flight period for trafficking, scheduling, and pacing purposes. EFFECTIVE_FROM per TRANSACTION_LINE canonical role.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Flight Start; IAB Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: is_bonus
attributes.description: Indicates whether this line item represents bonus or added-value inventory provided by the publisher at no additional cost as part of a negotiated package. Bonus lines are excluded from spend calculations but included in delivery and GRP reporting.
attributes.references: 4As Media Buying Standards; Mediaocean Prisma — Bonus Flag; ANA Media Transparency Guidelines

attributes.name: is_makegood
attributes.description: Indicates whether this line item is a makegood — compensatory inventory provided by the publisher to replace underdelivered or preempted spots from a prior buy. Makegood lines require special reconciliation treatment.
attributes.references: 4As Makegood Standards; Mediaocean Prisma — Makegood Flag; ANA Media Buying Guidelines

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this media plan line item record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED per TRANSACTION_LINE canonical role.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity; SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: line_name
attributes.description: Descriptive name for this media plan line item, typically combining the vehicle, placement, and flight period (e.g., 'CNN Homepage Takeover - Q1 2025'). Used for human-readable identification in reports and insertion orders.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Line Description; IAB Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: line_number
attributes.description: Sequential line number identifying the position of this line item within the parent media plan. Used for ordering, referencing, and reconciliation against insertion orders and vendor invoices. LINE_SEQUENCE per TRANSACTION_LINE canonical role.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Line Sequence; IAB Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: market
attributes.description: Geographic market or designated market area (DMA) where this media line item will run (e.g., 'New York DMA', 'National', 'Los Angeles DMA'). Critical for audience targeting, GRP/TRP calculation, and local vs. national budget allocation.
attributes.references: Nielsen DMA Definitions; IAB Geographic Targeting Standards

attributes.name: media_subtype
attributes.description: Granular classification within the media type (e.g., display, video, paid_search, social, connected_tv, digital_out_of_home, programmatic, direct). Enables detailed channel-level performance analysis and buying strategy differentiation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: display|video|paid_search|social|connected_tv|digital_out_of_home|programmatic|direct|audio|native — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Digital Ad Format Taxonomy; MRC Digital Measurement Standards

attributes.name: media_type
attributes.description: High-level classification of the media channel for this line item (e.g., digital, television, radio, print, out_of_home, cinema). Drives planning methodology, buying approach, and metric selection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: digital|television|radio|print|out_of_home|cinema|podcast|streaming — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Media Channel Taxonomy; MRC Media Measurement Standards

attributes.name: planned_cpa
attributes.description: Planned Cost Per Acquisition (CPA) — the negotiated or estimated cost for each conversion or acquisition event generated by this line item. Used for performance-based buying where payment is tied to measurable outcomes.
attributes.references: IAB CPA Measurement Standards; MRC Conversion Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: planned_cpc
attributes.description: Planned Cost Per Click (CPC) — the negotiated or estimated cost for each click generated by this line item. Primary pricing metric for paid search and performance-based digital campaigns. Applicable when buying_type is CPC.
attributes.references: IAB CPC Measurement Standards; Google Ads CPC Guidelines; 4As Digital Buying Standards

attributes.name: planned_cpm
attributes.description: Planned Cost Per Mille (CPM) — the negotiated or estimated cost per 1,000 impressions for this line item. Core pricing metric for digital and broadcast media buying. Used for rate benchmarking, budget allocation, and vendor negotiation.
attributes.references: IAB CPM Measurement Standards; MRC Impression Measurement Guidelines; 4As Rate Card Standards

attributes.name: planned_frequency
attributes.description: Planned average number of times a member of the target audience will be exposed to the ad during the flight period. Used with reach to calculate GRPs and to optimize against frequency caps and diminishing returns thresholds.
attributes.references: Nielsen Audience Measurement; MRC Reach and Frequency Standards

attributes.name: planned_grps
attributes.description: Planned Gross Rating Points (GRPs) for this line item, representing total audience delivery as a percentage of the total target population. Primary planning metric for broadcast TV, radio, and OOH. Calculated as reach × frequency.
attributes.references: Nielsen GRP Measurement Standards; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines; 4As GRP Standards

attributes.name: planned_impressions
attributes.description: The total number of ad impressions planned for this line item across the flight period. Standardized cross-channel metric used for reach estimation, CPM calculation, and campaign delivery forecasting regardless of buying type.
attributes.references: MRC Impression Measurement Guidelines; IAB Measurement Standards

attributes.name: planned_net_spend
attributes.description: Planned net media spend after agency commission, discounts, and negotiated rebates have been deducted from the gross planned spend. Used for vendor payment reconciliation and agency P&L reporting.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Net Cost; SAP S/4HANA FI; 4As Commission and Fee Standards

attributes.name: planned_reach_pct
attributes.description: Planned percentage of the target audience expected to be exposed to the ad at least once during the flight period. Key media planning metric used alongside frequency to evaluate campaign effectiveness and GRP decomposition.
attributes.references: Nielsen Audience Measurement; MRC Reach and Frequency Standards; 4As Media Planning Guidelines

attributes.name: planned_spend
attributes.description: Total planned gross media spend allocated to this line item for the full flight period, in the plan currency. Primary budget field used for financial planning, client billing, and P&L management. LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT per TRANSACTION_LINE canonical role.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Planned Cost; SAP S/4HANA PS Budget Management; 4As Financial Standards

attributes.name: planned_trps
attributes.description: Planned Target Rating Points (TRPs) for this line item, representing audience delivery specifically among the defined target demographic as a percentage of that target population. More precise than GRPs for audience-specific campaign planning.
attributes.references: Nielsen TRP Measurement Standards; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: planned_units
attributes.description: The planned quantity of media units to be purchased for this line item. Unit interpretation depends on buying_type: impressions for CPM, clicks for CPC, conversions for CPA, spots for broadcast, panels for OOH. LINE_QUANTITY per TRANSACTION_LINE canonical role.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Planned Units; MRC Impression Measurement Guidelines

products.name: media_placement
products.description: Operational record representing a specific ad placement purchased from a media vendor — the atomic unit of a media buy. Captures placement name, ad unit size/format, channel type, vendor/publisher, site/station/network, position, targeting parameters, flight start/end dates, contracted units, contracted impressions, rate type (CPM, CPC, CPA, flat), contracted rate, total contracted value, trafficking status, and ad server placement ID from Google Campaign Manager 360. Links to insertion order and media plan line.

attributes.name: media_placement_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the media placement record. This is the atomic unit of a media buy, representing a specific ad placement purchased from a media vendor. Role: TRANSACTION_LINE.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Ad Product Taxonomy

attributes.name: ad_channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.ad_channel. Business justification: placement defines specific ad placement positions within a publisher property and channel. Currently placement has no FK to ad_channel. This FK establishes that each placement operates within a specif

attributes.name: ad_format_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.ad_format. Business justification: media_placement currently has ad_format and ad_unit_size as STRING attributes. Each placement is for a specific ad format (e.g., 300x250 Banner, Pre-roll Video). This FK normalizes the placement-to-fo

attributes.name: ad_server_tech_partner_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Ad Server Tech Partner Id (Foreign Key)
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.tech_partner. Business justification: Media placements reference ad_server_platform as plain text. Linking to tech_partner normalizes this and enables ad serving vendor management: tracking tech fees per placement, SLA compliance for ad s

attributes.name: asset_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.creative_asset. Business justification: Placements are assigned specific creative assets for delivery. Essential for trafficking, ad server setup, and ensuring creative specs match placement requirements. Distinct from ad_server_placement_i

attributes.name: contract_rate_card_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_card. Business justification: Media placements carry contracted_rate and contracted_value fields that must be validated against the negotiated rate card. This link enables placement-level rate compliance auditing — verifying that

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Media placements incur contracted costs that require cost center attribution for financial reporting. Placement-level cost center coding supports granular P&L analysis by channel and client, and is re

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Placements are the billing unit for media reconciliation. GL account coding at placement level supports invoice line matching, accrual posting, and billing dispute resolution. Agency billing systems r

attributes.name: media_ad_server_placement_id
attributes.description: The placement identifier assigned by Google Campaign Manager 360 (CM360) ad server used for trafficking, creative assignment, and delivery reporting. This is the operational key used by ad operations teams to manage the placement in the ad server.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 API; IAB VAST/VPAID Standard

attributes.name: media_catalog_placement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.placement. Business justification: media_placement is the operational purchased placement record, while placement is the catalog/reference definition of a placement position. An operational media_placement should reference the catalog

attributes.name: media_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent Insertion Order (IO) under which this placement was purchased. An IO is the binding contract between the advertiser/agency and the media vendor authorizing the placement. Serves as the HEADER_REFERENCE for this transaction line.
attributes.references: IAB Standard Terms and Conditions for Internet Advertising; Mediaocean Prisma IO Module

attributes.name: original_placement_media_placement_id
attributes.description: For make-good placements (is_makegood = true), the reference to the original media_placement_id that this placement is compensating for. Enables traceability between under-delivered placements and their corresponding make-good replacements.
attributes.references: IAB Standard Terms and Conditions; Mediaocean Prisma Reconciliation Module

attributes.name: primary_media_audience_segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Placements are bought and trafficked against specific audience segments. Ad ops teams require explicit segment linkage for ad server targeting setup, delivery pacing, and performance reporting by audi

attributes.name: programmatic_deal_id
attributes.description: The unique deal identifier for programmatic private marketplace (PMP) or programmatic guaranteed deals, as assigned by the Supply-Side Platform (SSP) or publisher. Used to activate and track the deal in The Trade Desk Demand-Side Platform (DSP). Null for direct or open auction buys.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab OpenRTB 2.x Specification; The Trade Desk Deal Management

attributes.name: publisher_id
attributes.description: Reference to the media vendor or publisher from whom this placement was purchased (e.g., Google, Meta, NBC Universal, Clear Channel). Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE for this transaction line.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Seller.json Standard; TAG Certified Against Fraud

attributes.name: publisher_property_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.publisher_property. Business justification: placement defines specific ad positions within a publisher property (e.g., homepage leaderboard on NYTimes.com). Each placement belongs to one publisher property. This FK establishes the parent-child

attributes.name: spec_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.spec. Business justification: Ad ops teams must verify that a placement's accepted ad unit size, format, and technical requirements match the creative spec before trafficking. Placement-to-spec alignment is a mandatory pre-traffic

attributes.name: above_fold_classification
attributes.description: Classification indicating whether the placement typically appears above or below the fold on initial page load, critical for viewability.

attributes.name: ad_unit_size
attributes.description: Dimensions of the ad unit in pixels (e.g., 728x90, 300x250, 1920x1080) or duration for video/audio placements.

attributes.name: agency_commission_pct
attributes.description: The agency commission percentage applied to this placement's gross contracted value, typically 15% per industry standard (4As convention). Used to calculate net media cost billed to the client versus gross cost paid to the vendor. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET adjustment.
attributes.references: American Association of Advertising Agencies (4As) Commission Standard; IAB Standard Terms

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: The date on which this placement was formally approved by the client or account team, authorizing the media vendor to proceed with trafficking and delivery. Represents the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for the placement authorization event.
attributes.references: IAB Standard Terms and Conditions; Workfront Approval Workflow

attributes.name: brand_safety_category
attributes.description: The IAB content category exclusion or inclusion list applied to this placement to ensure brand-safe ad delivery (e.g., 'Exclude: Adult Content, Violence, Hate Speech'). Configured in Google CM360 or The Trade Desk per TAG Brand Safety standards and client brand guidelines.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Content Taxonomy; TAG Brand Safety Certified; GARM Brand Safety Floor

attributes.name: brand_safety_tier
attributes.description: Brand safety classification tier for this placement, indicating content adjacency risk level per Trustworthy Accountability Group (TAG) standards.

attributes.name: buying_method
attributes.description: The method by which this placement was purchased: direct IO with publisher, programmatic guaranteed, private marketplace (PMP) deal, open auction Real-Time Bidding (RTB), or preferred deal. Determines the applicable technology stack (e.g., The Trade Desk for programmatic, Mediaocean Prisma for direct).
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Programmatic Supply Chain; TAG Certified Against Fraud; MRC

attributes.name: cancellation_notice_days
attributes.description: Number of days advance notice required to cancel or modify a booked campaign on this placement without penalty.

attributes.name: channel_type
attributes.description: The media channel category for this placement (e.g., digital display, broadcast TV, Out-of-Home (OOH), Connected TV (CTV)/Over-the-Top (OTT), print). Drives channel-specific reporting, rate card application, and regulatory compliance requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: digital|broadcast_tv|radio|print|ooh|dooh|ctv_ott|cinema|podcast — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Channel Taxonomy; MRC Cross-Media Audience Measurement Standards

attributes.name: content_category
attributes.description: IAB Content Taxonomy category for the content surrounding this placement (e.g., News, Sports, Entertainment, Finance).

attributes.name: contracted_impressions
attributes.description: The total number of ad impressions guaranteed or estimated for this placement under the IO. For CPM-based buys this is the primary delivery metric; for GRP/TRP buys this is the estimated impression equivalent. Used for reach/frequency planning and MRC-compliant delivery verification.
attributes.references: MRC Impression Measurement Guidelines; IAB Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: contracted_rate
attributes.description: The negotiated unit rate for this placement as agreed in the Insertion Order (IO), expressed in the applicable rate type currency (e.g., CPM rate in USD, CPC rate in USD, flat fee in USD). This is commercially sensitive pricing data. Serves as the LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT for this transaction line.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Rate Card Module; IAB Standard Terms and Conditions

attributes.name: contracted_units
attributes.description: The total number of units (impressions, clicks, acquisitions, GRPs, or flat occurrences) contracted for this placement as specified in the Insertion Order. Serves as the LINE_QUANTITY for this transaction line. Used for pacing, delivery tracking, and reconciliation.
attributes.references: IAB Standard Terms and Conditions; Mediaocean Prisma Buying Module

attributes.name: contracted_value
attributes.description: The total gross monetary value of this placement as contracted in the Insertion Order (IO), calculated as contracted rate × contracted units. Represents the full financial commitment for this placement before any adjustments, make-goods, or agency commission. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Financial Module; SAP S/4HANA FI Module; IAB Standard Terms
