attributes.name: cpm_floor_price
attributes.description: Minimum CPM rate for this placement position, used in programmatic bidding and rate card negotiations.

attributes.name: cpm_list_price
attributes.description: Published list CPM rate for this placement on the rate card, before negotiation or discounts.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this media placement record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this transaction line. Used for data lineage and audit trail.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values on this placement (e.g., 'USD', 'GBP', 'EUR'). Required for multi-currency media buys and financial reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

attributes.name: daypart_availability
attributes.description: Time-of-day availability for this placement (e.g., Prime Time, Daytime, Late Night, Run-of-Schedule), relevant for broadcast and digital video.

attributes.name: device_targeting
attributes.description: Device type targeting parameters for this placement position.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date when this placement position will be or was retired from available inventory (nullable for ongoing placements).

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date when this placement position became available in inventory for booking and trafficking.

attributes.name: environment_type
attributes.description: The platform environment where the placement is delivered (web, mobile app, Connected TV (CTV), Over-the-Top (OTT), Digital Out-of-Home (DOOH), etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_web|mobile_app|ctv|ott|dooh|linear_tv|radio|print — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: estimated_monthly_impressions
attributes.description: Projected monthly impression volume available for this placement position, used in media planning and forecasting.

attributes.name: estimated_reach_pct
attributes.description: Estimated percentage of target audience reached by this placement within a standard campaign flight.

attributes.name: flight_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which the ad placement is scheduled to stop delivering. Defines the end of the placement's active flight period. Used for campaign scheduling, delivery completion tracking, and IO reconciliation.
attributes.references: IAB Standard Terms and Conditions; Mediaocean Prisma Scheduling Module

attributes.name: flight_start_date
attributes.description: The date on which the ad placement is scheduled to begin delivering impressions or airing. Defines the start of the placement's active flight period. Used for campaign scheduling, pacing calculations, and IO compliance verification.
attributes.references: IAB Standard Terms and Conditions; Mediaocean Prisma Scheduling Module

attributes.name: frequency_cap
attributes.description: The maximum number of times a unique user or household may be served this ad within the defined frequency cap period. Configured in Google CM360 or The Trade Desk to control ad fatigue and optimize reach. Null if no frequency cap is applied.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 Frequency Cap Settings; IAB Audience Measurement

attributes.name: frequency_cap_impressions
attributes.description: Maximum number of impressions per user within the frequency cap period, enforced at placement level.

attributes.name: frequency_cap_period
attributes.description: The time window over which the frequency cap applies (e.g., per day, per week, per month, lifetime of the campaign). Works in conjunction with frequency_cap to define the full frequency capping rule in the ad server.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 Frequency Cap Settings; IAB Audience Measurement

attributes.name: geo_targeting_scope
attributes.description: Geographic targeting granularity available for this placement (global, national, Designated Market Area (DMA), postal code, etc.).

attributes.name: grp_contracted
attributes.description: The total Gross Rating Points (GRP) contracted for this placement, applicable to broadcast TV, radio, and OOH buys. GRP = Reach% × Frequency. Used for broadcast media planning, Nielsen audience measurement reconciliation, and post-buy analysis. Null for digital-only placements.
attributes.references: Nielsen Ad Intel GRP Measurement; MRC Audience Measurement Standards; IAB

attributes.name: is_guaranteed_inventory
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this placement is sold as guaranteed inventory (direct buy) versus non-guaranteed (programmatic auction).

attributes.name: is_makegood
attributes.description: Indicates whether this placement was created as a make-good (compensatory placement) to replace under-delivered or cancelled inventory from a prior placement. Make-goods are tracked separately for financial reconciliation and vendor performance management.
attributes.references: IAB Standard Terms and Conditions for Make-Goods; Mediaocean Prisma Reconciliation

attributes.name: is_programmatic_eligible
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this placement is available for programmatic buying through Demand-Side Platforms (DSP) and Supply-Side Platforms (SSP).

attributes.name: makegood_policy
attributes.description: Publisher policy for makegoods (compensatory ad delivery) when this placement underdelivers against contracted impressions or performance benchmarks.

attributes.name: net_contracted_value
attributes.description: The net monetary value of this placement after deducting agency commission from the gross contracted value. Represents the actual cost remitted to the media vendor. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET net total.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Financial Module; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

attributes.name: placement_code
attributes.description: External business identifier or SKU for the placement position, used in media planning and buying systems.

attributes.name: placement_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name describing the placement position (e.g., Homepage Leaderboard, In-Stream Pre-Roll, Sidebar Rectangle, Sponsored Post Slot).

attributes.name: placement_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the placement position in inventory management.

attributes.name: placement_type
attributes.description: Classification of the placement format type according to IAB standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: display|video|native|audio|sponsored_content|interstitial|overlay|banner|rich_media — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: position
attributes.description: The specific position or placement location within the site, broadcast, or publication (e.g., above-the-fold, pre-roll, homepage takeover, run-of-site). Directly impacts CPM rate, viewability, and audience engagement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: above_the_fold|below_the_fold|pre_roll|mid_roll|post_roll|homepage|run_of_site|run_of_network — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Viewability Standards; MRC Viewable Impression Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: position_type
attributes.description: Specific position classification within the property page or content stream, critical for viewability and engagement benchmarks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: above_fold|below_fold|in_stream|pre_roll|mid_roll|post_roll|sidebar|footer|header|interstitial|native_feed|sponsored_tile — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: rate_type
attributes.description: The pricing model applied to this placement: Cost Per Mille (CPM), Cost Per Click (CPC), Cost Per Acquisition (CPA), Cost Per View (CPV), flat fee, Gross Rating Point (GRP), or Target Rating Point (TRP). Determines how contracted units are measured and billed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cpm|cpc|cpa|cpv|flat|grp|trp — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Standard Glossary of Terminology; MRC Audience Measurement Standards

attributes.name: site_name
attributes.description: The name of the specific website, TV station, radio station, publication, or network where the ad will run (e.g., 'CNN.com', 'NBC Nightly News', 'The New York Times'). Represents the RESOURCE_REFERENCE for the media property being purchased.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Sellers.json; Comscore Media Metrix

attributes.name: targeting_parameters
attributes.description: Descriptive summary of the audience and contextual targeting criteria applied to this placement (e.g., 'Adults 25-54, HHI $75K+, Interest: Auto, Geo: US-NY'). Captures demographic, geographic, behavioral, and contextual targeting as configured in The Trade Desk or Google CM360. Free-text summary for operational reference.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy; IAB Content Taxonomy; GDPR Article 6; CCPA

attributes.name: third_party_verification_required
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether third-party verification tags (viewability, brand safety, fraud detection) are required for this placement.

attributes.name: trafficking_notes
attributes.description: Free-text operational notes entered by the ad operations team during trafficking in Google Campaign Manager 360, including special instructions, creative rotation rules, technical requirements, or vendor-specific delivery notes.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 Trafficking Workflow; IAB Ad Operations Best Practices

attributes.name: trafficking_status
attributes.description: Current operational status of the placement in the ad trafficking workflow. Tracks the placement lifecycle from initial setup through live delivery to completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_trafficking|trafficked|live|paused|completed|cancelled — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 Trafficking Workflow; IAB Ad Operations Standards

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this media placement record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field. Tracks changes to placement details, rates, or trafficking status throughout the campaign lifecycle.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail

attributes.name: vast_tag_required
attributes.business_glossary_term: Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) Tag Required
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this placement requires VAST tag implementation for video ad serving.

attributes.name: viewability_rate_benchmark_pct
attributes.description: Historical or guaranteed viewability rate benchmark for this placement, measured per Media Rating Council (MRC) standards.

attributes.name: viewability_target_pct
attributes.description: The minimum viewability percentage contracted or targeted for this digital placement, per MRC viewability standards (e.g., 70% viewable impressions). Used to set vendor accountability thresholds and trigger make-good provisions in the IO.
attributes.references: MRC Viewable Impression Measurement Guidelines; IAB Viewability Standards

attributes.name: vpaid_supported
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this placement supports VPAID interactive video ad units.

products.name: buy
products.description: Transactional record capturing the execution of a media purchase — the business event of committing agency/client spend to a specific media vehicle against an authorized IO and plan line. Captures buy date, buyer name, vendor, channel, buy type (upfront, scatter, programmatic RTB, direct, retail media network, CTV/OTT addressable), buy status (pending, confirmed, cancelled), negotiated rate, total buy value, authorized spend ceiling, DSP/SSP reference (The Trade Desk deal ID for programmatic), market type (upfront commitment/scatter opportunistic/makeweight), upfront deal year reference for broadcast, and reconciliation status. Supports all buying modalities including traditional broadcast (linear, addressable), digital direct, programmatic (display, video, CTV, audio), and emerging retail media network purchases.

attributes.name: buy_id
attributes.description: Unique system-generated identifier for the media buy record. Primary key for this transactional entity. TRANSACTION_HEADER role — represents the discrete business event of committing agency/client spend to a specific media vehicle.

attributes.name: ad_channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.ad_channel. Business justification: buy currently has channel as a STRING attribute. buy is the transactional purchase event, and each buy is for a specific advertising channel (e.g., Display, Video, Search). While buy → media_placement

attributes.name: ad_format_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.ad_format. Business justification: buy currently has format as a STRING attribute. Each buy is for a specific ad format (e.g., 300x250 Banner, Pre-roll Video). This FK normalizes the buy-to-format relationship, replacing the string att

attributes.name: asset_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.creative_asset. Business justification: Buyers need to know which creative asset will run in each buy for trafficking instructions, publisher spec validation, and delivery verification. Critical for makegood and reconciliation processes.

attributes.name: contract_rate_card_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_card. Business justification: Media buyers must confirm negotiated buy rates conform to the contracted rate card — a core billing audit and reconciliation process. Linking buy to contract_rate_card enables rate variance reporting

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Media buys require cost center for expense tracking, financial reconciliation, and variance reporting - core financial control requirement.

attributes.name: finance_budget_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Media buys must reference budgets for spend tracking, variance analysis, and budget compliance monitoring in financial management.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Media buys are the transactional unit generating vendor payables. GL account coding at the buy level is required for accounts payable posting and cost accrual. Agency finance systems require buy-to-GL

attributes.name: initiative_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.initiative. Business justification: Media buys roll up to initiatives for consolidated financial tracking. Required for initiative-level ROI analysis, strategic performance measurement, and cross-campaign budget reconciliation in agency

attributes.name: media_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the authorizing Insertion Order (IO) under which this media buy is executed. Links the buy to its contractual authorization and spend ceiling.
attributes.references: IAB Insertion Order Standard; Mediaocean Prisma — IO Module

attributes.name: media_placement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific media placement or ad unit being purchased (e.g., a specific ad slot, daypart, or digital placement). Links to the placement master in the media domain.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 — Placement; IAB Ad Unit Guidelines

attributes.name: plan_line_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific media plan line item this buy executes against. Ties the transactional buy back to the approved media plan for reconciliation and pacing.

attributes.name: programmatic_deal_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.programmatic_deal. Business justification: buy is the transactional record of a media purchase. While buy → plan_line → programmatic_deal path exists, many buys are directly associated with a specific programmatic deal (PMP, programmatic guara

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Media buys execute against defined audience targets for delivery and measurement. Buyers negotiate rates and inventory based on specific segment reach and composition. Essential for buy reconciliation

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Media buys are authorized under a specific SOW that defines the contractual scope and budget. Linking buy to sow enables SOW budget consumption tracking and ensures each buy is executed within the cor

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the media vendor or publisher from whom the media inventory is purchased (e.g., broadcast network, digital publisher, OOH operator). Maps to the vendor master.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Vendor Master; IAB Seller Taxonomy

attributes.name: added_value_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this buy includes added-value inventory (bonus impressions, sponsored content, or value-add placements) negotiated as part of the overall media deal at no incremental cost to the client.
attributes.references: 4As Media Buying Standards; ANA Media Transparency Guidelines

attributes.name: agency_commission_amount
attributes.description: Agency commission deducted from the gross buy amount, typically 15% of gross per industry standard. Represents the agency's compensation component embedded in the media buy.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Commission Standards; SAP S/4HANA CO — Revenue Recognition; ANA Media Transparency Guidelines

attributes.name: authorized_spend_ceiling
attributes.description: Maximum spend amount authorized for this media buy as approved on the Insertion Order or client budget authorization. Acts as a financial control limit; actual spend must not exceed this value.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Budget Cap; SAP S/4HANA PS — Budget Control; IAB Insertion Order Standard

attributes.name: booked_units
attributes.description: Quantity of media delivery units contracted in this buy (e.g., number of impressions, GRPs, TRPs, clicks, or spots). The unit type is defined by the rate_basis field. Used for pacing and delivery reconciliation.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Units Booked; MRC Audience Measurement Standards; Nielsen Ad Intel — GRP Tracking

attributes.name: buy_date
attributes.description: The principal real-world business event date on which the media buy was executed — i.e., the date the agency committed spend to the media vehicle. Distinct from system audit timestamps.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Buy Date; IAB Insertion Order Standard

attributes.name: buy_number
attributes.description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the media buy, used in communications with vendors, on Insertion Orders, and in billing reconciliation. Corresponds to the buy reference number in Mediaocean Prisma.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Buy Reference; IAB Insertion Order Standard

attributes.name: buy_status
attributes.description: Current workflow lifecycle state of the media buy. Drives operational actions: pending = awaiting vendor confirmation; confirmed = vendor-accepted and active; cancelled = buy voided; paused = temporarily halted; completed = delivery fulfilled.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Buy Status; IAB Insertion Order Standard

attributes.name: buy_type
attributes.description: Classification of the buying modality used to acquire this media inventory. Upfront = advance commitment; scatter = opportunistic spot market; programmatic RTB = real-time bidding via DSP; direct = direct IO with publisher; retail media = retail media network purchase; CTV addressable = addressable CTV/OTT buy.
attributes.references: IAB Programmatic Buying Standards; 4As Media Buying Guidelines; MRC Audience Measurement Standards

attributes.name: cancellation_date
attributes.description: Date on which this media buy was cancelled, if applicable. Populated only when buy_status = 'cancelled'. Used for cancellation liability calculations and vendor notification tracking.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Cancellation; IAB Insertion Order Standard — Cancellation Terms

attributes.name: cancellation_reason
attributes.description: Reason code for the cancellation of this media buy. Required when buy_status = 'cancelled' to support financial liability assessment and vendor relationship management.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Cancellation Reason; IAB Insertion Order Standard

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: System audit timestamp recording when this media buy record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage, SLA tracking, and audit compliance.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 (Accountability); IAB Data Transparency Standards

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the buy amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multi-currency campaign management and financial consolidation.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI — Currency Management

attributes.name: delivery_status
attributes.description: Current state of media inventory delivery against the booked buy. Tracks whether the vendor has fulfilled the contracted impressions, GRPs, or other delivery units. Underdelivery triggers makegoods; overdelivery may require credit memos.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 — Delivery Reporting; Nielsen Ad Intel — Delivery Verification; MRC Audience Measurement Standards

attributes.name: flight_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which media delivery for this buy is scheduled to conclude. Defines the end of the active delivery window. Used for pacing, reconciliation, and billing cutoff.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 — Flight Dates; Mediaocean Prisma — Schedule

attributes.name: flight_start_date
attributes.description: The date on which media delivery for this buy is scheduled to begin. Defines the start of the active delivery window (flight period) for trafficking and scheduling purposes.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 — Flight Dates; Mediaocean Prisma — Schedule

attributes.name: gross_buy_amount
attributes.description: Total gross value of the media buy before agency commission, discounts, or adjustments. Represents the full billing value to the vendor as stated on the Insertion Order.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Gross Amount; SAP S/4HANA FI — Commitment Accounting; 4As Financial Standards

attributes.name: makegood_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this media buy is a makegood — compensatory inventory provided by the vendor to remedy underdelivery or audience guarantee shortfalls on a prior buy. True = this is a makegood buy.
attributes.references: 4As Makegood Standards; Nielsen Ad Intel — Audience Guarantee Tracking

attributes.name: market_type
attributes.description: Broadcast market commitment classification. Upfront = advance season commitment at negotiated rates; scatter = opportunistic in-season purchase at prevailing market rates; makeweight = inventory added to fulfill audience delivery guarantees (GRP/TRP shortfall).
attributes.references: 4As Broadcast Buying Standards; Nielsen Ad Intel — Market Type Classification

attributes.name: negotiated_rate
attributes.description: The unit rate negotiated with the vendor for this media buy, expressed in the applicable rate basis (e.g., CPM, CPC, CPA, GRP cost). This is the commercially sensitive contracted rate, distinct from rate card pricing.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Rate Field; IAB Pricing Standards; 4As Negotiation Guidelines

attributes.name: net_buy_amount
attributes.description: Net cost of the media buy after deducting agency commission from the gross amount. Represents the actual vendor payment obligation. Net = Gross minus Agency Commission.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Net Amount; SAP S/4HANA FI — Accounts Payable; 4As Financial Standards

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for buyer annotations, special deal terms, negotiation notes, or operational instructions associated with this media buy. Not used for structured data.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Notes Field; 4As Media Buying Standards

attributes.name: prisma_buy_code
attributes.description: Source system identifier for this buy record in Mediaocean Prisma, the system of record for media planning, buying, and billing. Used for system-of-record traceability and reconciliation.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Buy Record; 4As Media Buying Standards

attributes.name: rate_basis
attributes.description: The pricing metric on which the negotiated rate is denominated. CPM = Cost Per Mille (thousand impressions); CPC = Cost Per Click; CPA = Cost Per Acquisition; GRP = Gross Rating Point cost; flat = fixed fee; CPCV = Cost Per Completed View; vCPM = viewable CPM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cpm|cpc|cpa|grp|flat|cpcv|vcpm — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Pricing Standards; MRC Viewability Standards; 4As Media Buying Guidelines

attributes.name: reconciliation_status
attributes.description: Current state of the financial reconciliation process for this media buy. Tracks whether vendor invoices have been matched against booked buy amounts. Unreconciled = not yet matched; in_progress = reconciliation underway; reconciled = fully matched; disputed = discrepancy under review; written_off = irreconcilable variance closed.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Reconciliation Module; SAP S/4HANA FI — AP Reconciliation; 4As Financial Standards

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: System audit timestamp recording when this media buy record was last modified. Supports change tracking, reconciliation workflows, and data quality monitoring.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 (Accountability); Mediaocean Prisma — Audit Trail

attributes.name: upfront_deal_year
attributes.description: Broadcast season year reference for upfront market commitments (e.g., '2024-2025'). Identifies the upfront buying cycle to which this buy belongs, enabling season-level budget tracking and audience guarantee management.
attributes.references: 4As Broadcast Upfront Standards; Nielsen Ad Intel — Upfront Tracking

products.name: programmatic_deal
products.description: Master record for programmatic media deals negotiated with DSPs, SSPs, exchanges, and retail media networks — including Private Marketplace (PMP) deals, Preferred Deals, Programmatic Guaranteed (PG) deals, and retailer-specific programmatic inventory (Amazon DSP, Walmart Connect, Instacart Ads). Captures deal ID (The Trade Desk deal ID, Amazon DSP deal ID), deal name, deal type (PMP, PG, open auction, retailer PMP), DSP platform, SSP/exchange/retailer, publisher, CPM floor price, deal start/end dates, impression commitment, targeting parameters (including shopper/purchase data for RMN), deal status, RTB bid strategy, and closed-loop measurement availability flag. Sourced from The Trade Desk, Amazon DSP, and other programmatic platforms.
products.reference: PMP/PG Deal — The Trade Desk / IAB OpenRTB

attributes.name: programmatic_deal_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the programmatic deal record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Entity role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — governs a binding programmatic inventory commitment between the agency/advertiser and a DSP/SSP/exchange/retailer.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Programmatic Supply Chain Standards

attributes.name: ad_channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.ad_channel. Business justification: programmatic_deal currently has channel as a STRING attribute. Programmatic deals are negotiated for specific advertising channels (e.g., Programmatic Display deal, CTV deal). This FK normalizes the d

attributes.name: ad_format_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.ad_format. Business justification: programmatic_deal currently has ad_format as a STRING attribute. Programmatic deals specify the ad format for the deal inventory (e.g., 300x250 Banner, Pre-roll Video). This FK normalizes the deal-to-

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertiser (client brand) on whose behalf this programmatic deal is negotiated and executed. Used for client-level reporting, billing, and competitive conflict checks.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud Account Object; IAB Tech Lab Advertiser Taxonomy

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Programmatic deals (PMPs, preferred deals, guaranteed deals) are negotiated under a master agreement with the publisher. This link enables contract compliance tracking for programmatic inventory commi

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Programmatic deals (PMPs, preferred deals) are negotiated at the brand level — brand safety tiers, audience targeting, and floor CPMs are brand-specific. Brand-level deal tracking is essential for com

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent advertising campaign under which this programmatic deal is executed. Links the deal to campaign-level objectives, KPIs, and budget in Google Campaign Manager 360 and The Trade Desk.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 Campaign Object; The Trade Desk Campaign Management

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Programmatic deals are cost-center-specific financial commitments in agency operations. Cost center attribution at the deal level is required for P&L reporting by business unit and client, and for int

attributes.name: dsp_tech_partner_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.tech_partner. Business justification: Programmatic deals reference dsp_platform as plain text. Linking to tech_partner via dsp_tech_partner_id normalizes DSP vendor attribution, enabling programmatic tech fee tracking, DSP SLA management,

attributes.name: finance_budget_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Programmatic deals carry a budget_amount commitment that must be tracked against an approved finance budget for spend control. Deal-level budget authorization is required for programmatic trading desk

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Programmatic deals are financial commitments with budget amounts requiring GL account coding for accrual accounting and cost classification. Deal-level GL coding supports programmatic spend reporting

attributes.name: media_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.media_insertion_order. Business justification: programmatic_deal currently has io_number (STRING) which is a denormalized reference to the parent insertion order. Programmatic deals are executed under a master IO — the IO provides the contractual

attributes.name: publisher_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the publisher as assigned by the SSP, exchange, or DSP platform. Used for supply path optimization, publisher-level reconciliation, and cross-platform publisher deduplication.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab ads.txt / sellers.json Specification

attributes.name: publisher_property_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.publisher_property. Business justification: programmatic_deal currently has FK to publisher (parent entity). Many programmatic deals are property-specific (e.g., deal for NYTimes.com homepage, deal for WSJ Mobile App). While programmatic_deal →

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Programmatic deals (PMP/PG) are negotiated for specific audience segments with publishers and SSPs. Deal setup, activation, and performance measurement require explicit segment linkage for targeting c

attributes.name: spec_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.spec. Business justification: Programmatic deal setup requires matching the deal's accepted creative formats (VAST version, ad unit size, viewability thresholds) to a creative spec. Ad ops teams perform spec-to-deal alignment duri

attributes.name: brand_safety_tier
attributes.description: Brand safety classification applied to this deal, determining content adjacency restrictions and inventory quality controls. Configured per TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group) Brand Safety standards and enforced via The Trade Desk and Google Campaign Manager 360.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certification Standards; GARM Brand Safety Floor and Suitability Framework

attributes.name: budget_amount
attributes.description: Total monetary budget allocated to this programmatic deal in the deal currency. For PG deals, calculated as floor_cpm × impression_commitment / 1000. Used for financial planning, budget pacing, and P&L reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA and Mediaocean Prisma.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Budget Management; SAP S/4HANA CO Module

attributes.name: closed_loop_measurement
attributes.description: Indicates whether closed-loop measurement (linking ad exposure to purchase/conversion outcomes) is available for this deal. Particularly relevant for Retail Media Network (RMN) deals (Amazon DSP, Walmart Connect, Instacart Ads) where purchase data enables attribution. True = closed-loop measurement available.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Retail Media Standards; ANA Measurement Framework

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Audit timestamp when this programmatic deal record was first created in the source system (The Trade Desk, Amazon DSP, or Mediaocean Prisma). Used for data lineage and audit trail.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5(2) Accountability; IAB Tech Lab Data Transparency Standards

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values on this deal (floor CPM, budget, etc.). Defaults to USD for most programmatic deals but supports multi-currency for international campaigns.

attributes.name: deal_code
attributes.description: Externally-known deal identifier assigned by the DSP or SSP/exchange (e.g., The Trade Desk deal ID, Amazon DSP deal ID). Used for trafficking, bid requests, and reconciliation across platforms. Maps to the deal_id field in The Trade Desk and OpenRTB bid-request objects.
attributes.references: IAB OpenRTB 2.6 Specification §3.2.4; The Trade Desk Deal Management API

attributes.name: deal_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the programmatic deal as entered in the DSP or Mediaocean Prisma. Used for identification in media plans, trafficking sheets, and reporting dashboards.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Deal Management; The Trade Desk Deal Management

attributes.name: deal_source_system
attributes.description: The operational system of record from which this programmatic deal record was sourced. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and ETL pipeline routing. Drives system-specific field mapping in the Silver layer ingestion process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: the_trade_desk|amazon_dsp|google_cm360|mediaocean_prisma|walmart_dsp|instacart_ads|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Data Transparency Standards; Mediaocean Prisma Integration Specifications

attributes.name: deal_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the programmatic deal. Draft = in negotiation; Pending Approval = submitted for internal/publisher approval; Active = live and eligible for bidding; Paused = temporarily suspended; Expired = past end date; Cancelled = terminated before end date.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Programmatic Supply Chain Standards; The Trade Desk Deal Management

attributes.name: deal_type
attributes.description: Classification of the programmatic deal structure. PMP (Private Marketplace) = curated invite-only auction; Preferred Deal = fixed CPM, non-guaranteed; Programmatic Guaranteed (PG) = fixed CPM with impression commitment; Open Auction = RTB open exchange; Retailer PMP = retailer-specific private marketplace (e.g., Amazon DSP, Walmart Connect). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pmp|preferred_deal|programmatic_guaranteed|open_auction|retailer_pmp — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Programmatic Deal Types Taxonomy; OpenRTB 2.6 §3.2.4 dealtype

attributes.name: end_date
attributes.description: The date on which the programmatic deal expires and is no longer eligible for bidding. Nullable for evergreen deals with no fixed end. Used for pacing, reconciliation, and deal renewal workflows.
attributes.references: IAB OpenRTB 2.6; Mediaocean Prisma Flight Management

attributes.name: environment_type
attributes.description: The technical environment in which the ad inventory is served (web browser, mobile app, CTV device, DOOH screen, audio stream). Determines applicable measurement standards, viewability metrics, and brand safety controls.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab OpenRTB 2.6 §3.2.1 site/app/dooh; MRC Viewability Standards

attributes.name: external_deal_ref
attributes.description: Secondary external reference identifier for this deal as recorded in a partner system (e.g., publisher deal ID, SSP deal reference, Mediaocean Prisma order number). Enables cross-system reconciliation and deduplication.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Reconciliation Module; IAB Tech Lab Supply Chain Object

attributes.name: floor_cpm
attributes.description: The minimum Cost Per Mille (CPM) bid price in USD required to win impressions under this deal. For PMP and Preferred Deals, this is the negotiated floor; for PG deals, this is the fixed CPM. Critical for bid strategy configuration in The Trade Desk and financial forecasting.
attributes.references: IAB OpenRTB 2.6 §3.2.4 bidfloor; MRC Impression Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: frequency_cap
attributes.description: Maximum number of times a unique user/device may be served an ad under this deal within the frequency cap period. Configured in The Trade Desk to control ad fatigue and optimize audience reach. Null indicates no frequency cap applied.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Frequency Capping Standards; The Trade Desk Audience Management

attributes.name: frequency_cap_period
attributes.description: The time window over which the frequency cap applies (hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, or lifetime of the deal). Used in conjunction with frequency_cap to configure audience exposure limits in The Trade Desk.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Frequency Capping Standards; The Trade Desk Audience Management

attributes.name: geo_targeting
attributes.description: Geographic targeting scope for this deal, expressed as country codes, DMA regions, or postal codes (e.g., 'USA', 'USA:DMA:501', 'GBR'). Drives geo-based audience segmentation and regulatory compliance (GDPR for EU inventory, CCPA for California).
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab OpenRTB 2.6 Geo Object; GDPR Article 3; CCPA §1798.100

attributes.name: impression_commitment
attributes.description: The total number of impressions committed under this deal. Mandatory for Programmatic Guaranteed (PG) deals where delivery is contractually obligated. For PMP and Preferred Deals, represents the estimated or target impression volume. Used for pacing, delivery forecasting, and make-good calculations.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Programmatic Guaranteed Standards; MRC Impression Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: negotiated_timestamp
attributes.description: The real-world business event timestamp when the programmatic deal was formally agreed/struck between the buyer and seller. Distinct from the record audit created timestamp. Used for deal velocity reporting and SLA tracking.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Programmatic Supply Chain Standards

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for capturing deal-specific negotiation notes, special terms, publisher commitments, or operational instructions not captured in structured fields. Used by media buyers and account managers for deal context.

attributes.name: rtb_bid_strategy
attributes.description: The Real-Time Bidding (RTB) bid strategy configured in the DSP for this deal. Fixed CPM is standard for PG deals; Dynamic CPM and performance-based strategies (Target CPA, Target ROAS) apply to PMP and open auction deals. Configured in The Trade Desk Bid Management module.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab RTB Standards; The Trade Desk Bid Strategy Documentation

attributes.name: shopper_data_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether retailer shopper/purchase data is used for audience targeting in this deal. Applicable to Retail Media Network (RMN) deals on Amazon DSP, Walmart Connect, and Instacart Ads. True = first-party shopper data targeting is active.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Retail Media Standards; CCPA §1798.100; GDPR Article 6

attributes.name: start_date
attributes.description: The date on which the programmatic deal becomes active and eligible for bidding. Corresponds to the deal flight start in The Trade Desk and Mediaocean Prisma. Used for media plan scheduling and budget pacing.
attributes.references: IAB OpenRTB 2.6; Mediaocean Prisma Flight Management

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Audit timestamp when this programmatic deal record was last modified in the source system. Used for incremental ETL processing and change tracking.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5(2) Accountability; IAB Tech Lab Data Transparency Standards

attributes.name: viewability_threshold_pct
attributes.description: Minimum viewability rate (as a percentage) required for impressions under this deal to be counted as valid. Defined per MRC viewability standards (50% of pixels in view for 1 second for display; 50% for 2 seconds for video). Used for deal quality scoring and billing reconciliation.
attributes.references: MRC Viewability Measurement Guidelines; IAB/MRC Viewability Standards

products.name: schedule
products.description: Operational master record representing the detailed airing/delivery schedule for purchased media placements — specifying exactly when and where ads will run across all channels. Captures schedule name, channel/vehicle, market/DMA, scheduled air dates and times, daypart classification (early morning, daytime, prime, late night, overnight, weekend), ad unit/format, creative rotation instructions, pod position (for broadcast), scheduled impressions/units per period, frequency cap, trafficking status, and last-updated timestamp. Daypart classification follows Nielsen standard daypart definitions. Sourced from Google Campaign Manager 360 trafficking and Mediaocean Prisma scheduling modules.
products.reference: Media Schedule / Trafficking — Google Campaign Manager 360

attributes.name: schedule_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the media schedule record. Primary key for the media_schedule data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.

attributes.name: ad_channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.ad_channel. Business justification: schedule currently has channel_type as a STRING attribute. schedule is the detailed airing/delivery schedule for purchased media placements. Each schedule is for a specific advertising channel. While

attributes.name: ad_format_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.ad_format. Business justification: schedule currently has ad_unit_format as a STRING attribute. Each schedule specifies the ad format for delivery (e.g., 30-second TV spot, 300x250 Banner). This FK normalizes the schedule-to-format rel

attributes.name: asset_id
attributes.description: Reference to the primary creative asset assigned to this media schedule slot. For rotation schedules, this represents the default or primary creative. Links to the creative master record.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 — Creative Object; DAM Standards

attributes.name: buy_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.buy. Business justification: A schedule is the detailed airing/delivery schedule for a purchased media buy. The buy is the transactional commitment (the business event of purchasing media), and the schedule details WHEN and HOW t

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent advertising campaign under which this media schedule is executed. Enables campaign-level roll-up of all scheduled media activity.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 — Campaign Object; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Scheduled media requires cost center for accrual posting and expense recognition timing in financial close processes.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Broadcast schedules represent committed airtime costs requiring GL account coding for accrual accounting. TV/radio schedule entries drive media cost accruals; GL coding at schedule level is standard i

attributes.name: initiative_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.initiative. Business justification: Schedules execute initiative-level media strategies. Linking enables initiative performance dashboards, strategic flight pattern analysis, and cross-campaign reach/frequency optimization required for

attributes.name: media_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the Insertion Order (IO) that authorises the purchase of this scheduled media. The IO is the contractual document between the advertiser/agency and the media vendor.
attributes.references: IAB Insertion Order Standard; Mediaocean Prisma — IO Module

attributes.name: media_placement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the media placement this schedule governs — the specific ad unit slot purchased on a publisher property. Links to the placement master record.

attributes.name: plan_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.plan_line. Business justification: A schedule entry represents the detailed airing/delivery execution of a specific plan line. Adding plan_line_id to schedule provides direct traceability from the operational delivery schedule back to

attributes.name: programmatic_deal_id
attributes.description: Unique deal identifier for programmatic guaranteed or private marketplace (PMP) transactions, as assigned by the Supply-Side Platform (SSP) or publisher. Used to activate and reconcile programmatic deals in The Trade Desk DSP.
attributes.references: IAB OpenRTB Specification; The Trade Desk Deal Management

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Schedules define when and where ads run against specific audience segments for daypart and vehicle optimization. Planners use segment linkage for reach/frequency modeling and flighting strategy by aud

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Schedules represent execution of media buys authorized under a SOW. Linking schedule to sow enables SOW delivery tracking — confirming scheduled impressions/units are within contractually authorized s

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Schedule records represent actual media delivery execution by a specific supplier (TV station, radio network, digital vendor). Linking schedule to supplier enables vendor delivery performance reportin

attributes.name: trafficking_instruction_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.trafficking_instruction. Business justification: A schedule entry represents a specific airing/delivery slot that is governed by trafficking instructions sent to the ad server. One trafficking instruction can cover multiple schedule entries (e.g., m

attributes.name: vendor_rate_card_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_rate_card. Business justification: Schedule lines carry cpm_rate and net_cost. Rate card compliance audits require tracing each scheduled rate back to the contracted vendor_rate_card. This is a standard media billing reconciliation pro

attributes.name: ad_duration_seconds
attributes.description: Length of the ad creative in seconds, applicable to broadcast TV, radio, CTV/OTT, and digital video formats (e.g., 15, 30, 60). Used for broadcast trafficking, pod position management, and VAST/VPAID configuration.
attributes.references: IAB VAST Standard; Broadcast Standards and Practices

attributes.name: air_end_time
attributes.description: Precise date-time at which the ad unit is scheduled to stop airing or delivering on a given occurrence. Used alongside air_start_time to define the exact delivery window.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 — Trafficking Schedule; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: air_start_time
attributes.description: Precise date-time at which the ad unit is scheduled to begin airing or delivering on a given occurrence. Critical for broadcast trafficking and programmatic deal scheduling. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 — Trafficking Schedule; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: buying_type
attributes.description: Method by which the media inventory was purchased — direct IO, programmatic guaranteed, private marketplace (PMP) deal, open exchange, or Real-Time Bidding (RTB). Determines trafficking path and reconciliation process.
attributes.references: IAB Programmatic Buying Standards; The Trade Desk Deal Types; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: cpm_rate
attributes.description: Negotiated Cost Per Mille (CPM) — the cost per 1,000 ad impressions for this scheduled placement. Primary pricing metric for digital and programmatic media. Used for rate card benchmarking and reconciliation.
attributes.references: IAB CPM Pricing Standards; Mediaocean Prisma — Rate Card Module

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this media schedule record was first created in the source system. Provides the audit creation anchor for the record lifecycle and supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Audit Trail; Google Campaign Manager 360 — Change History

attributes.name: creative_rotation_type
attributes.description: Instruction governing how multiple creative assets are rotated across scheduled impressions — even (equal distribution), weighted (percentage-based), sequential (ordered), or optimised (performance-driven). Trafficked into Google Campaign Manager 360.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 — Creative Rotation Settings; IAB Trafficking Standards

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the net and gross cost amounts on this media schedule (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Enables multi-currency media buying and financial consolidation.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Configuration

attributes.name: daypart
attributes.description: Nielsen standard daypart classification indicating the time-of-day segment in which the ad is scheduled to air. Drives audience composition, CPM pricing, and GRP/TRP calculations. Values follow Nielsen daypart definitions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: early_morning|daytime|early_fringe|prime_access|prime|late_news|late_night|overnight|weekend — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Nielsen Standard Daypart Definitions; MRC Audience Measurement Standards

attributes.name: dma_code
attributes.description: Nielsen-assigned three-digit numeric code identifying the Designated Market Area (DMA) for broadcast and local media scheduling. Enables standardised geographic audience measurement and GRP/TRP reporting.
attributes.references: Nielsen DMA Code Registry; MRC Geographic Standards

attributes.name: flight_end_date
attributes.description: The last calendar date on which ads are scheduled to air or deliver under this media schedule. Defines the closing boundary of the campaign flight period.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Flight Dates; IAB Campaign Scheduling Standards

attributes.name: flight_start_date
attributes.description: The first calendar date on which ads are scheduled to air or deliver under this media schedule. Defines the opening boundary of the campaign flight period.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Flight Dates; IAB Campaign Scheduling Standards

attributes.name: frequency_cap
attributes.description: Maximum number of times a unique user or household may be served this ad within the defined frequency cap period. Controls ad fatigue and audience experience. Trafficked into Google Campaign Manager 360 and The Trade Desk.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 — Frequency Cap Settings; IAB Frequency Capping Standards

attributes.name: frequency_cap_period
attributes.description: Time window over which the frequency cap applies — hour, day, week, month, or lifetime of the campaign. Works in conjunction with frequency_cap to define the full frequency capping rule.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 — Frequency Cap Settings; IAB Frequency Capping Standards

attributes.name: gross_cost
attributes.description: Gross cost of the scheduled media buy before agency commission deduction, expressed in the billing currency. Represents the full rate card value billed by the media vendor.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Cost Module; SAP S/4HANA FI; 4As Agency Commission Standards

attributes.name: is_makegood
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this schedule record represents a make-good — a compensatory placement provided by the media vendor to offset an under-delivery or missed airing from a prior schedule. Critical for reconciliation and vendor performance tracking.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Make-Good Workflow; 4As Make-Good Standards

attributes.name: last_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this media schedule record was most recently modified in the source system or the Silver Layer. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Audit Trail; Google Campaign Manager 360 — Change History

attributes.name: market_name
attributes.description: Geographic market or Designated Market Area (DMA) where the media will air or be delivered — e.g., 'New York', 'Los Angeles', 'National'. Used for local vs. national media planning and GRP/TRP calculations.
attributes.references: Nielsen DMA Market Definitions; Mediaocean Prisma — Market Module

attributes.name: net_cost
attributes.description: Net cost of the scheduled media buy after agency commission and negotiated discounts, expressed in the billing currency. Used for budget management, P&L reporting, and financial reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Cost Module; SAP S/4HANA FI; 4As Agency Commission Standards

attributes.name: pod_position
attributes.description: Requested or guaranteed position of the ad within a commercial break pod for broadcast TV, radio, and CTV/OTT channels. First and last pod positions command premium pricing. Null for non-broadcast channels.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Broadcast Scheduling; Nielsen Ad Intel

attributes.name: preemptible
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this scheduled placement can be preempted by a higher-paying advertiser. Preemptible spots are typically priced lower than guaranteed placements. Relevant for broadcast and premium digital inventory.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Broadcast Scheduling; Broadcast Standards and Practices

attributes.name: schedule_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name assigned to this media schedule record, typically reflecting the campaign flight, channel, and market (e.g., 'Q3_BrandLaunch_PrimeTV_NYC'). Used for trafficking and reporting identification.

attributes.name: schedule_number
attributes.description: Externally-known alphanumeric reference number for this schedule, as assigned by the media planning system (Mediaocean Prisma) or trafficking system (Google Campaign Manager 360). Used for cross-system reconciliation.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Schedule Reference; Google Campaign Manager 360

attributes.name: scheduled_grp
attributes.description: Total Gross Rating Points (GRP) planned for this media schedule, representing the sum of ratings across all scheduled airings. GRP = Reach % × Frequency. Used for broadcast media planning and audience delivery measurement.
attributes.references: Nielsen GRP Measurement Standards; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: scheduled_impressions
attributes.description: Total number of ad impressions contracted and scheduled for delivery under this media schedule record for the defined flight period. Used for pacing, delivery tracking, and reconciliation against actuals.
attributes.references: MRC Impression Measurement Guidelines; IAB Measurement Standards

attributes.name: scheduled_trp
attributes.description: Total Target Rating Points (TRP) planned for this media schedule, measuring GRP delivery against a specific target audience demographic. TRP is the audience-qualified subset of GRP used for precision media planning.
attributes.references: Nielsen TRP Measurement Standards; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: scheduled_units
attributes.description: Number of discrete ad units (spots, insertions, or placements) scheduled for the flight period — applicable to broadcast (spots), print (insertions), and OOH (faces/panels). Distinct from impressions which measure audience exposure.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Unit Scheduling; IAB Measurement Standards

attributes.name: source_system_ref
attributes.description: Native record identifier from the originating operational system — Google Campaign Manager 360 placement ID or Mediaocean Prisma schedule line number. Enables traceability and reconciliation back to the system of record.

attributes.name: trafficking_notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for trafficking instructions, special placement requirements, or operational notes associated with this media schedule — e.g., 'Run adjacent to news content only', 'Avoid competitor adjacency'. Sourced from Google Campaign Manager 360 trafficking comments.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 — Trafficking Notes; IAB Trafficking Standards

attributes.name: trafficking_status
attributes.description: Current operational status of this media schedule in the trafficking workflow. Tracks progression from initial planning through creative assignment, trafficking into ad server, live delivery, and completion or cancellation. Sourced from Google Campaign Manager 360. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_creative|trafficked|live|paused|completed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 — Trafficking Workflow; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: vehicle_name
attributes.description: Name of the specific media vehicle (publisher, network, station, or platform) where the ad will be delivered — e.g., 'NBC Primetime', 'New York Times Digital', 'Spotify', 'Clear Channel OOH'. Sourced from Mediaocean Prisma publisher library.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma — Publisher/Vehicle Library; IAB Seller.json

products.name: trafficking_instruction
products.description: Transactional record capturing ad trafficking instructions issued to ad servers and media vendors for creative asset delivery. Captures instruction type (new, revision, cancellation), placement reference, creative asset reference, ad server (Google Campaign Manager 360), VAST/VPAID tag URL, click-through URL, impression tracking pixels, viewability tags, brand safety parameters, trafficking date, trafficker name, and instruction status (pending, sent, confirmed). Represents the operational event of dispatching creative to media.
products.reference: Trafficking — Google Campaign Manager 360 / VAST / VPAID

attributes.name: trafficking_instruction_id
attributes.description: Unique system-generated identifier for each ad trafficking instruction record issued to an ad server or media vendor. Serves as the primary key for this transactional entity. ROLE: TRANSACTION_HEADER.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Ad Trafficking; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

attributes.name: ad_channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.ad_channel. Business justification: A trafficking instruction is channel-specific — different channels use different ad servers, tag formats (VAST vs. display tags), and trafficking protocols. Linking trafficking_instruction to ad_chann

attributes.name: ad_format_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.ad_format. Business justification: trafficking_instruction currently has ad_format as a STRING attribute. Trafficking instructions specify the ad format for delivery to ad servers (e.g., 300x250 Banner, VAST 4.0 Video). This FK normali

attributes.name: asset_id
attributes.description: Reference to the creative asset (banner, video, rich media, etc.) being dispatched via this trafficking instruction. Identifies the specific creative unit to be served at the designated placement.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Creative Management; IAB Digital Creative Guidelines

attributes.name: buy_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.buy. Business justification: A trafficking instruction is issued to execute a specific media buy — it carries the creative assets, targeting parameters, and ad server tags needed to activate the purchased inventory. Linking traff

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent advertising campaign under which this trafficking instruction is issued. Provides campaign-level context for reporting and performance attribution.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Campaign Management; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Trafficking instructions drive ad server costs (third-party verification, ad serving fees) that require cost center attribution. Agency operations allocate trafficking costs to cost centers for client

attributes.name: creative_brief_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.creative_brief. Business justification: Trafficking teams must reference the creative brief to confirm spec compliance, messaging mandatories, and legal/regulatory requirements before sending ad tags. A trafficker needs to know which brief

attributes.name: media_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the Insertion Order (IO) authorizing the media buy associated with this trafficking instruction. Provides contractual and financial context for the ad delivery.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma - IO Management; IAB Standard Terms and Conditions for Internet Advertising

attributes.name: media_placement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the media placement for which this trafficking instruction is issued. Links the instruction to the specific ad unit, position, and publisher property where the creative will be delivered.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Placement Management; IAB Ad Unit Guidelines

attributes.name: parent_instruction_trafficking_instruction_id
attributes.description: Reference to the original trafficking instruction that this record revises or cancels. Enables lineage tracking across instruction versions and supports audit of changes to creative delivery parameters.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Ad Trafficking Versioning; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

attributes.name: plan_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.plan_line. Business justification: Trafficking instructions are issued to execute specific media placements that originate from plan lines. Adding plan_line_id to trafficking_instruction provides direct traceability from the ad server

attributes.name: programmatic_deal_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.programmatic_deal. Business justification: Trafficking instructions for programmatic placements must reference the programmatic deal that governs the inventory access, floor CPM, targeting parameters, and DSP seat configuration. This FK enable

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Trafficking instructions configure ad server targeting parameters for specific audience segments. Ad ops teams require explicit segment linkage to set up DMP/DSP integrations, frequency caps, and audi

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Trafficking is a deliverable service often specified in SOWs with defined SLAs. Linking instructions to SOWs enables service-level tracking, supports billing for trafficking services, and validates sc

attributes.name: spec_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.spec. Business justification: Trafficking instructions specify exact creative spec requirements (ad size, VAST tag, file format) that must be validated before sending tags. The existing creative_size plain attribute is a denormali

attributes.name: suppression_list_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.suppression_list. Business justification: Privacy-compliant ad trafficking: trafficking instructions configure ad server delivery and must reference suppression lists (opted-out users, converted customers) to ensure GDPR/CCPA-compliant servin

attributes.name: tech_partner_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.tech_partner. Business justification: Trafficking instructions specify the ad server platform and brand safety/verification provider. Linking to tech_partner normalizes the ad_server plain-text field and enables vendor SLA tracking, tech

attributes.name: ad_server_placement_code
attributes.description: The unique placement tag or code assigned by the ad server (e.g., Google Campaign Manager 360 placement ID) that maps this instruction to the correct ad unit within the ad serving platform. Used for trafficking reconciliation.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Placement Tagging; IAB Ad Unit Guidelines

attributes.name: brand_safety_provider
attributes.description: Name of the third-party brand safety and suitability verification provider applied to this trafficking instruction. Ensures ad creative is served only in brand-appropriate content environments per TAG and GARM standards.
attributes.references: TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group) Brand Safety Standards; GARM Brand Safety Floor; IAB Brand Safety Guidelines

attributes.name: brand_safety_segment
attributes.description: Specific brand safety content category or segment classification applied to this instruction (e.g., 'exclude adult content', 'exclude fake news', 'GARM Floor'). Defines the content adjacency restrictions enforced during ad delivery.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Standards; GARM Brand Safety Floor; IAB Content Taxonomy

attributes.name: click_through_url
attributes.description: The destination URL to which users are redirected upon clicking the served ad creative. Must be validated and approved prior to trafficking. Critical for campaign performance tracking and landing page attribution.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Click Tracking; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines; FTC Advertising Practices

attributes.name: confirmed_timestamp
attributes.description: Precise date and time when the ad server or media vendor confirmed receipt and acceptance of the trafficking instruction. Used for SLA compliance measurement and operational reporting.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Ad Trafficking Audit; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this trafficking instruction record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker for data governance and lineage tracking.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity; IAB Data Governance Guidelines

attributes.name: daypart_schedule
attributes.description: Day-of-week and time-of-day scheduling parameters defining when the ad creative should be served (dayparting). Expressed as a structured schedule string (e.g., 'Mon-Fri 08:00-20:00'). Used to optimize delivery timing per media plan.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Daypart Scheduling; Mediaocean Prisma - Media Planning

attributes.name: delivery_end_date
attributes.description: The scheduled date on which ad creative delivery is to cease per this trafficking instruction. Aligns with the media plan flight end dates and insertion order terms.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma - Flight Scheduling; Google Campaign Manager 360 - Campaign Scheduling; IAB Standard IO Terms

attributes.name: delivery_start_date
attributes.description: The scheduled date on which ad creative delivery is to commence per this trafficking instruction. Aligns with the media plan flight dates and insertion order terms.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma - Flight Scheduling; Google Campaign Manager 360 - Campaign Scheduling; IAB Standard IO Terms

attributes.name: device_targeting
attributes.description: Device type targeting parameters specifying which device categories the ad creative should be served on (e.g., desktop, mobile, tablet, Connected TV (CTV), or all devices). Aligns with media plan targeting specifications.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Device Targeting; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

attributes.name: frequency_cap_impressions
attributes.description: Maximum number of times the ad creative may be served to a single unique user within the defined frequency cap period. Controls ad exposure and prevents overexposure to individual users.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Frequency Capping; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

attributes.name: frequency_cap_period
attributes.description: Time window over which the frequency cap impressions limit applies. Defines the reset interval for the per-user impression count (e.g., no more than 3 impressions per day).
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Frequency Capping; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

attributes.name: geo_targeting
attributes.description: Geographic targeting parameters applied to this trafficking instruction, specifying the countries, regions, DMAs, or cities where the ad should be served. Expressed as a structured targeting string or comma-separated location codes.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Geo Targeting; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

attributes.name: impression_tracking_pixel_url
attributes.description: URL of the impression tracking pixel fired upon each ad impression to record delivery counts. Used for third-party verification, billing reconciliation, and campaign measurement. May include multiple pixels concatenated or the primary pixel URL.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Impression Tracking; MRC Digital Measurement Guidelines; IAB Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: instruction_number
attributes.description: Externally-known, human-readable reference number for this trafficking instruction, used in communications with ad servers, media vendors, and internal trafficking teams. Typically follows a structured naming convention (e.g., TI-2024-00123).
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Ad Trafficking; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

attributes.name: instruction_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the trafficking instruction: pending (created but not yet dispatched), sent (transmitted to ad server or vendor), confirmed (acknowledged by recipient), rejected (refused by recipient), or cancelled (voided before confirmation).
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Ad Trafficking Workflow; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

attributes.name: instruction_type
attributes.description: Classifies the nature of the trafficking instruction: new (initial dispatch of a creative), revision (update to an existing instruction), cancellation (termination of delivery), pause (temporary halt), or resume (restart after pause).
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Ad Trafficking Workflow; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional operational notes, special instructions, or contextual information relevant to this trafficking instruction. Used by ad operations teams to communicate nuances not captured in structured fields.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Ad Trafficking; Workfront - Project Notes

attributes.name: rejection_reason
attributes.description: Free-text description of the reason provided by the ad server or media vendor when a trafficking instruction is rejected. Populated only when instruction_status is 'rejected'. Used for troubleshooting and resubmission workflows.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Ad Trafficking Error Handling; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

attributes.name: revision_number
attributes.description: Sequential version number of this trafficking instruction, incremented each time a revision instruction is issued for the same placement and creative combination. Version 1 represents the original instruction.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Ad Trafficking Versioning; Workfront - Change Management

attributes.name: sent_timestamp
attributes.description: Precise date and time when the trafficking instruction was transmitted to the ad server or media vendor. Used for SLA tracking, audit trails, and delivery timeline analysis.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Ad Trafficking Audit; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

attributes.name: third_party_verification_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether third-party ad verification (e.g., DoubleVerify, Integral Ad Science) is mandated for this trafficking instruction per client or campaign requirements. When true, verification tags must be included in the ad serving setup.
attributes.references: TAG Certified Against Fraud; MRC Accreditation Standards; IAB Quality Assurance Guidelines

attributes.name: trafficking_date
attributes.description: The date on which the trafficking instruction was formally issued and dispatched to the ad server or media vendor. Represents the principal real-world business event date for this operational record.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Ad Trafficking; Mediaocean Prisma - Trafficking Workflow

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this trafficking instruction record was last modified. Tracks revisions to the instruction for audit, compliance, and change management purposes.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity; IAB Data Governance Guidelines

attributes.name: vast_tag_url
attributes.business_glossary_term: Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) Tag URL
attributes.description: The Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) tag URL used for video creative delivery. Provides the ad server with the XML-based instructions for serving video ads, including media file location, tracking events, and companion ads. Applicable for video placements.
attributes.references: IAB VAST Standard (Video Ad Serving Template); Google Campaign Manager 360 - Video Trafficking

attributes.name: viewability_tag_url
attributes.description: URL of the viewability measurement tag embedded in the ad creative to track whether the ad was viewable per MRC and IAB viewability standards (e.g., 50% of pixels in view for 1 continuous second for display). Used for viewability reporting and vendor compliance.
attributes.references: MRC Viewability Standards; IAB Viewability Guidelines; Google Campaign Manager 360 - Viewability Measurement

attributes.name: vpaid_tag_url
attributes.business_glossary_term: Video Player-Ad Interface Definition (VPAID) Tag URL
attributes.description: The Video Player-Ad Interface Definition (VPAID) tag URL enabling interactive video ad execution within the video player environment. Used for rich interactive video creatives requiring player-level communication. Applicable for VPAID-enabled placements.
attributes.references: IAB VPAID Standard (Video Player-Ad Interface Definition); Google Campaign Manager 360 - Video Trafficking

products.name: ad_channel
products.description: Master reference entity for all media and distribution channels through which advertising is delivered — including paid search (SEM/PPC), social, display, video, email, SEO, OOH/DOOH, CTV/OTT, and programmatic. Owns the authoritative channel taxonomy with IAB-aligned classifications, channel codes, and delivery modalities. Also owns platform-specific operational configuration including DSP seat settings (The Trade Desk), UTM parameter conventions, bid strategy defaults, frequency cap policies, brand safety keyword lists, and GDPR/CCPA consent signal handling. SSOT for channel identity, taxonomy, and operational configuration across the enterprise.

attributes.name: ad_channel_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the advertising channel. Primary key for the ad_channel entity.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Each ad channel (TV, digital, OOH, radio) maps to a specific GL account for revenue and cost classification in financial statements. This channel-to-GL mapping is standard chart-of-accounts configurat

attributes.name: taxonomy_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.taxonomy. Business justification: Ad channel taxonomy standard selection: media planners must know which audience taxonomy standard (IAB, Nielsen, Comscore) a channel supports to correctly map audience segments for targeting activatio

attributes.name: tech_partner_id
