attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.tech_partner. Business justification: Ad channels reference platform_name and dsp_seat_code as plain text. Linking to tech_partner normalizes the platform vendor relationship, enabling channel-level tech fee management, platform SLA track

attributes.name: agency_commission_pct
attributes.description: Standard agency commission percentage applied to gross media spend on this channel. Typically 15% for traditional media, 10-15% for digital. Null if commission varies by client or IO.

attributes.name: api_endpoint_url
attributes.description: Base URL for the channels API integration. Used by ETL processes to retrieve campaign performance data, update bids, and sync creative assets. Confidential system configuration.

attributes.name: api_version
attributes.description: Version of the API specification currently in use for this channel (e.g., v14, 2023-Q4). Critical for maintaining integration compatibility during platform upgrades.

attributes.name: attribution_window_days
attributes.description: Number of days after an ad interaction (click or view) during which conversions are attributed to this channel. Standard windows are 1, 7, 30, or 90 days depending on channel and business model.

attributes.name: bid_strategy_default
attributes.description: Default bidding strategy applied to campaigns on this channel unless overridden at campaign level. Determines how the platform optimizes bids to achieve campaign objectives. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manual_cpc|enhanced_cpc|maximize_clicks|maximize_conversions|target_cpa|target_roas|target_impression_share|viewable_cpm — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: brand_safety_keywords
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of keywords or content categories to exclude for brand safety (e.g., violence, adult content, controversial topics). Applied as negative targeting across all campaigns on this channel.

attributes.name: brand_safety_tier
attributes.description: Default brand safety filtering level applied to inventory on this channel. Standard allows broad reach; strict applies maximum content exclusions to protect brand reputation.

attributes.name: ccpa_opt_out_supported
attributes.business_glossary_term: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) Opt-Out Supported
attributes.description: Indicates whether this channel supports CCPA Do Not Sell signals for California users. Required for compliance with California privacy regulations.

attributes.name: channel_code
attributes.description: Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the channel across all systems (e.g., SEM_GOOGLE, SOCIAL_FB, DISPLAY_PROG). Used as the business identifier in campaign planning, trafficking, and reporting.

attributes.name: channel_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the advertising channel (e.g., Google Search, Facebook Social, Programmatic Display).

attributes.name: channel_status
attributes.description: Current operational status of the channel. Active channels are available for campaign planning and buying; deprecated channels are phased out but may have historical data.

attributes.name: channel_type
attributes.description: Primary classification of the channel by media type. Aligns with IAB taxonomy for cross-platform reporting and planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: paid_search|social|display|video|email|seo|ooh|dooh|ctv|ott|programmatic|audio|native|affiliate — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: click_attribution_window_days
attributes.description: Number of days after a click during which conversions are attributed to this channel. Typically longer than view attribution window due to higher intent signal.

attributes.name: consent_signal_format
attributes.description: Format of privacy consent signals passed to this channel. TCF v2 (Transparency and Consent Framework) for GDPR, US Privacy String for CCPA, GPP (Global Privacy Platform) for multi-jurisdiction compliance.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this channel record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Default currency for financial transactions on this channel. Three-letter ISO 4217 code (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used for rate cards, billing, and reconciliation.

attributes.name: delivery_modality
attributes.description: Method by which media is bought and delivered through this channel. Determines operational workflow, trafficking requirements, and reconciliation processes.

attributes.name: dsp_seat_code
attributes.description: Unique seat identifier within the DSP platform (e.g., The Trade Desk seat ID). Used for programmatic bid requests, deal configuration, and reconciliation. Confidential operational credential.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date when this channel configuration became active and available for campaign planning and buying. Used for historical tracking of channel availability.

attributes.name: expiration_date
attributes.description: Date when this channel configuration expires or was deprecated. Null for active channels. Used to track channel lifecycle and prevent planning on sunset channels.

attributes.name: frequency_cap_default
attributes.description: Default maximum number of times an ad can be shown to the same user within the frequency cap period. Prevents ad fatigue and optimizes reach. Null indicates no default cap.

attributes.name: frequency_cap_period_default
attributes.description: Time window over which the default frequency cap is applied (e.g., 3 impressions per day). Works in conjunction with frequency_cap_default.

attributes.name: gdpr_consent_required
attributes.business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Consent Required
attributes.description: Indicates whether GDPR consent signals must be passed for users in EU/EEA jurisdictions when serving ads on this channel. True for channels operating in or targeting European markets.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or configuration details specific to this channel. Used by media planners and buyers for reference.

attributes.name: platform_type
attributes.description: Classification of the underlying technology platform. DSP (Demand-Side Platform) for programmatic buying, SSP (Supply-Side Platform) for inventory supply, or direct publisher platforms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dsp|ssp|ad_network|publisher_direct|search_engine|social_platform|video_platform|email_platform|ad_server — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: pricing_model
attributes.description: Default pricing model for media buys on this channel. CPM (Cost Per Mille) for impressions, CPC (Cost Per Click), CPA (Cost Per Acquisition), CPV (Cost Per View), CPCV (Cost Per Completed View), or flat fee. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cpm|cpc|cpa|cpv|cpcv|flat_fee|performance — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: supports_audience_targeting
attributes.description: Indicates whether this channel supports audience segment targeting using first-party, third-party, or platform-native audience data. Critical for personalized campaign strategies.

attributes.name: supports_dayparting
attributes.description: Indicates whether this channel supports daypart scheduling (time-of-day and day-of-week targeting). Common in broadcast, CTV, and digital channels for optimizing reach and frequency.

attributes.name: supports_geo_targeting
attributes.description: Indicates whether this channel supports geographic targeting by country, region, DMA (Designated Market Area), postal code, or radius. Essential for local and regional campaigns.

attributes.name: supports_programmatic
attributes.description: Indicates whether this channel supports programmatic buying via DSP platforms and RTB (Real-Time Bidding) protocols. True for most digital channels; false for traditional media.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this channel record was last modified. Used for change tracking, cache invalidation, and audit purposes.

attributes.name: utm_medium_default
attributes.description: Default value for utm_medium parameter in campaign tracking URLs for this channel (e.g., cpc, social, display). Enables consistent channel attribution in web analytics.

attributes.name: utm_source_default
attributes.description: Default value for utm_source parameter in campaign tracking URLs for this channel. Standardizes attribution and analytics reporting across Google Analytics and other web analytics platforms.

attributes.name: view_attribution_window_days
attributes.description: Number of days after an ad view (impression without click) during which conversions are attributed to this channel. Typically 1-7 days for display and video channels.

attributes.name: viewability_standard
attributes.description: Viewability measurement standard applied to this channel. MRC (Media Rating Council) standard requires 50% of pixels in view for 1 second (display) or 2 seconds (video).

attributes.name: viewability_threshold_pct
attributes.description: Minimum viewability percentage required for impressions on this channel to be considered valid for billing and reporting purposes. Typically 50-70% based on MRC standards.

products.name: publisher_property
products.description: Represents individual media properties, sites, apps, networks, or placements owned or operated by a publisher — e.g., a specific website, mobile app, TV network, OOH billboard network, or streaming channel. Captures property-level attributes including URL/app ID, content vertical, audience composition, MRC-audited traffic certification, viewability benchmarks, and brand safety classification.

attributes.name: publisher_property_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the publisher property. Primary key.

attributes.name: ad_channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.ad_channel. Business justification: A publisher property operates within a primary advertising channel (e.g., a website is a display/digital channel property, a TV network is a broadcast channel property, a podcast is an audio channel p

attributes.name: primary_audience_segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.audience_segment. Business justification: Publisher audience profiling: media planners select inventory based on publisher audience composition. The denormalized `audience_composition` text field should be replaced with a proper FK to the aud

attributes.name: publisher_id
attributes.description: Reference to the publisher or media owner that operates this property.

attributes.name: taxonomy_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.taxonomy. Business justification: Inventory taxonomy classification: publisher properties are classified using audience taxonomy standards (IAB content categories) for targeting eligibility and audience-to-inventory matching. The deno

attributes.name: ad_fraud_risk_score
attributes.description: The fraud risk score for this property (0-100 scale, where higher scores indicate higher fraud risk) based on invalid traffic (IVT) detection and TAG certification.

attributes.name: ad_server_platform
attributes.description: The primary ad serving platform used by this property (e.g., Google Ad Manager, Freewheel, Xandr, Magnite).

attributes.name: app_bundle_code
attributes.description: The platform-specific bundle identifier for mobile or CTV apps (e.g., com.example.app).

attributes.name: app_store_code
attributes.description: The unique identifier for the mobile or CTV app in the app store (e.g., Apple App Store ID, Google Play Store ID, Roku Channel Store ID).

attributes.name: average_cpm
attributes.description: The average Cost Per Mille (CPM) rate achieved for ad placements on this property over the past 90 days.

attributes.name: brand_safety_classification
attributes.description: The brand safety tier or classification for this property based on content quality, adjacency risk, and third-party verification (e.g., TAG Certified, IAS/DV brand safety scores).

attributes.name: campaign_manager_site_code
attributes.description: The unique site identifier for this property in Google Campaign Manager 360 ad serving platform.

attributes.name: content_vertical
attributes.description: The primary content category or vertical of the property (e.g., News, Sports, Entertainment, Finance, Lifestyle) per IAB Content Taxonomy.

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: The primary country where this property operates, using ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this property record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: The currency used for pricing and billing for this property, using ISO 4217 three-letter currency code (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

attributes.name: floor_cpm
attributes.description: The minimum Cost Per Mille (CPM) rate accepted for ad placements on this property.

attributes.name: geographic_coverage
attributes.description: The primary geographic markets or regions served by this property (e.g., 'USA National', 'New York DMA', 'EMEA', 'Global').

attributes.name: language_code
attributes.description: The primary language of content on this property, using ISO 639-1 two-letter language code (e.g., en, es, fr).

attributes.name: monthly_impressions
attributes.description: The average number of ad impressions available per month for this property.

attributes.name: monthly_unique_visitors
attributes.description: The average number of unique visitors or users per month for this property.

attributes.name: mrc_accredited
attributes.description: Indicates whether the property's traffic and audience measurement has been audited and accredited by the Media Rating Council (MRC).

attributes.name: mrc_audit_date
attributes.description: The date of the most recent MRC audit or certification for this property.

attributes.name: pmp_deals_available
attributes.description: Indicates whether this property offers Private Marketplace (PMP) programmatic deals with negotiated terms and pricing.

attributes.name: prisma_property_code
attributes.description: The unique identifier for this property in the Mediaocean Prisma media planning and buying system.

attributes.name: programmatic_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether this property is available for programmatic buying through Demand-Side Platforms (DSPs) and Supply-Side Platforms (SSPs).

attributes.name: property_code
attributes.description: The externally-known unique identifier or code for this property used in media planning and buying systems (e.g., site ID, app ID, network code).

attributes.name: property_name
attributes.description: The human-readable name of the media property, site, app, network, or placement (e.g., 'ESPN Mobile App', 'The New York Times Homepage', 'Clear Channel Billboard Network').

attributes.name: property_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the property in the media inventory system.

attributes.name: property_type
attributes.description: The category of media property: website, mobile app, Connected TV (CTV) app, streaming channel, TV network, radio station, Out-of-Home (OOH) network, or print publication. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: website|mobile_app|ctv_app|streaming_channel|tv_network|radio_station|ooh_network|print_publication — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: property_url
attributes.description: The primary URL or web address for the property (for websites and web-based properties).

attributes.name: rtb_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether this property supports Real-Time Bidding (RTB) for ad inventory.

attributes.name: supported_ad_formats
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of ad formats supported by this property (e.g., display, video, native, audio, DOOH).

attributes.name: tag_certified
attributes.description: Indicates whether the property is certified by the Trustworthy Accountability Group (TAG) for brand safety and anti-fraud measures.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this property record was last modified in the system.

attributes.name: vast_version
attributes.description: The version of the Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) standard supported by this property (e.g., VAST 2.0, VAST 3.0, VAST 4.0).

attributes.name: video_player_type
attributes.description: The type of video player used on this property for video ad serving (e.g., JW Player, Brightcove, Video.js, native CTV player).

attributes.name: viewability_benchmark_pct
attributes.description: The average viewability rate for ad placements on this property, measured as percentage of impressions that meet MRC viewability standards (50% of pixels in view for 1+ seconds for display, 2+ seconds for video).

attributes.name: vpaid_supported
attributes.description: Indicates whether this property supports the Video Player-Ad Interface Definition (VPAID) standard for interactive video ads.

products.name: ad_format
products.description: Reference catalog of all standardized advertising formats, their creative specifications, and trafficking requirements across channels — including IAB standard display units, video formats (VAST/VPAID), native, audio, OOH/DOOH specs, CTV interstitials, and rich media. Owns format dimensions, file size limits, MIME types, accepted creative file formats, click-through URL structures, impression tracking pixel requirements, viewability measurement vendor compatibility, platform-specific tagging requirements (Google Campaign Manager 360, The Trade Desk), IAB format classification codes, and channel×format trafficking matrices defining which specifications apply per channel context. SSOT for format identity, creative delivery specifications, and trafficking requirements.

attributes.name: ad_format_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the advertising format. Primary key for the ad_format product.

attributes.name: accepted_file_formats
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of accepted file extensions for creative assets (e.g., JPG, PNG, GIF, MP4, MOV, HTML5, ZIP). Used for creative validation and trafficking.

attributes.name: accepted_mime_types
attributes.business_glossary_term: Accepted Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions (MIME) Types
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of accepted MIME types for creative files (e.g., image/jpeg, image/png, video/mp4, application/javascript). Defines technical compatibility.

attributes.name: ad_server_platform_compatibility
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of ad server platforms that support this format (e.g., Google Campaign Manager 360, The Trade Desk, Sizmek, Flashtalking). Used for trafficking validation.

attributes.name: aspect_ratio
attributes.description: Proportional relationship between width and height (e.g., 16:9, 4:3, 1:1). Critical for video and CTV formats.

attributes.name: brand_safety_tier
attributes.description: Brand safety classification tier for the format, indicating the level of content adjacency control and verification required (standard, enhanced, premium).
attributes.references: Trustworthy Accountability Group (TAG) Brand Safety Standards

attributes.name: channel_applicability
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of media channels where this format is applicable (e.g., digital, broadcast, OOH, DOOH, CTV, OTT, print, social, programmatic). Defines channel-format trafficking matrix.

attributes.name: click_through_url_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether a click-through URL is mandatory for this ad format. True for most digital formats; false for awareness-only formats (e.g., some OOH).

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the ad format record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

attributes.name: deprecation_date
attributes.description: Date when the ad format was deprecated and scheduled for retirement. Null for active formats. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.

attributes.name: device_type_compatibility
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of device types compatible with this format (e.g., desktop, mobile, tablet, CTV, smart_speaker, digital_billboard). Used for targeting and trafficking.

attributes.name: duration_seconds
attributes.description: Standard duration of the ad creative in seconds. Applicable to video, audio, and CTV/OTT formats. Null for static formats.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date when the ad format specification became effective and available for use in media planning and trafficking. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.

attributes.name: environment_type
attributes.description: Primary environment where the ad format is delivered (e.g., web, app, CTV, DOOH, print, broadcast, social). Aligns with media planning and buying strategies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|app|ctv|dooh|print|broadcast|social — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: format_category
attributes.description: High-level classification of the ad format by media type. Aligns with channel taxonomy and media planning categories. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: display|video|audio|native|rich_media|ooh|dooh|ctv|ott|print|social|email — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: format_code
attributes.description: Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the ad format across systems (e.g., DISPLAY_300X250, VIDEO_PREROLL_15S). Used for trafficking and system integration.

attributes.name: format_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the advertising format (e.g., Medium Rectangle, Pre-Roll Video, Native In-Feed).

attributes.name: format_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the ad format. Active formats are available for trafficking; deprecated formats are phased out; retired formats are no longer supported.

attributes.name: format_subcategory
attributes.description: Detailed classification within the format category (e.g., banner, interstitial, pre-roll, mid-roll, post-roll, in-stream, out-stream, expandable, takeover).

attributes.name: frequency_cap_recommended
attributes.description: Recommended frequency cap (number of impressions per user per period) for this format to optimize user experience and campaign performance. Null if no recommendation.

attributes.name: height_pixels
attributes.description: Standard height dimension of the ad creative in pixels. Null for non-display formats (e.g., audio, print).

attributes.name: iab_format_code
attributes.description: Official IAB classification code for the ad format, enabling standardized reporting and cross-platform compatibility.

attributes.name: impression_tracking_pixel_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether an impression tracking pixel is required for delivery measurement. True for most digital formats to enable campaign reporting.

attributes.name: is_native_format
attributes.description: Indicates whether the ad format is a native advertising format designed to match the form and function of the surrounding content. True for native in-feed, content recommendation, and sponsored content formats.

attributes.name: is_programmatic_eligible
attributes.description: Indicates whether the ad format is eligible for programmatic buying via Demand-Side Platforms (DSP) and Real-Time Bidding (RTB). True for formats supported by programmatic infrastructure.

attributes.name: max_file_size_kb
attributes.description: Maximum allowed file size for the creative asset in kilobytes. Enforced during creative upload and trafficking validation.

attributes.name: placement_position_options
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of allowed placement positions for this format (e.g., above_the_fold, below_the_fold, in_feed, interstitial, pre_roll, mid_roll, post_roll, sidebar, footer). Used for trafficking and optimization.

attributes.name: retirement_date
attributes.description: Date when the ad format was fully retired and removed from active use. Null for active and deprecated formats. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.

attributes.name: supports_animation
attributes.description: Indicates whether the ad format supports animated creative content (e.g., GIF, HTML5 animation, video). False for static-only formats.

attributes.name: supports_audio
attributes.description: Indicates whether the ad format supports audio playback. True for video, audio, and CTV/OTT formats; false for display and print.

attributes.name: supports_interactivity
attributes.description: Indicates whether the ad format supports interactive elements (e.g., expandable units, rich media, VPAID). True for formats allowing user engagement beyond click-through.

attributes.name: trafficking_notes
attributes.description: Free-text field capturing special trafficking instructions, technical requirements, or operational notes for this ad format. Used by trafficking teams during campaign setup.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the ad format record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

attributes.name: vast_version_supported
attributes.business_glossary_term: Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) Version Supported
attributes.description: Supported VAST specification version for video ad serving (e.g., 2.0, 3.0, 4.0, 4.1, 4.2). Null for non-video formats.

attributes.name: viewability_measurement_supported
attributes.description: Indicates whether the ad format supports viewability measurement per Media Rating Council (MRC) standards. True for formats compatible with viewability vendors.

attributes.name: viewability_vendor_compatibility
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of viewability measurement vendors compatible with this format (e.g., IAS, MOAT, DoubleVerify, Comscore). Used for third-party verification setup.

attributes.name: vpaid_version_supported
attributes.business_glossary_term: Video Player-Ad Interface Definition (VPAID) Version Supported
attributes.description: Supported VPAID specification version for interactive video ads (e.g., 1.0, 2.0). Null for non-interactive or non-video formats.

attributes.name: width_pixels
attributes.description: Standard width dimension of the ad creative in pixels. Null for non-display formats (e.g., audio, print).

products.name: placement
products.description: Defines specific ad placement positions within a publisher property and channel — e.g., homepage leaderboard, in-stream pre-roll, sidebar rectangle, sponsored post slot, or DOOH screen location. Captures placement-level attributes including position type, above/below-fold classification, viewability rate benchmarks, CPM floor pricing, audience reach, and platform-specific trafficking requirements for Google Campaign Manager 360 and The Trade Desk.

attributes.name: placement_id
attributes.description: Primary key for placement

attributes.name: ad_channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.ad_channel. Business justification: A placement position is defined within a specific advertising channel (display, video, social, OOH, etc.). The channel determines the technical specifications, trafficking requirements, and pricing mo

attributes.name: publisher_property_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.publisher_property. Business justification: A placement position (e.g., homepage leaderboard, pre-roll slot) is physically located within a specific publisher property. This is the fundamental parent-child relationship: placement belongs to pub

domains.name: creative
domains.description: Manages the full creative development and production lifecycle — from brief and concept through asset production, versioning, proofing, and final delivery. Serves as the SSOT for creative assets, DAM metadata, WIP status, format compliance, creative specifications, and brand compliance. Integrates with Adobe Creative Cloud and Workfront DAM.
domains.references: IAB New Ad Portfolio / VAST / VPAID Standards

products.name: creative_brief
products.description: Master record for the creative brief — the foundational document that initiates creative development. Captures client objectives, target audience, key messaging, tone of voice, deliverable formats, mandatories, budget envelope, and approval chain. Serves as the SSOT for all creative work initiated within a campaign or project. Originates from Workfront project intake and links to campaign strategy.

attributes.name: creative_brief_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the creative brief record. Primary key.

attributes.name: agency_relationship_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.agency_relationship. Business justification: Creative briefs are issued within a specific agency relationship (AOR or project engagement). Agency billing, scope-of-services compliance, and fee reconciliation all require knowing which agency rela

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Briefs execute under master service agreements (MSAs) that define scope, pricing, and deliverables. Agencies track which agreement governs each brief for billing validation, scope compliance, and cont

attributes.name: brand_guideline_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.brand_guideline. Business justification: creative_brief currently stores brand guideline information as a free-text STRING column `brand_guidelines_reference`. This should be normalized to a proper FK to brand_guideline, which is the authori

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific client brand for which this creative is being developed.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent campaign that this creative brief supports. Links the brief to the broader campaign strategy.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Creative briefs are initiated by departments/practice groups tracked as cost centers for budget ownership and chargeback accounting. Agency finance teams allocate brief costs to originating cost cente

attributes.name: finance_budget_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Briefs reference approved budgets for scope and cost constraints. Links brief requirements to formal budget allocations for financial planning, approval workflows, and spend tracking against allocated

attributes.name: initiative_id
attributes.description: Reference to the Workfront project that initiated this creative brief. Links to project intake and resource planning.

attributes.name: project_brief_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.project_brief. Business justification: Creative briefs are execution-level briefs derived from project briefs. Linking enables strategic alignment validation, requirements traceability, scope verification, and brief-to-execution tracking.

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Briefs are scoped and budgeted within specific SOWs. Agencies track this to ensure creative work stays within contracted scope, allocate costs to correct SOW line items, and validate budget availabili

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Creative briefs specify preferred production vendors (studios, post-production houses, animation vendors) for execution. Essential for production planning, vendor assignment workflows, and budget allo

attributes.name: target_persona_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.persona. Business justification: Briefs define creative strategy against structured personas; target_audience_description is prose, but campaigns require linking to defined persona profiles for consistent targeting and measurement ac

attributes.name: approved_by_name
attributes.description: Name of the individual who provided final approval for the creative brief.

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: Date when the creative brief received final approval and was authorized to proceed to production.

attributes.name: art_director_assigned
attributes.description: Name of the art director assigned to develop visual concepts and design for this creative brief.

attributes.name: brief_name
attributes.description: Descriptive name or title of the creative brief, typically reflecting the campaign or project name.

attributes.name: brief_number
attributes.description: Human-readable business identifier for the creative brief, typically following format CB-NNNNNN. Used for external communication and tracking.

attributes.name: brief_type
attributes.description: Classification of the creative brief based on its strategic purpose and scope.

attributes.name: business_objective
attributes.description: High-level business goal that this creative work is intended to achieve, such as brand awareness, lead generation, or product launch support.

attributes.name: call_to_action
attributes.description: Desired audience action or response that the creative should drive, such as visit website, call now, or download app.

attributes.name: channel_distribution
attributes.description: Media channels and platforms where the creative assets will be deployed, such as television, digital display, social media, OOH (Out-of-Home), or print.

attributes.name: competitive_context
attributes.description: Analysis of competitive landscape and positioning considerations that should inform the creative approach.

attributes.name: copywriter_assigned
attributes.description: Name of the copywriter assigned to develop written content for this creative brief.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the creative brief record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.

attributes.name: creative_brief_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the creative brief in the approval and production workflow.

attributes.name: creative_director_assigned
attributes.description: Name of the creative director assigned to oversee and approve the creative development for this brief.

attributes.name: deliverable_formats
attributes.description: List of required creative asset formats and specifications, such as video lengths, display ad dimensions, print sizes, or social media formats.

attributes.name: due_date
attributes.description: Target date by which the creative assets must be completed and delivered, driving production scheduling and resource allocation.

attributes.name: key_message
attributes.description: Primary message or value proposition that the creative must communicate to the target audience.

attributes.name: legal_regulatory_requirements
attributes.description: Specific legal disclaimers, regulatory compliance requirements, or industry standards that must be adhered to in the creative execution.
attributes.references: Advertising Standards Authority (ASA), Federal Trade Commission (FTC), Interactive Advertising Bureau (IAB)

attributes.name: mandatories
attributes.description: Required elements that must be included in the creative execution, such as logos, legal disclaimers, regulatory statements, or specific product features.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the creative brief record was last modified. Audit trail field for tracking changes.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional notes, comments, or context that provide supplementary information for the creative team.

attributes.name: priority
attributes.description: Business priority level assigned to this creative brief, influencing resource allocation and scheduling.

attributes.name: submitted_date
attributes.description: Date when the creative brief was formally submitted for review and approval.

attributes.name: success_metrics
attributes.description: Key Performance Indicators (KPI) and measurement criteria that will be used to evaluate the effectiveness of the creative execution.

attributes.name: supporting_messages
attributes.description: Secondary messages or proof points that reinforce the key message and provide additional context.

attributes.name: target_audience_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the intended audience for the creative, including demographic, psychographic, and behavioral characteristics.

attributes.name: tone_of_voice
attributes.description: Desired communication style and personality for the creative execution, such as professional, playful, authoritative, or empathetic.

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Version number of the creative brief, incremented with each revision or update to track changes over time.

products.name: asset
products.description: Core master entity representing a single creative asset in the Digital Asset Management system — including images, videos, audio files, copy documents, design files (PSD, AI, INDD), motion graphics, HTML5 rich media, and 3D/AR assets. Tracks asset type, format, file size, resolution, duration, codec, color profile, aspect ratio, DAM asset ID (Workfront DAM/Adobe AEM), version lineage, brand compliance status, rights clearance status, lifecycle stage (WIP, in-review, approved, archived, expired), expiry date, and descriptive metadata tags (subject, mood, channel, campaign theme). This is the SSOT for all creative asset metadata and taxonomy across the agency, supporting search, retrieval, reuse discovery, and rights monitoring.

attributes.name: asset_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the creative asset in the Digital Asset Management (DAM) system. Primary key.

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: FK to client.advertiser.advertiser_id — MUST-HAVE: Cannot determine asset ownership. Required for rights management, brand compliance, and client asset libraries.

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Reference to the client brand that owns or is represented by this asset. Enables brand-level asset organization and reporting.

attributes.name: creative_brief_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.creative_brief. Business justification: A creative asset is produced in direct response to a creative brief — the brief defines the key message, tone, mandatories, deliverable formats, and brand direction that govern the asset. Adding creat

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.initiative. Business justification: Assets are deliverables within initiatives. Linking enables asset inventory management, reuse tracking across projects, ROI analysis, and portfolio reporting. Essential for digital asset management an

attributes.name: parent_asset_creative_asset_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent or original asset from which this version was derived. Establishes version lineage and asset family relationships.

attributes.name: persona_id

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.production_job. Business justification: A creative asset is the output artifact of a production job. Linking creative_asset to the production_job that produced it provides operational traceability — enabling job-level asset inventory, cost

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Assets are optimized for specific audience segments; personalization and dynamic creative optimization require segment-level asset assignment to deliver relevant creative to each audience, core to mod

attributes.name: spec_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.creative_spec. Business justification: Creative assets are produced to meet specific creative specifications (format, dimensions, file size, codec, etc.). This link tracks which spec an asset was produced to, supporting validation and comp

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Assets created by external vendors (stock media providers, production companies, freelance studios) require vendor attribution for rights management, usage tracking, invoice reconciliation, and qualit

attributes.name: task_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.task. Business justification: Assets are outputs of specific tasks. Linking enables granular effort tracking, resource utilization analysis, task completion validation, and productivity measurement. Essential for time tracking and

attributes.name: work_package_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.work_package. Business justification: Creative assets are produced within specific work packages (e.g., 'Digital Asset Production WP'). Direct work_package_id on creative_asset enables work-package-level asset inventory and cost reporting

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the asset was approved for use. Establishes the point at which the asset became available for trafficking and deployment.

attributes.name: archived_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the asset was archived or retired from active use. Supports lifecycle management and storage optimization.

attributes.name: aspect_ratio
attributes.description: Proportional relationship between width and height (e.g., 16:9, 4:3, 1:1, 9:16). Determines display format and channel suitability.

attributes.name: asset_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name or title of the creative asset. Primary label used for search and identification.

attributes.name: asset_type
attributes.description: High-level classification of the creative asset format. Determines handling, rendering, and distribution workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: image|video|audio|copy_document|design_file|motion_graphics|html5_rich_media|3d_ar_asset — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Creative Format Guidelines; VAST/VPAID Standards

attributes.name: brand_compliance_status
attributes.description: Indicates whether the asset adheres to brand guidelines and standards. Critical for brand integrity and legal risk management.
attributes.references: Brand Guideline Standards; 4As Brand Compliance Framework

attributes.name: campaign_theme
attributes.description: Overarching creative theme or concept associated with the asset (e.g., Summer Sale, Back to School, Holiday 2024). Links asset to strategic messaging.

attributes.name: channel_suitability
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of media channels for which this asset is optimized or approved (e.g., TV, Digital, OOH, Social, CTV). Guides media planning and trafficking.
attributes.references: IAB Creative Format Guidelines; MRC Media Channel Standards

attributes.name: codec
attributes.description: Video or audio codec used for encoding (e.g., H.264, H.265, AAC, MP3). Critical for playback compatibility and quality.

attributes.name: color_profile
attributes.description: Color space or profile used in the asset (e.g., sRGB, Adobe RGB, CMYK). Ensures color accuracy across media and devices.
attributes.references: Adobe Creative Cloud Color Management Standards

attributes.name: copyright_holder
attributes.description: Name of the individual or organization that holds copyright to the asset. Critical for legal compliance and rights management.
attributes.references: ANA Rights Management Standards; Copyright Act

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the asset record was first created in the DAM system. Audit and lifecycle tracking.

attributes.name: creative_asset_description
attributes.description: Free-text description of the asset content, purpose, and usage notes. Provides context for search, reuse, and creative briefing.

attributes.name: dam_asset_ref
attributes.description: External unique identifier from the source DAM system (Workfront DAM or Adobe AEM). Used for cross-system reconciliation and asset retrieval.
attributes.references: Workfront DAM API Documentation; Adobe AEM Assets API

attributes.name: duration_seconds
attributes.description: Length of the asset in seconds. Applicable to video, audio, and motion graphics assets.
attributes.references: VAST Video Ad Serving Template; IAB Video Standards

attributes.name: file_format
attributes.description: Specific file format or extension of the asset (e.g., PSD, AI, INDD, MP4, PNG, JPG, HTML5). Critical for compatibility and production workflows.
attributes.references: Adobe Creative Cloud File Format Specifications; IAB Creative Format Guidelines

attributes.name: file_size_bytes
attributes.description: Size of the asset file in bytes. Used for storage management, bandwidth planning, and delivery optimization.

attributes.name: file_storage_path
attributes.description: Full path or URI to the asset file in the storage system (cloud storage, DAM repository, CDN). Used for retrieval and delivery.
attributes.references: Adobe AEM Assets Storage Standards; Workfront DAM Storage API

attributes.name: geographic_market
attributes.description: Target geographic market or region for which the asset was created (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN). Supports regional campaign management and compliance.
attributes.references: ISO 3166-1 Country Codes; IAB Geographic Targeting Standards

attributes.name: is_master_asset
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this asset is the master or source-of-truth version from which other versions are derived. True for master assets, false for derivatives.

attributes.name: language_code
attributes.description: ISO 639-1 two-letter language code indicating the primary language of the asset (e.g., EN, ES, FR). Critical for localization and regional targeting.

attributes.name: license_type
attributes.description: Type of license under which the asset is used (e.g., royalty-free, rights-managed, exclusive, non-exclusive). Governs usage terms and restrictions.

attributes.name: lifecycle_stage
attributes.description: Current stage in the creative asset lifecycle. Governs access, usage rights, and workflow routing.
attributes.references: Workfront DAM Lifecycle Management; IAB Digital Asset Management Standards

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the asset record or file was last modified. Supports version control and change tracking.

attributes.name: mood_tags
attributes.description: Comma-separated keywords describing the emotional tone or mood of the asset (e.g., energetic, calm, humorous, inspirational). Aids in creative selection and reuse.

attributes.name: resolution
attributes.description: Pixel dimensions or resolution of the asset (e.g., 1920x1080, 300 DPI). Applicable to images and videos.

attributes.name: rights_clearance_status
attributes.description: Status of legal rights and usage permissions for the asset (talent, music, stock imagery, etc.). Determines where and how the asset can be used.
attributes.references: ANA Rights Management Standards; GDPR Article 6; CCPA

attributes.name: rights_expiry_date
attributes.description: Date when usage rights for the asset expire. After this date, the asset may no longer be legally used without renewal.

attributes.name: subject_tags
attributes.description: Comma-separated descriptive keywords describing the subject matter or content of the asset (e.g., product, lifestyle, testimonial). Supports search and discovery.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Asset Management Standards; Adobe AEM Taxonomy

attributes.name: thumbnail_url
attributes.description: URL to a thumbnail or preview image of the asset. Enables quick visual identification in DAM interfaces and reporting tools.

attributes.name: usage_count
attributes.description: Number of times this asset has been used in campaigns, ads, or other placements. Indicates asset popularity and reuse value.

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Version identifier for the asset (e.g., v1.0, v2.3, final). Tracks iteration and revision history.

products.name: asset_version
products.description: Tracks the full version history of a creative asset — capturing each iteration produced during the creative development lifecycle. Records version number, version label (e.g., v1, v2_client_feedback), file reference, change summary, author, Adobe CC document version ID, Workfront proof version ID, and promotion status (draft, in-review, approved, superseded). Enables rollback, audit, and compliance traceability for all creative deliverables.

attributes.name: asset_version_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the asset version record. Primary key.

attributes.name: asset_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent creative asset for which this version was created.

attributes.name: creative_deliverable_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.creative_deliverable. Business justification: Asset versions represent iterative development states of deliverables. Production tracking requires linking each version to its parent deliverable to manage revision history, track which version was a

attributes.name: deliverable_tracker_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.deliverable_tracker. Business justification: Specific asset versions are submitted as deliverables and tracked in deliverable_tracker (which stores file_reference and version_number as plain text). A proper FK normalizes this and enables version

attributes.name: production_job_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.production_job. Business justification: Each asset version is produced within the context of a specific production job — revisions, re-renders, and format adaptations are all tracked as versions tied to the job that commissioned them. Addin

attributes.name: spec_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.creative_spec. Business justification: Each asset version should track which creative specification it conforms to. As assets are revised through the version history, they may be adjusted to meet different specs or updated spec versions. T

attributes.name: task_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.task. Business justification: In advertising production, each asset version is created during a specific revision or QA task. Linking asset_version to task enables task-level version history reporting and effort-to-version traceab

attributes.name: adobe_cc_document_version_ref
attributes.business_glossary_term: Adobe Creative Cloud (CC) Document Version Identifier (ID)
attributes.description: External version identifier from Adobe Creative Cloud system. Enables traceability to source Adobe application (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, Premiere Pro).

attributes.name: approval_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this version received formal approval. Null if not yet approved.

attributes.name: approved_by_name
attributes.description: Name of the individual who approved this version for promotion or final delivery. Null if not yet approved.

attributes.name: archive_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this version was archived. Null if not yet archived.

attributes.name: brand_compliance_status
attributes.description: Indicates whether this version adheres to brand guidelines and standards. Used for brand governance and quality control.

attributes.name: change_summary
attributes.description: Brief description of changes made in this version compared to the previous iteration. Captures rationale for the revision.

attributes.name: checksum_hash
attributes.description: Cryptographic hash (e.g., MD5, SHA-256) of the file for integrity verification and duplicate detection.

attributes.name: color_profile
attributes.description: Color space or profile used in this version (e.g., sRGB, Adobe RGB, CMYK). Critical for print and digital color accuracy.

attributes.name: compliance_check_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when brand compliance review was last performed on this version. Null if not yet reviewed.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this version record was first created in the system. Audit trail for version creation.

attributes.name: duration_seconds
attributes.description: Duration of video or audio creative in seconds. Null for static assets.

attributes.name: file_format
attributes.description: File format or extension of this version (e.g., PSD, AI, INDD, PDF, JPG, PNG, MP4, MOV). Indicates the Adobe Creative Cloud or export format. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: psd|ai|indd|pdf|jpg|png|mp4|mov|gif — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Interactive Advertising Bureau (IAB) Creative Format Guidelines

attributes.name: file_name
attributes.description: Original file name of this version as stored in the system.

attributes.name: file_path
attributes.description: Storage location or URI reference to the physical file for this version in the Digital Asset Management (DAM) system or cloud storage.

attributes.name: file_size_bytes
attributes.description: Size of the version file in bytes. Used for storage management and delivery optimization.

attributes.name: height_pixels
attributes.description: Height dimension of the creative asset in pixels. Applicable to image and video formats.
attributes.references: Interactive Advertising Bureau (IAB) Display Ad Guidelines

attributes.name: is_archived
attributes.description: Indicates whether this version has been archived and moved to long-term storage. True if archived, False if active.

attributes.name: is_promoted
attributes.description: Indicates whether this version has been promoted to production or final delivery status. True if promoted, False otherwise.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this version record was last modified. Audit trail for version updates.

attributes.name: promotion_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this version was promoted to production or approved for final delivery. Null if not yet promoted.

attributes.name: rejection_reason
attributes.description: Explanation or rationale for rejection if this version was not approved. Null if approved or pending.

attributes.name: resolution_dpi
attributes.description: Resolution of the asset in dots per inch. Typically 72 for digital, 300+ for print. Null for video assets.

attributes.name: version_label
attributes.description: Human-readable version label or tag (e.g., v1, v2_client_feedback, v3_final, draft_A). Used for identification and communication.

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Sequential numeric version number indicating the iteration sequence (1, 2, 3, etc.).

attributes.name: version_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of this version. Tracks progression through creative approval workflow.

attributes.name: width_pixels
attributes.description: Width dimension of the creative asset in pixels. Applicable to image and video formats.
attributes.references: Interactive Advertising Bureau (IAB) Display Ad Guidelines

attributes.name: workfront_proof_version_ref
attributes.description: External proof version identifier from Workfront proofing system. Links to approval and feedback workflow.

products.name: concept
products.description: Represents a creative concept or big idea developed in response to a creative brief. Captures concept title, creative territory, strategic rationale, mood board references, headline, tagline, visual direction, and approval status. A single brief may spawn multiple competing concepts; this entity tracks each concept through internal review and client presentation stages. Integrates with Workfront creative project milestones.

attributes.name: concept_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the creative concept. Primary key.

attributes.name: brand_id

attributes.name: milestone_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.project_milestone. Business justification: Concept presentations and approvals are formal project milestones in advertising (e.g., 'Concept Approval Gate'). Linking concept to project_milestone enables milestone-based concept approval tracking

attributes.name: parent_concept_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent concept if this is a revision or variation. Enables tracking of concept evolution and iteration history.

attributes.name: project_brief_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.project_brief. Business justification: Concept development is directly scoped by the project brief in advertising. Agencies track which concepts respond to which project brief for brief-compliance review and concept evaluation. concept→cre

attributes.description: The date when the concept received final approval to proceed to production. Marks the transition from concept to execution phase.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Specific approval state indicating whether the concept has been approved by internal stakeholders and/or client. Tracks formal sign-off milestones. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_submitted|pending_internal|pending_client|approved_internal|approved_client|rejected|conditional — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: brand_compliance_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the concept has been reviewed and confirmed to comply with brand guidelines and standards. True if compliant, false if non-compliant or not yet reviewed.

attributes.name: channel_applicability
attributes.description: Description of which media channels and platforms this concept is designed for or can be adapted to (e.g., TV, digital, OOH, social, print).

attributes.name: competitive_differentiation
attributes.description: Description of how this concept differentiates the brand from competitors. Explains the unique positioning and competitive advantage.

attributes.name: concept_code
attributes.description: Unique business identifier or code assigned to the concept for tracking and reference across systems. Often used in project management and DAM systems.

attributes.name: concept_description
attributes.description: Detailed narrative description of the creative concept. Explains the big idea, execution approach, and how the concept will come to life across touchpoints.

attributes.name: concept_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the concept. Tracks progression through internal review, client presentation, and approval stages. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_review|pending_approval|approved|rejected|on_hold|archived — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this concept record was first created in the system. Marks the beginning of the concept development lifecycle.

attributes.name: creative_territory
attributes.description: The overarching creative theme, space, or territory that this concept occupies. Defines the broad strategic positioning and thematic direction.

attributes.name: estimated_production_cost
attributes.description: Estimated cost to produce and execute this concept if approved. Used for budget planning and concept selection decisions.

attributes.name: headline
attributes.description: The primary headline or main message of the concept. The key communication element that captures attention and conveys the core idea.

attributes.name: key_message

attributes.name: legal_review_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether this concept requires legal review before approval. True if legal clearance is needed, false otherwise.

attributes.name: legal_review_status

attributes.name: modified_timestamp

attributes.name: mood_board_reference

attributes.name: presentation_date

attributes.name: production_complexity

attributes.name: rejection_reason

attributes.name: strategic_rationale
attributes.description: The strategic reasoning and business justification for this concept. Explains how the concept addresses the brief objectives and target audience insights.

attributes.name: tagline
attributes.description: The tagline or supporting message that reinforces the concept. Often used as a memorable phrase that encapsulates the brand promise or campaign theme.

attributes.name: target_audience

attributes.name: version
attributes.description: Version number of this concept iteration. Increments when significant revisions are made based on feedback.

attributes.name: visual_direction
attributes.description: Description of the visual style, aesthetic, and design direction for the concept. Includes guidance on imagery, color palette, typography, and overall look and feel.

products.name: production_job
products.description: Operational record representing a discrete creative production job — the unit of work assigned to an internal studio team, freelancer, or external production vendor to produce one or more creative deliverables. Tracks job type (print, digital, video, audio, OOH, DOOH, CTV, social, experiential), job status (briefed, in-production, internal-review, QA, client-review, delivered), assigned studio or production house, estimated and actual production hours, cost estimate vs actual cost, delivery deadline, and priority level. Links to creative brief requirements and spawns creative assets upon completion. Sourced from Workfront project tasks and integrates with SAP PS for cost tracking.

products.name: proof
products.description: Captures each formal proofing round submitted for client or internal review via Workfront Proofing. Records proof type (concept, copy, layout, final art, legal, accessibility), proof stage (internal, client round 1/2/3, final), reviewer assignments, due date, submission timestamp, overall proof decision (approved, approved-with-changes, rejected), and decision timestamp. Distinct from asset_version in that a proof is a formal review event with stakeholder decisions and SLA tracking, not merely a file iteration. Supports agency SLA compliance reporting on review turnaround times.

attributes.name: turnaround_time_hours
attributes.description: Calculated elapsed time in hours between submission_timestamp and decision_timestamp. Key metric for agency SLA compliance reporting on review cycle speed.

attributes.name: url

Versioned to track spec changes as platforms update requirements (e.g., Meta quarterly spec updates, IAB new format releases). Acts as the specification template against which assets are validated for format compliance before trafficking. Maintained by the creative technology team with platform update monitoring.

products.name: brand_guideline
products.description: Reference master for client brand guidelines governing creative production — including approved color palettes (hex, Pantone, CMYK, RGB), typography (typefaces, weights, sizes), logo usage rules, imagery style, tone of voice, do/don't examples, and brand compliance thresholds. Serves as the SSOT for brand compliance validation during creative review and production QA. Scope boundary: this entity owns the operational production ruleset (visual specs, typography rules, logo placement constraints) used by creative teams in day-to-day asset production. It does NOT own brand strategy, positioning, architecture, or competitive intelligence — those belong to the brand domain. Sourced from client brand portals and maintained by the creative services team.

domains.name: audience
domains.description: SSOT for audience definition, segmentation, and targeting data used across campaigns. Manages first-party, second-party, and third-party audience segments, personas, behavioral profiles, demographic attributes, psychographic insights, DMP/CDP integrations, and privacy-compliant consent records (GDPR, CCPA). Integrates with Nielsen, Comscore, and DSP audience tools.
domains.references: IAB Audience Taxonomy / NAI Code of Conduct

products.name: segment
products.description: Master record and SSOT for all audience segments — first-party, second-party, and third-party — used across campaigns. Captures segment name, type (behavioral, demographic, psychographic, contextual, lookalike), data source (1P/2P/3P), segment size, creation method (rules-based, ML-modeled, imported), activation status, refresh cadence, expiry date, IAB taxonomy classification, and owning team. Embeds full qualification criteria spanning demographic attributes (age band, gender, income tier, DMA, education, marital/parental status, language), behavioral signals (browsing categories, purchase history, RFM scores, app usage, cross-device activity, search intent, content consumption), and psychographic dimensions (VALS, brand affinity, lifestyle segments, interest taxonomies, purchase motivations, personality indicators, media attitudes). Qualification criteria are sourced from Nielsen, Comscore, first-party CRM, DMP/CDP integrations, survey data, and social listening. SSOT for all audience segment definitions, targeting rules, and profile-level qualification logic in the advertising platform.

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the audience segment. Primary key.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Audience segment data acquisition and maintenance costs are allocated to cost centers for internal P&L reporting and chargeback. Advertising agencies track which department owns each segment. 'owning_

attributes.name: data_supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Audience segments are frequently sourced from or enriched by third-party data suppliers. Supplier FK on audience_segment enables data sourcing cost attribution, vendor performance evaluation for data

attributes.name: master_agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Audience segments often governed by master agreements defining data usage rights, licensing terms, geographic restrictions, and activation permissions. Critical for compliance tracking, data rights ma

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Campaign-specific custom segments are frequently defined as SOW deliverables with specific build criteria, acceptance thresholds, and delivery timelines. Essential for scope validation, deliverable tr

attributes.name: taxonomy_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.audience_taxonomy. Business justification: Segments should be formally classified using the standardized audience taxonomy (aligned to IAB Audience Taxonomy per audience_taxonomy product description). The segment table currently has 'iab_taxon

attributes.name: activation_status
attributes.description: Indicates whether the segment has been pushed to DSPs, ad servers, or activation platforms for live campaign use.

attributes.name: age_band_max
attributes.description: Upper bound of the age range for demographic qualification (e.g., 24 for 18-24 age band). Nullable if age is not a qualification criterion.

attributes.name: age_band_min
attributes.description: Lower bound of the age range for demographic qualification (e.g., 18 for 18-24 age band). Nullable if age is not a qualification criterion.

attributes.name: app_usage_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether segment qualification includes mobile app usage or in-app behavior signals.

attributes.name: audience_segment_description
attributes.description: Detailed business description of the segment purpose, target audience characteristics, and intended use cases for campaign targeting.

attributes.name: audience_segment_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the audience segment used for identification and campaign targeting.

attributes.name: audience_segment_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the segment: active (available for targeting), inactive (paused), draft (under development), archived (historical reference), or expired (past validity period).

attributes.name: audience_segment_type
attributes.description: Classification of the segment based on the primary targeting methodology: behavioral (actions/browsing), demographic (age/gender/income), psychographic (attitudes/values), contextual (content affinity), lookalike (modeled similarity), or geographic (location-based).

attributes.name: brand_affinity_list
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of brands that segment members have shown affinity toward through engagement, purchase, or social signals. Nullable if brand affinity is not a qualification criterion.

attributes.name: browsing_categories
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of IAB content categories or site categories that segment members have browsed (e.g., IAB1 for Automotive, IAB3 for Business). Nullable if browsing behavior is not a qualification criterion.

attributes.name: ccpa_compliant_flag
attributes.business_glossary_term: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) Compliant Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the segment meets CCPA requirements for consumer opt-out rights and data sale restrictions.

attributes.name: consent_status
attributes.description: Privacy consent status for segment members under GDPR, CCPA, or other privacy regulations: consented (explicit opt-in), not_consented (no consent), pending (consent requested), or withdrawn (consent revoked).

attributes.name: content_consumption_topics
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of content topics or themes consumed by segment members (e.g., 'sports', 'finance', 'travel'). Nullable if content affinity is not a qualification criterion.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the segment record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: creation_method
attributes.description: Methodology used to build the segment: rules-based (Boolean logic), ML-modeled (algorithmic clustering/propensity), imported (from external platform), survey-derived (research panel), or manual (curated list).

attributes.name: cross_device_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether segment uses cross-device identity resolution to track users across desktop, mobile, tablet, and CTV.

attributes.name: data_source_tier
attributes.description: Origin tier of the segment data: first-party (owned CRM/web/app data), second-party (partner-shared data), or third-party (purchased from data providers).
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Data Transparency Standard

attributes.name: dma_codes
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of Nielsen DMA codes (e.g., 501 for New York) used for geographic targeting. Nullable if geography is not a qualification criterion.

attributes.name: education_level
attributes.description: Highest education attainment level used for demographic qualification (e.g., high school, bachelor, graduate). Nullable if education is not a qualification criterion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: less_than_high_school|high_school|some_college|associate|bachelor|graduate|postgraduate — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date when the segment becomes available for targeting and activation.

attributes.name: expiry_date
attributes.description: Date when the segment is no longer valid for targeting, either due to data staleness, consent expiration, or campaign end. Nullable for evergreen segments.

attributes.name: external_code
attributes.description: External identifier or code used by DSPs, DMPs, CDPs, and third-party platforms to reference this segment.

attributes.name: gdpr_compliant_flag
attributes.business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Compliant Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the segment meets GDPR requirements for lawful processing, consent, and data subject rights.

attributes.name: gender
attributes.description: Gender demographic filter applied to segment qualification. Nullable if gender is not a qualification criterion.

attributes.name: income_tier
attributes.description: Household income bracket or tier used for demographic qualification (e.g., <50K, 50K-100K, 100K+). Nullable if income is not a qualification criterion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: <25K|25K-50K|50K-75K|75K-100K|100K-150K|150K-250K|250K+ — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: interest_taxonomy
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of interest categories or hobbies (e.g., 'fitness', 'gaming', 'cooking') used for psychographic qualification. Nullable if interests are not a qualification criterion.

attributes.name: language_preference
attributes.description: Primary language preference for segment members (ISO 639-1 codes: EN, ES, FR, etc.). Nullable if language is not a qualification criterion.

attributes.name: last_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the segment record was last modified, including membership recalculation or metadata updates.

attributes.name: lifestyle_segment
attributes.description: Lifestyle or life-stage classification (e.g., 'young professionals', 'empty nesters', 'urban millennials') used for psychographic qualification. Nullable if lifestyle is not a qualification criterion.

attributes.name: marital_status
attributes.description: Marital status demographic filter applied to segment qualification. Nullable if marital status is not a qualification criterion.

attributes.name: parental_status
attributes.description: Indicates whether segment targets parents, non-parents, or all. Nullable if parental status is not a qualification criterion.

attributes.name: purchase_history_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether segment qualification includes past purchase behavior from CRM or e-commerce data.

attributes.name: refresh_cadence
attributes.description: Frequency at which segment membership is recalculated and updated: real-time (streaming), hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, or static (one-time build).

attributes.name: rfm_score
attributes.description: RFM segmentation score (e.g., 555 for best customers, 111 for low-value) used for behavioral qualification. Nullable if RFM is not a qualification criterion.

attributes.name: search_intent_keywords
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of search keywords or intent signals used for behavioral qualification (e.g., 'luxury cars', 'mortgage refinance'). Nullable if search intent is not a qualification criterion.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: Total count of unique users, devices, or households currently qualifying for this segment.

attributes.name: vals_segment
attributes.description: VALS psychographic segmentation classification (e.g., Innovators, Thinkers, Achievers, Experiencers) used for psychographic qualification. Nullable if VALS is not a qualification criterion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: innovators|thinkers|achievers|experiencers|believers|strivers|makers|survivors — promote to reference product]

products.name: persona
products.description: Synthesized audience persona profiles representing archetypal target audience groups for campaign planning and creative strategy. Captures persona name, narrative description, demographic summary, psychographic traits, media consumption habits, purchase intent signals, pain points, motivations, and associated brand affinity scores. Used by account teams and strategists to align creative briefs and media plans to defined audience archetypes.

Sourced from Nielsen, Comscore, and first-party CRM data. Serves as the demographic dimension for segment qualification and targeting criteria.

attributes.description: Number of individuals in the household. Used for household composition analysis and targeting.

attributes.name: marital_status
attributes.description: Marital status classification for the demographic profile. Used for household-based targeting and lifestyle segmentation.

attributes.name: occupation_category
attributes.description: Broad occupational category (e.g., 'Professional', 'Management', 'Service', 'Sales', 'Technical'). Used for B2B and professional targeting.
attributes.references: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics Standard Occupational Classification (SOC)

attributes.name: parental_status
attributes.description: Indicates whether the individual is a parent. Used for family-oriented campaign targeting.
attributes.references: Nielsen Demographic Standards; IAB Audience Taxonomy

attributes.name: postal_code
attributes.description: Postal code (ZIP code in U.S.) for geographic targeting and localization. Used for hyper-local campaign targeting.

attributes.name: primary_language
attributes.description: Primary language spoken by the demographic segment (e.g., 'English', 'Spanish', 'Mandarin'). Used for creative localization and media channel selection.

attributes.name: profile_code

attributes.name: profile_confidence_score

attributes.references: IAB Data Transparency Standards; MRC Audience Measurement Standards

attributes.name: profile_status

attributes.name: psychographic_segment

attributes.references: Nielsen PRIZM Segmentation; Comscore Behavioral Segments

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this demographic profile record was last modified. Used for audit and data lineage.

attributes.description: Classification of residential area type. Used for lifestyle-based targeting and media channel selection.
