products.name: behavioral_profile
products.description: Behavioral audience profile capturing observed digital and offline behaviors — including browsing categories, purchase history signals, app usage patterns, content consumption categories, search intent signals, recency/frequency/monetary (RFM) scores, and cross-device activity. Sourced from DMP/CDP integrations, The Trade Desk audience data, and first-party behavioral signals. Used for behavioral targeting and lookalike modeling.

attributes.name: behavioral_profile_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the behavioral audience profile record. Primary key.

attributes.name: primary_behavioral_audience_segment_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent audience segment this behavioral profile belongs to.

attributes.name: primary_taxonomy_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.audience_taxonomy. Business justification: behavioral_profile has browsing_category_primary and browsing_category_secondary (strings). audience_taxonomy provides standardized audience classification. Adding primary_taxonomy_id (FK to audience_

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Behavioral profiles are built from data sourced from third-party data suppliers (DMPs, data brokers). data_source_description is a denormalization signal. Supplier FK enables data lineage tracking, GD

attributes.name: tertiary_behavioral_lookalike_seed_segment_audience_segment_id
attributes.description: Reference to the seed audience segment used to generate this lookalike behavioral profile, if applicable.

attributes.name: app_usage_pattern
attributes.description: Description of observed mobile app usage patterns (e.g., 'Gaming Apps', 'Shopping Apps', 'News Apps', 'Social Media Apps').

attributes.name: brand_affinity_category
attributes.description: Category of brands or products the user has shown affinity toward based on observed behavior (e.g., 'Luxury Brands', 'Eco-Friendly Products', 'Tech Gadgets').

attributes.name: browsing_category_primary
attributes.description: The primary content category observed in the user's browsing behavior (e.g., 'Automotive', 'Travel', 'Finance', 'Health & Wellness').

attributes.name: browsing_category_secondary
attributes.description: The secondary content category observed in the user's browsing behavior, indicating additional interests.

attributes.name: consent_status
attributes.description: Privacy consent status for behavioral data collection and usage, ensuring compliance with GDPR, CCPA, and other privacy regulations.

attributes.name: consent_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when consent was granted or last updated for this behavioral profile.

attributes.name: content_consumption_category
attributes.description: Category of content consumed by the user (e.g., 'Video', 'Articles', 'Podcasts', 'Social Media').

attributes.name: cross_device_activity_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the behavioral profile includes cross-device activity tracking and identity resolution.

attributes.name: data_retention_expiry_date
attributes.description: Date when this behavioral profile data must be purged or anonymized per privacy regulations and data retention policies.

attributes.name: day_of_week_preference
attributes.description: Observed preference for day of week when the user is most active online.

attributes.name: device_type_primary
attributes.description: The primary device type observed in the user's digital behavior.

attributes.name: engagement_level
attributes.description: Overall engagement level classification based on interaction frequency, depth, and duration.

attributes.name: frequency_score
attributes.description: Frequency component of RFM scoring model, indicating how often the user exhibits the behavior (scale 1-5, 5 being most frequent).

attributes.name: geographic_behavior_pattern
attributes.description: Description of observed geographic or location-based behavior patterns (e.g., 'Urban Commuter', 'Frequent Traveler', 'Suburban Resident').

attributes.name: last_observed_behavior_date
attributes.description: Date when the most recent behavioral signal was observed for this profile.

attributes.name: lookalike_similarity_score
attributes.description: Similarity score (0.00 to 100.00) indicating how closely this profile matches the seed segment for lookalike modeling.

attributes.name: monetary_score
attributes.description: Monetary component of RFM scoring model, indicating the value associated with the user's behavior (scale 1-5, 5 being highest value).

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or comments about this behavioral profile for internal reference and documentation.

attributes.name: privacy_jurisdiction
attributes.description: Primary privacy jurisdiction applicable to this behavioral profile (e.g., 'EU', 'California', 'Global').

attributes.name: profile_creation_date
attributes.description: Date when this behavioral profile was first created in the system.

attributes.name: profile_last_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this behavioral profile was last updated with new behavioral signals or attributes.

attributes.name: profile_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name or label for this behavioral profile (e.g., 'High-Intent Auto Shoppers', 'Frequent Travelers').

attributes.name: profile_quality_score
attributes.description: Quality score (0.00 to 100.00) indicating the accuracy, completeness, and reliability of the behavioral profile data.

attributes.name: profile_reach_estimate
attributes.description: Estimated number of unique users or devices matching this behavioral profile, used for campaign planning and media buying.

attributes.name: profile_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the behavioral profile indicating availability for targeting.

attributes.name: profile_type
attributes.description: Classification of the behavioral profile based on data source and construction method.

attributes.name: purchase_intent_signal
attributes.description: Derived signal indicating the likelihood of purchase behavior based on observed actions (e.g., cart additions, product page views, price comparisons).

attributes.name: recency_score
attributes.description: Recency component of RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) scoring model, indicating how recently the user exhibited the behavior (scale 1-5, 5 being most recent).

attributes.name: search_intent_keywords
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of observed search keywords or phrases indicating user intent and interests.

attributes.name: seasonal_behavior_pattern
attributes.description: Description of observed seasonal or cyclical behavior patterns (e.g., 'Holiday Shopper', 'Back-to-School', 'Summer Traveler').

attributes.name: targeting_eligibility_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this behavioral profile is currently eligible for use in campaign targeting based on quality, consent, and compliance checks.

attributes.name: time_of_day_preference
attributes.description: Observed preference for time of day when the user is most active online.

products.name: segment_membership
products.description: Association record linking individual audience identifiers (cookie IDs, device IDs, hashed emails, MAIDs) to specific audience segments. Captures membership start date, expiry date, membership source, confidence score, inclusion/exclusion flag, and last refresh timestamp. Enables real-time segment population tracking and audience size reporting across DMP, CDP, and DSP platforms including The Trade Desk.

attributes.name: segment_membership_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the segment membership record. Primary key for the segment membership association.

attributes.name: budget_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.budget_line. Business justification: Segment membership has cost_per_member indicating data costs that must be tracked against a budget line. Audience data spend management requires linking membership costs to approved budget lines for v

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Segment membership records carry cost_per_member and currency_code, indicating per-member data costs. Cost center attribution is required for internal billing and data cost allocation reporting across

attributes.name: initiative_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.initiative. Business justification: Segment membership records are generated during initiative execution (e.g., audience onboarding initiative, data partnership initiative). Linking membership to the originating initiative enables attri

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific audience segment that this identifier belongs to. Links to the audience segment definition in the DMP or CDP.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Segment membership data is sourced from third-party data suppliers. Media planners need supplier attribution per membership record for data cost reconciliation, quality audits, and GDPR data processor

attributes.name: tech_partner_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.tech_partner. Business justification: Segment memberships are activated via DSP/tech platforms. dsp_platform is a plain-text denormalization of the tech partner. Linking to tech_partner enables platform fee reconciliation, activation trou

attributes.name: activation_status
attributes.description: The status of this segment membership in the DSP or activation platform. Activated indicates the membership is live and available for targeting. Pending indicates activation is in progress. Failed indicates activation encountered an error. Deactivated indicates the membership has been removed from the activation platform.

attributes.name: activation_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this segment membership was successfully activated in the DSP or targeting platform. Used for activation latency analysis and real-time audience availability reporting.

attributes.name: audience_identifier
attributes.description: The individual audience identifier (cookie ID, device ID, hashed email, MAID, or other pseudonymous identifier) that is a member of the segment. This is the core identifier used for targeting and activation across DSP, DMP, and CDP platforms.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy; GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.140

attributes.name: confidence_score
attributes.description: A numeric score between 0 and 1 representing the confidence or probability that the audience identifier truly belongs to this segment. Higher scores indicate stronger membership signals. Used for quality filtering and bid optimization in programmatic campaigns.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy; MRC Audience Measurement Standards

attributes.name: cost_per_member
attributes.description: The cost paid to acquire or license this specific segment membership from a third-party data provider, expressed in the campaign currency. Null for first-party segments. Used for data cost attribution and ROI analysis.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy; TAG Data Transparency Standards

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this segment membership record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and membership lifecycle tracking.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost per member amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used for multi-currency data cost reporting and financial reconciliation.
attributes.references: ISO 4217; IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy

attributes.name: device_type
attributes.description: The type of device associated with this audience identifier. Desktop indicates traditional computer. Mobile indicates smartphone. Tablet indicates tablet device. CTV indicates Connected TV. OTT indicates Over-The-Top streaming device. Other indicates unclassified device. Used for device-specific targeting and cross-device attribution.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy; MRC Cross-Device Measurement Standards

attributes.name: frequency_cap
attributes.description: The maximum number of times this audience identifier should be exposed to ads from campaigns targeting this segment within a defined time period. Used for frequency management and preventing ad fatigue. Nullable if no cap is applied.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy; MRC Viewable Impression Guidelines

attributes.name: geographic_region
attributes.description: The geographic region or market associated with this segment membership, if the segment has geographic targeting criteria. Used for geo-targeted campaign planning and regional audience analysis.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy; ISO 3166-1

attributes.name: identifier_type
attributes.description: The type of audience identifier used for this membership record. Indicates whether the identifier is a cookie ID, mobile advertising ID (MAID), device ID, hashed email, customer ID, or household ID.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy; TAG Inventory Quality Guidelines

attributes.name: inclusion_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this is an inclusion (true) or exclusion (false) membership. Inclusion memberships are targeted in campaigns; exclusion memberships are suppressed. Supports negative targeting and suppression list management.

attributes.name: last_refresh_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this segment membership record was last refreshed or re-evaluated. Critical for real-time segment population tracking and ensuring audience data freshness for DSP activation.

attributes.name: match_rate
attributes.description: The rate at which this audience identifier successfully matched to the segment criteria, expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1. Used for data quality assessment and segment match performance analysis across DMP and CDP integrations.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy; TAG Data Transparency Standards

attributes.name: membership_expiry_date
attributes.description: The date when the segment membership expires or is scheduled to expire. Nullable for evergreen segments. Used for time-bound segment management and automated exclusion workflows.

attributes.name: membership_source
attributes.description: The origin or derivation method of the segment membership. First-party indicates data collected directly by the advertiser. Second-party indicates data shared by a partner. Third-party indicates data purchased from a data provider. Modeled indicates membership derived from predictive modeling. Lookalike indicates membership based on similarity to a seed audience. Behavioral indicates membership based on observed actions. Contextual indicates membership based on content consumption. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: first_party|second_party|third_party|modeled|lookalike|behavioral|contextual — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy; TAG Data Transparency Standards

attributes.name: membership_start_date
attributes.description: The date when the audience identifier first became a member of this segment. Used for recency analysis and segment freshness reporting.

attributes.name: membership_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the segment membership. Active indicates the identifier is currently in the segment and eligible for targeting. Expired indicates membership has lapsed. Excluded indicates the identifier has been explicitly removed. Pending indicates membership is being processed. Suppressed indicates the identifier is blocked from targeting due to consent or compliance rules.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy; GDPR Article 21; CCPA Section 1798.120

attributes.name: recency_days
attributes.description: The number of days since the audience identifier last exhibited the behavior or attribute that qualifies them for this segment. Used for recency-based targeting strategies and segment decay analysis.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy; MRC Audience Measurement Standards

attributes.name: segment_priority
attributes.description: A numeric priority ranking for this segment membership when the audience identifier belongs to multiple overlapping segments. Higher values indicate higher priority. Used for bid decisioning and creative selection when multiple segments apply.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this segment membership record was last updated or modified. Used for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.

products.name: activation
products.description: Operational record of an audience segment being activated (pushed) to a specific DSP, SSP, or media platform for campaign targeting. Captures activation date, target platform (The Trade Desk, DV360, Meta, etc.), segment ID, activation status (pending/active/expired/paused), estimated reach, match rate, activation method (pixel, API, S2S), and associated campaign reference. Tracks the operational lifecycle of audience deployment.

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.lookalike_model. Business justification: When an audience activation pushes a lookalike-generated segment to a DSP or SSP, the activation record should reference the lookalike_model that produced the expanded audience. This enables performan

attributes.description: Date when the audience segment was activated and pushed to the target platform.

attributes.name: activation_method
attributes.description: Technical method used to push the audience segment to the target platform. API = Application Programming Interface, S2S = Server-to-Server integration.

attributes.name: notes

attributes.name: priority_level

attributes.name: retry_count

attributes.name: sync_frequency

attributes.name: unmatched_user_count
attributes.description: Number of users or devices from the segment that could not be matched by the target platform.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this audience activation record was last modified.

products.name: lookalike_model
products.description: Operational record of a lookalike audience model built from a seed segment — capturing seed segment reference, model algorithm type (cosine similarity, gradient boosting, neural net), expansion ratio, similarity threshold, model training date, model version, platform (The Trade Desk, DV360), estimated reach, validation accuracy, and model status. Enables scale expansion of high-value audience segments for prospecting campaigns.

attributes.name: lookalike_model_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the lookalike audience model record. Primary key for the lookalike model entity.

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertiser or client account that owns this lookalike model for campaign use and billing purposes.

attributes.name: brand_id

attributes.description: Optional reference to a specific campaign for which this lookalike model was created, enabling campaign-specific audience modeling strategies.

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_insertion_order. Business justification: Lookalike models are commissioned and billed under specific contract IOs in programmatic advertising. Linking to contract_insertion_order enables cost attribution, IO-level model performance reporting

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Lookalike models carry model_cost and currency_code attributes. Cost center attribution for model development and licensing costs is required for internal P&L reporting and chargeback in agencies that

attributes.name: development_initiative_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.initiative. Business justification: Lookalike model development is a distinct project deliverable with dedicated timelines, resources, and budgets. Agencies track which initiative funded model creation for cost recovery and to manage da

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Reference to the source audience segment used as the foundation for building the lookalike model. The seed segment contains high-value users whose characteristics are modeled.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Lookalike models are frequently built by third-party data science platforms or DSPs (e.g., Facebook, Trade Desk, LiveRamp). Tracking the supplier is essential for cost allocation, model performance at

attributes.name: taxonomy_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.taxonomy. Business justification: A lookalike model is built to expand a seed audience into a broader audience within a specific audience category. The taxonomy_id FK classifies the lookalike model under the standardized IAB Audience

attributes.name: tech_partner_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.tech_partner. Business justification: Lookalike models are built and hosted on specific DSP/ML tech platforms. platform_name is a plain-text denormalization of the tech partner. FK enables model governance, platform fee tracking, and plat

attributes.name: algorithm_type
attributes.description: Machine learning algorithm used to build the lookalike model. Determines the mathematical approach for identifying similar audience members. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cosine_similarity|gradient_boosting|neural_network|random_forest|logistic_regression|collaborative_filtering|k_nearest_neighbors|decision_tree|support_vector_machine — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: baseline_performance_value
attributes.description: Benchmark performance metric value from the seed segment, used to compare lookalike audience performance against the original high-value segment.

attributes.name: consent_compliant_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the lookalike model was built using only users who provided explicit consent for data usage under GDPR, CCPA, or other privacy regulations.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 6; CCPA Section 1798.100; IAB TCF v2.0

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the lookalike model record was first created in the system. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).
attributes.references: ISO 8601; IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the model cost and related financial metrics.

attributes.name: data_source_type
attributes.description: Classification of the data used to build the seed segment and train the model. First-party is owned data; second-party is partner data; third-party is purchased data.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy; GDPR Article 4

attributes.name: estimated_reach
attributes.description: Projected number of unique users or devices in the lookalike audience available for targeting across campaigns.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy; MRC Audience Measurement Standards

attributes.name: expansion_ratio
attributes.description: Multiplier indicating how many times larger the lookalike audience is compared to the seed segment (e.g., 10.00 means the lookalike is 10x the seed size). Used to balance reach vs. similarity.

attributes.name: expiration_date
attributes.description: Date when the lookalike model is scheduled to expire and become unavailable for targeting unless refreshed or extended.

attributes.name: feature_set
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of data attributes used as input features for model training (e.g., demographics, behavioral signals, purchase history, engagement metrics).

attributes.name: geographic_scope
attributes.description: Geographic boundary for the lookalike audience (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR, global). Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or 'global' for worldwide reach.
attributes.references: ISO 3166-1; IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy

attributes.name: last_refresh_date
attributes.description: Date when the lookalike model was most recently retrained or refreshed with updated seed segment data.

attributes.name: model_cost
attributes.description: Total cost incurred to build and deploy the lookalike model, including data acquisition, platform fees, and computational resources.

attributes.name: model_description
attributes.description: Detailed business description of the lookalike model purpose, target use cases, and strategic rationale for campaign planners and media buyers.

attributes.name: model_name
attributes.description: Business-friendly name assigned to the lookalike model for identification and campaign planning purposes.

attributes.name: model_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the lookalike model. Active models are available for campaign targeting; expired models require retraining.

attributes.name: model_version
attributes.description: Version identifier for the lookalike model, enabling tracking of model iterations and A/B testing across versions.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the lookalike model configuration or metadata. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).
attributes.references: ISO 8601; IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy

attributes.name: next_refresh_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next model retraining cycle based on the refresh frequency policy.

attributes.name: performance_metric_type
attributes.description: Primary Key Performance Indicator (KPI) used to evaluate the lookalike model effectiveness in campaigns. CTR = Click-Through Rate, CVR = Conversion Rate, ROAS = Return on Ad Spend, CPA = Cost Per Acquisition, LTV = Lifetime Value.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy; MRC Audience Measurement Standards

attributes.name: refresh_frequency_days
attributes.description: Number of days between scheduled model retraining cycles to maintain accuracy and relevance as audience behaviors evolve.

attributes.name: similarity_threshold
attributes.description: Minimum similarity score (0.0000 to 1.0000) required for a user to be included in the lookalike audience. Higher thresholds produce smaller, more similar audiences.

attributes.name: training_date
attributes.description: Date when the lookalike model was trained on the seed segment data. Critical for understanding model freshness and performance decay over time.

attributes.name: validation_accuracy
attributes.description: Model accuracy score (0.0000 to 1.0000) measured on a holdout validation dataset, indicating how well the model predicts high-value user characteristics.

products.name: suppression_list
products.description: Audience suppression list record defining identifiers to be excluded from campaign targeting — including converted customers, opted-out users, competitor employees, brand safety exclusions, and frequency-capped audiences. Captures list name, suppression type, identifier type (hashed email, cookie, MAID), list size, source system, creation date, expiry date, and associated campaign or advertiser scope. Critical for privacy compliance and media efficiency.

attributes.name: suppression_list_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the suppression list record. Primary key.

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the advertiser to which this suppression list is scoped. Nullable if the list is shared across multiple advertisers.

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Suppression lists are brand-specific in multi-brand advertisers — Brand A's opted-out customers must not suppress Brand B campaigns. Brand-level suppression management is a standard privacy compliance

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the specific campaign to which this suppression list is applied. Nullable if the list is applied at advertiser or account level.

attributes.name: contract_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_insertion_order. Business justification: Suppression lists are applied at the IO level in media buying — the IO specifies which suppression lists govern a given buy to prevent serving ads to opted-out or excluded audiences. This link enables

attributes.name: creation_initiative_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.initiative. Business justification: Suppression list creation and maintenance is often a project task within compliance or campaign setup initiatives. Agencies track which project funded list development for billing and to manage data o

attributes.name: data_supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: data_provider on suppression_list is a plain-text denormalization of the vendor supplier. Suppression list data is sourced from specific vendors (opt-out providers, CRM data vendors). FK enables vendo

attributes.name: enforcement_tech_partner_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.tech_partner. Business justification: Suppression lists are synced to and enforced by specific DSP/tech platforms. platform_integration_status and platform_sync_timestamp on suppression_list confirm a platform relationship exists. Complia

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.flight. Business justification: Suppression lists are applied at the flight level to exclude opted-out users or existing customers from specific flight executions. Media planners need flight-level suppression control for granular au

attributes.name: governing_agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Suppression obligations are frequently contractually mandated (competitor exclusions, brand safety requirements, regulatory compliance). Critical for compliance verification, audit trails, and ensurin

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Suppression lists are often derived from segments (e.g., 'suppress all users who converted' or 'suppress all users in segment X'). Adding source_segment_id tracks which segment the suppression list wa

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Approval workflow status for the suppression list (approved, pending, rejected). Lists must be approved before activation in campaigns.

attributes.name: approval_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the suppression list was approved for use in campaigns.

attributes.name: archive_date
attributes.description: Date when the suppression list was archived and removed from active campaign targeting systems.

attributes.name: consent_withdrawal_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the suppression list contains identifiers for users who have withdrawn marketing consent (True/False).

attributes.name: data_cost
attributes.description: Cost incurred to acquire or license the suppression list data from a third-party provider, in US Dollars (USD).
attributes.references: Association of National Advertisers (ANA) Financial Standards

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date when the suppression list expires and is no longer applied to campaign targeting. Nullable for indefinite suppression lists.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date from which the suppression list becomes active and is applied to campaign targeting.

attributes.name: frequency_cap_period
attributes.description: Time period over which the frequency cap threshold is measured (hour, day, week, month, campaign lifetime).

attributes.name: frequency_cap_threshold
attributes.description: Maximum number of impressions or interactions allowed before an identifier is added to the suppression list. Applicable for frequency-capped suppression types.

attributes.name: hashing_algorithm
attributes.description: Cryptographic hashing algorithm applied to identifiers for privacy protection (SHA256, MD5, SHA1, none if identifiers are not hashed).
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy; Network Advertising Initiative (NAI) Code

attributes.name: identifier_type
attributes.description: Type of identifier stored in the suppression list (hashed email, cookie ID, Mobile Advertising ID (MAID), device ID, customer ID, IP address). Determines matching strategy in Demand-Side Platforms (DSPs) and Supply-Side Platforms (SSPs).
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy; Network Advertising Initiative (NAI) Code

attributes.name: last_refresh_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent data refresh or sync operation for this suppression list.

attributes.name: last_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the suppression list record or its identifier membership.

attributes.name: list_code
attributes.description: Unique business code or external identifier for the suppression list used in campaign systems and Data Management Platforms (DMPs).

attributes.name: list_name
attributes.description: Business-friendly name of the suppression list (e.g., 'Q4 Converted Customers', 'GDPR Opt-Out Master', 'Competitor Employees').

attributes.name: list_size
attributes.description: Total count of unique identifiers in the suppression list. Used for reach estimation and media planning.

attributes.name: list_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the suppression list (active, inactive, expired, pending approval, archived). Only active lists are applied to campaign targeting.

attributes.name: match_rate_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of identifiers in the suppression list that successfully matched against campaign targeting pools in Demand-Side Platforms (DSPs) or Data Management Platforms (DMPs). Used to assess list quality and reach impact.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-form notes or comments about the suppression list for internal reference, including special handling instructions or business context.

attributes.name: platform_integration_status
attributes.description: Status of the suppression list synchronization with downstream advertising platforms such as Demand-Side Platforms (DSPs), Supply-Side Platforms (SSPs), or ad servers (synced, pending sync, sync failed, not applicable).

attributes.name: platform_sync_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent successful synchronization of the suppression list to downstream advertising platforms.

attributes.name: privacy_compliance_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the suppression list was created to satisfy privacy regulations such as General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) or California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) opt-out requests (True/False).

attributes.name: refresh_frequency
attributes.description: Cadence at which the suppression list is refreshed or re-synced from the source system (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, on-demand, real-time).

attributes.name: retention_period_days
attributes.description: Number of days the suppression list and its identifiers are retained before automatic archival or deletion, per data retention policies and privacy regulations.

attributes.name: scope_level
attributes.description: Organizational level at which the suppression list is applied (global across all advertisers, advertiser-specific, campaign-specific, line-item-specific).

attributes.name: source_file_name
attributes.description: Name of the file or data feed from which the suppression list was ingested, if applicable. Supports audit and lineage tracking.

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: Originating system or platform that generated the suppression list (e.g., 'Salesforce CRM', 'The Trade Desk', 'Adobe Audience Manager', 'Google Campaign Manager 360', 'Internal CRM').

attributes.name: suppression_list_description
attributes.description: Detailed business description of the suppression list purpose, use case, and any special handling instructions.

attributes.name: suppression_type
attributes.description: Category of suppression defining why identifiers are excluded from targeting (converted customer, opted-out user, frequency-capped audience, competitor employee, brand safety exclusion, fraud prevention).
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy; GDPR Article 6; CCPA Section 1798.120

attributes.name: creation_date
attributes.description: Date when the suppression list was first created in the system.

products.name: taxonomy
products.description: Reference taxonomy for standardized audience classification categories — aligned to IAB Audience Taxonomy v1.1 and custom agency extensions. Captures taxonomy node ID, parent node, category name, category level (tier 1/2/3), IAB standard code, description, applicable data types (behavioral/demographic/contextual), and active status. Provides the controlled vocabulary for consistent segment classification across campaigns, platforms, and reporting.

attributes.name: taxonomy_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the audience taxonomy node. Primary key for the audience taxonomy reference structure.

attributes.name: parent_taxonomy_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent node in the taxonomy hierarchy. Null for top-level (tier 1) categories. Enables hierarchical navigation and roll-up reporting.

attributes.name: primary_replacement_taxonomy_id
attributes.description: Reference to the taxonomy node that replaces this deprecated category. Null for active categories or deprecated categories without direct replacement. Enables automated migration of historical segments.

attributes.name: applicable_platforms
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of digital platforms and channels where this taxonomy category is applicable (e.g., display, video, social, search, CTV, DOOH). Guides media planners on appropriate channel selection.

attributes.name: average_cpm_usd
attributes.business_glossary_term: Average Cost Per Mille (CPM) United States Dollars (USD)
attributes.description: Average cost per thousand impressions in US dollars for media buys targeting this audience category. Based on historical campaign data and current market rates. Used for budget planning and cost estimation.
attributes.references: IAB Audience Taxonomy v1.1; Media Planning Standards

attributes.name: category_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the audience category, including defining characteristics, typical behaviors, and use cases. Provides guidance for media planners and data analysts on appropriate segment application.

attributes.name: category_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the audience taxonomy category. Used in campaign planning interfaces, reporting dashboards, and client presentations.

attributes.name: cdp_integration_enabled
attributes.business_glossary_term: Customer Data Platform (CDP) Integration Enabled Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this taxonomy category is integrated with Customer Data Platform for unified customer profile enrichment and personalization. True enables real-time audience orchestration across owned channels.
attributes.references: IAB Audience Taxonomy v1.1; CDP Integration Standards

attributes.name: comscore_compatible
attributes.description: Indicates whether this taxonomy category can be mapped to Comscore digital audience measurement segments for cross-platform analytics and validation. True enables independent third-party audience verification.
attributes.references: Comscore Audience Measurement Standards; Media Rating Council (MRC) Standards

attributes.name: consent_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether explicit user consent is required under GDPR, CCPA, or other privacy regulations before using this audience category for targeting. True requires documented consent; false indicates legitimate interest or non-regulated data.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 6; GDPR Article 7; CCPA Section 1798.120

attributes.name: cpm_last_updated_date
attributes.business_glossary_term: Cost Per Mille (CPM) Last Updated Date
attributes.description: Date when the average CPM value was last refreshed from campaign performance data and market intelligence. Indicates pricing data freshness.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this taxonomy record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: data_source_type
attributes.description: Classification of the data source origin for this audience category. First-party data is owned by the advertiser, second-party is partner-shared, third-party is purchased from data providers. Deterministic uses known identifiers; probabilistic uses inferred signals.
attributes.references: IAB Audience Taxonomy v1.1; GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.140

attributes.name: data_type
attributes.description: Primary data classification type for this audience category. Indicates the source and nature of the audience signal used for segmentation and targeting.
attributes.references: IAB Audience Taxonomy v1.1; Data Management Platform (DMP) Standards

attributes.name: deprecation_reason
attributes.description: Explanation for why this taxonomy category was deprecated or retired. Includes migration guidance to replacement categories. Null for active categories.

attributes.name: dmp_integration_enabled
attributes.business_glossary_term: Data Management Platform (DMP) Integration Enabled Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this taxonomy category is integrated with the agency Data Management Platform for audience activation and cross-platform targeting. True enables automated segment sync to DSPs and SSPs.
attributes.references: IAB Audience Taxonomy v1.1; DMP Integration Standards

attributes.name: dsp_activation_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether this taxonomy category is available for programmatic activation via Demand-Side Platforms such as The Trade Desk. True enables real-time bidding and automated audience targeting in programmatic campaigns.
attributes.references: IAB Audience Taxonomy v1.1; The Trade Desk Audience Activation Standards

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date when this taxonomy category was deprecated or retired. Null for currently active categories. Supports historical campaign analysis and taxonomy lifecycle management.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date when this taxonomy category became available for use. Supports historical analysis and version control of taxonomy evolution.

attributes.name: estimated_reach
attributes.description: Estimated total addressable audience size (unique users or devices) for this taxonomy category across all integrated platforms. Updated periodically based on DMP and CDP data. Used for media planning and feasibility assessment.
attributes.references: IAB Audience Taxonomy v1.1; Nielsen Audience Measurement Standards

attributes.name: exclusion_categories
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of taxonomy codes that should be excluded when this category is used, to prevent audience overlap or conflicting targeting logic. Enforces mutually exclusive segment rules.

attributes.name: iab_standard_code
attributes.description: Official IAB Audience Taxonomy standard code for this category. Null for custom agency extensions not covered by IAB standard. Enables industry-standard audience classification and cross-publisher comparison.

attributes.name: is_active
attributes.description: Indicates whether this taxonomy category is currently active and available for use in campaign planning and targeting. False indicates deprecated or sunset categories that should not be used in new campaigns.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this taxonomy record was last updated. Supports change tracking and data quality monitoring.

attributes.name: last_reviewed_date
attributes.description: Date when this taxonomy category was last reviewed for accuracy, relevance, and compliance. Supports taxonomy governance and quality assurance processes.

attributes.name: minimum_segment_size
attributes.description: Minimum number of unique users or devices required for this audience category to be activated in campaigns. Enforces privacy thresholds and prevents re-identification risk. Typically set to 1000+ for GDPR compliance and platform minimums.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 25; IAB Audience Taxonomy v1.1; Platform Minimum Segment Size Requirements

attributes.name: nielsen_compatible
attributes.description: Indicates whether this taxonomy category can be mapped to Nielsen audience measurement segments for GRP and TRP calculation. True enables cross-platform reach and frequency planning using Nielsen Ad Intel data.
attributes.references: Nielsen Ad Intel Audience Measurement Standards; Media Rating Council (MRC) Standards

attributes.name: path
attributes.description: Full hierarchical path from root to this node, using forward-slash delimiter (e.g., Demographics/Age/18-24). Enables breadcrumb navigation and full-path filtering in reporting tools.

attributes.name: privacy_classification
attributes.description: Privacy sensitivity level of the audience data in this category. Non-PII contains no personal identifiers, pseudonymous uses hashed or tokenized IDs, PII-based includes direct identifiers, sensitive includes special category data under GDPR Article 9.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4; GDPR Article 9; CCPA Section 1798.140

attributes.name: reach_last_updated_date
attributes.description: Date when the estimated reach value was last refreshed from source systems. Indicates data freshness for media planning decisions.

attributes.name: taxonomy_code
attributes.description: Unique alphanumeric code identifying this taxonomy node. Used for system integration and cross-platform mapping. Follows agency naming convention.

attributes.name: taxonomy_level
attributes.description: Hierarchical tier level of this taxonomy node. Tier 1 represents top-level categories, tier 2 represents sub-categories, tier 3 represents granular segments. Supports drill-down analysis and hierarchical reporting.

attributes.name: usage_guidelines
attributes.description: Best practice guidance and restrictions for using this audience category in campaigns. Includes recommended use cases, exclusion scenarios, and compliance considerations. Supports media planners and account teams in appropriate segment application.
attributes.references: IAB Audience Taxonomy v1.1; Agency Audience Strategy Guidelines

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Version identifier for this taxonomy node, following semantic versioning convention (major.minor.patch). Tracks taxonomy evolution and enables version-specific reporting and analysis.
attributes.references: IAB Audience Taxonomy v1.1; Semantic Versioning Standards

domains.name: contract
domains.description: SSOT for all commercial agreements between the agency and its clients or media vendors — including MSAs, SOWs, RFP/RFI responses, IOs, and vendor contracts. Manages terms, pricing models, deliverables, SLAs, renewal cycles, scope, and contract compliance. Supports Proposal Development and Pitching and Budget Management processes.

products.name: agreement
products.description: Master record for all commercial agreements between the agency and its clients or media vendors — including MSAs (Master Service Agreements), SOWs (Statements of Work), IOs (Insertion Orders), vendor media supply agreements, technology licenses, and production services contracts. Captures contract type (client or vendor), counterparty reference, status, effective and expiry dates, governing law, jurisdiction, auto-renewal flags, notice periods, total committed value, and the owning account team. Serves as the SSOT for all contractual relationships — both client-facing and supply-side — and is the anchor entity for the contract domain.

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the commercial agreement. Primary key for the agreement entity.

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the client advertiser party to this agreement.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Master service agreements are budgeted and tracked against specific cost centers for P&L management, overhead allocation, and profitability analysis in agency operations.

attributes.name: parent_agreement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent or master agreement if this is a child agreement (e.g., SOW under an MSA, Amendment to an IO). Nullable for top-level agreements.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Master Service Agreements in advertising are negotiated with specific vendors/suppliers. Linking agreement.supplier_id enables vendor contract portfolio management, renewal tracking, and compliance re

attributes.name: agreement_number
attributes.description: Externally-known business identifier for the agreement, used in client communications and invoicing.

attributes.name: agreement_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the agreement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Draft|Pending Approval|Active|Suspended|Terminated|Expired|Renewed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: agreement_type
attributes.description: Classification of the commercial agreement. MSA = Master Service Agreement, SOW = Statement of Work, IO = Insertion Order, RFP = Request for Proposal, RFI = Request for Information. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MSA|SOW|IO|RFP Response|RFI Response|Vendor Media Supply|Technology License|Production Services|Amendment — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date when internal approval was granted to execute this agreement.

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the internal authority who approved this agreement.

attributes.name: auto_renewal_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the agreement automatically renews at expiry unless terminated.

attributes.name: compliance_review_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent compliance or legal review of this agreement.

attributes.name: confidentiality_clause_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the agreement includes a confidentiality or non-disclosure provision.

attributes.name: contract_owner
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the individual responsible for ongoing management and compliance of this agreement.

attributes.name: counterparty_type
attributes.description: Indicates whether the agreement is with a client (revenue-generating), vendor (cost-generating), or strategic partner.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this agreement record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this agreement.

attributes.name: deliverables_summary
attributes.description: High-level summary of key deliverables, milestones, or outputs expected under this agreement.

attributes.name: dispute_resolution_method
attributes.description: Agreed mechanism for resolving disputes arising from this agreement.

attributes.name: document_storage_url
attributes.description: URL or file path to the signed agreement document in the Digital Asset Management (DAM) or document management system.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date when the agreement becomes legally binding and enforceable.

attributes.name: exclusivity_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the agreement grants exclusive rights to the agency or client within a defined scope or territory.

attributes.name: expiry_date
attributes.description: Date when the agreement terminates. Nullable for open-ended agreements.

attributes.name: governing_law
attributes.description: Legal jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of this agreement (e.g., State of New York, England and Wales).

attributes.name: indemnity_clause_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the agreement includes an indemnity provision protecting one or both parties from third-party claims.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this agreement record was last updated in the system.

attributes.name: liability_cap_amount
attributes.description: Maximum liability amount either party can be held responsible for under this agreement. Nullable if no cap applies.

attributes.name: next_review_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next periodic review or renewal discussion of this agreement.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional comments, special terms, or internal notes about this agreement.

attributes.name: payment_terms_days
attributes.description: Number of days from invoice date within which payment is due (e.g., Net 30, Net 60).

attributes.name: performance_bonus_clause_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the agreement includes performance-based bonus or incentive provisions.

attributes.name: pricing_model
attributes.description: Commercial pricing structure for this agreement. CPM = Cost Per Mille, CPC = Cost Per Click, CPA = Cost Per Acquisition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Fixed Fee|Time and Materials|Retainer|Commission|CPM|CPC|CPA|Hybrid — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: renewal_notice_period_days
attributes.description: Number of days advance notice required to prevent auto-renewal or to initiate renewal negotiation.

attributes.name: scope_of_work_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the services, deliverables, and work products covered by this agreement.

attributes.name: signed_date
attributes.description: Date when all parties executed the agreement.

attributes.name: sla_included_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the agreement includes defined Service Level Agreements with performance targets and penalties.

attributes.name: termination_date
attributes.description: Actual date when the agreement was terminated, if applicable. Nullable for active agreements.

attributes.name: termination_notice_period_days
attributes.description: Number of days advance notice required for either party to terminate the agreement.

attributes.name: termination_reason
attributes.description: Explanation or business reason for early termination of the agreement, if applicable.

attributes.name: total_committed_value
attributes.description: Total monetary value committed under this agreement over its full term. For client agreements, represents expected revenue; for vendor agreements, represents committed spend.

products.name: sow
products.description: Statement of Work entity capturing the detailed scope, deliverables, milestones, timelines, and pricing for a specific client engagement under a parent MSA or standalone agreement. Tracks SOW number, version, approval status, total value, start and end dates, billing model (fixed-fee, T&M, retainer), and the responsible account manager. Supports Proposal Development and Pitching and Budget Management processes.

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the Statement of Work record. Primary key.

attributes.name: agency_relationship_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.agency_relationship. Business justification: SOWs are executed under a specific agency-client AOR relationship governing commission rates, scope, and approval workflows. Linking SOW to agency_relationship enables relationship-level SOW tracking

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent Master Service Agreement under which this SOW is executed. Null if this is a standalone SOW without a parent MSA.

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: SOWs in advertising are scoped to a specific brand — media budgets, deliverables, and fee structures are brand-specific. Brand-level SOW reporting and budget reconciliation require this link. A domain

attributes.name: contract_rate_card_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_card. Business justification: A Statement of Work may reference a specific rate card that governs the pricing for its deliverables and services. While the rate card is linked to the parent agreement, different SOWs under the same

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: SOWs are executed within cost center budgets; finance tracks SOW spend by cost center for profitability analysis and resource allocation decisions.

attributes.name: sow_advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific advertiser entity under the client organization. May differ from client_id in holding company structures.

attributes.name: sow_client_advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the client or advertiser for whom this SOW is executed. Links to the client master record.

attributes.name: agency_fee_amount
attributes.description: Total agency service fees for the SOW, excluding media costs and third-party pass-throughs. Represents the agency's revenue from this engagement.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date when the SOW was formally approved by the client and agency, making it legally binding.

attributes.name: auto_renewal_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the SOW automatically renews unless either party provides notice of termination. True if auto-renewal clause exists.

attributes.name: billing_model
attributes.description: Pricing structure for the SOW. Determines how the client is invoiced (fixed price, hourly rates, monthly retainer, media commission, performance incentives, or combination).

attributes.name: confidentiality_clause
attributes.description: Text of the confidentiality and non-disclosure provisions governing how both parties handle sensitive information shared during the engagement.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this SOW record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this SOW (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

attributes.name: deliverables_summary
attributes.description: High-level summary of key deliverables expected under this SOW (e.g., '3 TV spots, 10 digital banners, monthly performance reports').

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date when the SOW expires or the engagement concludes. Null for open-ended retainer agreements.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date when the SOW becomes effective and work may commence. Marks the beginning of the engagement period.

attributes.name: intellectual_property_ownership
attributes.description: Specifies who owns the intellectual property rights to work created under this SOW (client owns all, agency retains rights, shared ownership, or work-for-hire arrangement).

attributes.name: invoicing_frequency
attributes.description: Cadence at which the client will be invoiced under this SOW. Aligns with billing model and cash flow requirements.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this SOW record was last updated. Audit trail for record changes.

attributes.name: media_budget_amount
attributes.description: Total media spend budget allocated under this SOW for media buying and placement. Excludes agency fees and production costs.

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: Contractual payment terms specifying when and how invoices are to be paid (e.g., 'Net 30', 'Net 60', '50% upfront, 50% on completion').

attributes.name: performance_bonus_amount
attributes.description: Maximum potential performance bonus amount payable to the agency if all performance criteria are met. Null if no performance incentives exist.

attributes.name: performance_bonus_criteria
attributes.description: Description of performance metrics and thresholds that trigger bonus payments to the agency (e.g., 'ROAS exceeds 5:1', 'Campaign CTR above 2%').

attributes.name: production_budget_amount
attributes.description: Total budget allocated for creative production, including video, photography, design, and other content creation costs.

attributes.name: renewal_notice_days
attributes.description: Number of days advance notice required to terminate or opt out of renewal. Null if no renewal option exists.

attributes.name: renewal_option_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the SOW includes an option for renewal or extension beyond the initial term. True if renewal clause exists.

attributes.name: scope_of_work
attributes.description: Comprehensive description of all services, deliverables, and activities included in the SOW. Defines what is in-scope and out-of-scope.

attributes.name: sla_deliverable_turnaround_days
attributes.business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Deliverable Turnaround Days
attributes.description: Standard number of business days committed for deliverable turnaround under normal circumstances. Part of service level commitments.

attributes.name: sla_response_time_hours
attributes.business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Response Time Hours
attributes.description: Maximum number of hours the agency commits to respond to client requests or issues under this SOW. Part of service level commitments.

attributes.name: sow_description
attributes.description: Detailed narrative description of the SOW scope, objectives, and high-level deliverables. Provides context for the engagement.

attributes.name: sow_number
attributes.description: Business-facing unique identifier for the SOW, typically formatted as SOW-XXXXXX. Used in client communications, invoicing, and contract references.

attributes.name: sow_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the SOW. Tracks progression from draft through approval, execution, and closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|completed|cancelled|on_hold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: sow_type
attributes.description: Classification of the SOW by engagement type. Determines billing model, resource allocation, and deliverable expectations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: project|retainer|campaign|media_buy|creative_services|consulting|production|research|strategy — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: termination_clause
attributes.description: Text of the termination provisions, specifying conditions under which either party may terminate the SOW and associated notice periods or penalties.

attributes.name: title
attributes.description: Descriptive title of the SOW, summarizing the engagement or campaign scope (e.g., 'Q1 2024 Brand Awareness Campaign', 'Digital Media Planning Services').

attributes.name: total_contract_value
attributes.description: Total monetary value of the SOW across its entire duration, including all fees, media spend, and pass-through costs. Expressed in the contract currency.

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Version identifier for the SOW document, typically in major.minor format (e.g., 1.0, 2.1). Incremented with each amendment or revision.

products.name: contract_insertion_order
products.description: Formal IO (Insertion Order) record authorizing media spend with a specific publisher or vendor for a defined campaign flight. Captures IO number, media vendor reference, campaign reference, total authorized spend, flight start and end dates, placement specifications, rate type (CPM, CPC, CPA, flat fee), and IO status (draft, pending signature, executed, paused, cancelled). The authoritative transactional record linking media buying commitments to contractual obligations under a parent agreement. Supports media cost reconciliation and vendor payment authorization workflows.

attributes.name: contract_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the insertion order record. Primary key for the insertion order entity.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Simplified Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the client advertiser on whose behalf this media spend is authorized.

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.media_insertion_order. Linking enables reconciliation between contract commitments and media executi

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the publisher or media vendor with whom this insertion order establishes a media buying commitment.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Simplified Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: agency_commission_rate
attributes.description: The percentage commission rate earned by the agency on media spend under this insertion order, typically ranging from 0% to 20%.

attributes.name: billing_contact_email
attributes.description: Email address of the billing contact for invoice delivery and payment-related communications.

attributes.name: billing_contact_name

attributes.description: The method by which media inventory is purchased under this insertion order. RTB (Real-Time Bidding) indicates auction-based programmatic buying.

attributes.name: cancellation_policy
attributes.description: Terms and conditions governing cancellation or early termination of the insertion order, including notice periods and financial penalties.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Simplified Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: committed_clicks
attributes.description: The total number of clicks committed or guaranteed under this insertion order for CPC-based campaigns. Null for non-click-based rate types.

attributes.name: committed_conversions
attributes.description: The total number of conversions or acquisitions committed under this insertion order for CPA-based campaigns. Null for non-conversion-based rate types.

attributes.name: committed_impressions
attributes.description: The total number of ad impressions committed or guaranteed by the media vendor under this insertion order. Null for non-guaranteed or performance-based deals.

attributes.name: created_by_user

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this insertion order record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this insertion order.

attributes.name: flight_end_date

attributes.name: flight_start_date
attributes.description: The date when the media campaign flight begins and ad serving is authorized to commence under this insertion order.

attributes.name: io_approved_date
attributes.description: The date when the insertion order was approved by the media vendor, authorizing campaign setup and trafficking.

attributes.name: io_name
attributes.description: Human-readable descriptive name for the insertion order, typically including campaign reference and media vendor for easy identification.

attributes.name: io_number
attributes.description: The externally-known unique business identifier for this insertion order, used in communications with media vendors and internal tracking systems.

attributes.name: io_signed_date
attributes.description: The date when the insertion order was executed and signed by all required parties, establishing the binding commitment.

attributes.name: io_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the insertion order in the approval and execution workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_signature|executed|active|paused|cancelled|completed|expired — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: io_submitted_date
attributes.description: The date when the insertion order was submitted to the media vendor for review and approval.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Simplified Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: io_type
attributes.description: Classification of the insertion order based on the media buying model and inventory commitment type.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Simplified Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: make_good_policy
attributes.description: Terms governing make-good provisions when the media vendor fails to deliver committed impressions or performance metrics, including remediation options.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Simplified Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: media_channel
attributes.description: The primary media channel or platform type for this insertion order. OOH (Out-of-Home), DOOH (Digital Out-of-Home), OTT (Over-the-Top), CTV (Connected TV). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: display|video|mobile|social|search|native|audio|OOH|DOOH|OTT|CTV|email — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Digital Simplified Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: modified_by_user
attributes.description: Identifier of the user who last modified this insertion order record, supporting change accountability and audit compliance.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Simplified Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this insertion order record was last modified, supporting change tracking and audit compliance.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Simplified Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-form notes capturing additional terms, special instructions, or contextual information relevant to this insertion order.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Simplified Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: The agreed payment terms specifying when payment is due after invoice receipt (e.g., Net 30 means payment due within 30 days).
attributes.references: IAB Digital Simplified Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: placement_specifications
attributes.description: Detailed specifications for ad placements including ad formats, sizes, positions, targeting criteria, and creative requirements as agreed with the media vendor.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Simplified Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: rate_amount
attributes.description: The unit rate or price associated with the rate type (e.g., $5.00 CPM, $0.50 CPC). Precision supports fractional cent pricing common in programmatic media.

attributes.name: rate_type
attributes.description: The pricing model used for this insertion order. CPM (Cost Per Mille), CPC (Cost Per Click), CPA (Cost Per Acquisition), CPV (Cost Per View), CPCV (Cost Per Completed View), or other billing structure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CPM|CPC|CPA|CPV|CPCV|flat_fee|cost_plus|percentage_of_spend — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Digital Simplified Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: third_party_ad_serving_fee
attributes.description: Additional fee charged for third-party ad serving technology, tracking, and verification services associated with this insertion order.

attributes.name: total_authorized_spend
attributes.description: The total budget amount authorized for media spend under this insertion order, representing the maximum financial commitment.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Simplified Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: trafficking_contact_email
