attributes.name: trafficking_contact_name
attributes.description: Name of the primary trafficking contact responsible for campaign setup, creative delivery, and ad serving coordination.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Simplified Insertion Order Standards

products.name: term
products.description: Granular terms and conditions clauses attached to an agreement or SOW — including payment terms (net-30, net-60), IP ownership clauses, confidentiality obligations, indemnification provisions, termination-for-convenience rights, and audit rights. Each term record captures clause type, clause text, effective date, negotiated flag, and approval status. Enables clause-level compliance tracking and legal review workflows.

attributes.name: term_id
attributes.description: Primary key for term

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent contract or agreement to which this term belongs.

attributes.name: contract_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_insertion_order. Business justification: Terms and conditions clauses can be attached to specific Insertion Orders (e.g., IO-specific cancellation terms, make-good provisions, trafficking requirements). While term already links to agreement

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Terms and conditions can be specific to a SOW (not just agreement-level). N:1 relationship (many terms can belong to one SOW). No reverse FK exists. This allows SOW-specific terms to be tracked separa

attributes.name: amendment_count
attributes.description: Number of times this term clause has been amended or modified since the original contract execution.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Current approval state of the term clause within the contract review and approval workflow (e.g., draft, pending legal review, pending finance review, pending executive approval, approved, rejected, superseded). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_legal_review|pending_finance_review|pending_executive_approval|approved|rejected|superseded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the individual or role who provided final approval for this term clause.

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: Date when the term clause received final approval and was authorized for inclusion in the executed contract.

attributes.name: audit_frequency
attributes.description: Specified frequency or trigger for audit rights when audit_rights_flag is True (e.g., annual, semi-annual, quarterly, upon request).

attributes.name: audit_rights_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this term grants audit rights to either party (True) or does not include audit provisions (False).

attributes.name: auto_renewal_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this term includes automatic renewal provisions (True) or requires explicit renewal action (False).

attributes.name: clause_category
attributes.description: High-level categorization of the clause into legal, commercial, operational, compliance, financial, or technical domains for organizational and reporting purposes.

attributes.name: clause_summary
attributes.description: Plain-language summary of the clause for business users, highlighting key obligations, rights, and implications.

attributes.name: clause_text
attributes.description: Full legal text of the term clause as negotiated and executed. Contains the complete wording of the contractual provision.
attributes.references: American Association of Advertising Agencies (4As) Contract Standards

attributes.name: clause_title
attributes.description: Short descriptive title or heading of the term clause as it appears in the contract document.

attributes.name: clause_type
attributes.description: Classification of the term clause by its legal or commercial function (e.g., payment terms, IP ownership, confidentiality, indemnification, termination rights, audit rights, liability limitation, warranty, SLA, scope, deliverable, pricing, renewal). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: payment|intellectual_property|confidentiality|indemnification|termination|audit_rights|liability_limitation|warranty|service_level|scope_of_work|deliverable|pricing|renewal — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: American Association of Advertising Agencies (4As) Contract Standards

attributes.name: compliance_framework
attributes.description: Name of the regulatory or compliance framework that governs this term (e.g., GDPR, CCPA, IAB Standards, MRC Guidelines, TAG Certification) when compliance_requirement_flag is True.
attributes.references: Interactive Advertising Bureau (IAB), General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA)

attributes.name: compliance_requirement_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this term clause is subject to regulatory compliance monitoring and reporting requirements (True) or is a standard commercial term (False).
attributes.references: Federal Trade Commission (FTC) - Advertising Practices Division

attributes.name: confidentiality_duration_years
attributes.description: Number of years that confidentiality obligations remain in effect after contract termination when the clause type is confidentiality-related.
attributes.references: American Association of Advertising Agencies (4As) Contract Standards

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this term record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date when this specific term clause becomes binding and enforceable within the contract lifecycle.

attributes.name: expiration_date
attributes.description: Date when this term clause ceases to be in effect, if applicable. Null for terms that remain in force for the duration of the contract.

attributes.name: indemnification_scope
attributes.description: Description of the scope and limitations of indemnification obligations when the clause type is indemnification-related.
attributes.references: American Association of Advertising Agencies (4As) Contract Standards

attributes.name: intellectual_property_ownership
attributes.description: Specifies the ownership model for intellectual property created under the contract when the clause type is IP-related (e.g., client owns, agency owns, joint ownership, work-for-hire, licensed).
attributes.references: American Association of Advertising Agencies (4As) Contract Standards

attributes.name: last_amended_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent amendment to this term clause.

attributes.name: liability_cap_amount
attributes.description: Maximum liability amount specified in the term clause when the clause type is liability limitation, expressed in the contract currency.
attributes.references: American Association of Advertising Agencies (4As) Contract Standards

attributes.name: liability_cap_currency
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the liability cap amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this term record was last modified in the system.

attributes.name: negotiated_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this term was negotiated and modified from the standard template (True) or accepted as-is from the standard contract language (False).

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional notes, comments, or context regarding the term clause, including negotiation history, special considerations, or implementation guidance.

attributes.name: payment_method
attributes.description: Specified payment instrument or mechanism for financial settlement (e.g., wire transfer, ACH, check, credit card, EFT) when applicable to the term.
attributes.references: Association of National Advertisers (ANA) Financial Standards

attributes.name: payment_term_days
attributes.description: Number of days for payment terms (e.g., net-30, net-60, net-90) when the clause type is payment-related.
attributes.references: Association of National Advertisers (ANA) Financial Standards

attributes.name: penalty_provision
attributes.description: Description of financial or contractual penalties for non-compliance with the term, including service level breaches, late payments, or other violations.

attributes.name: renewal_notice_days
attributes.description: Number of days advance notice required to opt out of automatic renewal or to initiate renewal discussions.

attributes.name: risk_level
attributes.description: Assessment of the legal or financial risk associated with this term clause, used for prioritization of legal review and compliance monitoring.

attributes.name: sequence
attributes.description: Ordering or numbering of the term within the parent contract or Statement of Work (SOW), used to maintain clause hierarchy and presentation order.

attributes.name: service_level_metric
attributes.description: Name of the performance metric or Key Performance Indicator (KPI) specified in the service level term (e.g., response time, uptime percentage, delivery timeframe).
attributes.references: Association of National Advertisers (ANA) Service Standards

attributes.name: service_level_target
attributes.description: Target value or threshold for the service level metric (e.g., 99.9% uptime, 24-hour response time, 5-day delivery).
attributes.references: Association of National Advertisers (ANA) Service Standards

attributes.name: standard_clause_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this term is part of the organization's standard contract template (True) or is a custom clause specific to this agreement (False).
attributes.references: American Association of Advertising Agencies (4As) Contract Standards

attributes.name: term_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the term clause, often used for legal reference and cross-referencing within contract documents.

attributes.name: termination_notice_days
attributes.description: Number of days advance notice required for contract termination when the clause type is termination-related (e.g., 30-day notice, 60-day notice).

Captures deliverable name, description, due date, acceptance criteria, responsible team, delivery format, and current status (pending, in-progress, submitted, accepted, rejected). Bridges contract obligations to operational execution tracked in Workfront.

products.name: sla
products.description: Service Level Agreement record defining measurable performance commitments within a client or vendor contract — including creative turnaround times, campaign go-live SLAs, reporting delivery deadlines, platform uptime guarantees, and media trafficking response times. Captures SLA type, metric name, target value, measurement unit, measurement frequency, breach threshold, penalty mechanism (credit, fee reduction, termination right), and remediation terms. Supports contract compliance monitoring, QBR scorecards, and automated breach alerting.

attributes.name: sla_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the service level agreement record.

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent contract or statement of work (SOW) that contains this SLA commitment.

attributes.name: contract_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_insertion_order. Business justification: SLAs in advertising frequently govern specific media buys at the IO level — e.g., impression delivery guarantees, viewability thresholds, brand safety commitments tied to a specific publisher IO. Whil

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: SLA performance is tracked by the cost center responsible for delivery; finance monitors SLA compliance costs and penalty exposure.

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Service Level Agreements can be defined at SOW level with specific performance commitments for that scope of work. N:1 relationship (many SLAs can belong to one SOW). No reverse FK exists. This allows

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: SLAs in advertising are negotiated with specific vendors (ad servers, publishers, DSPs). Linking sla.supplier_id enables vendor SLA breach tracking, penalty enforcement, and supplier performance repor

attributes.name: approval_authority
attributes.description: Role or individual who has authority to approve SLA modifications, waivers, or breach remediation plans.

attributes.name: auto_renewal_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this SLA automatically renews at the end of its term (true) or requires explicit renegotiation (false).

attributes.name: baseline_measurement_period
attributes.description: The time period over which the baseline performance was measured (e.g., 'Q4 2023', 'Last 6 months', 'Prior contract term').

attributes.name: baseline_performance
attributes.description: Historical or benchmark performance level used as a reference point for setting the SLA target, captured at contract negotiation.

attributes.name: compliance_tracking_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether automated compliance monitoring and breach alerting is enabled for this SLA (true) or manual tracking only (false).

attributes.name: created_timestamp

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date when this SLA commitment expires or is superseded, nullable for open-ended commitments.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date when this SLA commitment becomes active and enforceable.

attributes.name: escalation_contact
attributes.description: Name or role of the individual or team to be notified when an SLA breach occurs or is imminent.

attributes.name: exclusions
attributes.description: Circumstances or conditions explicitly excluded from SLA measurement (e.g., force majeure, client-caused delays, third-party platform outages, holiday periods).

attributes.name: last_review_date
attributes.description: The most recent date when this SLA was reviewed during a quarterly business review (QBR) or contract review cycle.

attributes.name: measurement_frequency
attributes.description: How often the SLA performance is measured and reported (per transaction, daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, annual).

attributes.name: measurement_methodology
attributes.description: Detailed description of how the SLA metric is calculated, what data sources are used, and any rounding or aggregation rules applied.

attributes.name: measurement_unit
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the target value (hours, business days, calendar days, percentage, minutes, count).

attributes.name: metric_name

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional context, clarifications, or special conditions related to this SLA that do not fit in structured fields.

attributes.name: penalty_amount
attributes.description: The monetary value or percentage of the penalty applied per breach occurrence, if applicable.

attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the penalty amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

attributes.description: Detailed description of the corrective actions, escalation procedures, and resolution commitments when an SLA breach occurs.

attributes.name: reporting_schedule

attributes.name: sla_code
attributes.description: Business identifier or code for the SLA, often referenced in contract documents and quarterly business reviews (QBRs).

attributes.name: sla_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the SLA commitment (e.g., 'Creative Turnaround Time', 'Campaign Go-Live SLA', 'Reporting Delivery Deadline').

attributes.name: sla_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the SLA (active, suspended, expired, terminated, draft, under review).

attributes.name: sla_type
attributes.description: Category of SLA defining the nature of the performance commitment (creative turnaround, campaign go-live, reporting delivery, platform uptime, trafficking response, client approval).

attributes.name: target_value
attributes.description: The committed performance target value that must be met or exceeded (e.g., 24 hours, 99.9 percent, 2 business days).

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: User ID or name of the individual who created this SLA record.

products.name: contract_rate_card
products.description: Negotiated pricing schedule attached to a client agreement or vendor contract — defining unit rates for agency services (hourly FTE rates by role, production cost rates, media commission percentages) or vendor media inventory pricing (CPM floors, CPC rates, flat placement fees). Captures rate card version, effective date range, currency, rate type, and approval status. The SSOT for contractual pricing used in billing reconciliation.

attributes.name: contract_rate_card_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the contract rate card. Primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: ad_channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.ad_channel. Business justification: Rate cards are channel-scoped (e.g., a digital rate card vs. broadcast rate card). Linking contract_rate_card to ad_channel enables channel-specific rate card lookup during media planning and buying.

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.advertiser. Business justification: Rate cards in advertising are negotiated per advertiser. Direct advertiser-level rate card linkage enables advertiser pricing governance, rate card audits, and pricing reports without joining through

attributes.name: agency_relationship_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.agency_relationship. Business justification: Rate cards are negotiated as part of the agency-client relationship and govern fees across all work under that relationship. Linking rate card to agency_relationship enables relationship-level pricing

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent contract or agreement to which this rate card is attached. Links to the master contract entity.

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Brand-specific rate cards are common in advertising — different brands under the same advertiser may have distinct negotiated rates. Brand-level rate card management and pricing reports require this d

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Rate cards define billing rates that vary by cost center (creative vs. strategy teams have different overhead structures); finance uses this for margin calculation.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Contract rate cards represent pricing negotiated with a specific vendor. Linking contract_rate_card.supplier_id enables vendor rate management, rate compliance auditing, and spend analysis by vendor —

attributes.name: vendor_rate_card_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_rate_card. Business justification: Negotiated contract rate cards are derived from vendor-published rate cards. This link enables rate negotiation tracking — comparing contracted rates against vendor's published rates — a standard proc

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the person or role who approved this rate card for use. Supports approval audit trail.

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: Date when this rate card was formally approved for use. Supports approval audit trail and compliance tracking.

attributes.name: audience_segment
attributes.description: Target audience segment or demographic to which this rate applies, such as 'Adults 18-34', 'High-Income Households', 'B2B Decision Makers'. Supports audience-based pricing.

attributes.name: base_rate
attributes.description: The standard or base rate amount for this rate card entry, before any adjustments, discounts, or surcharges. Precision supports fractional currency units and CPM calculations.

attributes.name: billing_frequency
attributes.description: How often charges under this rate card are invoiced: weekly, biweekly, monthly, quarterly, milestone (upon reaching project milestones), on_completion (at project end), or custom (bespoke schedule). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weekly|biweekly|monthly|quarterly|milestone|on_completion|custom — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: contract_rate_card_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the rate card: draft (being prepared), pending_approval (submitted for review), approved (authorized but not yet effective), active (currently in force), suspended (temporarily inactive), expired (past effective period), superseded (replaced by newer version). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|suspended|expired|superseded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this rate card record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all rates in this rate card (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent currency handling in multi-currency environments.

attributes.name: discount_percentage
attributes.description: Negotiated discount percentage applied to the base rate, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15% discount). Supports volume discounts or client-specific pricing.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date when this rate card expires and is no longer applicable. Nullable for open-ended rate cards.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date when this rate card becomes effective and applicable for billing. Supports time-bound pricing agreements.

attributes.name: geography_scope
attributes.description: Geographic scope or market to which this rate card applies, such as 'USA', 'EMEA', 'APAC', 'New York DMA', 'Global'. Supports geo-specific pricing.

attributes.name: is_default
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this is the default rate card for the associated contract or service line. True if default, False otherwise.

attributes.name: is_negotiable
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether rates in this card are open to negotiation or are fixed. True if negotiable, False if fixed.

attributes.name: markup_percentage
attributes.description: Agency markup or commission percentage applied to vendor costs or media spend, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 20.00 for 20% markup).

attributes.name: maximum_rate
attributes.description: Ceiling rate or maximum charge applicable under this rate card. Used for rate caps or maximum allowable charges.

attributes.name: minimum_rate
attributes.description: Floor rate or minimum charge applicable under this rate card. Used for CPM floors, minimum hourly charges, or minimum project fees.

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: Identifier of the user or system that last modified this rate card record. Supports audit trail and accountability.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this rate card record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional notes, special terms, conditions, or clarifications related to this rate card. Supports business context and exception handling.

attributes.name: payment_terms_days
attributes.description: Number of days from invoice date within which payment is due under this rate card (e.g., 30 for Net 30, 60 for Net 60).

attributes.name: pricing_model
attributes.description: The pricing structure used in this rate card: hourly (time-based), daily, monthly, per_unit (per deliverable), CPM (Cost Per Mille), CPC (Cost Per Click), CPA (Cost Per Acquisition), flat_fee (fixed price), commission_percentage (percentage of media spend), tiered (volume-based pricing), or custom (bespoke arrangement). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hourly|daily|monthly|per_unit|cpm|cpc|cpa|flat_fee|commission_percentage|tiered|custom — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: rate_card_name
attributes.description: Descriptive name of the rate card for business reference, such as 'Q1 2024 Agency Services Rate Card' or 'Premium Media Inventory Pricing'.

attributes.name: rate_card_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the rate card, typically used in invoicing and billing reconciliation. Format: RC- followed by alphanumeric code.

attributes.name: rate_card_type
attributes.description: Classification of the rate card by its purpose: agency_services (hourly FTE rates by role), media_inventory (CPM/CPC rates for ad placements), production (creative production cost rates), vendor_services (third-party vendor pricing), commission (media commission percentages), or hybrid (combination of multiple types).

attributes.name: rate_category
attributes.description: Business classification or grouping for the rates in this card, such as 'Senior Creative Staff', 'Digital Media Placements', 'Video Production Services', or 'Programmatic Inventory'. Supports rate organization and reporting.

attributes.name: role_level
attributes.description: For agency services rate cards, the seniority or role level to which this rate applies, such as 'Junior Designer', 'Senior Account Director', 'Executive Creative Director'. Supports role-based billing.

attributes.name: service_line
attributes.description: The agency service line or practice area covered by this rate card, such as 'Creative Services', 'Media Planning', 'Digital Strategy', 'Production', 'Analytics'. Supports service-based rate organization.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit by which the rate is measured and billed: hour (hourly rate), day (daily rate), month (monthly retainer), impression (per 1000 impressions for CPM), click (per click for CPC), acquisition (per conversion for CPA), unit (per deliverable), project (per project), percentage (commission), or flat (one-time fee). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hour|day|month|impression|click|acquisition|unit|project|percentage|flat — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Version identifier for the rate card, supporting version control and change tracking. Format: v1.0, v2.1, etc.

attributes.name: volume_tier
attributes.description: Volume-based pricing tier identifier for tiered rate cards, such as 'Tier 1: 0-100K impressions', 'Tier 2: 100K-500K impressions'. Supports tiered pricing models.

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: Identifier of the user or system that created this rate card record. Supports audit trail and accountability.

products.name: rate_card_line
products.description: Individual line-item entry within a rate card defining the unit price for a specific service, role, or media placement type. Captures line item code, service or role description, unit of measure (hour, impression, click, placement), unit rate, volume tier thresholds, discount percentage, and effective date range. Enables granular billing calculations and spend reconciliation against contracted rates.

attributes.name: rate_card_line_id
attributes.description: Primary key for rate_card_line

attributes.name: ad_channel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.ad_channel. Business justification: Rate card lines are structured by channel (digital, broadcast, OOH). Linking rate_card_line to ad_channel enables channel-specific rate enforcement during media buying and supports channel-level rate

attributes.name: ad_format_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.ad_format. Business justification: Rate card lines are priced by ad format (e.g., :30 video spot vs. 300x250 display). Linking rate_card_line to ad_format enables format-specific rate validation during media buying and supports rate ca

attributes.name: contract_rate_card_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent rate card document that contains this line item. Links this line to the master rate card agreement.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Approval workflow status for this rate card line. Tracks whether pricing has been reviewed and authorized by appropriate stakeholders.

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the person who approved this rate card line item. Supports audit trail and accountability for pricing decisions.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this rate card line item was approved. Provides temporal audit trail for pricing authorization.

attributes.name: billing_frequency
attributes.description: Cadence at which this line item is invoiced to the client. Aligns billing cycles with contract terms and cash flow management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hourly|daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|milestone|upon_completion — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: cost_center_code
attributes.description: Internal cost center or department code for financial allocation and profit and loss (P&L) tracking. Links rate card revenue to organizational units.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this rate card line record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record inception.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit rate (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Enables multi-currency rate card management.

attributes.name: discount_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage discount applied to the unit rate for this line item. Used for volume discounts, promotional pricing, or negotiated rate reductions.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date when this rate card line item expires or is superseded. Null for open-ended rates. Enables rate change management and historical tracking.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date when this rate card line item becomes active and applicable for billing. Supports rate versioning and temporal pricing.

attributes.name: gl_account_code
attributes.description: General ledger account code for revenue recognition. Maps rate card line items to chart of accounts for financial reporting.

attributes.name: is_retainer_eligible
attributes.description: Indicates whether this line item can be included in retainer-based billing arrangements. Supports hybrid fee structures combining retainer and project-based work.

attributes.name: is_taxable
attributes.description: Indicates whether this line item is subject to sales tax or value-added tax (VAT). Drives tax calculation in billing systems.

attributes.name: line_item_code
attributes.description: Business identifier code for this rate card line item. Used for billing system integration and invoice reconciliation.

attributes.name: line_sequence_number
attributes.description: Sequential ordering of this line item within the parent rate card. Determines display order in proposals and invoices.

attributes.name: line_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of this rate card line item. Controls whether the line is available for use in proposals and billing.

attributes.name: markup_percentage
attributes.description: Agency markup or commission percentage applied on top of base cost for pass-through expenses or media buys. Common in media buying and vendor management.
attributes.references: ANA (Association of National Advertisers) Compensation Standards

attributes.name: maximum_commitment_quantity
attributes.description: Maximum volume or hours available for this line item under the contract terms. Null if no cap applies. Manages scope and resource allocation.

attributes.name: minimum_commitment_quantity
attributes.description: Minimum volume or hours the client must purchase for this line item under the contract terms. Null if no minimum applies. Enforces contractual commitments.

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: Identifier of the user or system that last modified this rate card line record. Supports change tracking and accountability.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this rate card line record was last updated. Enables change detection and temporal analysis.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional context, special terms, or clarifications related to this rate card line item. Supports operational communication and edge case documentation.

attributes.name: overage_rate
attributes.description: Rate charged for usage exceeding the maximum commitment quantity. Typically higher than the standard unit rate. Null if no overage pricing applies.

attributes.name: role_title
attributes.description: Job title or role designation for labor-based rate card lines (e.g., Senior Art Director, Media Planner, Account Executive). Null for non-labor items.

attributes.name: seniority_level
attributes.description: Experience tier for labor-based rate card lines. Determines rate differential based on expertise and responsibility level.

attributes.name: service_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the service, role, or media placement type covered by this line item. Provides clarity on scope and deliverables.

attributes.name: service_type
attributes.description: Category of service or deliverable covered by this rate card line. Classifies the type of work being priced.
attributes.references: 4As (American Association of Advertising Agencies) Service Classification

attributes.name: tax_category_code
attributes.description: Tax classification code for this line item. Used by financial systems to apply correct tax rates and comply with jurisdictional tax rules.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit by which this service or media placement is quantified and billed (e.g., hourly rate, cost per thousand impressions, flat fee per placement). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hour|day|week|month|impression|click|placement|project|deliverable — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: unit_rate
attributes.description: The base price charged per unit of measure. Core pricing element used for billing calculations and budget forecasting.

attributes.name: volume_tier_threshold_max
attributes.description: Maximum quantity threshold for volume-based pricing tier. Null for open-ended top tier. Defines upper bound of pricing tier range.

attributes.name: volume_tier_threshold_min
attributes.description: Minimum quantity threshold for volume-based pricing tier. Null if no volume discounting applies. Used with volume_tier_threshold_max to define tier ranges.

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: Identifier of the user or system that created this rate card line record. Supports data lineage and accountability.

products.name: amendment
products.description: Formal change record modifying an existing agreement, SOW, or IO — capturing scope changes, budget adjustments, timeline extensions, or term revisions. Each amendment records the parent contract reference, amendment number, change type, original value, revised value, effective date, reason for change, and approval chain. Maintains a complete audit trail of all contractual modifications throughout the agreement lifecycle.

attributes.name: amendment_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the contract amendment record. Primary key.

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent contract, Statement of Work (SOW), Master Service Agreement (MSA), or Insertion Order (IO) being amended.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.campaign. Business justification: Amendments to insertion orders or SOWs frequently adjust campaign budgets, timelines, or scope. Linking amendment→campaign enables budget reconciliation, change order tracking, and campaign financial

attributes.name: contract_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.insertion_order. Business justification: Amendments can modify Insertion Orders (budget changes, flight date extensions, placement adjustments). N:1 relationship (many amendments can apply to one IO). No reverse FK exists. This allows tracki

attributes.name: contract_rate_card_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_card. Business justification: Amendments frequently modify pricing terms, which may involve updating or replacing a rate card. Adding contract_rate_card_id to amendment allows tracking which rate card was introduced or modified by

attributes.name: sla_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sla. Business justification: Amendments can modify SLA terms — e.g., changing performance targets, penalty thresholds, or measurement methodologies. Adding sla_id to amendment allows tracking which SLA record was affected by the

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Amendments can modify SOWs (scope changes, deliverable adjustments) in addition to modifying master agreements. N:1 relationship (many amendments can apply to one SOW). No reverse FK exists. This allo

attributes.name: superseded_by_amendment_id
attributes.description: Reference to a subsequent amendment that supersedes or replaces this amendment, if applicable.

attributes.name: agency_signatory_name
attributes.description: Full name of the agency representative who signed the amendment on behalf of the agency.

attributes.name: agency_signatory_title
attributes.description: Job title or role of the agency representative who signed the amendment.

attributes.name: amendment_number
attributes.description: Sequential or hierarchical amendment identifier (e.g., A-001, Amendment 1, 2024-A-03) used for external reference and tracking.

attributes.name: amendment_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the amendment in the approval and execution workflow.

attributes.name: amendment_type
attributes.description: Classification of the amendment based on the nature of the contractual change being made.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date on which the amendment received formal approval from the authorized approver.

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the individual or authority who approved the amendment.

attributes.name: budget_delta_amount
attributes.description: The net change in budget amount resulting from the amendment (revised minus original).

attributes.name: change_description
attributes.description: Detailed narrative describing the specific contractual changes, modifications, or adjustments being made through this amendment.

attributes.name: change_reason
attributes.description: Business justification or rationale for the amendment, explaining why the contractual modification is necessary.

attributes.name: client_signatory_name
attributes.description: Full name of the client representative who signed the amendment on behalf of the client organization.

attributes.name: client_signatory_title
attributes.description: Job title or role of the client representative who signed the amendment.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the amendment record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this amendment.

attributes.name: document_url
attributes.description: URL or file path to the signed amendment document stored in the Digital Asset Management (DAM) system or document repository.

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date on which the amendment terms become binding and enforceable.

attributes.name: execution_date
attributes.description: Date on which all required parties signed and executed the amendment.

attributes.name: expiration_date
attributes.description: Date on which the amendment terms expire or are no longer applicable, if applicable.

attributes.name: legal_review_completed
attributes.description: Indicates whether the required legal review has been completed for this amendment.

attributes.name: legal_review_date
attributes.description: Date on which the legal review of the amendment was completed.

attributes.name: legal_review_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether the amendment requires formal legal review before execution.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the amendment record was last modified or updated in the system.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional free-form notes, comments, or internal observations related to the amendment.

attributes.name: original_budget_amount
attributes.description: The original contracted budget amount before the amendment, if the amendment involves budget changes.

attributes.name: original_end_date
attributes.description: The original contract end date before the amendment, if the amendment involves timeline changes.

attributes.name: original_value
attributes.description: The original contractual value, term, or specification before the amendment (stored as text to accommodate various data types).

attributes.name: requested_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the individual or party who initiated the amendment request.

attributes.name: requested_date
attributes.description: Date on which the amendment was formally requested by the client or agency.

attributes.name: revised_budget_amount
attributes.description: The new contracted budget amount after the amendment is applied, if the amendment involves budget changes.

attributes.name: revised_end_date
attributes.description: The new contract end date after the amendment is applied, if the amendment involves timeline changes.

attributes.name: revised_value
attributes.description: The new contractual value, term, or specification after the amendment is applied (stored as text to accommodate various data types).

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Version number of the amendment document, incremented with each revision during the draft and approval process.

products.name: renewal
products.description: Contract renewal event record tracking the lifecycle of agreement renewals — including auto-renewal triggers, manual renewal negotiations, pricing adjustments, and final outcome (renewed, renegotiated, lapsed, terminated). Captures renewal notice date, renewal effective date, prior term value, new term value, renewal type (auto, manual, renegotiation), responsible account manager, and client decision-maker. Supports proactive renewal pipeline management, revenue retention forecasting, and 90/60/30-day renewal alerting workflows.

attributes.name: renewal_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the contract renewal event record. Primary key.

attributes.name: agency_relationship_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.agency_relationship. Business justification: Contract renewals in advertising are directly tied to agency-client relationship reviews. When an AOR relationship is up for renewal, the contract renewal process is triggered. Relationship-level rene

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent contract being renewed. Links this renewal event to the master agreement.

attributes.name: amendment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.amendment. Business justification: In agency practice, a contract renewal is frequently formalized through an amendment to the existing agreement rather than a new standalone agreement. Adding amendment_id to renewal links the renewal

attributes.name: contract_rate_card_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_card. Business justification: Contract renewals typically involve rate renegotiation, resulting in a new or revised rate card. Adding contract_rate_card_id to renewal links the renewal event to the rate card that will govern the r

attributes.name: finance_budget_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Contract renewals in advertising require a new or extended budget allocation. Linking renewal to finance_budget enables finance teams to confirm budget availability before renewal approval and support

attributes.name: sla_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sla. Business justification: Renewals often involve SLA renegotiation — performance targets may be raised, penalty structures revised, or new metrics introduced. Adding sla_id to renewal links the renewal event to the SLA record

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Renewals can be specific to SOWs (renewing a specific scope of work under a master agreement). N:1 relationship (many renewals can relate to one SOW). No reverse FK exists. This allows tracking SOW-le

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Contract renewals are managed per vendor relationship. Linking renewal.supplier_id enables vendor renewal pipeline reporting, churn risk analysis by supplier, and preferred vendor tier decisions — a n

attributes.name: alert_sent_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether automated renewal alerts (90/60/30-day notifications) were successfully sent to the account team. True if alerts sent, False otherwise.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date when internal approval was granted for this renewal. Null if no approval was required.

attributes.name: approval_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this renewal required executive or legal approval due to value threshold, term length, or risk factors. True if approval required, False otherwise.

attributes.name: auto_renewal_clause_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the renewed contract includes an automatic renewal clause for the next term. True if auto-renewal is enabled, False if manual renewal required.

attributes.name: churn_risk_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this renewal was flagged as high churn risk based on client health indicators, payment issues, or competitive threats. True if at-risk, False otherwise.

attributes.name: competitive_threat_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the renewal process involved known competitive pressure or client evaluation of alternative agencies. True if competitive threat identified, False otherwise.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this renewal record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this renewal record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

attributes.name: decision_date
attributes.description: Date when the client or vendor formally communicated their renewal decision (renew, renegotiate, or decline).

attributes.name: effective_date
attributes.description: Date when the renewed contract terms become binding and the new contract period begins. Typically aligns with the expiration date of the prior term.

attributes.name: negotiation_end_date
attributes.description: Date when renewal negotiations concluded, either with agreement or termination of discussions.

attributes.name: negotiation_start_date
attributes.description: Date when formal renewal negotiations began with the client. Marks the transition from renewal notice to active discussion phase.

attributes.name: new_term_end_date
attributes.description: End date of the renewed contract term. Defines the duration of the new agreement period.

attributes.name: new_term_start_date
attributes.description: Start date of the renewed contract term. Typically matches renewal_effective_date but may differ if there is a gap or overlap period.

attributes.name: new_term_value
attributes.description: Total contract value of the renewed term, expressed in the contract currency. Captures pricing adjustments, scope changes, or inflationary increases.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for account team notes, client feedback, negotiation highlights, or special considerations related to this renewal event.

attributes.name: notice_date
attributes.description: Date when the renewal notice was sent to the client or vendor, triggering the renewal evaluation period. Critical for tracking compliance with contractual notice requirements (e.g., 90-day notice clauses).

attributes.name: outcome
attributes.description: Final result of the renewal process: renewed (client accepted renewal on existing or similar terms), renegotiated (client renewed with material changes), lapsed (client did not respond or declined passively), terminated (client actively ended relationship).

attributes.name: pricing_model_change_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the renewal involved a change to the pricing model (e.g., shift from CPM to CPA, or from fixed fee to performance-based). True if pricing model changed, False otherwise.

attributes.name: prior_term_end_date
attributes.description: End date of the contract term that is being renewed. Marks the expiration of the prior agreement and the trigger point for renewal evaluation.

attributes.name: prior_term_start_date
attributes.description: Start date of the contract term that is being renewed. Used to calculate term length and renewal cycle metrics.

attributes.name: prior_term_value
attributes.description: Total contract value of the term being renewed, expressed in the contract currency. Used to calculate renewal rate and revenue retention metrics.

attributes.name: probability_score
attributes.description: Predictive score (0-100) representing the likelihood of successful renewal at the time of renewal notice. Derived from client health metrics, engagement scores, and historical renewal patterns.

attributes.name: reason
attributes.description: Free-text explanation of the drivers behind the renewal outcome. Captures client feedback, competitive factors, performance issues, or strategic rationale.

attributes.name: renewal_number
attributes.description: Business-facing identifier for this renewal event, typically formatted as contract number plus renewal sequence (e.g., MSA-2024-001-R2).

attributes.name: renewal_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the renewal event: pending (renewal window opened, no action yet), in_negotiation (active discussions), approved (client committed), renewed (executed and effective), lapsed (client did not renew), terminated (client ended relationship), cancelled (renewal process aborted). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|in_negotiation|approved|renewed|lapsed|terminated|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: renewal_type
attributes.description: Classification of the renewal mechanism: auto (automatic renewal per contract terms), manual (proactive renewal negotiation), renegotiation (renewal with material term changes), amendment (renewal with scope or pricing adjustments).

attributes.name: scope_change_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the renewal involved a change to the scope of services or deliverables (e.g., added channels, reduced markets, new service lines). True if scope changed, False otherwise.

attributes.name: sow_updated_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the renewal required an updated Statement of Work (SOW) document due to scope, deliverable, or Service Level Agreement (SLA) changes. True if SOW updated, False otherwise.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this renewal record was last modified. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.

attributes.name: value_change_amount
attributes.description: Net change in contract value from prior term to new term (new_term_value minus prior_term_value). Positive values indicate upsell, negative values indicate downsell or discount.

attributes.name: value_change_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage change in contract value from prior term to new term, calculated as (value_change_amount / prior_term_value) * 100. Key metric for renewal pricing analysis.

domains.name: finance
domains.description: Corporate financial domain managing agency billing, revenue recognition, client invoicing, media cost reconciliation, and P&L reporting. Owns budget allocations, cost centers, general ledger entries, EBITDA tracking, vendor payables, payment terms, margin analysis, and financial reporting for campaigns and accounts. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA FI/CO and Mediaocean Prisma billing.
domains.references: SAP S/4HANA FI — Financial Accounting Standards / GAAP Revenue Recognition

products.name: cost_center
products.description: Master record for agency cost centers used to allocate and track expenditures across departments, practice groups, and client accounts. Captures cost center code, name, type (overhead, billable, shared services), owning department, P&L responsibility flag, budget owner, SAP CO cost center reference, active status, and fiscal year applicability. Serves as the foundational organizational unit for management accounting, budget-vs-actual reporting, and overhead allocation in SAP S/4HANA CO module.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the cost center record. Primary key.

attributes.name: parent_cost_center_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent cost center in a hierarchical cost center structure. Enables roll-up reporting and multi-level budget consolidation. Null for top-level cost centers.

attributes.name: allocation_method
attributes.description: The method used to allocate costs from this cost center to other cost objects or client accounts. Direct allocation assigns costs explicitly, activity-based uses cost drivers, headcount allocates by employee count, revenue allocates by revenue contribution, square footage by physical space, and Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) by staffing levels.

attributes.name: annual_budget_amount
attributes.description: The total approved budget amount allocated to this cost center for the fiscal year. Used for budget-versus-actual variance analysis and financial planning.

attributes.name: approval_required_flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator of whether expenditures charged to this cost center require explicit approval before posting. True indicates approval workflow is enforced; false indicates automatic posting.

attributes.name: approval_threshold_amount
attributes.description: The monetary threshold above which expenditures charged to this cost center require additional approval. Amounts below this threshold may be auto-approved based on delegation rules.

attributes.name: billable_flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator of whether costs incurred in this cost center are directly billable to clients. True indicates billable work; false indicates non-billable overhead or internal support.
attributes.references: Agency Financial Management Standards - American Association of Advertising Agencies (4As)

attributes.name: budget_owner_email
attributes.description: The email address of the budget owner for communication regarding budget approvals, variance reporting, and financial planning.

attributes.name: budget_owner_name
attributes.description: The name of the individual responsible for managing and approving the budget for this cost center. Typically a department head, practice leader, or senior manager.

attributes.name: company_code
attributes.description: The company code or legal entity identifier to which this cost center belongs. Used for multi-entity financial consolidation and reporting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Financial Accounting (FI) Module Standards

attributes.name: controlling_area
attributes.description: The controlling area in SAP S/4HANA CO that groups cost centers for management accounting and internal reporting purposes. A controlling area may span multiple company codes.

attributes.name: cost_center_category
attributes.description: High-level functional category of the cost center. Production centers create deliverables, service centers provide internal services, support centers enable operations, sales centers drive revenue, marketing centers manage campaigns, and administration centers handle corporate governance.
attributes.references: Management Accounting Best Practices - Institute of Management Accountants (IMA)

attributes.name: cost_center_code
attributes.description: The externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the cost center in financial systems and reports. Used as the business identifier in SAP S/4HANA CO module and Mediaocean Prisma billing.

attributes.name: cost_center_name
attributes.description: The human-readable name or title of the cost center, used for display and reporting purposes.

attributes.name: cost_center_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the cost center. Active centers are operational and accepting charges, inactive centers are temporarily disabled, suspended centers are under review, closed centers are permanently retired, and pending approval centers are awaiting activation.

attributes.name: cost_center_type
attributes.description: Classification of the cost center based on its function and cost allocation model. Overhead centers support general operations, billable centers track client-facing work, shared services centers provide cross-functional support, direct centers relate to revenue-generating activities, indirect centers support operations, and administrative centers manage corporate functions.
attributes.references: Management Accounting Best Practices - Institute of Management Accountants (IMA)

attributes.name: cost_pool_flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator of whether this cost center functions as a cost pool that aggregates costs for subsequent allocation to other cost centers or client accounts. True indicates a cost pool; false indicates a standard cost center.
attributes.references: Management Accounting Best Practices - Institute of Management Accountants (IMA)

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this cost center record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which this cost center's budget and expenses are denominated. Examples include USD, EUR, GBP, CAD.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which this cost center configuration ceases to be effective. Null for currently active configurations. Used for historical analysis and audit trails.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date on which this cost center configuration becomes effective and begins accepting cost allocations. Supports temporal validity and historical tracking.

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: The fiscal year for which this cost center configuration is applicable. Cost centers may have different configurations across fiscal years for budget planning and reporting purposes.

attributes.name: last_modified_by
attributes.description: The username or identifier of the user who last modified this cost center record. Supports audit requirements and change management.
attributes.references: Audit Trail Standards - Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) Section 404

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this cost center record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and audit trails.

attributes.name: location_code
attributes.description: Three-letter code identifying the primary geographic location or office where this cost center operates. Examples include NYC, LON, SFO, TOR. Used for location-based cost analysis and reporting.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information about the cost center. Used for documentation and knowledge transfer.

attributes.name: overhead_allocation_percentage
attributes.description: The percentage of overhead costs allocated to this cost center from shared services or administrative cost pools. Used in fully-loaded cost calculations and margin analysis.

attributes.name: owning_department
attributes.description: The department or organizational unit that owns and manages this cost center. Examples include Creative Services, Media Planning, Account Management, Finance, Human Resources, IT, Operations.

attributes.name: practice_group
attributes.description: The specialized practice area or service line associated with this cost center, such as Digital Marketing, Brand Strategy, Public Relations, Data Analytics, Creative Production, Media Buying.
attributes.references: Agency Practice Management Standards - American Association of Advertising Agencies (4As)

attributes.name: profit_and_loss_responsibility_flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator of whether this cost center has direct profit and loss accountability. True indicates the center is a profit center with revenue and expense responsibility; false indicates a pure cost center.
attributes.references: Management Accounting Best Practices - Institute of Management Accountants (IMA)

attributes.name: quarterly_budget_amount
attributes.description: The budget amount allocated to this cost center for the current quarter. Supports quarterly business review (QBR) processes and short-term financial management.

attributes.name: sap_co_cost_center_reference
attributes.description: The corresponding cost center identifier in the SAP S/4HANA Controlling (CO) module. Used for integration and reconciliation between the lakehouse and the source ERP system.

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: The username or identifier of the user who created this cost center record. Supports audit requirements and accountability.
attributes.references: Audit Trail Standards - Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) Section 404

products.name: gl_account
products.description: General ledger chart of accounts master for the agency, aligned to SAP S/4HANA FI module. Captures account code, account name, account type (asset, liability, equity, revenue, expense), financial statement category, P&L vs balance sheet classification, cost element linkage, currency, consolidation group, IFRS/GAAP mapping, and active status. Serves as the SSOT for all ledger account definitions used in journal entries, billing, and financial reporting.

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the general ledger account. Primary key for the GL account master data.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Chart of Accounts

attributes.name: account_code
attributes.description: The externally-known unique account number used in financial transactions and reporting. Typically a 4-10 digit numeric code aligned to the chart of accounts structure.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Chart of Accounts; IFRS; GAAP

attributes.name: account_description_long
attributes.description: Extended description or explanatory text for the general ledger account, providing additional context, usage guidelines, or business rules for posting.

attributes.name: account_group
attributes.description: The hierarchical grouping or category within the chart of accounts (e.g., 'Current Assets', 'Operating Expenses', 'Client Revenue'). Used for rollup and reporting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Account Group; IFRS

attributes.name: account_name
attributes.description: The human-readable name or title of the general ledger account (e.g., 'Media Costs - Digital', 'Client Revenue - Retainer', 'Salaries and Wages').
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Chart of Accounts

attributes.name: account_type
attributes.description: The fundamental accounting classification of the account: asset, liability, equity, revenue, or expense. Determines the account's role in the balance sheet or profit and loss statement.
attributes.references: IFRS Conceptual Framework; GAAP; SAP S/4HANA FI

attributes.name: balance_sheet_classification
attributes.description: The detailed balance sheet classification for asset, liability, and equity accounts: current asset, non-current asset, current liability, non-current liability, equity, or not applicable (for P&L accounts).

attributes.name: chart_of_accounts
attributes.description: The chart of accounts identifier to which this account belongs. Defines the account structure and numbering scheme for the organization or group.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Chart of Accounts

attributes.name: company_code
attributes.description: The SAP company code (legal entity identifier) to which this account belongs. Supports multi-entity chart of accounts with company-specific configurations.

attributes.name: consolidation_group
attributes.description: The consolidation grouping code used for corporate group financial consolidation and elimination entries. Aligns accounts across legal entities for consolidated reporting.
attributes.references: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; SAP S/4HANA FI Consolidation

attributes.name: cost_center_applicable
attributes.description: Indicates whether this account requires cost center assignment for expense tracking and allocation. True if cost center is mandatory for postings to this account.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Center Accounting

attributes.name: cost_element
attributes.description: The cost element code linking this GL account to the controlling (CO) module for cost center and internal order tracking. Used for management accounting and margin analysis.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Element; Management Accounting Standards

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this general ledger account record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the account's local currency (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP'). Determines the currency in which balances are maintained.
attributes.references: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Configuration

attributes.name: field_status_group
attributes.description: The field status group code that controls which fields are required, optional, or hidden when posting to this account. Enforces data entry rules at the account level.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Field Status Variant

attributes.name: financial_statement_category
attributes.description: The primary financial statement to which this account belongs: balance sheet, income statement (P&L), cash flow statement, or statement of equity.
attributes.references: IFRS; GAAP; SAP S/4HANA FI Financial Statement Version

attributes.name: gaap_mapping
attributes.description: The US GAAP account classification or line item code to which this account maps for GAAP-compliant financial reporting.
attributes.references: US GAAP; SAP S/4HANA FI Financial Statement Version

attributes.name: gl_account_status
attributes.description: The current lifecycle status of the general ledger account: active (in use), inactive (not in use but retained), pending (awaiting approval), or archived (historical only).

attributes.name: ifrs_mapping
attributes.description: The IFRS account classification or line item code to which this account maps for IFRS-compliant financial reporting.
attributes.references: IFRS; SAP S/4HANA FI Financial Statement Version

attributes.name: line_item_display
attributes.description: Indicates whether individual line items (journal entry details) are stored and displayed for this account. True if line-item reporting is enabled; false if only totals are maintained.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Line Item Management

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: The username or identifier of the user who last modified this general ledger account record.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this general ledger account record was last modified in the system.

attributes.name: open_item_management
attributes.description: Indicates whether this account uses open item management for tracking and clearing individual postings (e.g., invoices, payments). True if open item clearing is required.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Open Item Management

attributes.name: pl_classification
attributes.description: The detailed profit and loss classification for revenue and expense accounts: operating revenue, non-operating revenue, cost of sales, operating expense, non-operating expense, or not applicable (for balance sheet accounts).

attributes.name: posting_blocked
attributes.description: Indicates whether direct postings to this account are currently blocked. True if the account is locked for new transactions (e.g., during period close or account deactivation).

attributes.name: profit_center_applicable
attributes.description: Indicates whether this account requires profit center assignment for P&L segmentation and profitability analysis. True if profit center is mandatory for postings to this account.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Profit Center Accounting

attributes.name: reconciliation_account
attributes.description: Indicates whether this is a reconciliation account (control account) that summarizes subledger balances (e.g., accounts receivable, accounts payable, vendor accounts). True if this account is managed by a subledger.

attributes.name: sort_key
attributes.description: The sort key or allocation field used for automatic assignment of posting keys, document numbers, or payment terms during transaction entry.

attributes.name: tax_category
attributes.description: The tax category or tax code applicable to this account for automatic tax calculation and reporting (e.g., 'VAT Standard', 'Sales Tax Exempt', 'Input Tax').
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Configuration; Local Tax Regulations

attributes.name: valid_from_date
attributes.description: The date from which this general ledger account is valid and available for use in financial transactions. Supports time-dependent account structures.

attributes.name: valid_to_date
attributes.description: The date until which this general ledger account is valid. Null if the account is open-ended. Used for account retirement and historical tracking.

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: The username or identifier of the user who created this general ledger account record in the system.

products.name: finance_budget
products.description: Approved financial budget records for campaigns, accounts, cost centers, and agency operations, serving as the control baseline for spend authorization and variance analysis. Captures budget version (original, revised, final), budget type (campaign, overhead, media, production, talent), fiscal year, fiscal period, approved amount, currency, budget owner, approval status, approval date, lock status, SAP CO internal order reference, and Mediaocean Prisma budget reference. Supports budget-vs-actual reporting, EBITDA tracking, and financial governance processes.
products.reference: SAP CO Budget / Mediaocean Prisma Budget

attributes.name: finance_budget_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the budget record. Primary key.

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Master agreements (retainers, MSAs) in advertising have budgets allocated at the agreement level, not just SOW level. Finance teams need to link budgets directly to agreements for annual budget planni

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the campaign this budget is allocated to, if applicable.

attributes.name: client_brief_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brief. Business justification: Client briefs define campaign objectives, budgets, and deliverables that directly drive budget creation and allocation in advertising operations. Budget planning starts with approved client briefs spe

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Reference to the cost center this budget is allocated to for internal financial tracking.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO (Controlling) Cost Center Accounting

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Budget references GL account via natural key (gl_account_code). Adding surrogate key FK gl_account_id enables proper referential integrity. Remove redundant gl_account_code as it can be retrieved via

attributes.name: initiative_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.initiative. Business justification: Finance budgets in advertising agencies are created to fund specific project initiatives. Finance teams need to link budget records to the initiative for project cost tracking, billing, and budget uti

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: SOWs define the scope and budget for client engagements; finance creates budgets based on SOW commitments for spend control and forecasting.

attributes.name: allocated_amount
attributes.description: Amount of the budget that has been allocated or committed to specific activities or sub-budgets, but not yet spent.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date when the budget was officially approved by the authorized approver.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Current approval workflow status of the budget: draft (being prepared), pending (awaiting approval), approved (authorized for use), rejected (not approved), cancelled (voided).

attributes.name: approved_amount
attributes.description: The total approved budget amount in the specified currency. This is the baseline for spend authorization and variance analysis.

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the individual who approved the budget.

attributes.name: budget_category
attributes.description: High-level financial category of the budget: operating (day-to-day operational expenses), capital (long-term asset investments), project (specific project-based allocation), discretionary (flexible spend allocation).

attributes.name: budget_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the budget purpose, scope, and any special conditions or notes.

attributes.name: budget_name
attributes.description: Descriptive name of the budget for easy identification and reporting purposes.

attributes.name: budget_number
attributes.description: Externally-known unique business identifier for the budget record, used for reference in financial documents and approvals.

attributes.name: budget_type
attributes.description: Classification of the budget by its purpose: campaign (client campaign spend), overhead (agency operational costs), media (media buying allocation), production (creative production costs), talent (talent and usage rights fees), creative (creative development costs).

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this budget record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the approved budget amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date when this budget expires and spend authorization ends. Nullable for open-ended budgets.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date when this budget becomes effective and spend authorization begins.

attributes.name: fiscal_period
attributes.description: The fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year this budget applies to, typically 1-12 for monthly periods or 1-4 for quarterly periods.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI (Financial Accounting) Posting Period

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: The fiscal year this budget applies to, represented as a four-digit year (e.g., 2024).
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI (Financial Accounting) Fiscal Year Variant

attributes.name: lock_status
attributes.description: Indicates whether the budget is locked (true) and cannot be modified, or unlocked (false) and can still be edited. Locked budgets are typically final versions used for variance reporting.

attributes.name: mediaocean_prisma_budget_reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier to the corresponding budget record in Mediaocean Prisma for media planning and billing reconciliation.

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: User identifier or name of the person who last modified this budget record.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this budget record was last modified.

attributes.name: owner
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the individual or role responsible for managing and authorizing spend against this budget.

attributes.name: profit_center_code
attributes.description: Profit center code in SAP S/4HANA CO for Profit and Loss (P&L) reporting and margin analysis.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO (Controlling) Profit Center Accounting

attributes.name: remaining_amount
attributes.description: Remaining unspent budget amount, calculated as approved amount minus spent amount and allocated amount.

attributes.name: revision_number
attributes.description: Sequential revision number tracking how many times this budget has been revised. Original budget is revision 0.

attributes.name: revision_reason
attributes.description: Business justification or reason for the most recent budget revision.

attributes.name: sap_co_internal_order
attributes.description: Reference to the SAP S/4HANA CO internal order number used for cost collection and budget control in the ERP system.

attributes.name: spent_amount
attributes.description: Actual amount spent against this budget to date, used for budget-vs-actual variance analysis.

attributes.name: variance_threshold_percent
attributes.description: Acceptable variance threshold as a percentage of the approved budget. Exceeding this threshold triggers alerts or requires additional approval.

attributes.name: version
attributes.description: Version status of the budget: original (initial approved budget), revised (updated after changes), final (locked final version), forecast (projected budget for planning).

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: User identifier or name of the person who created this budget record.

products.name: budget_line
