products.description: Granular line-item breakdown of approved budgets, enabling detailed tracking of spend by campaign flight, media channel, creative production phase, or cost category. Captures parent budget reference, line description, cost category, channel type, flight start/end dates, allocated amount, revised amount, committed amount, actual spend to date, variance amount, variance percentage, and approval status. Feeds margin analysis and P&L reporting at the campaign and account level.

attributes.name: budget_line_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the budget line item. Primary key for the budget line entity.

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Budget lines in media planning are allocated to specific client brands for brand portfolio budget management and spend tracking. budget_line has campaign_id and cost_center_id but no brand reference;

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the campaign this budget line supports. Enables campaign-level budget tracking and reconciliation.

attributes.name: contract_deliverable_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_deliverable. Business justification: Budget lines fund specific contract deliverables in advertising project accounting. Linking budget_line to contract_deliverable enables deliverable-level cost tracking and actual-vs-estimated cost rep

attributes.name: contract_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_insertion_order. Business justification: Contract IOs authorize spend that is tracked at the budget line level. Linking budget_line to contract_insertion_order enables spend-vs-commitment reporting — a core financial control in advertising a

attributes.name: contract_rate_card_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_card. Business justification: Budget lines in advertising are priced against negotiated rate cards. Linking budget_line to contract_rate_card enables rate compliance reporting — finance can verify that committed spend aligns with

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Budget lines are allocated to cost centers for granular expense tracking. Currently references via natural key (cost_center_code). Adding cost_center_id FK enables proper joins and removes redundant c

attributes.name: finance_budget_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent budget that this line item belongs to. Links to the master budget allocation.

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.flight. Business justification: Budget lines in advertising are structured around campaign flights. budget_line currently stores flight_start_date and flight_end_date as denormalized plain columns — a flight_id FK normalizes this, e

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Budget line items reference GL accounts for detailed cost tracking. Adding gl_account_id FK provides referential integrity. Remove redundant gl_account_code.

attributes.name: io_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.io_line. Business justification: A finance budget line maps directly to a vendor IO line — the financial authorization corresponds to the contractual commitment. Advertising finance teams use this link to reconcile committed budget a

attributes.name: plan_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.media_plan_line. Business justification: Budget lines allocated to plan lines for detailed budget management and plan-to-actual variance tracking in media planning.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the vendor or supplier associated with this budget line spend. Links to vendor master data for payment processing and reconciliation.

attributes.name: work_package_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.work_package. Business justification: Budget lines are allocated to specific WBS work packages for granular project cost tracking and WBS-level budget control — standard agency project accounting. Finance teams need to see which work pack

attributes.name: actual_spend
attributes.description: The actual amount spent to date against this budget line, including invoiced and paid expenses. Used for budget variance analysis and burn rate tracking.

attributes.name: allocated_amount
attributes.description: The original approved budget amount allocated to this line item at the time of budget creation. Represents the baseline budget before any revisions.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Current approval workflow status of this budget line. Controls whether the line can be committed or spent against.

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the person who approved this budget line. Provides audit trail for budget authorization and accountability.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this budget line was approved. Critical for audit trail and compliance with financial controls.

attributes.name: billing_category
attributes.description: Classification of how this budget line will appear on client invoices. Aligns with industry-standard billing practices and client contract terms.
attributes.references: Association of National Advertisers (ANA) Billing Guidelines

attributes.name: channel_type
attributes.description: The media channel or platform type this budget line is allocated to. Supports channel-level budget analysis and Return on Ad Spend (ROAS) optimization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: digital|tv|radio|print|ooh|social|search|programmatic|ctv|ott — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: committed_amount
attributes.description: The amount of budget that has been committed through Insertion Orders (IO), contracts, or purchase orders but not yet invoiced or paid. Represents encumbered funds.

attributes.name: cost_category
attributes.description: Classification of the budget line by cost type. Determines how the expense is categorized in financial reporting and Profit and Loss (P&L) statements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: media|creative_production|talent|technology|research|overhead|contingency — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Element Classification

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this budget line record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for budget lifecycle tracking.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this budget line. Supports multi-currency budget management and consolidation.

attributes.name: is_active
attributes.description: Indicates whether this budget line is currently active and available for spending. Inactive lines are retained for historical reporting but cannot be spent against.

attributes.name: is_billable
attributes.description: Indicates whether this budget line represents costs that will be billed to the client or absorbed as agency overhead. Critical for margin analysis and client invoicing.

attributes.name: line_description
attributes.description: Detailed textual description of what this budget line covers, including scope, deliverables, and any special conditions or assumptions.

attributes.name: line_number
attributes.description: Sequential line number within the parent budget. Used for ordering and referencing specific line items in budget documentation.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Line Item Management

attributes.name: markup_percentage
attributes.description: The percentage markup applied to cost for client billing. Used to calculate gross margin and agency revenue on pass-through costs.

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: Identifier of the user who last modified this budget line record. Provides audit trail for all budget changes.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this budget line record was last updated. Critical for change tracking and data freshness validation.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-form notes and comments about this budget line. Captures assumptions, constraints, dependencies, or special instructions for budget execution.

attributes.name: revised_amount
attributes.description: The current approved budget amount after any revisions, amendments, or reforecasts. Reflects the most up-to-date budget allocation for this line.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Budget Revision Management

attributes.name: revision_number
attributes.description: Sequential version number tracking how many times this budget line has been revised. Supports change management and audit requirements.

attributes.name: revision_reason
attributes.description: Textual explanation for why this budget line was revised. Required for financial governance and Quarterly Business Review (QBR) reporting.

attributes.name: variance_amount
attributes.description: The absolute difference between revised budget amount and actual spend. Positive values indicate under-spend, negative values indicate over-spend.

attributes.name: variance_percentage
attributes.description: The variance amount expressed as a percentage of the revised budget. Key Performance Indicator (KPI) for budget management and financial control.

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: Identifier of the user who created this budget line record. Supports accountability and audit requirements.

products.name: client_invoice
products.description: Authoritative record of invoices issued by the agency to advertising clients for services rendered, media placements, and production work. Captures invoice number, invoice date, due date, billing period, client account reference, SOW/IO reference, invoice type (retainer, project, media, production), line item summary, subtotal, tax amount, total amount, currency, payment terms (net 30/60/90), payment status, SAP FI document number, and Mediaocean Prisma billing reference. Core to revenue recognition and accounts receivable management.
products.reference: SAP FI Customer Invoice / Mediaocean Prisma Client Bill

attributes.name: client_invoice_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the client invoice record. Primary key for the client invoice entity.

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the client advertiser account being invoiced. Links invoice to the client master record.

attributes.name: agency_relationship_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.agency_relationship. Business justification: Invoices are issued under specific AOR or project-based agency-client agreements. Linking invoices to agency_relationship supports commission rate application, relationship-level billing reconciliatio

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Client invoices in advertising are issued at the brand level (brand-specific campaigns and services). Brand-level accounts receivable aging and invoice reporting are standard agency finance operations

attributes.name: buy_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.buy. Business justification: Client invoices for media services reference specific buys for billing substantiation. Buy-level invoice tracking enables agencies to provide clients with detailed billing support and supports billing

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.campaign. Business justification: Client invoices in advertising are issued per campaign. A campaign_id FK on client_invoice enables campaign-level billing reconciliation, revenue attribution reporting, and dispute resolution — a name

attributes.name: contract_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.insertion_order. Business justification: IOs are invoiced to clients; finance links invoices back to the authorizing IO for reconciliation and payment tracking. Replaces denormalized io_reference_number.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Client invoices are attributed to cost centers for revenue tracking and P&L allocation. Adding cost_center_id FK replaces natural key reference (cost_center_code).

attributes.name: delivery_pacing_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.delivery_pacing. Business justification: Client invoices are validated against delivery pacing records to confirm billed amounts reflect actual delivery. Finance teams use pacing data to support invoice accuracy, dispute resolution, and clie

attributes.name: milestone_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.project_milestone. Business justification: Agency client invoices are frequently triggered by project milestone completion (campaign launch, creative delivery gates). project_milestone has is_billing_trigger flag confirming this pattern. Billi

attributes.name: performance_kpi_target_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.performance_kpi_target. Business justification: Performance-based invoicing validates delivery against contractual KPI targets; billing adjustments require linking to target definitions.

attributes.name: plan_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.plan. Business justification: Client invoices for media services reference the media plan for billing reconciliation and client reporting. Agencies invoice clients against approved media plans; this link enables plan-level billing

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: SOWs are the contractual basis for client invoicing; invoice lines reference SOW terms and deliverables. Replaces denormalized sow_reference_number with proper FK.

attributes.name: adjustment_amount
attributes.description: Additional adjustments applied to the invoice (credits, surcharges, reconciliation adjustments). Can be positive or negative.

attributes.name: amount_outstanding
attributes.description: Remaining unpaid balance on the invoice. Calculated as total_amount minus amount_paid. Zero when invoice is fully paid.

attributes.name: amount_paid
attributes.description: Total amount received from the client against this invoice. Used to track partial payments and calculate outstanding balance.

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the agency employee who approved the invoice for issuance. Used for internal approval audit trail.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the invoice was approved for issuance. Marks transition from draft to issued status.

attributes.name: billing_contact_email
attributes.description: Email address of the billing contact for invoice delivery and payment correspondence. Primary communication channel for accounts receivable.

attributes.name: billing_contact_name
attributes.description: Name of the client contact responsible for invoice receipt and payment processing. Used for invoice delivery and payment follow-up.

attributes.name: billing_period_end_date
attributes.description: End date of the period covered by this invoice. Defines the scope of services or media placements included in the billing cycle.

attributes.name: billing_period_start_date
attributes.description: Start date of the period covered by this invoice. Relevant for retainer billing, subscription services, and time-based billing.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the invoice record was first created in the system. Audit field for record lifecycle tracking.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the invoice (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Defines the monetary unit for all amounts.

attributes.name: discount_amount
attributes.description: Total discount applied to the invoice. May include volume discounts, early payment discounts, or negotiated rate reductions.

attributes.name: dispute_date
attributes.description: Date when the client raised a dispute on this invoice. Used for dispute aging and resolution tracking.

attributes.name: dispute_reason
attributes.description: Reason provided by client for disputing the invoice. Populated when invoice_status is disputed. Used for dispute resolution tracking.

attributes.name: due_date
attributes.description: Date by which payment is expected from the client based on agreed payment terms. Used for accounts receivable aging and collections management.

attributes.name: invoice_date
attributes.description: Date the invoice was issued to the client. Used for revenue recognition timing and aging analysis.

attributes.name: invoice_notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or special instructions related to the invoice. May include payment instructions, billing clarifications, or client-specific requirements.

attributes.name: invoice_number
attributes.description: Externally-visible unique invoice number issued to the client. Used for client communication, payment reference, and accounts receivable tracking.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Numbering Standard

attributes.name: invoice_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the invoice: draft (not yet finalized), issued (approved for sending), sent (delivered to client), partially_paid (payment in progress), paid (fully settled), overdue (past due date), disputed (under client review), cancelled (voided). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued|sent|partially_paid|paid|overdue|disputed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: invoice_type
attributes.description: Classification of the invoice based on billing model: retainer (recurring fee), project (fixed scope), media (placement costs), production (creative development), pass_through (third-party costs), or adjustment (credit/debit memo).

attributes.name: mediaocean_billing_reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier from Mediaocean Prisma billing system for media cost reconciliation and invoice generation. Links to media buy billing records.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the invoice record was last modified. Audit field for change tracking and data lineage.

attributes.name: payment_method
attributes.description: Expected or preferred payment method for this invoice: wire transfer, ACH (Automated Clearing House), check, credit card, or direct debit.

attributes.name: payment_received_date
attributes.description: Date when full or final payment was received from the client. Null if invoice is unpaid or partially paid.

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: Agreed payment terms defining when payment is due: net_15 (15 days), net_30 (30 days), net_45 (45 days), net_60 (60 days), net_90 (90 days), due_on_receipt (immediate), custom (non-standard terms). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: net_15|net_30|net_45|net_60|net_90|due_on_receipt|custom — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: purchase_order_number
attributes.description: Client purchase order number required for invoice processing. Many clients require PO reference for accounts payable approval.

attributes.name: revenue_recognition_date
attributes.description: Date when revenue from this invoice is recognized in financial statements. May differ from invoice date based on revenue recognition rules.

attributes.name: sap_fi_document_number
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA FI document number for the accounting entry created from this invoice. Links invoice to general ledger posting.

attributes.name: sent_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the invoice was sent to the client. Used for tracking invoice delivery and aging calculations.

attributes.name: subtotal_amount
attributes.description: Total invoice amount before taxes, discounts, or adjustments. Sum of all line item charges.

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: Total tax amount applied to the invoice (sales tax, VAT, GST). Calculated based on jurisdiction and tax rules.

attributes.name: total_amount
attributes.description: Final total amount due from the client. Calculated as subtotal + tax - discount + adjustment. This is the amount the client must pay.

products.name: invoice_line
products.description: Individual line items on a client invoice, capturing the detailed breakdown of billable services, media costs, production fees, and agency commissions. Captures invoice reference, line number, service description, billing code, quantity, unit rate, line amount, tax code, revenue recognition category, campaign reference, cost center, GL account code, and delivery confirmation flag. Enables granular revenue recognition and client billing transparency per IAB billing standards.

attributes.name: invoice_line_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the invoice line item. Primary key for the invoice_line product.

attributes.name: budget_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.budget_line. Business justification: Each invoice line item should be traceable to the specific budget line it is billing against. invoice_line already has revenue_recognition_category and revenue_recognition_date, indicating it is a fin

attributes.name: buy_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.media_buy. Business justification: Invoice lines bill specific media buys for detailed cost recovery and margin calculation - essential billing granularity.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertising campaign associated with this billable line item. Links revenue to campaign performance.

attributes.name: click_event_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.click_event. Business justification: Invoice lines for CPC campaigns bill specific click volumes; delivery confirmation requires linking to click events.

attributes.name: client_invoice_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent invoice header. Links this line item to the overall client invoice.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Invoice line items are allocated to cost centers for detailed revenue attribution. Currently has cost_center (STRING) natural key. Adding cost_center_id FK provides referential integrity.

attributes.name: creative_deliverable_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.creative_deliverable. Business justification: Invoice lines bill clients for specific creative deliverables. Links billing to deliverables for revenue attribution, deliverable-level profitability analysis, and reconciliation of billed vs. deliver

attributes.name: deliverable_tracker_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.deliverable_tracker. Business justification: Invoice lines in advertising are billed for specific project deliverables (TV spots, banner sets, reports). Linking invoice_line to deliverable_tracker enables billing reconciliation against project d

attributes.name: delivery_pacing_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.delivery_pacing. Business justification: Invoice lines are reconciled against delivery pacing at placement/line-item level to validate billed quantities. This line-level delivery validation is essential for accurate CPM/CPC billing and suppo

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Invoice line items post to GL revenue accounts. Adding gl_account_id FK replaces natural key reference (gl_account_code).

attributes.name: io_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.io_line. Business justification: Client invoice lines in advertising are derived from vendor IO line delivery data. This link supports billing reconciliation and agency margin analysis by tracing each billed line back to the specific

attributes.name: media_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.media_insertion_order. Business justification: Invoice lines reference insertion orders for billing detail, reconciliation, and revenue allocation - core billing granularity in advertising finance.

attributes.name: media_placement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific media placement being billed. Links invoice line to media execution details.

attributes.name: performance_kpi_target_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.performance_kpi_target. Business justification: Invoice lines for performance-based billing reference specific KPI targets (guaranteed viewability, CPA rates). Linking invoice_line to performance_kpi_target supports performance-based billing valida

attributes.name: plan_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.media_plan_line. Business justification: Invoice lines reference plan lines for billing against planned spend and budget variance tracking in client billing processes.

attributes.name: programmatic_deal_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.programmatic_deal. Business justification: Programmatic deal invoice lines must reference the originating deal for billing reconciliation and DSP platform cost verification. Deal-level invoice line tracking is required for programmatic spend a

attributes.name: reconciliation_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.reconciliation. Business justification: Client invoice lines are generated after vendor reconciliation confirms delivery. Linking invoice_line to the reconciliation record that validated delivery is standard advertising billing practice, en

attributes.name: revenue_recognition_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_recognition. Business justification: invoice_line already carries revenue_recognition_category and revenue_recognition_date attributes, indicating that revenue recognition is determined at the line level (different service lines may use

attributes.name: schedule_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.schedule. Business justification: Broadcast invoice lines reference specific schedule entries for billing verification against aired spots. Affidavit reconciliation in broadcast billing requires matching invoice lines to schedule reco

attributes.name: viewability_measurement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.viewability_measurement. Business justification: Invoice lines for viewability-guaranteed placements bill based on viewable impressions; requires linking to viewability measurement data.

attributes.name: billing_code
attributes.description: Internal billing classification code used to categorize the type of service or cost being invoiced. Maps to agency billing taxonomy.

attributes.name: billing_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of this invoice line item. Tracks progression through billing workflow from draft to final billing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|billed|disputed|adjusted|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: commission_amount
attributes.description: Total agency commission earned on this line item. Represents agency revenue on media pass-through costs.

attributes.name: commission_percentage
attributes.description: Agency commission rate applied to media costs. Standard agency markup percentage per client contract terms.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this invoice line record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record origination.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the line amount. Supports multi-currency billing for global clients.

attributes.name: delivery_confirmation_date
attributes.description: Date on which service delivery was confirmed by client or operations. Triggers revenue recognition eligibility.

attributes.name: delivery_confirmed_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether service delivery has been confirmed and validated. True when performance obligation is fulfilled and documented.

attributes.name: discount_amount
attributes.description: Absolute monetary discount applied to this line item. Reduces the line amount before tax calculation.

attributes.name: discount_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage discount applied to this line item. Reflects negotiated client discounts, volume rebates, or promotional adjustments.

attributes.name: dispute_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this line item is under client dispute. True when client has challenged the charge and resolution is pending.

attributes.name: dispute_reason
attributes.description: Client-provided reason for disputing this invoice line item. Documents the nature of the billing disagreement for resolution tracking.

attributes.name: line_amount
attributes.description: Total monetary value for this invoice line before tax. Calculated as quantity multiplied by unit rate, adjusted for discounts or markups.

attributes.name: line_number
attributes.description: Sequential line number within the invoice. Determines the ordering and presentation sequence of line items on the invoice document.

attributes.name: line_total
attributes.description: Total amount for this invoice line including tax. Sum of line amount and tax amount, representing the final billable value.

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: User identifier of the person or system that last modified this invoice line record. Tracks responsibility for billing changes.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this invoice line record was last modified. Audit trail for tracking changes to billing details.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-form notes and comments related to this invoice line item. Captures billing instructions, special terms, or clarifications.

attributes.name: purchase_order_number
attributes.description: Client purchase order number authorizing this expenditure. Required for client accounts payable processing and payment matching.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: Numeric quantity of units being billed. Represents impressions, hours, days, or other unit of measure depending on service type.

attributes.name: revenue_recognition_category
attributes.description: Classification of revenue recognition status for this line item. Determines timing of revenue realization per accounting standards.

attributes.name: revenue_recognition_date
attributes.description: Date on which revenue for this line item is recognized in the general ledger. Drives financial period allocation.

attributes.name: service_category
attributes.description: High-level categorization of the billable service. Enables revenue analysis by service type and supports P&L reporting by business line. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: media_cost|creative_production|agency_fee|strategic_planning|research_insights|technology_platform|third_party_cost — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: service_description
attributes.description: Detailed narrative description of the billable service, media cost, production fee, or agency commission. Appears on the client-facing invoice document.

attributes.name: service_end_date
attributes.description: End date of the service period being billed. Defines the completion of the performance obligation delivery window.

attributes.name: service_start_date
attributes.description: Start date of the service period being billed. Defines the beginning of the performance obligation delivery window.

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: Total tax amount applied to this invoice line. Calculated based on tax code and applicable jurisdiction rates.

attributes.name: tax_code
attributes.description: Tax classification code determining the applicable tax treatment for this line item. Maps to tax jurisdiction and rate tables.

attributes.name: unit_of_measure
attributes.description: The unit by which quantity is measured. Defines how the billable service is quantified for pricing purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: impressions|clicks|hours|days|units|flat_fee|cpm|cpc|cpa — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: unit_rate
attributes.description: Price per unit of measure. The rate applied to quantity to calculate the line amount before adjustments.

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: User identifier of the person or system that created this invoice line record. Supports audit and accountability requirements.

products.name: vendor_payable
products.description: Accounts payable records for amounts owed by the agency to media vendors, publishers, production suppliers, freelancers, and technology partners. Captures vendor reference, payable document number, invoice date, due date, payment terms (net 30/60/90/120), gross amount, net amount, early payment discount amount, discount deadline, currency, payment status (open, scheduled, paid, disputed), SAP FI vendor invoice document number, Mediaocean Prisma payable reference, media buy reference, sequential liability flag, and reconciliation status. Supports media cost reconciliation, vendor payment processing, cash flow planning, and sequential liability management.
products.reference: SAP FI Vendor Invoice / Mediaocean Prisma Payable

attributes.name: vendor_payable_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the vendor payable record. Primary key for the vendor payable entity.

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the client account for which this vendor expense was incurred. Supports client-level profitability analysis and budget tracking.

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Vendor payables for media buys and production are attributed to specific client brands for brand-level cost of goods reporting and profitability analysis. vendor_payable has advertiser_id but brand-le

attributes.name: budget_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.budget_line. Business justification: A vendor payable represents the actual AP liability incurred against a planned budget line. Linking vendor_payable.budget_line_id -> budget_line.budget_line_id enables direct committed-vs-actual spend

attributes.name: buy_id
attributes.description: Reference to the media buy that generated this vendor payable. Links the financial liability to the underlying media purchase for campaign cost tracking and Return on Ad Spend (ROAS) analysis.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertising campaign for which this vendor cost was incurred. Enables campaign-level margin analysis and Return on Investment (ROI) calculation.

attributes.name: contract_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.insertion_order. Business justification: Media IOs create vendor payables when media is delivered; finance reconciles payables against IO terms for three-way match and payment authorization.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Vendor payables are attributed to cost centers for expense tracking. Adding cost_center_id FK replaces natural key reference.

attributes.name: delivery_pacing_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.delivery_pacing. Business justification: Finance teams validate vendor payables against actual delivery pacing data before approving payment. This delivery-to-payable validation is a standard media finance reconciliation step ensuring vendor

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Vendor payables post to GL accounts (typically accounts payable liability accounts). Adding gl_account_id FK replaces natural key reference.

attributes.name: io_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.io_line. Business justification: In advertising AP workflows, a vendor payable is generated against the specific IO line that authorized the spend. This enables 3-way match (IO line → delivery → payable) and is standard practice for

attributes.name: media_placement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.media_placement. Business justification: Payables reference specific placements for detailed cost tracking and delivery-based payment verification in vendor reconciliation.

attributes.name: performance_kpi_target_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.performance_kpi_target. Business justification: Performance-based vendor contracts include KPI-contingent payment terms. Linking vendor_payable to performance_kpi_target supports pay-for-performance payment processing and audit trails for KPI-gated

attributes.name: plan_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.plan_line. Business justification: Vendor payables should reference plan lines for planned-vs-actual cost reconciliation at the line level. Agency finance teams require this link to produce budget variance reports by media channel and

attributes.name: production_job_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.production_job. Business justification: Vendor payables (freelancers, studios, post-production) are tied to specific production jobs. Links payables to jobs for job costing, vendor cost reconciliation, and production budget variance analysi

attributes.name: reconciliation_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.reconciliation. Business justification: Advertising AP teams release vendor payments only after reconciliation confirms delivery. Linking vendor_payable to the reconciliation record that validated delivery is the core AP release workflow an

attributes.name: schedule_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.schedule. Business justification: Broadcast vendor payables are generated from schedule entries representing aired spots. Linking vendor payables to schedules enables affidavit-based billing verification — a standard broadcast media a

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the vendor, publisher, production supplier, freelancer, or technology partner to whom payment is owed.

attributes.name: vendor_invoice_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.invoice. Business justification: Vendor payables track liabilities for specific vendor invoices. Finance teams reconcile payables against vendor.invoice records for payment matching, dispute resolution, and three-way match (PO-invoic

attributes.name: vendor_rate_card_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_rate_card. Business justification: Vendor payable amounts are validated against the contracted rate card during AP processing. Advertising finance teams perform rate card verification as part of invoice approval — linking payable to ra

attributes.name: video_completion_event_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.video_completion_event. Business justification: Video vendor payables reconcile against delivered completions; CPCV payment validation requires completion event evidence.

attributes.name: viewability_measurement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.viewability_measurement. Business justification: Viewability-guaranteed vendor contracts pay only for viewable impressions; payable validation requires viewability measurement evidence.

attributes.name: work_package_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.work_package. Business justification: Vendor payables for production work (video production, photography, studio time) are incurred against specific WBS work packages. Finance needs to match vendor costs to the work package for project co

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: The date the vendor payable was approved for payment. Marks the transition from pending review to authorized for payment processing.

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: The user identifier of the person who approved the vendor payable for payment. Required for financial controls and audit trail compliance.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this vendor payable record was first created in the system. Required for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payable amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency financial reporting and foreign exchange management.

attributes.name: discount_amount
attributes.description: The discount amount available if payment is made by the early payment discount deadline. Supports cash management optimization through early payment incentives.

attributes.name: discount_deadline
attributes.description: The last date by which payment must be made to qualify for the early payment discount. Used for cash flow optimization decisions.

attributes.name: dispute_date
attributes.description: The date the payable was flagged as disputed. Used for aging analysis of unresolved disputes and vendor relationship management.

attributes.name: dispute_reason
attributes.description: Free-text description of the reason for disputing the vendor payable. Populated when payment_status is disputed. Used for vendor negotiation and dispute tracking.

attributes.name: due_date
attributes.description: The date by which payment must be made to the vendor according to the agreed payment terms. Used for cash flow planning and aging analysis.

attributes.name: fiscal_period
attributes.description: The fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year in which this vendor payable is recorded. Used for period-based financial reporting and variance analysis.

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: The fiscal year in which this vendor payable is recorded for financial reporting purposes. Derived from posting date and fiscal calendar configuration.

attributes.name: gross_amount
attributes.description: The total amount owed to the vendor before any discounts or adjustments are applied. Represents the full invoice value.

attributes.name: invoice_date
attributes.description: The date the vendor invoice was issued. This is the principal business event timestamp for the payable transaction and serves as the baseline for payment term calculations.

attributes.name: mediaocean_payable_reference
attributes.description: The unique payable reference identifier from Mediaocean Prisma billing system. Enables reconciliation between media buying platform and financial system for media cost tracking.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this vendor payable record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.

attributes.name: net_amount
attributes.description: The final amount payable to the vendor after applying early payment discounts and any other adjustments. This is the actual payment amount.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes or comments related to the vendor payable. Used for documenting special payment instructions, reconciliation notes, or other relevant information.

attributes.name: payable_document_number
attributes.description: The unique business document number assigned to this accounts payable record, used for tracking and reconciliation purposes.

attributes.name: payment_date
attributes.description: The date the payment was actually made to the vendor. Null for unpaid payables. Used for cash flow reporting and payment performance analysis.

attributes.name: payment_method
attributes.description: The payment instrument or mechanism used to settle the vendor payable. ACH (Automated Clearing House) for electronic bank transfers, wire transfer for same-day payments, check for paper-based payments, credit card for card-based payments, EFT (Electronic Funds Transfer) for direct bank transfers, virtual card for single-use card payments.

attributes.name: payment_reference_number
attributes.description: The unique reference number assigned to the payment transaction (e.g., check number, wire confirmation number, ACH trace number). Used for payment tracking and bank reconciliation.

attributes.name: payment_status
attributes.description: Current status of the vendor payment in the accounts payable lifecycle. Open indicates unpaid liability, scheduled indicates payment is queued, paid indicates payment completed, disputed indicates vendor dispute in progress, cancelled indicates payable voided, on_hold indicates payment temporarily suspended.

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: The contractual payment terms agreed with the vendor, typically expressed as net days (net_30, net_60, net_90, net_120). Determines the due date calculation from invoice date.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Terms Configuration

attributes.name: posting_date
attributes.description: The date the vendor payable was posted to the general ledger. Determines the accounting period for financial reporting and may differ from invoice date for period-end accruals.

attributes.name: reconciliation_status
attributes.description: Status of the three-way reconciliation process between vendor invoice, media buy order, and actual delivery. Pending indicates awaiting reconciliation, matched indicates full agreement, discrepancy indicates variance requiring investigation, resolved indicates discrepancy closed, escalated indicates dispute requiring management intervention.

attributes.name: sap_fi_document_number
attributes.business_glossary_term: SAP Financial Accounting (FI) Vendor Invoice Document Number
attributes.description: The SAP S/4HANA FI document number for the vendor invoice posting. Links the payable to the general ledger entry and enables full financial audit trail.

attributes.name: sequential_liability_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this payable is subject to sequential liability rules, where the agency is liable for vendor payment only after receiving payment from the client. Critical for cash flow management and risk mitigation in media buying.
attributes.references: American Association of Advertising Agencies (4As) Sequential Liability Guidelines

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: The total tax amount included in the vendor payable (e.g., sales tax, VAT, GST). Required for tax reporting and compliance with jurisdictional tax regulations.

attributes.name: withholding_tax_amount
attributes.description: The amount of tax withheld from the vendor payment as required by tax regulations. Reduces the net payment amount and must be remitted to tax authorities.

products.name: payment
products.description: Records of actual cash payments made to vendors (AP disbursements) and received from clients (AR receipts), serving as the SSOT for all cash movement events. Captures payment reference number, payment date, payment direction (inbound/outbound), payment method (ACH, wire, check, credit card), payer/payee reference, linked invoice or payable reference, payment amount, currency, exchange rate applied, bank account reference, clearing document number, SAP FI payment document number, reconciliation status, and value date. Supports cash flow forecasting, bank reconciliation, DSO/DPO calculation, and working capital management.

attributes.name: payment_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the payment transaction record. Primary key for the payment entity.

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the client or advertiser associated with this payment. Populated for inbound AR receipts and client-related outbound payments.

attributes.name: buy_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.buy. Business justification: Vendor payments are made against specific media buys in agency operations. Direct buy-to-payment linkage enables buy-level payment status tracking and cash flow reporting. Agency finance teams require

attributes.name: client_invoice_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.client_invoice. Business justification: Payments can be AR receipts from clients applied to client invoices. The existing invoice_id FK points to vendor.invoice for AP disbursements; this new FK handles client payment receipts. Enables trac

attributes.name: invoice_id
attributes.description: Reference to the invoice being settled by this payment. Links payment to the originating billing document for reconciliation.

attributes.name: media_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.media_insertion_order. Business justification: Payments allocated to insertion orders for cash flow tracking and payment reconciliation in media finance operations.

attributes.name: original_payment_id
attributes.description: Reference to the original payment record that this payment reverses. Populated only when is_reversal is True.

attributes.name: reconciliation_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.reconciliation. Business justification: Advertising agency payments to vendors are authorized by an approved reconciliation record. Linking payment to the reconciliation that triggered it supports the payment release audit trail and is requ

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the vendor, publisher, or supplier associated with this payment. Populated for outbound AP disbursements.

attributes.name: vendor_payable_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.vendor_payable. Business justification: Payments to vendors settle vendor payables (AP liabilities). This FK links the payment to the vendor payable record it is settling, enabling AP aging and payment application tracking. The existing inv

attributes.name: amount
attributes.description: The gross monetary value of the payment transaction in the specified currency. Represents the total cash movement before any adjustments.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: The date on which the payment was approved. Null if not yet approved or if approval is not required.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Indicates whether the payment has been approved for processing: pending approval (awaiting authorization), approved (authorized for execution), or rejected (denied by approver).

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: The name or identifier of the person who approved the payment for processing. Used for audit trail and segregation of duties compliance.

attributes.name: bank_transaction_reference
attributes.description: The unique transaction identifier assigned by the bank or payment processor. Used for bank reconciliation and dispute resolution.

attributes.name: channel
attributes.description: The interface or channel through which the payment was initiated or received: online portal, mobile app, direct bank transfer, lockbox service, manual entry by AP/AR clerk, or API integration.

attributes.name: clearing_document_number
attributes.description: The document number assigned during the clearing process in the financial system. Links payment to the clearing entry that offsets the receivable or payable.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this payment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the currency in which the payment was made (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).

attributes.name: direction
attributes.description: Indicates whether this is a payment received from a client (inbound/AR receipt) or a payment made to a vendor (outbound/AP disbursement).

attributes.name: discount_amount
attributes.description: The monetary value of any early payment discount or settlement discount applied to this payment. Null if no discount was taken.

attributes.name: exchange_rate
attributes.description: The foreign exchange rate applied to convert the payment amount to the agency's functional currency. Null for payments in functional currency.

attributes.name: fiscal_period
attributes.description: The fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year in which this payment was recorded. Used for period-based financial reporting.

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: The fiscal year in which this payment was recorded, based on the payment date. Used for financial period reporting and year-end closing.

attributes.name: functional_currency_amount
attributes.description: The payment amount converted to the agency's functional currency using the exchange rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting and P&L (Profit and Loss) analysis.

attributes.name: is_reversal
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this payment is a reversal of a previous payment transaction. True if this is a reversal entry, False otherwise.

attributes.name: memo
attributes.description: Free-text memo or notes field providing additional context about the payment. May include invoice references, campaign details, or special instructions.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this payment record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.

attributes.name: payee_name
attributes.description: The name of the party receiving the payment. For inbound payments, this is the agency entity. For outbound payments, this is the vendor, publisher, or supplier.

attributes.name: payer_name
attributes.description: The name of the party making the payment. For inbound payments, this is the client or advertiser. For outbound payments, this is the agency entity.

attributes.name: payment_date
attributes.description: The date on which the payment transaction was executed or received. This is the business event date for cash flow recognition.

attributes.name: payment_method
attributes.description: The financial instrument or mechanism used to execute the payment: ACH (Automated Clearing House), wire transfer, check, credit card, debit card, or electronic transfer.

attributes.name: payment_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the payment: pending (initiated but not cleared), cleared (funds transferred), failed (transaction rejected), cancelled (voided before clearing), reversed (funds returned after clearing), or reconciled (matched to invoice/payable).

attributes.name: payment_type
attributes.description: Classification of the payment purpose: invoice payment (standard settlement), advance payment (prepayment), refund (return of funds), rebate (volume discount payment), commission (agency fee), or retainer (upfront fee).

attributes.name: reconciliation_date
attributes.description: The date on which the payment was successfully reconciled to its corresponding invoice or bank statement. Null if not yet reconciled.

attributes.name: reconciliation_status
attributes.description: Indicates whether the payment has been matched to its corresponding invoice, payable, or bank statement entry: unreconciled (no match), partially reconciled (partial match), fully reconciled (complete match), or disputed (discrepancy identified).

attributes.name: reference_number
attributes.description: Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this payment transaction for tracking and reconciliation purposes. Used in bank statements and remittance advice.

attributes.name: remittance_advice_reference
attributes.description: Reference number or identifier from the remittance advice document accompanying the payment. Used to match payment to specific invoices or line items.

attributes.name: sap_payment_document_number
attributes.description: The payment document number generated in SAP S/4HANA FI module. Primary reference for integration with the ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) system.

attributes.name: terms
attributes.description: The contractual payment terms applicable to this payment (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30). Indicates the agreed-upon payment schedule and any early payment discounts.

attributes.name: value_date
attributes.description: The date on which the funds become available in the receiving account or are debited from the paying account. Used for cash position and liquidity management.

attributes.name: withholding_tax_amount
attributes.description: The amount of tax withheld from the payment as required by tax regulations. Common for cross-border payments and vendor payments subject to withholding.

products.name: revenue_recognition
products.description: Records governing when and how agency revenue is recognized from client contracts, retainers, and project-based engagements per ASC 606 / IFRS 15 standards. Captures contract reference, performance obligation description, recognition method (straight-line, milestone, percentage-of-completion), recognition start/end date, total contract value, recognized amount to date, deferred revenue amount, recognition status, fiscal period, and SAP FI revenue recognition document reference. Critical for accurate P&L reporting.

attributes.name: revenue_recognition_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the revenue recognition record. Primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the client (advertiser) for whom revenue is being recognized.

attributes.name: agency_relationship_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.agency_relationship. Business justification: Revenue recognition in advertising agencies is governed by the fee structure of the agency relationship (retainer, commission, project). Linking revenue_recognition to agency_relationship supports rel

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the client contract or Statement of Work (SOW) that governs this revenue recognition event.
attributes.references: ASC 606 - Revenue from Contracts with Customers

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: ASC 606 / IFRS 15 revenue recognition in advertising agencies is performed at the brand level as the performance obligation unit. Brand-level revenue recognition reporting is a standard finance requir

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertising campaign associated with this revenue recognition, if applicable.

attributes.name: client_invoice_id
attributes.description: Reference to the client invoice associated with this revenue recognition event, if revenue has been billed.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Revenue recognition is attributed to cost centers for P&L reporting. Adding cost_center_id FK replaces natural key reference.

attributes.name: delivery_pacing_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.delivery_pacing. Business justification: ASC 606 percentage-of-completion revenue recognition requires delivery milestone data. Linking revenue_recognition to delivery_pacing supports delivery-based revenue recognition calculations and audit

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue recognition records post to specific GL revenue accounts. Adding gl_account_id FK replaces natural key reference.

attributes.name: media_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.media_insertion_order. Business justification: Revenue recognized against insertion orders for agency commission and service fees - fundamental revenue accounting in advertising.

attributes.name: media_placement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.media_placement. Business justification: Granular ASC 606 revenue recognition at placement level is required when each placement represents a distinct performance obligation. Agency finance teams recognize revenue as placements deliver; this

attributes.name: milestone_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.project_milestone. Business justification: ASC 606/IFRS 15 revenue recognition in advertising agencies is tied to project milestone completion (performance obligations). revenue_recognition has denormalized milestone_name and milestone_date pl

attributes.name: performance_kpi_target_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.performance_kpi_target. Business justification: Revenue recognition for performance contracts depends on meeting KPI targets; ASC 606 performance obligations require target linkage.

attributes.name: plan_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.plan. Business justification: Revenue recognition for media agency services is tied to media plan delivery milestones under ASC 606 percentage-of-completion method. Linking revenue recognition to the media plan enables plan-level

attributes.name: adjustment_reason
attributes.description: Explanation for any adjustment, reversal, or correction made to the revenue recognition record. Required when recognition status is adjusted or reversed.

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: User ID or name of the finance manager or controller who approved this revenue recognition entry, ensuring compliance with ASC 606 and internal controls.
attributes.references: Internal Financial Controls and Approval Workflow

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this revenue recognition entry was approved by the finance manager or controller.
attributes.references: Internal Financial Controls and Approval Workflow

attributes.name: contract_reference_number
attributes.description: External business identifier for the contract or Statement of Work (SOW) document.
attributes.references: ASC 606 - Revenue from Contracts with Customers

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this revenue recognition record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for data lineage and compliance.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this revenue recognition record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

attributes.name: deferred_revenue_amount
attributes.description: Amount of revenue that has been billed or received but not yet recognized, representing the contract liability. Calculated as total contract value minus recognized amount to date.
attributes.references: ASC 606 - Contract Liabilities; SAP S/4HANA FI Deferred Revenue Account

attributes.name: fiscal_period
attributes.description: Fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year when revenue is recognized, typically 1-12 for monthly periods or 1-4 for quarterly periods.

attributes.name: fiscal_quarter
attributes.description: Fiscal quarter (1-4) within the fiscal year for quarterly business review (QBR) and financial reporting purposes.

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: Fiscal year in which this revenue recognition event occurs, used for financial reporting and Profit and Loss (P&L) aggregation.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI - Fiscal Year Variant

attributes.name: is_active
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this revenue recognition record is currently active (True) or has been superseded or voided (False). Used for soft deletes and historical tracking.

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: User ID or name of the person who last modified this revenue recognition record. Part of audit trail for accountability.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this revenue recognition record was last modified. Updated whenever any field changes. Part of audit trail for data lineage.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional notes, comments, or context regarding this revenue recognition event, including any special considerations or exceptions.

attributes.name: percentage_complete
attributes.description: Percentage of completion for this performance obligation, used when recognition method is percentage-of-completion. Value between 0.00 and 100.00.

attributes.name: performance_obligation_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the specific performance obligation being fulfilled (e.g., media planning services, creative production, campaign execution, retainer services).

attributes.name: profit_center_code
attributes.description: Profit center code in SAP S/4HANA CO for segment reporting and Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization (EBITDA) tracking.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA CO - Profit Center Accounting

attributes.name: recognition_end_date
attributes.description: Date when revenue recognition is expected to complete for this performance obligation, typically aligned with contract end date or service completion.

attributes.name: recognition_method
attributes.description: Method used to recognize revenue for this performance obligation per accounting standards. Straight-line for retainers, milestone for project-based, percentage-of-completion for long-term campaigns, point-in-time for discrete deliverables.

attributes.name: recognition_start_date
attributes.description: Date when revenue recognition begins for this performance obligation, typically aligned with contract effective date or service commencement.

attributes.name: recognition_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the revenue recognition record. Pending indicates not yet started, in-progress for ongoing recognition, completed when fully recognized, deferred for postponed recognition, reversed for corrections, adjusted for amendments.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI - Revenue Recognition Status

attributes.name: recognized_amount_to_date
attributes.description: Cumulative revenue amount recognized to date for this performance obligation. Updated as revenue is recognized over time or at point in time.

attributes.name: revenue_type
attributes.description: Classification of revenue stream type. Retainer for ongoing monthly fees, project for fixed-price engagements, commission for percentage-based fees, media markup for agency margin on media buys, production for creative services, consulting for advisory services, performance fee for outcome-based compensation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: retainer|project|commission|media_markup|production|consulting|performance_fee — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: sap_fi_document_number
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA FI document number for the revenue recognition posting, providing traceability to the general ledger entry.

attributes.name: sap_fi_posting_date
attributes.description: Date when the revenue recognition entry was posted in SAP S/4HANA FI general ledger.

attributes.name: service_line
attributes.description: Agency service line or business unit delivering the performance obligation (e.g., Media Planning, Creative Services, Digital Marketing, Account Management, Data Analytics).

attributes.name: total_contract_value
attributes.description: Total monetary value allocated to this specific performance obligation from the contract. Represents the transaction price allocated per ASC 606 Step 4.

attributes.name: unbilled_revenue_amount
attributes.description: Amount of revenue recognized but not yet billed to the client, representing a contract asset or accrued revenue.

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: User ID or name of the person who created this revenue recognition record. Part of audit trail for accountability.

products.name: media_cost_reconciliation
products.description: Reconciliation records matching planned media costs (from IOs and media plans) against actual vendor invoices and third-party delivery confirmations from Mediaocean Prisma, Google Campaign Manager 360, and DSP platforms. Captures media buy reference, IO number, planned cost, billed cost, delivered impressions, reconciled amount, variance amount, variance reason (under-delivery, rate discrepancy, make-good), reconciliation status (open, in-dispute, cleared), billing period, vendor reference, and Mediaocean Prisma reconciliation document number. Core to sequential liability management and client billing integrity.

attributes.name: media_cost_reconciliation_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the media cost reconciliation record. Primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: budget_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.budget_line. Business justification: Media cost reconciliation matches planned media costs against actual vendor costs. The budget_line is the planning instrument that defines the planned_cost_amount, channel_type, and flight dates for a

attributes.name: buy_id
attributes.description: Reference to the media buy that this reconciliation record pertains to. Links to the media buy master record.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.campaign. Business justification: Media cost reconciliation is executed at the campaign level — comparing planned vs. billed media costs per campaign is a core advertising finance process. Enables campaign-level reconciliation reports

attributes.name: click_event_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.click_event. Business justification: Click discrepancy reconciliation between ad server and publisher systems; financial adjustments require linking to click events.

attributes.name: conversion_event_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.conversion_event. Business justification: CPA/CPC media buys require reconciliation of billed costs against conversion events. Finance teams validate vendor invoices by matching billed conversion counts to actual conversion event records duri

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: FK to finance.cost_center

attributes.name: delivery_pacing_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.delivery_pacing. Business justification: Pacing data informs reconciliation of planned vs actual delivery and spend; variance analysis requires pacing metrics.

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.flight. Business justification: Billing periods in advertising align with campaign flights. Reconciling media costs at the flight level (planned impressions/spend per flight vs. vendor invoice) is standard practice. Enables flight-l

attributes.name: io_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.io_line. Business justification: Media cost reconciliation in advertising is performed at the IO line level — each contracted line has its own planned units and spend. Linking reconciliation to the specific IO line enables line-level

attributes.name: line_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.line_item. Business justification: Line-item-level media cost reconciliation is standard in programmatic and direct advertising — each line item has booked impressions/cost that must be reconciled against vendor delivery. Enables granu

attributes.name: media_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.media_insertion_order. Business justification: Reconciliation is performed at the IO level in agency billing operations. The existing io_number plain attribute is a denormalized reference; a proper FK replaces it. IO-level reconciliation is requir

attributes.name: media_placement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.media_placement. Business justification: Media cost reconciliation matches billed costs against contracted placement values. Linking reconciliation records to specific placements enables placement-level billing dispute resolution and varianc

attributes.name: performance_kpi_target_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.performance_kpi_target. Business justification: Media cost reconciliation validates actual delivery against contracted KPI targets (guaranteed CPM, viewability thresholds). Linking to performance_kpi_target supports contractual delivery validation

attributes.name: plan_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.plan_line. Business justification: Reconciliation against plan lines enables planned-vs-actual spend variance reporting at the line level — a standard agency financial control process. Finance teams require this link to produce budget

attributes.name: reconciliation_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.reconciliation. Business justification: Finance-side media cost reconciliation must be matched to the vendor-side reconciliation record to close the billing cycle. Advertising operations teams use this cross-domain match to confirm both par

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Identifier for the media vendor or publisher who provided the media inventory and submitted the invoice.

attributes.name: vendor_invoice_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.invoice. Business justification: Media cost reconciliation is always performed against a specific vendor invoice — comparing billed amounts to planned costs. This is the foundational link for advertising media billing reconciliation

attributes.name: vendor_payable_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.vendor_payable. Business justification: Media cost reconciliations match planned media costs against actual vendor invoices and result in vendor payables being created or adjusted. This FK links the reconciliation to the resulting vendor pa

attributes.name: video_completion_event_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.video_completion_event. Business justification: CPCV video buys are reconciled by matching billed completion counts to actual video completion events. This reconciliation step is mandatory for validating vendor invoices on completion-based video ca

attributes.name: viewability_measurement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.viewability_measurement. Business justification: Viewability discrepancies drive make-goods and financial adjustments; reconciliation requires linking to viewability measurement data.

attributes.name: actual_cpm
attributes.description: The actual cost per thousand impressions calculated from billed cost and delivered impressions. Calculated as (billed_cost_amount / delivered_impressions) * 1000.

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the person who approved the final reconciled amount. Used for accountability and audit trail.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the reconciliation was formally approved for payment processing and client billing. Represents the completion of the reconciliation workflow.

attributes.name: billed_cost_amount
attributes.description: The actual cost amount invoiced by the vendor for the media delivery. Represents the vendor's claim for payment based on delivered impressions or placements.

attributes.name: billing_period_end_date
attributes.description: End date of the billing period covered by this reconciliation record. Defines the conclusion of the media delivery window being reconciled.

attributes.name: billing_period_start_date
attributes.description: Start date of the billing period covered by this reconciliation record. Defines the beginning of the media delivery window being reconciled.

attributes.name: cpm_variance
attributes.description: The difference between planned CPM and actual CPM. Used to identify rate discrepancies and pricing issues.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this reconciliation record was first created in the system. Represents the start of the reconciliation lifecycle.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this reconciliation record. Ensures consistent currency handling across multi-currency campaigns.

attributes.name: delivered_impressions
attributes.description: The actual number of impressions delivered by the vendor as confirmed by third-party ad serving platforms (Google Campaign Manager 360, DSP platforms). Used for delivery variance calculation.

attributes.name: dispute_flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator of whether this reconciliation record is currently under dispute. True indicates active dispute; False indicates no dispute or resolved dispute.

attributes.name: dispute_opened_date
attributes.description: Date when a dispute was formally opened for this reconciliation record. Null if no dispute has been raised.

attributes.name: dispute_resolution_notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes documenting the dispute resolution process, including vendor communications, adjustments made, and final agreement terms.

attributes.name: dispute_resolved_date
attributes.description: Date when the dispute was formally resolved and the reconciliation was cleared. Null if dispute is still open or no dispute was raised.

attributes.name: gl_posting_date
attributes.description: Date when the reconciled amount was posted to the general ledger in SAP S/4HANA FI. Used for financial period closing and P&L reporting.

attributes.name: impression_variance
attributes.description: The difference between planned impressions and delivered impressions. Positive values indicate over-delivery; negative values indicate under-delivery.

attributes.name: impression_variance_percentage
attributes.description: The impression variance expressed as a percentage of planned impressions. Calculated as (impression_variance / planned_impressions) * 100. Used for delivery performance tracking.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this reconciliation record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail.

attributes.name: make_good_flag
attributes.description: Boolean indicator of whether this reconciliation includes make-good impressions provided by the vendor to compensate for under-delivery or quality issues.

attributes.name: make_good_impressions
attributes.description: Number of additional impressions provided by the vendor as make-good compensation. Null or zero if no make-good was provided.

attributes.name: payment_due_date
attributes.description: Date by which payment to the vendor is due based on contract payment terms. Used for accounts payable scheduling and cash flow management.

attributes.name: planned_cost_amount
attributes.description: The originally planned media cost as specified in the insertion order and media plan. Represents the budgeted amount for this media buy.

attributes.name: planned_cpm
attributes.description: The planned cost per thousand impressions as negotiated in the media buy. Used as the baseline rate for cost reconciliation.

attributes.name: planned_impressions
