attributes.description: The number of impressions originally planned and contracted in the insertion order. Used as the baseline for delivery reconciliation.

attributes.name: reconciled_amount
attributes.description: The final agreed-upon cost amount after reconciliation adjustments, dispute resolution, and variance analysis. This is the amount approved for payment and client billing.

attributes.name: reconciliation_document_number
attributes.description: Unique document number generated by Mediaocean Prisma for this reconciliation transaction. Used for audit trail and cross-system reference.

attributes.name: reconciliation_status
attributes.description: Current workflow status of the reconciliation record. Tracks the lifecycle from initial variance detection through dispute resolution to final clearance and payment approval.

attributes.name: third_party_verification_date
attributes.description: Date when the third-party verification data was received or confirmed. Used for audit trail and data freshness tracking.

attributes.name: third_party_verification_source
attributes.description: Name of the third-party ad serving or verification platform that provided the delivered impressions data (e.g., Google Campaign Manager 360, The Trade Desk, Nielsen, Comscore).

attributes.name: variance_amount
attributes.description: The difference between planned cost and reconciled amount. Positive values indicate over-billing or over-delivery; negative values indicate under-delivery or cost savings.

attributes.name: variance_percentage
attributes.description: The variance amount expressed as a percentage of the planned cost. Calculated as (variance_amount / planned_cost_amount) * 100. Used for threshold-based alerting and variance reporting.

attributes.name: variance_reason_code
attributes.description: Standardized code indicating the primary reason for the cost variance. Used for root cause analysis and vendor performance tracking.

attributes.name: variance_reason_description
attributes.description: Detailed free-text explanation of the variance reason, including context, supporting evidence, and resolution notes. Provides audit trail for financial reconciliation.

attributes.name: vendor_reference
attributes.description: External reference identifier provided by the media vendor or publisher for this billing cycle. May include invoice number or billing statement reference.

domains.name: performance
domains.description: Operational domain capturing real-time and near-real-time campaign delivery metrics and performance signals — including impressions, clicks, conversions, ROAS, CPA, CTR, SOV, and viewability. Owns raw delivery event data and KPI actuals at the placement and line-item level. Distinct from analytics aggregations: focuses on operational measurement infrastructure, tracking pixels, conversion tags, and data quality validation.
domains.references: MRC Viewability and Impression Measurement Standards

products.name: impression_event
products.description: Raw delivery event record capturing each individual ad impression served across all channels and placements. Stores the atomic unit of campaign delivery — timestamp, placement, creative, device, geo, and auction-level signals from ad servers (Google Campaign Manager 360) and DSPs (The Trade Desk). Foundation for all downstream delivery measurement and billing reconciliation. Distinct from aggregated metrics: this is the raw event log at the impression grain.

attributes.name: impression_event_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for each individual ad impression event. Primary key for the impression event log. Serves as the atomic unit for all downstream delivery measurement, billing reconciliation, and performance analytics.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Digital Ad Impression Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: activation_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.audience_activation. Business justification: Activations push audience segments to DSPs/platforms where impressions are served. Links impression delivery to the specific activation job, enables match rate analysis, activation performance measure

attributes.name: ad_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.ad. Business justification: Ad-level impression tracking drives frequency capping enforcement and creative rotation analysis. Ad ops must know which specific ad unit served each impression to validate rotation weights and freque

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertiser client on whose behalf this impression was served. Enables client-level reporting and cross-campaign analysis.

attributes.name: asset_id
attributes.description: Reference to the creative asset that was displayed in this impression. Links to creative master data containing asset specifications, format, dimensions, and approval status.

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Brand-level performance reporting is core to advertising operations. Brand managers and clients require impression delivery metrics aggregated by brand for campaign optimization, media mix analysis, a

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent advertising campaign under which this impression was served. Enables campaign-level aggregation and performance rollup.

attributes.name: contract_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_insertion_order. Business justification: IO-level billing reconciliation: impression events must be reconciled against IO committed impressions for invoicing and make-good determination. click_event and conversion_event already carry this FK

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.flight. Business justification: Flight-level impression reporting is essential for budget pacing and delivery reconciliation. Ad ops teams monitor impressions per flight daily to manage spend against flight budgets. No existing FK l

attributes.name: line_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the media plan line item or insertion order line that governs this impression delivery. Critical for pacing, budget tracking, and billing reconciliation.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Insertion Order Standards

attributes.name: lookalike_model_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.lookalike_model. Business justification: Lookalike model performance reporting: when impressions are served to lookalike-expanded audiences, analysts must trace which model drove targeting to evaluate model ROI, reach accuracy, and CPM effic

attributes.name: media_placement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific media placement where this impression was delivered. Links to the media placement master data defining publisher, site, position, and format.

attributes.name: programmatic_deal_id
attributes.description: The identifier for the programmatic guaranteed deal, private marketplace (PMP) deal, or preferred deal under which this impression was transacted. Links to deal master data for supply-path analysis.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Programmatic Deal Standards

attributes.name: publisher_id
attributes.description: Reference to the publisher or media property where the impression was delivered. Links to publisher master data for supply-side analysis and reconciliation.

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Core targeting relationship - every impression is served to a targeted audience segment. Essential for segment performance reporting, ROAS analysis, and optimization decisions. Fundamental to campaign

attributes.name: suppression_list_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.suppression_list. Business justification: Impressions must respect suppression lists (frequency caps, opt-outs, competitive exclusions). Critical for compliance with privacy regulations, honoring user preferences, and preventing over-exposure

attributes.name: trafficking_instruction_id
attributes.description: Reference to the trafficking instruction that governed the delivery of this impression. Links to ad server trafficking configuration for delivery validation and troubleshooting.

attributes.name: trafficking_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.trafficking_order. Business justification: Trafficking QA requires validating that impressions are being served under the correct trafficking order. Ad servers tag impressions with the order that served them; linking enables SLA validation (sl

attributes.name: ad_position
attributes.description: The position of the ad unit on the page or within the app. Indicates placement prominence and impacts viewability and engagement rates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: above_the_fold|below_the_fold|header|footer|sidebar|in_content|interstitial|overlay — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Ad Placement Standards

attributes.name: ad_server_source
attributes.description: The ad serving platform or demand-side platform (DSP) that recorded and delivered this impression. Identifies the operational system of record for this event.

attributes.name: ad_size
attributes.description: The pixel dimensions of the ad creative served (e.g., 300x250, 728x90, 1920x1080). Used for format performance analysis and creative optimization.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Universal Ad Package Standards

attributes.name: audience_segment_ids
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of audience segment IDs that the user matched at impression time. Links to audience taxonomy from DMP (Data Management Platform) or CDP (Customer Data Platform). Used for audience targeting validation and performance analysis.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Audience Taxonomy Standards

attributes.name: bid_price_cpm
attributes.description: The winning bid price for this impression expressed in CPM (Cost Per Mille / cost per thousand impressions). Represents the clearing price in the programmatic auction. Used for media cost analysis and ROAS calculation.

attributes.name: brand_safety_score
attributes.description: A score from 0.00 to 100.00 indicating the brand safety level of the content context where the impression was served. Higher scores indicate safer, more brand-appropriate environments.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Brand Safety Standards; TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group) - Brand Safety Certification

attributes.name: browser
attributes.description: The web browser or app environment in which the impression was displayed (e.g., Chrome, Safari, Firefox, in-app). Used for browser-specific performance and compatibility analysis.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - User Agent Standards

attributes.name: channel
attributes.description: The digital channel or device environment where the impression was delivered. Includes desktop web, mobile web, mobile app, Connected TV (CTV), Over-the-Top (OTT), and Digital Out-of-Home (DOOH).

attributes.name: consent_status
attributes.description: Indicates whether user consent was obtained for data collection and ad personalization at impression time. Critical for GDPR and CCPA compliance. Values: granted (consent obtained), denied (consent refused), not_required (jurisdiction does not require consent), unknown (consent status not captured).
attributes.references: GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) Article 7; CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act); IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Transparency and Consent Framework (TCF)

attributes.name: content_category
attributes.description: The IAB content category or taxonomy classification of the page or app content where the impression was served. Used for contextual targeting validation and brand safety analysis.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Content Taxonomy Standards

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the media cost and bid price. Enables multi-currency campaign analysis and financial reconciliation.

attributes.name: device_type
attributes.description: The category of device on which the impression was rendered. Used for device-level performance analysis and cross-device attribution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: desktop|smartphone|tablet|smart_tv|gaming_console|wearable|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Device Classification Standards

attributes.name: event_source_system
attributes.description: The name and version of the source system that generated this impression event record. Used for data lineage tracking and cross-system reconciliation.

attributes.name: fraud_score
attributes.description: A risk score from 0.00 to 100.00 indicating the likelihood that this impression is fraudulent or invalid. Generated by fraud detection vendors or internal algorithms. Higher scores indicate higher fraud risk.
attributes.references: TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group) - Fraud Detection Standards

attributes.name: frequency_cap_count
attributes.description: The number of times this user has been exposed to this campaign or creative prior to this impression. Used for frequency capping enforcement and ad fatigue analysis.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Frequency Management Standards

attributes.name: geo_city
attributes.description: The city or designated market area (DMA) where the impression was delivered. Enables hyper-local targeting validation and metro-level performance analysis.

attributes.name: geo_country_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO country code representing the geographic location where the impression was delivered. Derived from IP geolocation or device GPS. Used for geo-targeting validation and regional performance analysis.

attributes.name: geo_postal_code
attributes.description: The postal or ZIP code where the impression was delivered. Supports neighborhood-level targeting and local market analysis.

attributes.name: geo_region
attributes.description: The state, province, or region where the impression was delivered. Supports sub-national geographic analysis and local market performance tracking.

attributes.name: impression_timestamp
attributes.description: Exact date and time when the ad impression was served to the end user. Represents the real-world event time of ad delivery. Critical for time-series analysis, dayparting optimization, and billing reconciliation. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Ad Serving Timestamp Standards

attributes.name: impression_type
attributes.description: The format or modality of the ad impression served. Distinguishes between display banners, video ads, native content, audio spots, and rich media executions.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Ad Format Standards

attributes.name: ingestion_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this impression event record was ingested into the lakehouse silver layer. Used for data freshness monitoring and ETL (Extract Transform Load) pipeline auditing. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

attributes.name: ip_address
attributes.description: The IP address of the device that received the impression. Used for geo-targeting validation, fraud detection, and audience verification. May be considered PII under GDPR and CCPA.
attributes.references: GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) Article 4; CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act)

attributes.name: is_autoplay
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the video ad started playing automatically without user initiation. Applicable to video impressions. Impacts user experience and engagement metrics.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Digital Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) Standards

attributes.name: is_bot_traffic
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the impression was identified as non-human bot traffic. Used for invalid traffic (IVT) filtering and billing adjustment.
attributes.references: TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group) - Invalid Traffic (IVT) Standards; MRC (Media Rating Council) - Invalid Traffic Detection

attributes.name: is_muted
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the video or audio ad was muted at the time of impression. Applicable to video and audio impression types. Impacts engagement and completion rates.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Digital Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) Standards

attributes.name: latitude
attributes.description: The latitude coordinate of the impression delivery location. Used for proximity-based targeting validation and location-based analytics, particularly for DOOH and mobile campaigns.

attributes.name: longitude
attributes.description: The longitude coordinate of the impression delivery location. Used for proximity-based targeting validation and location-based analytics, particularly for DOOH and mobile campaigns.

attributes.name: media_cost
attributes.description: The actual media cost incurred for this single impression. Calculated from the bid price or rate card. Used for billing reconciliation, budget tracking, and ROI (Return on Investment) analysis.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Ad Serving and Billing Standards

attributes.name: operating_system
attributes.description: The operating system of the device that rendered the impression (e.g., iOS, Android, Windows, macOS). Supports device compatibility analysis and targeting optimization.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - User Agent Standards

attributes.name: page_url
attributes.description: The full URL of the page where the impression was displayed. Supports contextual analysis, brand safety verification, and content alignment validation.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Content Taxonomy Standards

attributes.name: session_code
attributes.description: The unique identifier for the user session during which this impression was served. Enables session-level analysis and cross-impression journey tracking.

attributes.name: site_domain
attributes.description: The domain name of the website or app where the impression was served. Used for brand safety analysis, contextual targeting validation, and publisher performance tracking.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Content Taxonomy Standards

attributes.name: supply_chain_object
attributes.description: The sellers.json or SupplyChain object captured at impression time, identifying all intermediaries in the programmatic supply chain. Used for supply-path transparency and optimization per IAB ads.txt and sellers.json standards.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Ads.txt and Sellers.json Standards

attributes.name: tcf_consent_string
attributes.business_glossary_term: Transparency and Consent Framework (TCF) Consent String
attributes.description: The IAB Transparency and Consent Framework (TCF) consent string captured at impression time. Encodes user consent choices for data processing purposes. Required for GDPR compliance in programmatic advertising.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Transparency and Consent Framework (TCF) v2.0; GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation)

attributes.name: time_in_view_seconds
attributes.description: The total duration in seconds that the impression was visible in the users viewport. Critical metric for engagement analysis and viewability quality assessment.
attributes.references: MRC (Media Rating Council) - Viewable Impression Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: user_agent
attributes.description: The full user agent string captured at impression time. Contains detailed device, browser, and operating system information for advanced device fingerprinting and fraud detection.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - User Agent Standards

attributes.name: user_identifier
attributes.description: The pseudonymized or hashed user identifier (cookie ID, device ID, or universal ID) associated with this impression. Used for cross-device tracking, frequency management, and attribution. Subject to GDPR and CCPA privacy regulations.
attributes.references: GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) Article 4; CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act); IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - User ID Standards

attributes.name: video_duration_seconds
attributes.description: The total duration of the video ad creative in seconds. Applicable only to video impression types. Used for video completion rate analysis and engagement measurement.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Digital Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) Standards

attributes.name: video_player_size
attributes.description: The size category of the video player at impression time. Applicable to video impressions. Impacts viewability and user engagement.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Digital Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) Standards

attributes.name: viewability_percentage
attributes.description: The percentage of the ad creative that was visible in the viewport at the time of measurement. Ranges from 0.00 to 100.00. Used for granular viewability analysis and quality scoring.
attributes.references: MRC (Media Rating Council) - Viewable Impression Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: viewability_status
attributes.description: Indicates whether the impression met the MRC (Media Rating Council) viewability standard at the time of delivery. Viewable means the ad was in-view per IAB/MRC guidelines (50% of pixels visible for 1+ seconds for display, 2+ seconds for video).
attributes.references: MRC (Media Rating Council) - Viewable Impression Measurement Guidelines; IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) - Viewability Standards

products.name: click_event
products.description: Raw click interaction event record capturing each user click on a served ad unit. Stores click timestamp, placement identifier, creative identifier, redirect URL, device fingerprint, and click validation status (invalid traffic / IVT flags per TAG/MRC standards). Source of CPC billing actuals and CTR computation inputs. Distinct from impression_event: represents a user-initiated interaction, not a passive delivery.

attributes.name: click_event_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for each click event record. Primary key for the click event entity.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab - Digital Ad Event Standards

attributes.name: ad_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.ad. Business justification: Ad-level click attribution is essential for creative A/B testing and rotation weight optimization. Creative strategists compare click rates across ad variants to determine winning creatives. creative_

attributes.name: asset_id
attributes.description: Reference to the creative asset that was clicked. Identifies the specific ad unit, banner, video, or interactive element that received the user interaction.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertising campaign under which this click occurred. Enables campaign-level click aggregation and performance analysis.

attributes.name: contract_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.insertion_order. Business justification: CPC-based IOs require click-level tracking for budget consumption and invoice reconciliation. Enables automated billing validation against contracted click rates and caps. Essential for performance-ba

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.flight. Business justification: Flight-level CTR and click volume reporting is a standard campaign performance dashboard metric. Media planners evaluate click performance per flight to reallocate budget across flights mid-campaign.

attributes.name: impression_event_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent impression event that was served before this click occurred. Links click to the originating ad delivery event.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab - Digital Ad Event Standards

attributes.name: line_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.line_item. Business justification: Line-item CTR is a fundamental optimization metric in programmatic and direct buying. Traders use line-item click data to adjust bids, targeting, and creative rotation. No existing FK on click_event t

attributes.name: media_placement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the media placement where the clicked ad was served. Identifies the specific inventory position and publisher context.

attributes.name: publisher_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.publisher. Business justification: Publisher-level click-through rate reporting is a standard ad ops function. Agencies track CTR by publisher for inventory quality assessment and vendor scorecards. click_event has no publisher_id; joi

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Clicks occur within targeted segments - critical for segment-level CTR analysis, engagement measurement, and audience quality scoring. Enables advertisers to identify high-performing segments and opti

attributes.name: tracking_pixel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.tracking_pixel. Business justification: click_event currently has a `tracking_pixel_fired_flag` BOOLEAN column indicating whether a tracking pixel fired on this click, but no FK to identify WHICH pixel fired. Adding tracking_pixel_id as a n

attributes.name: ad_server_name
attributes.description: The name or identifier of the ad server that recorded and tracked this click event (e.g., Google Campaign Manager 360, Sizmek, Flashtalking). Used for data reconciliation and discrepancy analysis.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab - Ad Serving Standards

attributes.name: attribution_window_hours
attributes.description: The number of hours after the click during which a conversion can be attributed to this click event. Typically set at campaign or advertiser level (e.g., 24, 72, 168 hours).

attributes.name: billing_status
attributes.description: Indicates whether this click event is billable under the cost-per-click (CPC) pricing model. Non-billable clicks include invalid traffic, duplicate clicks, and clicks outside contracted parameters.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab - Billing Standards; MRC Viewability Guidelines

attributes.name: click_fraud_score
attributes.description: Numeric fraud risk score assigned by the invalid traffic detection system, typically ranging from 0 (legitimate) to 100 (high fraud risk). Used for sophisticated IVT filtering and billing dispute resolution.
attributes.references: TAG Invalid Traffic Guidelines; MRC IVT Standards

attributes.name: click_interaction_type
attributes.description: The type of user interaction that triggered the click event. Distinguishes between standard clicks, video overlay clicks, expandable ad interactions, and mobile gestures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard_click|call_to_action|video_overlay|expandable_panel|swipe|tap|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab - Rich Media Guidelines

attributes.name: click_latency_ms
attributes.description: Time elapsed in milliseconds between the impression render and the click event. Used to detect rapid-fire bot clicks and analyze user engagement timing patterns.

attributes.name: click_position_x
attributes.description: Horizontal pixel coordinate of the click within the ad unit. Used for heatmap analysis and creative engagement optimization.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab - Creative Interaction Standards

attributes.name: click_position_y
attributes.description: Vertical pixel coordinate of the click within the ad unit. Used for heatmap analysis and creative engagement optimization.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab - Creative Interaction Standards

attributes.name: click_redirect_url
attributes.description: The destination URL to which the user was redirected after clicking the ad. May include tracking parameters, campaign identifiers, and attribution tokens.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab - Click Tracking Standards

attributes.name: click_source_platform
attributes.description: The platform or channel through which the click was delivered. Distinguishes between web, mobile app, connected TV (CTV), digital out-of-home (DOOH), and other channels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_web|mobile_app|ctv|dooh|email|social|search|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: click_timestamp
attributes.description: Precise date and time when the user clicked the ad unit, captured in UTC. Primary business event timestamp for click-through rate (CTR) calculation and time-series analysis.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab - Event Timestamp Standards

attributes.name: click_validation_status
attributes.description: Validation status indicating whether the click is considered legitimate traffic or flagged as invalid per TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group) and MRC (Media Rating Council) invalid traffic (IVT) detection standards. Determines billability for cost-per-click (CPC) campaigns. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: valid|invalid_bot|invalid_duplicate|invalid_fraud|invalid_datacenter|suspicious|pending_validation — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: TAG Invalid Traffic (IVT) Guidelines; MRC Invalid Traffic Detection Standards

attributes.name: conversion_attributed_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this click event was later attributed to a conversion event within the attribution window. Used for click-to-conversion analysis and return on ad spend (ROAS) calculation.

attributes.name: cpc_rate
attributes.description: The contracted cost per click rate for this event in the campaign currency. Used to calculate click-level revenue and cost-per-acquisition (CPA) metrics.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the CPC rate and billing amounts. Supports multi-currency campaign reconciliation.

attributes.name: data_source_system
attributes.description: The operational system or platform that generated this click event record (e.g., Google Campaign Manager 360, The Trade Desk, Mediaocean Prisma). Used for data lineage tracking and multi-source reconciliation.

attributes.name: device_fingerprint
attributes.description: Hashed or anonymized device identifier derived from browser characteristics, IP address, user agent, and other signals. Used for fraud detection, frequency capping, and cross-session attribution without relying on cookies.
attributes.references: TAG Device Fingerprinting Best Practices; GDPR Article 4

attributes.name: device_type
attributes.description: Category of device on which the click occurred. Supports device-level performance segmentation and cross-device attribution analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: desktop|mobile|tablet|ctv|ott|wearable|console|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab - Device Type Taxonomy

attributes.name: geographic_city
attributes.description: City or metropolitan area where the click occurred, derived from IP geolocation. Supports hyper-local campaign performance analysis.

attributes.name: geographic_country_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the geographic location of the click based on IP geolocation. Supports geo-targeting validation and regional performance analysis.

attributes.name: geographic_region
attributes.description: State, province, or administrative region where the click occurred, derived from IP geolocation. Enables sub-national geographic performance segmentation.

attributes.name: ip_address
attributes.description: The IP address from which the click originated. Used for geographic targeting validation, fraud detection, and datacenter traffic filtering. May be anonymized or hashed per GDPR and CCPA requirements.

attributes.name: ivt_detection_method
attributes.description: The method or layer at which invalid traffic detection was applied: general IVT (GIVT) for automated bot detection, sophisticated IVT (SIVT) for advanced fraud analysis, or manual review.
attributes.references: MRC Invalid Traffic Detection and Filtration Guidelines

attributes.name: record_created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this click event record was first ingested into the lakehouse silver layer. Used for data pipeline monitoring and audit trail purposes.

attributes.name: record_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this click event record was last modified or enriched (e.g., validation status updated, conversion attribution applied). Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.

attributes.name: referrer_url
attributes.description: The URL of the page or application context where the ad was displayed and clicked. Used for contextual analysis and publisher verification.

attributes.name: tracking_pixel_fired_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the click tracking pixel successfully fired and recorded the event. Used for data quality validation and discrepancy troubleshooting.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab - Pixel Tracking Standards

attributes.name: user_agent
attributes.description: The HTTP user agent string reported by the browser or application at the time of the click. Used for device detection, browser identification, and bot filtering.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab - User Agent Standards

products.name: conversion_event
products.description: Conversion attribution event record capturing post-click and post-view conversion actions (purchases, sign-ups, form fills, app installs) tied to a specific campaign, line item, and creative. Stores conversion type, attribution window, attribution model (last-click, data-driven, linear), conversion value, and deduplication key. Source of CPA and ROAS actuals. Linked to conversion tags and tracking pixels deployed on advertiser properties.

attributes.name: conversion_event_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the conversion event record. Primary key.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Conversion Measurement Standards

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the advertiser client who owns this conversion event.

attributes.name: asset_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the creative asset that was served and led to this conversion.

attributes.name: attribution_model_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.attribution_model. Business justification: Each conversion event is attributed using a specific attribution model configuration. The conversion_event table currently has attribution_model (STRING) storing the model name/code. Adding attributio

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Conversion attribution to specific brands is essential for brand-level ROI calculation, brand performance scorecards, and budget allocation decisions. Multi-brand advertisers require separate conversi

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the campaign that drove this conversion event.

attributes.name: click_event_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.click_event. Business justification: Post-click conversions are attributed to specific click events. The conversion_event table currently has click_timestamp (STRING) but no FK to click_event. Adding click_event_id FK enables direct link

attributes.name: contract_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.insertion_order. Business justification: CPA/CPL insertion orders bill on conversion delivery. Direct IO link enables automated billing calculation, pacing against conversion commitments, and contractual performance reporting. Critical for p

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.flight. Business justification: Flight-level conversion reporting drives budget reallocation decisions in phased campaigns. Performance analysts attribute conversions to flights to evaluate which flight periods drove the most effici

attributes.name: impression_event_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.impression_event. Business justification: Post-view conversions are attributed to specific impression events. The conversion_event table currently has impression_timestamp (STRING) but no FK to impression_event. Adding impression_event_id FK

attributes.name: line_item_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the line item within the campaign that generated this conversion.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Ad Serving Standards

attributes.name: lookalike_model_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.lookalike_model. Business justification: Lookalike model conversion validation: advertisers measure conversion lift from lookalike expansion by linking conversions back to the model that generated the audience. This is a standard model valid

attributes.name: media_placement_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the media placement where the ad was displayed.

attributes.name: programmatic_deal_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.programmatic_deal. Business justification: Deal-level conversion attribution is a standard programmatic reporting requirement — advertisers evaluate which PMP/PG deals drive conversions to optimize deal selection. conversion_event has media_pl

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Conversions attributed to audience segments - fundamental for calculating segment-level ROAS, CPA, and conversion rates. Enables audience valuation, budget allocation decisions, and segment performanc

attributes.name: tracking_pixel_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.tracking_pixel. Business justification: Conversion events are generated by tracking pixels/conversion tags deployed on advertiser sites. The conversion_event table has conversion_tag_id (STRING) which is a denormalized reference. Adding tra

attributes.name: attribution_type
attributes.description: Indicates whether this conversion was attributed to a click-through or view-through interaction.

attributes.name: attribution_window_days
attributes.description: The number of days after ad interaction (click or impression) during which a conversion can be attributed to that interaction.

attributes.name: browser
attributes.description: The web browser used by the user at the time of conversion (e.g., Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge).
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab User Agent Standards

attributes.name: conversion_currency
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the conversion value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

attributes.name: conversion_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name or label for the specific conversion action as defined by the advertiser (e.g., 'Product Purchase', 'Newsletter Signup', 'Demo Request').
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Conversion Measurement Standards

attributes.name: conversion_page_url
attributes.description: The URL of the advertiser page where the conversion action was completed.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Conversion Measurement Standards

attributes.name: conversion_quantity
attributes.description: The number of units or items involved in the conversion (e.g., number of products purchased, number of form submissions).
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Conversion Measurement Standards

attributes.name: conversion_status
attributes.description: The validation status of the conversion event (e.g., confirmed, pending verification, rejected, flagged as fraud).
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Conversion Validation Standards

attributes.name: conversion_timestamp
attributes.description: The exact date and time when the conversion action occurred on the advertiser property. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Conversion Measurement Standards

attributes.name: conversion_type
attributes.description: The category of conversion action that occurred (e.g., purchase, sign-up, form fill, app install, lead generation, download).

attributes.name: conversion_value
attributes.description: The monetary value associated with this conversion event (e.g., purchase amount, estimated lead value). Used to calculate ROAS (Return on Ad Spend).
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Conversion Measurement Standards

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this conversion event record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Data Governance Standards

attributes.name: data_source
attributes.description: The system or platform that captured and reported this conversion event (e.g., Google Campaign Manager 360, The Trade Desk, Salesforce Marketing Cloud).
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Data Source Standards

attributes.name: deduplication_key
attributes.description: Unique key used to prevent duplicate counting of the same conversion event across multiple tracking sources or attribution models.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Conversion Deduplication Standards

attributes.name: device_type
attributes.description: The type of device on which the conversion occurred (e.g., desktop, mobile, tablet, CTV - Connected TV, other).

attributes.name: fraud_score
attributes.description: A numeric score (0-100) indicating the likelihood that this conversion is fraudulent, as determined by fraud detection systems.

attributes.name: geo_city
attributes.description: City where the conversion occurred, derived from IP geolocation.

attributes.name: geo_country
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the conversion occurred (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).

attributes.name: geo_region
attributes.description: State, province, or region where the conversion occurred (e.g., California, Ontario, Bavaria).

attributes.name: ip_address
attributes.description: The IP address of the user at the time of conversion. May be hashed or truncated for privacy compliance.

attributes.name: is_duplicate
attributes.description: Flag indicating whether this conversion event was identified as a duplicate and excluded from reporting (True/False).
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Conversion Deduplication Standards

attributes.name: landing_page_url
attributes.description: The URL of the advertiser page where the user landed after clicking the ad.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Landing Page Standards

attributes.name: operating_system
attributes.description: The operating system of the device on which the conversion occurred (e.g., Windows, macOS, iOS, Android).
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab User Agent Standards

attributes.name: order_reference
attributes.description: The advertiser's internal order or transaction identifier associated with this conversion (e.g., e-commerce order number).
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Conversion Measurement Standards

attributes.name: product_category
attributes.description: The category of product or service involved in the conversion (e.g., Electronics, Apparel, Financial Services).

attributes.name: referrer_url
attributes.description: The URL of the page from which the user navigated to the conversion page.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this conversion event record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Data Governance Standards

attributes.name: user_identifier
attributes.description: Pseudonymized or hashed identifier for the user who completed the conversion action. Used for cross-device and cross-session attribution.

products.name: viewability_measurement
products.description: Viewability measurement record per impression or placement-day, capturing MRC-compliant viewability signals: in-view percentage, time-in-view (seconds), viewable impression count, measurable impression count, and viewability rate. Sourced from third-party measurement vendors (Comscore, IAS, DoubleVerify) and ad server signals. Supports viewability-guaranteed IO commitments and brand safety compliance reporting per MRC and IAB standards.

attributes.name: viewability_measurement_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the viewability measurement record. Primary key.

attributes.name: asset_id
attributes.description: Reference to the creative asset that was served and measured for viewability.

attributes.name: buy_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.buy. Business justification: Viewability guarantees are contracted at the buy level (guaranteed_viewability buys). viewability_measurement results must be reconciled against the buy to verify contractual compliance and trigger ma

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertising campaign associated with this viewability measurement.

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.flight. Business justification: Flight-level viewability reporting validates IO commitments scoped to specific flight windows. Guaranteed viewability IOs are often structured per flight; viewability_guaranteed_io_flag on this table

attributes.name: impression_event_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the individual ad impression being measured. Links to ad server impression logs.

attributes.name: line_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.line_item. Business justification: Line-item viewability tracking is required for contractual compliance on guaranteed viewability buys. Ad ops teams report viewability_rate per line_item against viewability_target_percentage on the li

attributes.name: media_placement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the media placement being measured for viewability.

attributes.name: programmatic_deal_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.programmatic_deal. Business justification: Programmatic deals specify viewability_threshold_pct as a contractual term. Deal health dashboards compare viewability_measurement.viewability_rate against programmatic_deal.viewability_threshold_pct.

attributes.name: publisher_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.publisher. Business justification: Publisher-level viewability rate reporting is a core ad ops and vendor scorecard function. viewability_measurement already has supplier_id (measurement vendor) but lacks publisher_id (inventory owner)

attributes.name: sla_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sla. Business justification: Viewability SLAs are common contractual guarantees in media agreements. Direct link enables automated compliance monitoring, breach detection, penalty calculation, and make-good triggering. Core to co

attributes.name: spec_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.spec. Business justification: Viewability measurement is validated against the ad spec's dimensions and IAB standard unit. Spec defines guaranteed_viewability_threshold context; linking enables spec-level viewability compliance re

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Viewability measurements are provided by third-party verification vendors (IAS, MOAT, DoubleVerify). Agencies need FK to supplier for vendor performance scorecards, contract compliance tracking, discr

attributes.name: ad_format
attributes.description: The format type of the ad unit being measured (display, video, native, rich media, interstitial, expandable).
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) Ad Format Standards

attributes.name: audible_on_complete_flag
attributes.description: For video ads, indicates whether the ad was audible when the video completed. Supports audible and viewable measurement.

attributes.name: brand_safety_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the placement passed brand safety verification at the time of viewability measurement.
attributes.references: TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group) Brand Safety Standards

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this viewability measurement record was first created in the data platform.

attributes.name: data_quality_score
attributes.description: A quality score (0-100) assigned by the measurement vendor indicating the reliability and accuracy of the viewability measurement.

attributes.name: device_type
attributes.description: The type of device on which the ad was served and measured (desktop, mobile, tablet, CTV, OTT, DOOH).

attributes.name: environment
attributes.description: The digital environment where the ad was served (web browser, in-app, CTV, DOOH).

attributes.name: guaranteed_viewability_threshold
attributes.description: The contractual viewability rate threshold (percentage) guaranteed in the insertion order, if applicable.

attributes.name: in_view_percentage
attributes.description: The percentage of the ad creative pixels that were visible in the viewport at the time of measurement.

attributes.name: invalid_traffic_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the impression was flagged as invalid traffic (bot, fraud, or non-human traffic) by the measurement vendor.
attributes.references: TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group) Invalid Traffic Standards

attributes.name: measurability_rate
attributes.description: The percentage of total impressions that were measurable for viewability, calculated as (measurable_impression_count / total_impression_count) * 100.

attributes.name: measurable_impression_count
attributes.description: The total number of impressions for which viewability could be measured (excludes unmeasurable impressions due to technical limitations).

attributes.name: measurement_date
attributes.description: The calendar date of the viewability measurement, used for daily aggregation and reporting.

attributes.name: measurement_method
attributes.description: The technical methodology used to measure viewability (geometric, browser optimization, or reach extension).

attributes.name: measurement_status
attributes.description: The current status of the viewability measurement record (measured, unmeasurable, pending, error).

attributes.name: measurement_timestamp
attributes.description: The exact date and time when the viewability measurement was captured. Principal business event timestamp for this measurement record.

attributes.name: mrc_compliant_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this viewability measurement meets MRC accreditation standards and methodology requirements.

attributes.name: player_size_height
attributes.description: The height of the video player or ad container in pixels at the time of measurement.

attributes.name: player_size_width
attributes.description: The width of the video player or ad container in pixels at the time of measurement.

attributes.name: source_system
attributes.description: The operational system or platform that originated this viewability measurement record (e.g., Google Campaign Manager 360, Comscore, The Trade Desk).

attributes.name: time_in_view_seconds
attributes.description: The total duration in seconds that the ad was in-view (meeting the pixel threshold) during the measurement window.

attributes.name: total_impression_count
attributes.description: The total number of ad impressions served, including both measurable and unmeasurable impressions.

attributes.name: unmeasurable_reason
attributes.description: The reason why viewability could not be measured for this impression (e.g., cross-domain iframe, ad blocker, technical limitation).

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this viewability measurement record was last updated or modified.

attributes.name: video_quartile_reached
attributes.description: For video ads, the furthest quartile reached while the ad was viewable (0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%). Not applicable for non-video formats.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) Video Ad Serving Template (VAST)

attributes.name: viewability_guaranteed_io_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this impression is part of a viewability-guaranteed insertion order commitment.

attributes.name: viewability_rate
attributes.description: The percentage of measurable impressions that were viewable, calculated as (viewable_impression_count / measurable_impression_count) * 100.

attributes.name: viewability_standard
attributes.description: The viewability standard applied for this measurement (MRC display: 50%/1s, MRC video: 50%/2s, GroupM, or custom client standard).

attributes.name: viewable_impression_count
attributes.description: The number of impressions that met the MRC viewability standard (50% of pixels in-view for 1 second for display, 2 seconds for video).

products.name: tracking_pixel
products.description: Master record and complete operational event log for each tracking pixel or conversion tag deployed on advertiser web properties, app environments, or landing pages. Header section stores pixel definition: name, type (impression tracker, click tracker, conversion tag, retargeting pixel, audience pixel), implementation method (JavaScript, image pixel, server-to-server), firing rules, consent flags, and deployment status. Detail section stores the complete fire event history: individual fire timestamps, firing URL, user agent, IP hash (GDPR-compliant), referrer URL, custom parameters passed, fire status (success/blocked/timeout), and error details. Single source of truth for all pixel infrastructure configuration AND raw fire event data. Serves as the unified measurement infrastructure registry, pixel health monitoring source, and raw fire event stream for conversion deduplication and data quality validation. Supports GDPR/CCPA consent compliance workflows and tag governance audits.

attributes.name: tracking_pixel_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the tracking pixel or conversion tag record. Primary key.

attributes.name: ad_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.ad. Business justification: Ad-level pixel assignment is standard for impression and click tracking tags embedded in specific ad units. Trafficking teams assign view_tracker_url and impression_tracker_url pixels to individual ad

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertiser client whose property or campaign this pixel is deployed on.

attributes.name: agency_relationship_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.agency_relationship. Business justification: Pixel deployment is governed by the agency relationship agreement — consent requirements, data sharing rights, and pixel ownership are defined per AOR. Pixel audits and agency transitions require know

attributes.name: asset_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.creative_asset. Business justification: Tracking pixels are deployed within specific creative assets for conversion tracking. Trafficking and analytics teams need this link to validate pixel deployment, troubleshoot firing issues, and recon

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Tracking pixels are deployed for brand-specific conversion measurement. Brand managers need to audit which pixels are active for their brands, manage brand-specific conversion definitions, and ensure

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertising campaign this pixel is associated with for measurement purposes.

attributes.name: creative_deliverable_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.creative_deliverable. Business justification: Tracking pixels are deployed against specific creative deliverables for conversion and interaction tracking. Ad ops teams associate pixels with deliverables during trafficking — a direct operational d

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.flight. Business justification: Pixels are deployed with flight-scoped lookback windows to capture conversions within specific flight periods. Flight-level pixel deployment is standard practice for phased campaigns where each flight

attributes.name: line_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.line_item. Business justification: Line-item-level pixel deployment enables granular conversion attribution for programmatic and direct line items. Ad ops assigns specific pixels to line items to measure post-click and post-view conver

attributes.name: media_placement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific media placement or ad unit this pixel is tracking.

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.audience_segment. Business justification: Pixel-to-segment mapping for retargeting: tracking pixels are deployed to populate specific audience segments (e.g., site-visitor retargeting pixel feeds a 'cart abandoner' segment). This link is requ

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: SOWs define measurement and tracking requirements as contractual deliverables. Linking pixels to SOWs enables scope validation, ensures deployed tracking matches contracted specifications, and support

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Tracking pixels are often provided by third-party tag management or analytics vendors (Google Tag Manager, Tealium, Adobe). Required for troubleshooting pixel fires, SLA management, vendor consolidati

attributes.name: tech_partner_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.tech_partner. Business justification: Pixels are issued by tech partners (e.g., CM360 Floodlight, DSP conversion tags). Linking pixel to originating tech partner is required for data governance audits, tech stack rationalization, and GDPR

attributes.name: trafficking_instruction_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.trafficking_instruction. Business justification: Trafficking instructions specify pixel deployment (impression_tracking_pixel_url, vast_tag_url, viewability_tag_url). Pixels are created and deployed as part of trafficking instructions. A trafficking

attributes.name: browser_name
attributes.description: Name of the web browser in which the pixel fired, parsed from the user agent string.

attributes.name: consent_category
attributes.description: Privacy consent category under which this pixel operates, determining when it may fire based on user consent preferences.

attributes.name: consent_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether user consent is required before the pixel can fire, per privacy regulations.

attributes.name: conversion_currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the conversion value.

attributes.name: conversion_value
attributes.description: Monetary value associated with a conversion event tracked by this pixel, used for ROAS calculation.

attributes.name: created_by_user
attributes.description: Username or identifier of the person who created this tracking pixel configuration.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this tracking pixel record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: custom_parameters
attributes.description: Additional key-value pairs or query string parameters passed with the pixel fire for custom tracking dimensions.

attributes.name: deduplication_key
attributes.description: Unique identifier used to prevent duplicate counting of the same conversion event across multiple pixels or platforms.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Measurement Guidelines; MRC Standards

attributes.name: deployment_environment
attributes.description: The digital environment or platform type where the pixel is implemented.

attributes.name: deployment_status
attributes.description: Current operational status of the tracking pixel in the measurement infrastructure.

attributes.name: deployment_url
attributes.description: The advertiser web page, app screen, or landing page URL where this pixel is deployed.

attributes.name: device_type
attributes.description: Classification of the device on which the pixel fired, derived from user agent analysis.

attributes.name: error_code
attributes.description: Technical error code returned when a pixel fire fails, used for troubleshooting and data quality monitoring.

attributes.name: error_message
attributes.description: Human-readable description of the error that occurred during pixel fire, providing diagnostic detail.

attributes.name: fire_status
attributes.description: Outcome status of the pixel fire attempt, indicating whether the tracking event was successfully recorded.

attributes.name: fire_timestamp
attributes.description: Exact date and time when the pixel fire event occurred, representing the principal business event for this tracking record.

attributes.name: firing_rule
attributes.description: Business logic or condition that determines when the pixel should fire (e.g., page load, button click, form submission, time on page).

attributes.name: geo_city
attributes.description: City derived from IP geolocation at time of pixel fire.

attributes.name: geo_country_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code derived from IP geolocation at time of pixel fire.

attributes.name: geo_region
attributes.description: State, province, or region derived from IP geolocation at time of pixel fire.

attributes.name: implementation_method
attributes.description: Technical method used to deploy and fire the tracking pixel on the target property.

attributes.name: ip_address_hash
attributes.description: One-way cryptographic hash of the user IP address, stored for fraud detection and geographic analysis while maintaining GDPR compliance.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 32; IAB Tech Lab Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: lookback_window_days
attributes.description: Number of days after an ad interaction during which a conversion can be attributed to that interaction.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Measurement Guidelines; MRC Standards

attributes.name: modified_by_user
attributes.description: Username or identifier of the person who last modified this tracking pixel configuration.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this tracking pixel record was last updated or modified.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-form text field for additional comments, implementation instructions, or troubleshooting notes related to this pixel.

attributes.name: operating_system
attributes.description: Operating system of the device on which the pixel fired, parsed from the user agent string.

attributes.name: pixel_code
attributes.description: Unique external code or identifier for the pixel used in trafficking and reporting systems.

attributes.name: pixel_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name assigned to the tracking pixel for identification and management purposes.

attributes.name: pixel_type
attributes.description: Classification of the tracking pixel based on its measurement purpose and firing trigger.

attributes.name: pixel_url
attributes.description: Full URL endpoint that the pixel fires to when triggered, including protocol and domain.

attributes.name: referrer_url
attributes.description: The URL of the page that referred the user to the page where the pixel fired, used for traffic source analysis.

attributes.name: user_agent
attributes.description: Browser or application user agent string captured at the time of pixel fire, used for device and browser classification.

products.name: delivery_pacing
products.description: Real-time and near-real-time delivery pacing record tracking actual vs. planned delivery progress for each campaign line item and placement. Stores pacing period (hourly, daily), planned impression/spend target, actual delivered impressions/spend, pacing percentage, pacing status (on-pace, under-delivering, over-delivering), and recommended bid/budget adjustment signals. Sourced from Google Campaign Manager 360 and The Trade Desk pacing APIs. Critical for operational campaign management and budget stewardship.

attributes.name: delivery_pacing_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the delivery pacing record. Primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: activation_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.activation. Business justification: Activation-level delivery pacing: pacing records monitor spend and impression delivery for a specific audience activation (the push of a segment to a DSP). Linking pacing to activation enables activat

attributes.name: buy_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.buy. Business justification: Delivery pacing monitors actual vs. authorized spend against executed buys. buy.authorized_spend_ceiling and buy.delivery_status are the operational targets for pacing alerts. Media ops teams run paci

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent campaign for which delivery pacing is being tracked.

attributes.name: deliverable_tracker_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.deliverable_tracker. Business justification: Campaign delivery pacing records must reconcile against project deliverable status for campaign health reporting and client billing. Advertising ops teams routinely cross-reference actual impression/s

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.flight. Business justification: Flight is the primary operational unit for pacing management. Delivery pacing monitors spend and impression delivery against flight budgets; pacing alerts are flight-scoped. No existing FK links deliv

attributes.name: io_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_io_line. Business justification: Pacing monitors delivery against contracted IO line commitments. Required for under-delivery alerts, budget reallocation decisions, vendor delivery performance tracking, and triggering makegood clause

attributes.name: line_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific campaign line item being paced. Line items represent individual media buys or placements within a campaign.

attributes.name: media_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.media_insertion_order. Business justification: IO-level pacing is a primary operational report — pacing against media_insertion_order.authorized_spend_amount and guaranteed_impressions. delivery_pacing has contract_insertion_order_id (different do

attributes.name: media_placement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific media placement being tracked for delivery pacing.

attributes.name: pacing_rule_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.pacing_rule. Business justification: Pacing rules define the algorithm (even, front-loaded, custom curve) governing delivery; delivery_pacing records actual execution against those rules. Linking enables pacing rule performance auditing

attributes.name: plan_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.plan_line. Business justification: Plan lines define planned_impressions and planned_spend — the targets against which pacing is measured. delivery_pacing has line_item_id (campaign domain) but not plan_line_id (media domain). Plan-vs-

attributes.name: programmatic_deal_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.programmatic_deal. Business justification: Programmatic deal pacing against impression_commitment is a named DSP operational process. delivery_pacing monitors spend and impression delivery; programmatic_deal.impression_commitment is the contra

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.audience_segment. Business justification: Segment-level pacing monitoring: programmatic ops teams track delivery pacing broken down by audience segment to identify which segments are over- or under-delivering against plan, enabling real-time

attributes.name: sla_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sla. Business justification: SLA breach alerting: delivery_pacing monitors real-time spend and impression delivery against plan. Linking to the governing SLA enables automated breach threshold monitoring, escalation triggering, a

attributes.name: trafficking_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.trafficking_order. Business justification: Trafficking orders are the ad server instructions that drive delivery; pacing records measure whether those instructions are delivering correctly. Linking enables diagnosis of pacing issues caused by

attributes.name: actual_impressions
attributes.description: The actual number of impressions delivered during this pacing period as reported by the ad server or demand-side platform (DSP).

attributes.name: actual_spend_amount
attributes.description: The actual spend amount incurred during this pacing period as reported by the billing system or DSP.

attributes.name: alert_severity
attributes.description: The severity level of the pacing alert if triggered. None means no alert, low/medium/high/critical indicate escalating levels of deviation requiring intervention.

attributes.name: bid_win_rate
attributes.description: For programmatic campaigns, the percentage of bid requests that resulted in won auctions during this pacing period. Low win rates may indicate bid prices are too low.

attributes.name: click_through_rate
attributes.description: The percentage of impressions that resulted in clicks during this pacing period, calculated as (clicks / impressions) * 100. Key performance indicator for engagement.

attributes.name: conversion_count
attributes.description: The total number of conversions attributed to this line item or placement during the pacing period, based on conversion tracking pixels and attribution models.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this pacing record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Part of audit trail for data lineage.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the spend amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

attributes.name: data_source_system
attributes.description: The operational system from which this pacing data was sourced. Typically Google Campaign Manager 360 for direct ad serving or The Trade Desk for programmatic campaigns.

attributes.name: delivery_method
attributes.description: The delivery pacing strategy configured for this line item. Standard spreads delivery evenly, accelerated delivers as fast as possible, even maintains consistent daily delivery, ASAP prioritizes immediate delivery, frontloaded concentrates delivery early in the flight.

attributes.name: effective_cpa
attributes.description: The effective cost per conversion for this pacing period, calculated as actual_spend_amount / conversion_count. Key metric for performance-based campaigns.

attributes.name: effective_cpm
attributes.description: The effective cost per thousand impressions for this pacing period, calculated as (actual_spend_amount / actual_impressions) * 1000. Used to evaluate media efficiency.

attributes.name: frequency_cap_reached
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the frequency cap has been reached for this line item during the pacing period, which may impact delivery velocity.

attributes.name: impression_pacing_percentage
attributes.description: The percentage of planned impressions that have been delivered, calculated as (actual_impressions / planned_impressions) * 100. Values above 100 indicate over-delivery.

attributes.name: inventory_availability_score
attributes.description: A score (0-100) representing the availability of ad inventory matching the targeting criteria during this pacing period. Low scores may explain under-delivery.

attributes.name: is_active
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this pacing record represents the current active measurement period. False indicates historical or superseded records.

attributes.name: measurement_timestamp
attributes.description: The exact timestamp when this pacing measurement was captured from the source system. Critical for understanding data freshness and real-time vs near-real-time reporting.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes from campaign managers or automated systems explaining pacing anomalies, corrective actions taken, or contextual information about delivery performance.

attributes.name: pacing_alert_triggered
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether an automated pacing alert was triggered for this period due to significant variance from plan, requiring campaign manager attention.

attributes.name: pacing_granularity
attributes.description: The time granularity at which pacing is measured and reported. Hourly for real-time optimization, daily for standard monitoring, weekly for longer flights, or flight for entire campaign duration.

attributes.name: pacing_period_end
attributes.description: The end timestamp of the pacing measurement period. Defines the conclusion of the window for which delivery progress is being measured.

attributes.name: pacing_period_start
attributes.description: The start timestamp of the pacing measurement period. Defines the beginning of the window for which delivery progress is being measured.

attributes.name: pacing_status
attributes.description: Current delivery pacing status indicating whether the line item or placement is delivering as planned. On-pace means within acceptable variance, under-delivering means behind target, over-delivering means ahead of target, paused means delivery is stopped, completed means flight has ended, at-risk means significant deviation detected.

attributes.name: pacing_variance_threshold
attributes.description: The acceptable variance threshold percentage used to determine pacing status. If actual pacing percentage deviates from 100% by more than this threshold, status changes from on-pace to under/over-delivering.

attributes.name: planned_impressions
attributes.description: The target number of impressions planned to be delivered during this pacing period based on the media plan and flight schedule.

attributes.name: planned_spend_amount
attributes.description: The target spend amount planned for this pacing period based on the media budget allocation and flight schedule.

attributes.name: quality_score
attributes.description: Platform-assigned quality score (typically 0-10 scale) reflecting ad relevance, landing page experience, and expected CTR. Higher scores improve auction competitiveness.

attributes.name: recommended_bid_adjustment
attributes.description: System-generated recommendation for bid adjustment to correct pacing issues. Positive values suggest increasing bids to accelerate delivery, negative values suggest decreasing bids to slow delivery.

attributes.name: recommended_budget_adjustment
attributes.description: System-generated recommendation for budget adjustment to correct pacing issues. Positive values suggest increasing budget allocation, negative values suggest reducing budget allocation.

attributes.name: source_record_reference
attributes.description: The unique identifier of this pacing record in the source operational system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.

attributes.name: spend_pacing_percentage
attributes.description: The percentage of planned spend that has been consumed, calculated as (actual_spend_amount / planned_spend_amount) * 100. Values above 100 indicate over-spend.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this pacing record was last updated in the lakehouse silver layer. Tracks data refresh cycles and enables incremental processing.

attributes.name: viewability_rate
attributes.description: The percentage of delivered impressions that met viewability standards (MRC: 50% of pixels in view for 1+ seconds for display, 2+ seconds for video) during this pacing period.

products.name: attribution_model
products.description: Configuration master defining conversion attribution models, rules, and parameters for each advertiser and campaign. Stores model type (last-click, first-click, linear, time-decay, position-based, data-driven/algorithmic, custom), attribution windows (view-through duration, click-through duration), cross-device graph enablement, cross-channel scope, lookback window constraints, fractional credit distribution weights, model training metadata (for data-driven models), and activation status with effective date range. Governs how conversion_event records distribute credit across campaign touchpoints. Model changes trigger reprocessing of historical conversions within the affected attribution window. Supports A/B model comparison and incrementality testing workflows.

attributes.name: attribution_model_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the attribution model configuration. Primary key.

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertiser for whom this attribution model is configured.

attributes.name: agency_relationship_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.agency_relationship. Business justification: Attribution methodology is negotiated and agreed upon within the agency relationship contract. Attribution disputes between agencies and advertisers are resolved by referencing the model agreed under

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Different brands within the same advertiser use different attribution models (e.g., luxury brand uses data-driven, mass-market uses last-touch). Brand-level attribution configuration is standard in mu

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Attribution models provided by third-party platforms (Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics, Neustar, Visual IQ). Needed for licensing management, support escalation, model validation, platform fee alloca

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Username or identifier of the user who approved this attribution model configuration for activation. Null if not yet approved.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this attribution model configuration was approved for activation. Null if not yet approved.

attributes.name: attribution_model_description
