attributes.description: Detailed business description of the attribution model configuration, including rationale, use cases, and any special rules or exclusions.

attributes.name: attribution_model_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the attribution model configuration. Active models are applied to conversion events; inactive models are retained for historical reference; draft models are under development; archived models are deprecated; testing models are in A/B comparison.

attributes.name: channel_scope
attributes.description: Defines which marketing channels this attribution model includes in the customer journey. May be a comma-separated list of channel codes (e.g., 'display,search,social,video') or 'all' for universal application.

attributes.name: click_through_window_days
attributes.description: Number of days after a click during which a conversion can be attributed to that click event. Standard values range from 1 to 90 days.
attributes.references: IAB Attribution Standards; MRC Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: comparison_group_code
attributes.description: Identifier linking this attribution model to an A/B testing or incrementality testing group for model comparison workflows. Null if not part of a comparison test.

attributes.name: conversion_event_scope
attributes.description: Defines which conversion events this attribution model applies to. May be a comma-separated list of conversion event types (e.g., 'purchase,lead_form,signup') or 'all' for universal application.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this attribution model configuration record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: cross_channel_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether the attribution model tracks and attributes conversions across multiple marketing channels (display, search, social, video, email, OOH, CTV, etc.).

attributes.name: cross_device_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether the attribution model uses cross-device identity graph to track and attribute conversions across multiple devices (desktop, mobile, tablet, CTV) for the same user.

attributes.name: data_driven_algorithm_version
attributes.description: For data-driven (algorithmic) attribution models, the version identifier of the machine learning algorithm or model used to calculate fractional credit distribution. Null for rule-based models.

attributes.name: data_driven_minimum_conversions
attributes.description: For data-driven attribution models, the minimum number of conversions required in the training dataset for the model to be statistically valid. Null for rule-based models.
attributes.references: IAB Attribution Standards; MRC Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: data_driven_minimum_paths
attributes.description: For data-driven attribution models, the minimum number of unique customer journey paths required in the training dataset for the model to be statistically valid. Null for rule-based models.
attributes.references: IAB Attribution Standards; MRC Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: data_driven_training_end_date
attributes.description: For data-driven attribution models, the end date of the historical data period used to train the machine learning model. Null for rule-based models.

attributes.name: data_driven_training_start_date
attributes.description: For data-driven attribution models, the start date of the historical data period used to train the machine learning model. Null for rule-based models.

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which this attribution model configuration ceases to be active. Null for open-ended models.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date from which this attribution model configuration becomes active and begins applying to conversion events.

attributes.name: lookback_window_days
attributes.description: Maximum number of days to look back in the customer journey when attributing conversions. Defines the outer boundary for all touchpoint consideration.

attributes.name: model_code
attributes.description: Short alphanumeric code or identifier for the attribution model used in reporting and system integration.

attributes.name: model_name
attributes.description: Business-friendly name for the attribution model configuration (e.g., 'Q1 2024 Last-Click Model', 'Brand Awareness Linear Model').

attributes.name: model_type
attributes.description: The type of attribution methodology applied: last-click (100% credit to final touchpoint), first-click (100% credit to initial touchpoint), linear (equal credit across all touchpoints), time-decay (exponentially increasing credit toward conversion), position-based (U-shaped: 40% first, 40% last, 20% distributed), or data-driven (algorithmic/machine-learning-based credit distribution).
attributes.references: IAB Attribution Standards; Google Analytics Attribution Models

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: Username or identifier of the user who last modified this attribution model configuration.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this attribution model configuration record was last modified.

attributes.name: position_based_first_touch_weight
attributes.description: For position-based (U-shaped) attribution models, the fractional credit weight assigned to the first touchpoint (e.g., 0.4000 for 40%). Null for non-position-based models.

attributes.name: position_based_last_touch_weight
attributes.description: For position-based (U-shaped) attribution models, the fractional credit weight assigned to the last touchpoint (e.g., 0.4000 for 40%). Null for non-position-based models.

attributes.name: position_based_middle_touch_weight
attributes.description: For position-based (U-shaped) attribution models, the fractional credit weight distributed equally among all middle touchpoints (e.g., 0.2000 for 20% total). Null for non-position-based models.

attributes.name: reprocessing_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether historical conversion events within the attribution window need to be reprocessed due to changes in this model configuration.

attributes.name: reprocessing_start_date
attributes.description: The earliest date from which historical conversion events should be reprocessed when model configuration changes. Null if no reprocessing is required.

attributes.name: time_decay_half_life_days
attributes.description: For time-decay attribution models, the number of days at which a touchpoint receives half the credit of the most recent touchpoint. Defines the exponential decay rate. Null for non-time-decay models.

attributes.name: view_through_window_days
attributes.description: Number of days after an impression (view) during which a conversion can be attributed to that impression event, even without a click. Standard values range from 1 to 30 days.
attributes.references: IAB Attribution Standards; MRC Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: Username or identifier of the user who created this attribution model configuration.

products.name: performance_kpi_target
products.description: Operational KPI target record defining the planned performance benchmarks for each campaign, line item, or placement. Stores KPI type (CPM, CPC, CPA, CTR, ROAS, VTR, viewability rate, GRP, SOV), target value, target period, tolerance band (acceptable variance percentage), and source (IO commitment, client brief, internal benchmark). Used for pacing alerts, delivery_pacing comparisons, and client performance reporting. Distinct from finance budgets: this is the performance measurement target, not the financial spend target.

attributes.name: performance_kpi_target_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Performance Key Performance Indicator (KPI) Target Identifier
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the performance KPI target record. Primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: agency_relationship_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.agency_relationship. Business justification: Agency performance reviews and AOR renewal decisions require linking KPI targets to the specific agency relationship under which they were committed. Agencies are contractually accountable for KPI del

attributes.name: attribution_model_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.attribution_model. Business justification: performance_kpi_target currently stores attribution_model as a STRING column, which is a denormalized reference to the attribution_model configuration entity that lives in this same domain. Replacing

attributes.name: buy_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.buy. Business justification: KPI targets are negotiated and set at the buy level — e.g., guaranteed viewability or CPM on a specific buy. performance_kpi_target.is_contractual_commitment maps directly to buy-level contractual obl

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the campaign for which this KPI target is defined. Links to the campaign master entity.

attributes.name: campaign_kpi_target_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.campaign_kpi_target. Business justification: campaign_kpi_target holds planned KPI targets; performance_kpi_target holds operationalized measurement targets. Linking enables planned-vs-actual KPI variance reporting — a core post-campaign analysi

attributes.name: contract_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_insertion_order. Business justification: IO commitment tracking: contract_insertion_order carries committed_impressions, committed_clicks, committed_conversions. Linking KPI targets to the IO that mandates them enables IO-level delivery vs.

attributes.name: creative_brief_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.creative_brief. Business justification: KPI targets are set to fulfill the success_metrics defined in the creative brief. Media planners reference the originating brief when configuring KPI targets to ensure alignment with stated business o

attributes.name: creative_deliverable_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.creative_deliverable. Business justification: KPI targets are set at the deliverable level (e.g., a 30s video must achieve 70% completion rate). Linking enables deliverable-level performance accountability reporting — a real advertising ops workf

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.flight. Business justification: KPI targets are set at flight level for phased campaigns with distinct objectives per flight period. Performance analysts track flight-level KPI attainment (e.g., target CPA per flight) to evaluate wh

attributes.name: io_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_io_line. Business justification: KPI targets often set at IO line level per vendor contracts (guaranteed viewability, CTR floors, completion rates). Needed to track contractual performance guarantees, trigger makegood clauses, and ev

attributes.name: line_item_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific line item within a media plan or insertion order for which this KPI target applies. Nullable if target is set at campaign or placement level.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Video Ad Serving Template (VAST)

attributes.name: media_placement_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific media placement for which this KPI target is defined. Nullable if target is set at campaign or line item level.

attributes.name: plan_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.plan_line. Business justification: Plan lines carry planned_cpm, planned_cpa, planned_impressions — the baseline KPI targets set during media planning. performance_kpi_target tracks whether those planned KPIs are achieved. Plan-vs-actu

attributes.name: revenue_target_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.revenue_target. Business justification: Performance KPI targets (e.g., ROAS, CPA) are set to achieve the advertiser's revenue targets. QBR and annual planning reports cross-reference performance KPI attainment against revenue targets; this

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.audience_segment. Business justification: Segment-level KPI target setting: advertisers set distinct KPI targets per audience segment (e.g., target CPA of $15 for high-intent segment vs. $40 for awareness segment). This link enables segment-l

attributes.name: sla_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sla. Business justification: SLA compliance reporting: performance_kpi_target.is_contractual_commitment flag indicates KPI targets derived from SLA obligations. Linking to the governing SLA enables automated SLA breach detection,

attributes.name: alert_enabled
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether automated alerts should be triggered when performance deviates from target thresholds. True = alerts active, False = monitoring only without alerts.

attributes.name: alert_frequency
attributes.description: Frequency at which performance is evaluated against thresholds and alerts are generated. Real-time for critical KPIs, daily or weekly for less time-sensitive metrics.

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Username or identifier of the user who approved this KPI target for activation. Nullable if approval workflow is not required or target is still in draft status.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this KPI target was approved for activation. Nullable if approval workflow is not required or target is still in draft status.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this KPI target record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.

attributes.name: is_contractual_commitment
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this KPI target represents a contractual commitment to the client (typically from Insertion Order or Statement of Work). True = contractual obligation, False = internal goal or aspiration.

attributes.name: kpi_type
attributes.description: The type of performance metric being targeted. CPM = Cost Per Mille (thousand impressions), CPC = Cost Per Click, CPA = Cost Per Acquisition, CTR = Click-Through Rate, ROAS = Return on Ad Spend, VTR = View-Through Rate, GRP = Gross Rating Point, TRP = Target Rating Point, SOV = Share of Voice. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CPM|CPC|CPA|CTR|ROAS|VTR|viewability_rate|GRP|TRP|SOV|conversion_rate|engagement_rate|completion_rate|brand_lift — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Performance Measurement Guidelines; MRC Viewability Standards

attributes.name: lookback_window_days
attributes.description: Number of days after ad exposure during which conversions are attributed to the campaign for conversion-based KPIs. Standard windows are 1, 7, 14, 30, or 90 days.
attributes.references: IAB Attribution Standards; MRC Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: lower_threshold
attributes.description: The minimum acceptable value for this KPI. Performance below this threshold triggers alerts or corrective actions. Calculated as target_value minus tolerance band or set explicitly.

attributes.name: measurement_methodology
attributes.description: Description of how this KPI is measured and calculated, including data sources, attribution models, and calculation logic. Ensures consistency in performance evaluation.
attributes.references: MRC Measurement Standards; IAB Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: Username or identifier of the user who last modified this KPI target record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this KPI target record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional context, assumptions, or special instructions related to this KPI target. May include client expectations, historical context, or measurement caveats.

attributes.name: priority_level
attributes.description: Business priority assigned to this KPI target. Critical targets require immediate attention when thresholds are breached; low priority targets are monitored but may not trigger immediate action.

attributes.name: target_currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary KPI targets (CPM, CPC, CPA, ROAS). Nullable for non-monetary KPIs.

attributes.name: target_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name or label for this KPI target record, used for identification and reporting purposes.

attributes.name: target_period_end_date
attributes.description: The end date of the period for which this KPI target is applicable. Used for time-bound performance measurement and pacing analysis.

attributes.name: target_period_start_date
attributes.description: The start date of the period for which this KPI target is applicable. Used for time-bound performance measurement and pacing analysis.

attributes.name: target_source
attributes.description: The origin or basis for this KPI target. Insertion Order (IO) commitment represents contractual obligation, client brief represents client expectation, internal benchmark represents agency standard, historical average represents past performance baseline.
attributes.references: IAB Insertion Order Standards; Internal Performance Management Standards

attributes.name: target_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the KPI target. Draft = under definition, active = in measurement period, suspended = temporarily paused, achieved = target met, missed = target not met, expired = measurement period ended.

attributes.name: target_unit
attributes.description: The unit of measure for the target value. Currency for cost-based KPIs (CPM, CPC, CPA), percentage for rate-based KPIs (CTR, VTR, viewability_rate), ratio for ROAS, points for GRP/TRP, count for absolute metrics.

attributes.name: target_value
attributes.description: The numeric target value for the specified KPI. Interpretation depends on kpi_type (e.g., dollar amount for CPM/CPC/CPA, percentage for CTR/VTR/viewability_rate, ratio for ROAS, points for GRP/TRP).

attributes.name: tolerance_percentage
attributes.description: Acceptable variance percentage from the target value before triggering alerts or escalations. For example, 10.00 means ±10% from target is acceptable. Used for pacing alerts and performance monitoring.

attributes.name: upper_threshold
attributes.description: The maximum acceptable value for this KPI (relevant for cost-based metrics where exceeding target is negative). Performance above this threshold triggers alerts. Calculated as target_value plus tolerance band or set explicitly.

attributes.name: viewability_standard
attributes.description: The viewability measurement standard applied for viewability_rate KPI targets. MRC standard = 50% of pixels in view for 1 second (display) or 2 seconds (video). GroupM may have stricter requirements.
attributes.references: MRC Viewability Standards; IAB Viewability Guidelines

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: Username or identifier of the user who created this KPI target record. Used for audit trail and accountability.

products.name: video_completion_event
products.description: Video ad completion event record capturing quartile-level video engagement signals (25%, 50%, 75%, 100% completion) per video ad impression. Stores video ad unit identifier, completion quartile reached, autoplay flag, sound-on flag, skip action (skipped, completed), player size, and VTR (View-Through Rate) computation inputs. Sourced from VAST/VPAID event signals from video ad servers. Supports VTR-based IO commitments and video campaign performance measurement.

attributes.name: video_completion_event_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the video completion event record. Primary key for this event log entity.
attributes.references: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) Digital Video Guidelines

attributes.name: ad_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.ad. Business justification: Ad-level video completion tracking is essential for creative performance analysis. Creative teams compare completion_quartile distributions across ad variants to identify which video creative lengths

attributes.name: asset_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the video creative asset that was played. Links to the specific video ad unit served.

attributes.name: buy_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.buy. Business justification: Video buys often carry completion rate guarantees; makegood eligibility is assessed by comparing video_completion_event data against buy-level commitments. video_completion_event has media_placement_i

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the advertising campaign this video completion event belongs to.

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.flight. Business justification: Flight-level video completion rate (VCR) is a standard video campaign KPI used for pacing and creative optimization. Video buyers evaluate VCR per flight to determine whether video creative is resonat

attributes.name: impression_event_id
attributes.description: Identifier linking this completion event to the parent video ad impression that was served. References the initial ad serving event.

attributes.name: line_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.line_item. Business justification: Line-item VCR is a standard programmatic video optimization metric. Traders use line-item completion rates to adjust bids and targeting for video line items. No existing FK links video_completion_even

attributes.name: media_placement_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the media placement where this video ad was served. Links to the specific inventory position purchased.

attributes.name: programmatic_deal_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.programmatic_deal. Business justification: Programmatic video deals carry completion rate commitments. Deal-level video completion reporting (quartile rates, skip rates) is a standard DSP/publisher reconciliation report. video_completion_event

attributes.name: publisher_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the publisher or content owner on whose property the video ad was served. Links to the supply-side partner.

attributes.name: spec_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.spec. Business justification: Video completion rates are analyzed by spec (15s vs 30s, skippable vs non-skippable). Linking video_completion_event to spec enables spec-level completion rate benchmarking — a standard creative optim

attributes.name: ad_server
attributes.description: Name of the ad server platform that served the video ad and captured this completion event (e.g., Google Campaign Manager 360, Innovid, Sizmek). Used for data quality and discrepancy analysis.

attributes.name: browser
attributes.description: Web browser or app environment where the video ad was served (e.g., Chrome, Safari, Firefox, in-app). Null for CTV (Connected TV) and OTT (Over-the-Top) environments.

attributes.name: completion_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of the video that was viewed, calculated as (time_to_completion_seconds / video_duration_seconds) * 100. Supports VTR (View-Through Rate) calculation.

attributes.name: completion_quartile
attributes.description: The quartile milestone reached in video playback: start (0%), first_quartile (25%), midpoint (50%), third_quartile (75%), or complete (100%). Used to calculate VTR (View-Through Rate) and engagement depth.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Video Guidelines; VAST (Video Ad Serving Template) 4.x

attributes.name: connection_type
attributes.description: Type of network connection used during video playback: wifi, cellular (3G/4G/5G), wired (ethernet), or unknown. Impacts video quality and completion rates.

attributes.name: content_category
attributes.description: IAB Content Taxonomy category of the content adjacent to the video ad (e.g., Sports, News, Entertainment). Used for contextual targeting and brand safety verification.

attributes.name: device_type
attributes.description: Type of device on which the video ad was viewed: desktop, mobile (smartphone), tablet, CTV (Connected TV), console (gaming console), or other. Critical for cross-device campaign analysis.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Video Guidelines; IAB Tech Lab Device Type Taxonomy

attributes.name: event_timestamp
attributes.description: Precise date and time when the video completion event was captured by the ad server or video player, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).

attributes.name: geo_city
attributes.description: City where the video ad was viewed. Used for hyper-local campaign performance analysis and geo-targeting verification.

attributes.name: geo_country_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the video ad was viewed (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN). Used for geo-targeting verification and regional performance analysis.

attributes.name: geo_region
attributes.description: State, province, or region where the video ad was viewed (e.g., California, Ontario, Bavaria). Used for sub-national geo-targeting analysis.

attributes.name: ip_address_hashed
attributes.description: Hashed IP address of the device that viewed the video ad. Used for fraud detection and geo-verification while maintaining privacy compliance.

attributes.name: is_autoplay
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the video ad started playing automatically without user initiation (True) or required user action to start (False). Impacts viewability and engagement quality.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Video Guidelines; Coalition for Better Ads Standards

attributes.name: is_fullscreen
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the video was playing in fullscreen mode at the time of the completion event (True) or in standard embedded player (False).

attributes.name: is_skipped
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the user actively skipped the video ad before completion (True) or allowed it to play through the recorded quartile (False).

attributes.name: is_sound_on
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the video ad played with sound enabled (True) or muted (False). Critical metric for video engagement quality and IO (Insertion Order) compliance.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Video Guidelines; MRC (Media Rating Council) Standards

attributes.name: is_viewable
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the video ad met MRC (Media Rating Council) viewability standards at the time of the completion event: 50% of pixels in-view for 2+ consecutive seconds. Used for viewable VTR (View-Through Rate) calculation.
attributes.references: MRC Viewability Standards; IAB Digital Video Guidelines

attributes.name: operating_system
attributes.description: Operating system of the device where the video ad was viewed (e.g., iOS, Android, Windows, macOS, tvOS, Roku OS). Used for device-level performance segmentation.

attributes.name: player_height_pixels
attributes.description: Height of the video player in pixels at the time of the completion event. Used for precise viewability and player size analysis.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Video Guidelines; MRC Viewability Standards

attributes.name: player_size
attributes.description: Size classification of the video player at the time of the event: small (<400x300), medium (400x300 to 640x480), large (>640x480 but not fullscreen), or fullscreen. Impacts viewability measurement.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Video Guidelines; MRC Viewability Standards

attributes.name: player_width_pixels
attributes.description: Width of the video player in pixels at the time of the completion event. Used for precise viewability and player size analysis.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Video Guidelines; MRC Viewability Standards

attributes.name: session_code
attributes.description: Identifier for the user browsing or viewing session during which this video completion event occurred. Used for session-level engagement analysis.

attributes.name: site_domain
attributes.description: Domain name of the website or app where the video ad was served (e.g., youtube.com, espn.com). Used for brand safety and contextual analysis.
attributes.references: IAB Digital Video Guidelines; TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group) Brand Safety Standards

attributes.name: skip_offset_seconds
attributes.description: Number of seconds into the video when the skip button became available to the user. Null if video was non-skippable.

attributes.name: time_to_completion_seconds
attributes.description: Actual elapsed time in seconds from video start to the recorded completion quartile. May differ from video_duration_seconds if user paused, rewound, or fast-forwarded.

attributes.name: user_id_hashed
attributes.description: Hashed or pseudonymized identifier for the user who viewed the video ad. Used for frequency capping and cross-device attribution while maintaining privacy compliance. Must comply with GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) and CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act).
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.140; IAB Tech Lab Privacy Standards

attributes.name: vast_version
attributes.description: Version of the VAST (Video Ad Serving Template) specification used to serve this video ad (e.g., 2.0, 3.0, 4.0, 4.1, 4.2). Indicates ad serving protocol capabilities and tracking fidelity.

attributes.name: video_duration_seconds
attributes.description: Total duration of the video creative in seconds. Used to calculate completion percentage and time-based engagement metrics.

attributes.name: video_format
attributes.description: Format type of the video ad: linear (pre-roll, mid-roll, post-roll that interrupts content), non_linear (overlay ads that run concurrently with content), or companion (static/rich media ads displayed alongside video).

attributes.name: video_position
attributes.description: Position of the video ad within the content stream: pre_roll (before content), mid_roll (during content), or post_roll (after content). Applies to linear video formats only.

attributes.name: viewable_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of the video player that was in the viewable area of the browser viewport at the time of the completion event. Used for granular viewability analysis beyond binary MRC threshold.
attributes.references: MRC Viewability Standards; IAB Digital Video Guidelines

attributes.name: vpaid_version
attributes.description: Version of the VPAID (Video Player-Ad Interface Definition) specification used for interactive video ads (e.g., 1.0, 2.0). Null for standard VAST-only video ads.

domains.name: vendor
domains.description: SSOT for all third-party media vendors, publishers, technology partners (DSPs, SSPs, DMPs, CDPs), and production suppliers. Manages vendor profiles, accreditation status (TAG, MRC), rate cards, onboarding records, and supply chain transparency. Supports media buying, creative production, and programmatic operations. Ensures brand safety compliance.
domains.references: TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group) Certified Against Fraud

products.name: supplier
products.description: Canonical master record and Single Source of Truth (SSOT) for all third-party vendor identities in the advertising ecosystem. Encompasses media publishers (digital, broadcast, OOH, DOOH, CTV/OTT, print), technology platforms (DSPs, SSPs, DMPs, CDPs, ad servers, verification vendors such as IAS and DoubleVerify, attribution platforms, data providers), production houses, research providers, and supply chain partners. Captures legal entity details, vendor type classification (enum: publisher, tech_platform, production, research, other), IAB content category (for publishers), platform type per IAB Tech Lab taxonomy and API integration status (for tech partners), audience reach metrics, MRC-audited measurement status, TAG/MRC accreditation status, TAG Certified Against Fraud, brand safety tier rating, supply path transparency (SPO) attributes, diversity certification, data sharing agreement status, programmatic supply chain role, onboarding lifecycle state, and primary agency relationship owner. All other vendor-domain products reference this entity via supplier_id. Sourced from Mediaocean Prisma vendor records, Salesforce CRM, The Trade Desk, and Google CM360 integrations.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the supplier record. Primary key for the vendor.supplier data product. All other vendor-domain products reference this entity via supplier_id. MASTER_PARTY role.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Vendor Master; Salesforce CRM Account Object

attributes.name: ads_txt_verified
attributes.description: Indicates whether the publisher supplier's authorized digital sellers (ads.txt) or sellers.json file has been verified by the agency's programmatic operations team, confirming legitimate inventory authorization and reducing counterfeit inventory risk per IAB Tech Lab standards.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Ads.txt Standard v1.1; IAB Tech Lab Sellers.json Standard

attributes.name: agency_relationship_owner
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the primary agency employee (e.g., media director, programmatic lead, or account director) responsible for managing the commercial relationship with this supplier. Used for accountability, QBR scheduling, and escalation routing.
attributes.references: 4As Account Management Standards; Salesforce CRM Owner Field

attributes.name: api_integration_status
attributes.description: Current status of the API integration between the agency's technology stack and the supplier's platform. Applicable primarily to tech_platform vendor types. Tracks whether automated data exchange (bid management, reporting, audience sync) is live, pending setup, inactive, or not applicable.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab API Standards; The Trade Desk Partner API; Google CM360 API

attributes.name: audience_reach
attributes.description: Estimated total unique audience reach of the supplier's media properties, expressed as number of unique users/devices per month. Sourced from Comscore or Nielsen Ad Intel measurement. Used in media planning for reach and frequency analysis and GRP/TRP calculations.
attributes.references: Comscore Digital Audience Measurement; Nielsen Ad Intel; MRC Audience Measurement Standards

attributes.name: brand_safety_tier
attributes.description: Agency-assigned brand safety tier rating for the supplier based on content quality, TAG certification, MRC accreditation, and supply chain transparency. Tier 1 = premium brand-safe inventory; Tier 2 = standard; Tier 3 = restricted/monitored; Unrated = pending assessment. Controls eligibility for client campaigns with brand safety mandates.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certification; GARM Brand Safety Floor & Suitability Framework; ANA

attributes.name: cm360_vendor_code
attributes.description: Unique vendor or site identifier assigned to the supplier within Google Campaign Manager 360 for ad trafficking, placement management, and delivery reporting. Enables reconciliation between CM360 delivery data and the vendor master.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 API; IAB VAST/VPAID Standards

attributes.name: contract_expiry_date
attributes.description: Expiry date of the master service agreement (MSA) or master vendor contract with the supplier. Triggers contract renewal workflows and controls whether new insertion orders (IOs) can be issued against this supplier in Mediaocean Prisma.
attributes.references: 4As Standard Agency-Vendor Contract Terms; SAP S/4HANA MM Contract Management

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country where the supplier is legally incorporated. Drives regulatory compliance checks (GDPR, CCPA), tax treatment in SAP S/4HANA, and supply path transparency (SPO) reporting.
attributes.references: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; GDPR Article 44; IAB Tech Lab

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the supplier record was first created in the vendor master system (Mediaocean Prisma or Salesforce CRM), captured in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and GDPR data subject request processing.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the supplier invoices the agency (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Used for multi-currency financial reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA and Mediaocean Prisma billing workflows.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: data_sharing_agreement
attributes.description: Indicates whether a formal data sharing agreement is in place with the supplier, governing the exchange of audience data, campaign performance data, and measurement signals. Required for GDPR/CCPA compliance when audience data flows between the agency and the supplier.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 28 (Data Processing Agreement); CCPA Section 1798.100; IAB Europe Transparency & Consent Framework

attributes.name: diversity_cert_type
attributes.description: Specific type of diversity certification held by the supplier (e.g., MBE, WBE, LGBTBE, VOSB, SDVOSB, SBE). Populated only when diversity_certified is True. Used for granular diversity spend categorization and client reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MBE|WBE|LGBTBE|VOSB|SDVOSB|SBE|HUBZone|other — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: ANA Supplier Diversity Standards; NMSDC; WBENC; NGLCC

attributes.name: diversity_certified
attributes.description: Indicates whether the supplier holds a recognized diversity certification (e.g., MBE - Minority Business Enterprise, WBE - Women Business Enterprise, LGBTBE, VOSB). Supports client diversity spend reporting mandates and ANA supplier diversity program compliance.
attributes.references: ANA Supplier Diversity Standards; NMSDC MBE Certification; WBENC WBE Certification

attributes.name: iab_content_category
attributes.description: IAB Content Taxonomy category code assigned to the publisher's primary content vertical (e.g., IAB1 - Arts & Entertainment, IAB19 - Technology & Computing). Applicable to publisher vendor types. Used for brand safety targeting, contextual alignment, and programmatic supply curation in The Trade Desk and Google CM360.
attributes.references: IAB Content Taxonomy v3.0; IAB Tech Lab

attributes.name: last_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the supplier record in the vendor master. Used for change data capture (CDC) in ETL pipelines, data freshness monitoring, and audit trail compliance.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5(2) Accountability Principle; ISO 8601; Databricks Delta Lake CDC

attributes.name: legal_name
attributes.description: Full registered legal name of the supplier entity as filed with the relevant government authority. Used for contract execution, insertion orders (IOs), and financial reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA and Mediaocean Prisma.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Vendor Taxonomy; SAP S/4HANA Vendor Master (LFA1)

attributes.name: media_channel
attributes.description: Primary media channel through which the publisher or supplier delivers advertising inventory. Applicable to publisher vendor types. Drives media planning and buying workflows in Mediaocean Prisma and The Trade Desk. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: digital|broadcast|ooh|dooh|ctv_ott|print|audio|social|search|other — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab; MRC Cross-Media Audience Measurement Standards

attributes.name: mediaocean_vendor_code
attributes.description: Unique vendor identifier assigned within Mediaocean Prisma, the agency's primary media planning, buying, and billing system of record. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and Mediaocean operational records.

attributes.name: mrc_accredited
attributes.description: Indicates whether the supplier's audience measurement methodology or ad serving has been audited and accredited by the Media Rating Council (MRC). Critical for validating impression counting, viewability measurement, and GRP/TRP reporting integrity.
attributes.references: Media Rating Council (MRC) Accreditation Standards; MRC Viewability Guidelines

attributes.name: mrc_audit_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent MRC audit completion for the supplier's measurement methodology or ad serving infrastructure. Used to assess currency of accreditation and trigger re-audit workflows when accreditation lapses.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for capturing supplementary information about the supplier that does not fit structured fields, such as special commercial terms, relationship history, escalation contacts, or onboarding exceptions noted by the agency relationship owner.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Vendor Notes; Salesforce CRM Account Notes

attributes.name: onboarding_date
attributes.description: Date on which the supplier was formally approved and activated in the agency's vendor management system following completion of onboarding due diligence (financial, legal, brand safety, and compliance checks).
attributes.references: ANA Supplier Onboarding Standards; Mediaocean Prisma Vendor Lifecycle

attributes.name: onboarding_status
attributes.description: Current state of the supplier in the agency onboarding and vendor management lifecycle. Controls whether the supplier is eligible for media buys, IO issuance, and payment processing in Mediaocean Prisma and SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: ANA Supplier Diversity & Onboarding Standards; Mediaocean Prisma Vendor Lifecycle

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: Standard payment terms agreed with the supplier for invoice settlement, as configured in SAP S/4HANA and Mediaocean Prisma. Drives accounts payable scheduling and cash flow management in financial reconciliation workflows.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Vendor Payment Terms; Mediaocean Prisma Billing Configuration; 4As Standard Payment Terms

attributes.name: platform_type
attributes.business_glossary_term: Technology Platform Type (IAB Tech Lab Taxonomy)
attributes.description: Specific platform classification per IAB Tech Lab taxonomy for tech_platform vendor types. Identifies the supplier's role in the programmatic supply chain (e.g., DSP, SSP, DMP, CDP, ad server, verification vendor such as IAS/DoubleVerify, attribution platform, data provider). Null for non-tech-platform vendors. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dsp|ssp|dmp|cdp|ad_server|verification|attribution|data_provider|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Programmatic Supply Chain Transparency Standards; OpenRTB 3.0

attributes.name: primary_contact_email
attributes.description: Primary business email address for the supplier's account representative or partnership contact. Used for IO delivery, billing correspondence, and campaign trafficking communications.

attributes.name: primary_contact_name
attributes.description: Full name of the primary business contact at the supplier organization responsible for account management, campaign trafficking, and billing inquiries.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 4; Salesforce CRM Contact Object

attributes.name: primary_contact_phone
attributes.description: Primary business phone number for the supplier contact, formatted per ITU-T E.164 standard. Used for urgent campaign and billing escalations.

attributes.name: programmatic_supply_role
attributes.description: Defines the supplier's role in the programmatic supply chain as declared in ads.txt/sellers.json: direct (authorized direct seller of inventory), reseller (authorized to resell another seller's inventory), both (acts as both), or not_applicable (non-programmatic supplier). Used for supply chain transparency and SPO analysis.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Ads.txt Standard v1.1; OpenRTB 3.0 Supply Chain Object

attributes.name: salesforce_account_code
attributes.description: Unique account identifier for the supplier record in Salesforce CRM (Sales Cloud). Enables cross-system linkage between the vendor master in the lakehouse and the CRM account for relationship management, contract tracking, and QBR reporting.
attributes.references: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud Account Object

attributes.name: spo_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether the supplier participates in the agency's Supply Path Optimization (SPO) program, meaning the agency has a direct or preferred supply relationship with this vendor to reduce intermediary hops, improve transparency, and lower CPM costs in programmatic buying via The Trade Desk.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Ads.txt / Sellers.json Standards; ANA Programmatic Transparency Guidelines

attributes.name: tag_certified_against_fraud
attributes.business_glossary_term: Trustworthy Accountability Group (TAG) Certified Against Fraud Status
attributes.description: Indicates whether the supplier holds current TAG Certified Against Fraud certification, a key brand safety and ad fraud prevention credential. Required for programmatic supply curation and brand safety tier assignment. Sourced from TAG registry.
attributes.references: Trustworthy Accountability Group (TAG) Certified Against Fraud Program; ANA Brand Safety Standards

attributes.name: tag_seal_certification_code
attributes.description: The unique TAG Seal identifier issued to the supplier upon achieving TAG certification (e.g., TAG Certified Against Fraud, TAG Brand Safety Certified). Used to verify certification authenticity via the TAG registry and enforce brand safety compliance in programmatic buying.
attributes.references: Trustworthy Accountability Group (TAG) Registry; TAG Certified Against Fraud Program

attributes.name: tax_identifier
attributes.description: Government-issued tax identification number (e.g., EIN in the US, VAT number in the EU) for the supplier's legal entity. Required for W-9/W-8 compliance, SAP S/4HANA vendor master setup, and financial reconciliation in Mediaocean Prisma.
attributes.references: IRS EIN Requirements; EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; SAP S/4HANA Vendor Master (LFA1)

attributes.name: trade_desk_partner_code
attributes.description: Unique partner or supply partner identifier assigned to the supplier within The Trade Desk DSP platform. Used for programmatic buying configuration, bid management, and supply path optimization (SPO) tracking.
attributes.references: The Trade Desk Partner API; IAB Tech Lab OpenRTB

attributes.name: trading_name
attributes.description: The brand or 'doing business as' (DBA) name used in day-to-day media buying, campaign trafficking, and agency communications. May differ from the legal entity name.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Vendor Profile; IAB Tech Lab

attributes.name: vendor_type
attributes.description: Primary classification of the supplier within the advertising ecosystem. Drives downstream workflow routing, rate card applicability, and compliance checks. Enum: publisher (media owner), tech_platform (DSP/SSP/DMP/CDP/ad server/verification/attribution), production (creative/production house), research (audience/measurement provider), other.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Vendor Taxonomy v2; 4As Supplier Classification

products.name: publisher
products.description: Master record for media publishers and inventory owners — including digital publishers, broadcast networks, OOH operators, DOOH networks, CTV/OTT platforms, and print media owners. Captures publisher profile, IAB content category classifications, audience reach, MRC-audited measurement status, brand safety tier, and supply path transparency (SPO) attributes. Distinct from DSP/SSP technology partners.

attributes.name: publisher_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the publisher record in the vendor domain master. Primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Publisher Identification Standards

attributes.name: accreditation_id
attributes.description: Unique TAG-issued certification identifier for the publisher. Used to verify certification status via the TAG registry and to validate supply chain transparency in programmatic buying workflows.
attributes.references: TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group) Certified Against Fraud Program

attributes.name: parent_publisher_id
attributes.description: Self-referencing identifier pointing to the parent publisher entity, enabling hierarchical modelling of publisher groups and media conglomerates (e.g., a local TV station under a national broadcast network). Null for top-level publishers.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Publisher Identification Standards

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Publisher is a specialized subtype of supplier (media publishers and inventory owners). This FK establishes the subtype-to-supertype relationship, allowing publisher to inherit common supplier master

attributes.name: ads_txt_verified
attributes.description: Indicates whether the publisher's ads.txt file has been validated, confirming that authorized digital sellers are declared and reducing the risk of unauthorized inventory reselling. Critical for supply path optimization (SPO) and programmatic fraud prevention.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab ads.txt Standard v1.1

attributes.name: audience_measurement_source
attributes.description: Identifies the third-party audience measurement provider or methodology used to validate the publisher's audience reach figures. Determines the credibility and comparability of audience data for media planning and post-campaign reporting.
attributes.references: MRC Audience Measurement Standards; Comscore; Nielsen Ad Intel

attributes.name: base_cpm_rate
attributes.description: Publisher's standard base CPM (Cost Per Mille) rate in the agency's operating currency, representing the cost per 1,000 ad impressions. Serves as the benchmark rate for media planning, rate card negotiations, and budget estimation in Mediaocean Prisma. Classified as confidential commercial pricing data.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab CPM Pricing Standards; Mediaocean Prisma Rate Card Management

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp indicating when the publisher record was first created in the vendor management system. Provides audit trail for vendor onboarding governance and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 (Accountability); ANA Vendor Management Guidelines

attributes.name: deal_type
attributes.description: Primary deal structure type through which the publisher's inventory is transacted. Determines buying workflow, pricing model, and IO/insertion order requirements in Mediaocean Prisma. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open_market|private_marketplace|preferred_deal|programmatic_guaranteed|direct_io — promote to reference product if additional deal types are required]
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab OpenRTB Deal Types; Mediaocean Prisma Deal Management

attributes.name: grp_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether the publisher supports Gross Rating Point (GRP) or Target Rating Point (TRP) based audience buying and reporting, typically applicable to broadcast, CTV/OTT, and premium digital video inventory. Enables GRP-based media planning in Nielsen Ad Intel integrated workflows.
attributes.references: Nielsen Ad Intel GRP Tracking; MRC Cross-Media Measurement Standards

attributes.name: iab_content_category_code
attributes.business_glossary_term: Interactive Advertising Bureau (IAB) Content Category Code
attributes.description: Primary IAB Content Taxonomy category code (e.g., IAB1, IAB2-1) classifying the publisher's editorial content vertical. Used for audience targeting, brand safety filtering, and contextual advertising alignment per IAB Content Taxonomy v3.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Content Taxonomy v3

attributes.name: inventory_type
attributes.description: Primary ad inventory format type offered by the publisher. Drives format-specific buying workflows, creative specifications, and measurement methodologies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: display|video|audio|native|ooh|dooh|print|sponsorship — promote to reference product if additional formats are required]
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Ad Format Standards; MRC Viewability Standards

attributes.name: last_review_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent periodic vendor review or Quarterly Business Review (QBR) conducted for this publisher, covering brand safety compliance, performance metrics, and accreditation status. Supports vendor governance and renewal workflows.
attributes.references: ANA Vendor Management Guidelines; TAG Brand Safety Standards

attributes.name: monthly_impressions
attributes.description: Estimated or audited total monthly ad impression volume available from the publisher across all inventory types. Used for media planning capacity assessment, share of voice (SOV) calculations, and programmatic deal sizing.
attributes.references: MRC Impression Measurement Guidelines; IAB Tech Lab

attributes.name: monthly_unique_visitors
attributes.description: Estimated or MRC-audited monthly unique visitor count for the publisher's primary digital property, as reported by Comscore or Nielsen. Used for audience reach planning, CPM (Cost Per Mille) benchmarking, and media plan audience delivery estimation.
attributes.references: Comscore Digital Audience Measurement; Nielsen Ad Intel; MRC Audience Measurement Standards

attributes.name: mrc_accreditation_scope
attributes.description: Description of the specific measurement types and platforms covered under the publisher's MRC accreditation (e.g., 'Display Impressions, Video Viewability, Cross-Platform'). Null if mrc_audited is false.

attributes.name: next_review_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next periodic vendor review or accreditation renewal check for this publisher. Used to trigger compliance review workflows and ensure TAG/MRC certifications remain current.
attributes.references: ANA Vendor Management Guidelines; TAG Certification Renewal Requirements

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for capturing additional operational notes, special buying conditions, negotiation history, or account management observations relevant to the publisher relationship. Used by media buying and account management teams.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Vendor Profile; Workfront Project Management

attributes.name: preferred_ssp
attributes.description: Name of the publisher's primary or preferred Supply-Side Platform (SSP) used for programmatic inventory monetization (e.g., Google Ad Manager, Magnite, PubMatic, Index Exchange). Used for supply path optimization (SPO) and deal activation in The Trade Desk.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Supply Chain Object Specification; The Trade Desk SPO Framework

attributes.name: programmatic_enabled
attributes.description: Indicates whether the publisher's inventory is available for programmatic buying via Demand-Side Platforms (DSPs) and Supply-Side Platforms (SSPs), including Real-Time Bidding (RTB) and private marketplace (PMP) deal structures.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab OpenRTB Standard; The Trade Desk Programmatic Buying Framework

attributes.name: publisher_type
attributes.description: Classification of the publisher by inventory channel category. Distinguishes digital publishers, broadcast networks, Out-of-Home (OOH) operators, Digital Out-of-Home (DOOH) networks, Connected TV (CTV) / Over-the-Top (OTT) platforms, and print media owners. Drives channel-specific buying workflows and rate card application.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Content Taxonomy; MRC Cross-Media Measurement Standards

attributes.name: rate_currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the publisher's rate card pricing (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Ensures correct currency conversion and financial reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA and Mediaocean Prisma billing workflows.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

attributes.name: sellers_json_verified
attributes.description: Indicates whether the publisher's sellers.json file has been validated, providing transparency into the supply chain by identifying all entities authorized to sell the publisher's inventory. Supports supply path optimization (SPO) and anti-fraud compliance.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab sellers.json Standard v1.0

attributes.name: spo_tier
attributes.description: Supply Path Optimization (SPO) classification indicating the preferred supply path designation for this publisher. 'Direct' = direct publisher relationship; 'Preferred' = approved SSP path; 'Standard' = open market; 'Excluded' = removed from programmatic buying paths. Used by The Trade Desk and DSP buying teams to optimize supply chain efficiency.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Supply Chain Object Specification; The Trade Desk SPO Framework

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the publisher record, capturing any changes to profile, accreditation status, brand safety tier, or rate card information. Supports change data capture (CDC) and audit trail requirements.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 (Accountability); Databricks Delta Lake CDC

attributes.name: vast_vpaid_support
attributes.business_glossary_term: Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) / Video Player-Ad Interface Definition (VPAID) Support
attributes.description: Indicates the publisher's supported video ad serving standards: VAST (Video Ad Serving Template) only, VPAID (Video Player-Ad Interface Definition) only, both, or neither. Determines video creative compatibility and trafficking requirements in Google Campaign Manager 360.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab VAST 4.x Standard; IAB Tech Lab VPAID 2.0 Standard

attributes.name: website_url
attributes.description: Primary public-facing website URL of the publisher. Serves as the canonical digital identity reference for brand safety audits, supply path optimization (SPO) verification, and ads.txt/sellers.json validation.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab ads.txt Standard; TAG Brand Safety Certification

products.name: tech_partner
products.description: Master record for advertising technology platform partners — DSPs, SSPs, DMPs, CDPs, ad servers, verification vendors (IAS, DoubleVerify), attribution platforms, and data providers. Captures platform type (IAB Tech Lab taxonomy), API integration status, data sharing agreements, TAG certification, and programmatic supply chain role. Supports The Trade Desk, Google CM360, and DMP/CDP integrations.

attributes.name: tech_partner_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the advertising technology platform partner record in the vendor master. Primary key for the tech_partner data product.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Taxonomy; Mediaocean Prisma Vendor Master

attributes.name: accreditation_id
attributes.description: Official TAG-issued certification identifier for the tech partner. Used to validate brand safety compliance and anti-fraud accreditation during programmatic deal setup and IO execution.
attributes.references: Trustworthy Accountability Group (TAG) Certified Companies Registry

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Tech_partner is a specialized subtype of supplier (advertising technology platform partners - DSPs, SSPs, DMPs, CDPs, ad servers, verification providers). This FK establishes the subtype-to-supertype

attributes.name: ads_txt_authorized
attributes.description: Indicates whether the tech partner is listed as an authorized digital seller in the relevant ads.txt files, confirming legitimate inventory resale rights per IAB Tech Lab standards. Critical for anti-fraud and brand safety compliance.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab ads.txt Specification v1.0.3; TAG Anti-Fraud Guidelines

attributes.name: annual_platform_fee
attributes.description: Annual licensing or platform access fee payable to the tech partner, denominated in the contract currency. Used for budget planning, P&L reporting, and financial reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Vendor Invoice Management; ANA Programmatic Fee Transparency Guidelines

attributes.name: api_endpoint_url
attributes.description: Primary API endpoint URL for programmatic integration with the tech partner's platform. Used by ETL pipelines and DSP/SSP connectors for bid requests, reporting pulls, and audience segment synchronization. Classified confidential as it represents proprietary integration infrastructure.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab OpenRTB Specification; The Trade Desk Partner Integration Guide

attributes.name: api_integration_status
attributes.description: Current status of the API integration between the agency's systems (The Trade Desk, Google CM360, Mediaocean Prisma) and the tech partner's platform. Drives automated bid management, reporting ingestion, and audience sync workflows.
attributes.references: The Trade Desk Partner API Documentation; Google CM360 API Reference; IAB Tech Lab OpenRTB

attributes.name: attribution_model_support
attributes.description: Attribution modeling methodology supported by the tech partner's platform for campaign performance measurement. Informs ROAS and CPA reporting configurations in Google CM360 and The Trade Desk. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: last_click|first_click|linear|data_driven|multi_touch|time_decay|position_based|none — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Google CM360 Attribution Modeling; IAB Attribution Measurement Standards; MRC

attributes.name: audience_targeting_capability
attributes.description: Indicates whether the tech partner provides audience targeting capabilities (e.g., DMP/CDP segment activation, lookalike modeling, contextual targeting). Relevant for DMP and CDP partners integrated with The Trade Desk audience management.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Audience Taxonomy v1.1; The Trade Desk Audience API

attributes.name: brand_safety_tier
attributes.description: Internal brand safety tier assigned to the tech partner based on TAG certification, MRC accreditation, IAS/DoubleVerify verification scores, and supply chain transparency. Tier 1 = highest trust; Tier 3 = restricted use. Governs which client campaigns may activate this partner.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified; IAB Brand Safety Standards; 4As Brand Safety Guidelines

attributes.name: contract_end_date
attributes.description: Expiry date of the master service agreement or platform access contract with the tech partner. Nullable for evergreen agreements. Triggers renewal workflow in Workfront and SAP S/4HANA contract management.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Contract Management; IAB Standard Terms and Conditions for Internet Advertising

attributes.name: contract_start_date
attributes.description: Effective start date of the master service agreement or platform access contract with the tech partner. Used for contract lifecycle management in SAP S/4HANA PS and financial accrual scheduling.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Contract Management; IAB Standard Terms and Conditions for Internet Advertising

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the tech partner record was first created in the vendor master system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and onboarding SLA tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5(2) Accountability Principle; SAP S/4HANA Vendor Master Audit Log

attributes.name: data_residency_region
attributes.description: Geographic region where the tech partner stores and processes data (e.g., EU, US, APAC). Critical for GDPR cross-border data transfer compliance (Standard Contractual Clauses) and CCPA jurisdiction assessments.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 44-49 (International Data Transfers); CCPA; EASA Digital Advertising Guidelines

attributes.name: data_sharing_agreement_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether a formal data sharing agreement (DSA) is in place with the tech partner governing the exchange of audience data, campaign performance data, and first-party data. Required for DMP/CDP integrations under GDPR and CCPA.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 28 (Data Processing Agreement); CCPA Section 1798.100; IAB Transparency and Consent Framework (TCF)

attributes.name: data_sharing_agreement_ref
attributes.description: Reference number or document identifier for the executed data sharing agreement with the tech partner. Links to the contract management system (SAP S/4HANA PS or Workfront DAM) for legal and compliance audit purposes.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 28; CCPA; IAB Transparency and Consent Framework (TCF) v2.2

attributes.name: fee_currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the annual platform fee and any transactional fees charged by the tech partner (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multi-currency financial reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA FI.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Multi-Currency Configuration

attributes.name: iab_tech_lab_member
attributes.business_glossary_term: Interactive Advertising Bureau (IAB) Tech Lab Member Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the tech partner is an active member of the IAB Tech Lab, which governs programmatic standards including OpenRTB, VAST, VPAID, and ads.txt. Membership status informs standards compliance expectations.
attributes.references: Interactive Advertising Bureau (IAB) Tech Lab Membership Registry

attributes.name: last_audit_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent vendor compliance audit conducted for the tech partner, covering TAG certification, MRC accreditation, GDPR DPA, and brand safety assessments. Supports annual vendor review cycles.
attributes.references: TAG Certified Companies Registry; MRC Audit Standards; ANA Vendor Transparency Guidelines

attributes.name: measurement_capability
attributes.description: Indicates whether the tech partner provides independent measurement, viewability verification, or attribution services (e.g., IAS, DoubleVerify, Nielsen, Comscore). Determines eligibility as a third-party measurement vendor on campaign IOs.
attributes.references: MRC Viewability Standards; IAB Measurement Guidelines; Nielsen Ad Intel; Comscore

attributes.name: platform_type
attributes.description: Categorical classification of the advertising technology platform per IAB Tech Lab taxonomy. Determines the partner's role in the programmatic supply chain and which integration workflows apply. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DSP|SSP|DMP|CDP|Ad Server|Verification|Attribution|Data Provider|CRM|Analytics — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Programmatic Supply Chain Taxonomy; OpenRTB 2.x Specification

attributes.name: rtb_protocol_version
attributes.description: OpenRTB protocol version supported by the tech partner for programmatic buying and selling (e.g., OpenRTB 2.5, OpenRTB 2.6, OpenRTB 3.0). Governs bid request/response schema compatibility with The Trade Desk and other DSP integrations.

attributes.name: sellers_json_verified
attributes.description: Indicates whether the tech partner's sellers.json file has been validated per IAB Tech Lab specification, confirming supply chain transparency for programmatic inventory. Required for SSP and ad exchange partners.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab sellers.json Specification v1.0; TAG Supply Chain Transparency

attributes.name: soc2_certified
attributes.description: Indicates whether the tech partner holds a current SOC 2 Type II certification, confirming security, availability, and confidentiality controls. Required for partners handling first-party audience data or campaign performance data.
attributes.references: AICPA SOC 2 Type II Standard; ANA Data Security Guidelines; TAG Data Protection

attributes.name: supply_chain_role
attributes.description: The partner's functional role within the programmatic advertising supply chain as defined by IAB ads.txt and sellers.json standards. Supports supply chain transparency audits and brand safety compliance. Values: buyer (DSP), seller (SSP/publisher), intermediary (reseller), data_provider, measurement.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab ads.txt / sellers.json Specification; TAG Supply Chain Transparency

attributes.name: supported_channels
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of advertising channels supported by the tech partner's platform (e.g., Display, Video, CTV, OTT, DOOH, Audio, Native, Search). Informs media planning channel eligibility in Mediaocean Prisma and The Trade Desk.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Channel Taxonomy; MRC Cross-Media Measurement Standards

attributes.name: tech_fee_rate
attributes.description: Percentage-based technology fee charged by the partner per media dollar transacted through their platform (e.g., DSP tech fee as a percentage of media spend). Used in CPM/CPC rate card calculations and ROAS reporting in Mediaocean Prisma.
attributes.references: ANA Programmatic Fee Transparency Guidelines; IAB Standard Terms; Mediaocean Prisma Rate Card

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the tech partner record in the vendor master. Used for change data capture (CDC) in ETL pipelines and compliance audit trails. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5(2) Accountability Principle; SAP S/4HANA Vendor Master Audit Log

attributes.name: vast_vpaid_support
attributes.business_glossary_term: Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) / Video Player-Ad Interface Definition (VPAID) Support Version
attributes.description: Supported video ad serving standard(s) for the tech partner's platform. Determines compatibility with video campaign trafficking in Google CM360 and The Trade Desk. VAST 4.x is the current IAB Tech Lab standard; VPAID 2.0 is legacy.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab VAST 4.2 Specification; IAB Tech Lab VPAID 2.0 Specification

products.name: accreditation
products.description: Tracks formal accreditation, certification, and compliance status records for vendors — including TAG Brand Safety Certified, TAG Certified Against Fraud, MRC accreditation, IAB Gold Standard, and ASA/FTC compliance certifications. Each record captures the certifying body, certification type, issue date, expiry date, certificate reference number, and current validity status. Enables brand safety and supply chain transparency enforcement.

attributes.name: accreditation_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each vendor accreditation, certification, or compliance record within the vendor domain SSOT.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Data Standards; MRC Minimum Standards

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the vendor (media publisher, technology partner, DSP, SSP, DMP, CDP, or production supplier) that holds this accreditation.
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Supply Chain Transparency; TAG Brand Safety Standards

attributes.name: accreditation_status
attributes.description: Current validity status of the accreditation record. 'active' indicates the certification is current and enforceable; 'expired' indicates the certification period has lapsed; 'suspended' indicates temporary hold pending review; 'revoked' indicates the certifying body has withdrawn the certification; 'pending' indicates an application in progress.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified Program; MRC Minimum Standards; IAB Gold Standard; ASA CAP Code

attributes.name: accreditation_tier
attributes.description: The tier or level of the accreditation where the certifying body defines multiple levels (e.g., IAB Gold Standard tiers, MRC provisional vs. full accreditation). Supports vendor segmentation and preferential buying decisions.
attributes.references: IAB Gold Standard; MRC Minimum Standards for Media Rating

attributes.name: anti_fraud_certified
attributes.description: Indicates whether this accreditation record confers TAG Certified Against Fraud status on the vendor. True when the certification type is TAG Certified Against Fraud and status is active. Supports invalid traffic (IVT) mitigation in programmatic buying.
attributes.references: TAG Certified Against Fraud Program; MRC Invalid Traffic Detection and Filtration Guidelines; IAB Tech Lab IVT Standards

attributes.name: application_date
attributes.description: The date on which the vendor first submitted the application for this accreditation or certification. Supports onboarding workflow tracking and time-to-accreditation analytics.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified Program; MRC Minimum Standards; IAB Gold Standard

attributes.name: audit_cycle
attributes.description: The frequency at which the certifying body requires the vendor to undergo re-audit or re-assessment to maintain the accreditation (e.g., annual TAG re-certification, biennial MRC audit). Drives renewal workflow scheduling.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified Program; MRC Minimum Standards; IAB Gold Standard

attributes.name: brand_safety_certified
attributes.description: Indicates whether this accreditation record confers TAG Brand Safety Certified status on the vendor. True when the certification type is TAG Brand Safety Certified and status is active. Enables rapid brand safety filtering in media buying workflows.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified Program; 4As Brand Safety Guidelines; ANA Brand Safety Principles

attributes.name: ccpa_compliant
attributes.business_glossary_term: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) Compliant Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the vendor has demonstrated CCPA compliance as part of this accreditation record. Relevant for US-based audience data operations, programmatic buying, and DMP/CDP integrations.
attributes.references: CCPA (California Civil Code §1798.100); NAI Code of Conduct; IAB CCPA Compliance Framework

attributes.name: certificate_reference_number
attributes.description: The official certificate or registration number issued by the certifying body (e.g., TAG seal ID, MRC accreditation number, IAB Gold Standard reference). Used for external verification and audit trail.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified Program; MRC Minimum Standards for Media Rating; IAB Gold Standard

attributes.name: certification_name
attributes.description: The full official name of the certification or accreditation program as designated by the certifying body (e.g., 'TAG Brand Safety Certified Seal', 'MRC Digital Audience-Based Buying Accreditation'). Provides human-readable identification beyond the type code.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified Program; MRC Minimum Standards for Media Rating

attributes.name: certification_type
attributes.description: The specific type of accreditation or certification held by the vendor. Covers brand safety, anti-fraud, audience measurement, content standards, and regulatory compliance certifications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TAG Brand Safety Certified|TAG Certified Against Fraud|MRC Accredited|IAB Gold Standard|ASA Compliant|FTC Compliant|EASA Compliant|4As Member|ANA Member — promote to reference product]
attributes.value_regex: TAG Brand Safety Certified|TAG Certified Against Fraud|MRC Accredited|IAB Gold Standard|ASA Compliant|FTC Compliant
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified Program; MRC Minimum Standards; IAB Gold Standard; ASA CAP Code; FTC Advertising Practices

attributes.name: certifying_body
attributes.description: The governing or standards body that issued the accreditation or certification (e.g., TAG, MRC, IAB, ASA, FTC). Determines the authority and scope of the certification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TAG|MRC|IAB|ASA|FTC|EASA|4As|ANA|NAI|ICC — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: TAG — Trustworthy Accountability Group; MRC — Media Rating Council; IAB — Interactive Advertising Bureau; ASA — Advertising Standards Authority; FTC — Federal Trade Commission; EASA — European Advertising Standards Alliance; 4As — American Association of Advertising Agencies; ANA — Association of National Advertisers; NAI — Network Advertising Initiative; ICC — International Chamber of Commerce

attributes.name: certifying_body_contact
attributes.description: Name or email of the primary contact at the certifying body responsible for managing this accreditation record. Used for renewal correspondence and audit coordination.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified Program; MRC Minimum Standards; IAB Gold Standard

attributes.name: channel_scope
attributes.description: The advertising channel(s) covered by this accreditation (e.g., programmatic display, OTT/CTV, DOOH). Aligns with IAB channel taxonomy and supports media buying compliance checks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: display|video|audio|search|social|OOH|DOOH|OTT|CTV|programmatic|native|all — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Channel Taxonomy; MRC Digital Measurement Guidelines; TAG Brand Safety Certified Program

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp at which this accreditation record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting requirements.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5(2) — Accountability; MRC Minimum Standards; IAB Tech Lab Data Standards

attributes.name: expiry_date
attributes.description: The date on which the accreditation or certification expires and must be renewed. Null for certifications with no defined expiry. Critical for brand safety enforcement and supply chain transparency checks.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified Program; MRC Minimum Standards; IAB Gold Standard; ASA CAP Code

attributes.name: fee_usd
attributes.description: The fee paid by the vendor to the certifying body to obtain or renew this accreditation, denominated in US Dollars. Supports vendor cost tracking, budget management, and P&L reconciliation for supply chain compliance expenditure.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified Program Fee Schedule; MRC Accreditation Fee Schedule; SAP S/4HANA FI

attributes.name: gdpr_compliant
attributes.business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Compliant Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the vendor has demonstrated GDPR compliance as part of this accreditation record. Relevant for European media buying, data processing agreements, and audience targeting operations.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 28 (Processor obligations); IAB Europe Transparency and Consent Framework (TCF); EASA
