attributes.name: geography_scope
attributes.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code(s) indicating the geographic markets covered by this accreditation (e.g., 'USA', 'GBR|DEU|FRA'). Pipe-delimited for multi-country scope. Supports GDPR, CCPA, and regional compliance enforcement.
attributes.references: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; GDPR Article 3; CCPA; EASA Cross-Border Complaints Procedure

attributes.name: iab_gold_standard
attributes.business_glossary_term: Interactive Advertising Bureau (IAB) Gold Standard Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the vendor holds IAB Gold Standard certification as part of this accreditation record. Covers ads.txt/sellers.json adoption, brand safety, and anti-fraud commitments.
attributes.references: IAB Gold Standard; IAB Tech Lab ads.txt; IAB Tech Lab sellers.json

attributes.name: issue_date
attributes.description: The date on which the certifying body formally issued or granted the accreditation to the vendor. Marks the start of the certification's validity period.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified Program; MRC Minimum Standards; IAB Gold Standard

attributes.name: last_audit_date
attributes.description: The date of the most recent audit or assessment conducted by the certifying body against this accreditation. Used to assess currency of compliance and schedule next audit.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified Program; MRC Minimum Standards; IAB Gold Standard

attributes.name: mrc_accredited
attributes.description: Indicates whether this accreditation record reflects MRC accreditation for audience measurement or ad verification. True when certifying_body is MRC and status is active. Supports Nielsen/Comscore measurement validation and media buying quality assurance.
attributes.references: MRC Minimum Standards for Media Rating; MRC Digital Audience-Based Buying Accreditation

attributes.name: next_audit_date
attributes.description: The scheduled date for the next audit or re-assessment by the certifying body. Supports proactive compliance management and vendor onboarding risk assessment.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified Program; MRC Minimum Standards; IAB Gold Standard

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional context, conditions, or observations related to this accreditation record (e.g., conditional approval notes, scope limitations, certifying body commentary). Supports operational transparency.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified Program; MRC Minimum Standards

attributes.name: reinstatement_date
attributes.description: The date on which a previously suspended or revoked accreditation was reinstated by the certifying body. Supports vendor lifecycle management and historical compliance audit trails.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified Program; MRC Minimum Standards

attributes.name: renewal_alert_days
attributes.description: Number of days before expiry_date at which automated renewal alerts should be triggered for this accreditation. Configurable per certification type to support proactive compliance management (e.g., 90 days for TAG, 60 days for MRC).
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified Program; MRC Minimum Standards; IAB Gold Standard

attributes.name: renewal_date
attributes.description: The date on which the vendor submitted or completed the renewal application for this accreditation. Distinct from expiry_date; tracks the renewal action rather than the deadline.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified Program; MRC Minimum Standards

attributes.name: scope_description
attributes.description: Narrative description of the scope of the accreditation, including which services, channels, geographies, or business units are covered (e.g., 'Programmatic display and video buying via DSP', 'Digital audience measurement for desktop and mobile'). Enables precise brand safety and supply chain enforcement.
attributes.references: MRC Minimum Standards for Media Rating; IAB Gold Standard; TAG Brand Safety Certified Program

attributes.name: supporting_document_url
attributes.description: URL pointing to the official certificate document, audit report, or compliance evidence stored in the DAM (Digital Asset Management) system or secure document repository. Enables direct access to certification evidence for brand safety audits.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified Program; MRC Minimum Standards; IAB Gold Standard; Workfront DAM

attributes.name: suspension_date
attributes.description: The date on which the certifying body suspended or revoked this accreditation. Populated only when accreditation_status is 'suspended' or 'revoked'. Enables point-in-time compliance reporting.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified Program; MRC Minimum Standards; FTC Advertising Practices

attributes.name: suspension_reason
attributes.description: Narrative explanation of why the accreditation was suspended or revoked by the certifying body. Populated only when accreditation_status is 'suspended' or 'revoked'. Supports vendor risk management and supply chain transparency reporting.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certified Program; MRC Minimum Standards; FTC Advertising Practices

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp at which this accreditation record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and compliance audit requirements in the Databricks Silver Layer.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5(2) — Accountability; MRC Minimum Standards; IAB Tech Lab Data Standards

products.name: vendor_rate_card
products.description: Vendor-published rate card defining standard pricing for media inventory, production services, or technology platform fees. Captures rate card version, effective date range, currency, pricing model (CPM, CPC, CPA, flat fee, GRP-based), rate tiers by volume or audience segment, and negotiated vs. open-market rate flags. Sourced from Mediaocean Prisma rate card management and vendor IO terms.

attributes.name: vendor_rate_card_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the vendor rate card record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.advertiser. Business justification: Negotiated rate cards in advertising are frequently specific to an advertiser (is_negotiated_rate flag exists). Agencies negotiate advertiser-specific pricing with publishers/suppliers; billing valida

attributes.name: source_system_rate_card_vendor_rate_card_id
attributes.description: The native identifier of this rate card record in the originating source system (Mediaocean Prisma). Enables lineage tracing and reconciliation between the Silver Layer lakehouse and the operational system of record.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the vendor (media publisher, technology partner, or production supplier) who issued this rate card.

attributes.name: ad_format
attributes.description: The specific creative ad format covered by this rate card entry (e.g., banner, video pre-roll, native, audio, rich media, interstitial). Aligns with IAB standard ad unit taxonomy and Google Campaign Manager 360 trafficking formats. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: banner|video|native|audio|rich_media|interstitial|DOOH_static|DOOH_dynamic — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB New Ad Portfolio Standard; Google Campaign Manager 360

attributes.name: ad_size
attributes.description: The dimensions or specification of the ad unit covered by this rate (e.g., '300x250', '728x90', '15s', '30s', '6s bumper'). Follows IAB standard ad unit sizes for display and video formats.

attributes.name: added_value_description
attributes.description: Free-text description of any added-value elements bundled with this rate card (e.g., bonus impressions, free creative production, data targeting inclusion, brand safety tools). Common in direct media deals and IO negotiations.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma; IAB Insertion Order Standard Terms

attributes.name: agency_commission_pct
attributes.description: The standard agency commission percentage applicable to this rate card (typically 15% per 4As convention). Distinct from discount_rate_pct; represents the agency's earned commission on media spend billed at this rate.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Commission Standards; Mediaocean Prisma Billing

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the internal stakeholder (media buyer, procurement manager) who approved this rate card for use. Supports internal governance and audit trail.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma; SAP S/4HANA MM Procurement

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: The date on which the rate card was internally approved by the media buying or procurement team for use in campaign planning and IO execution. Supports governance and audit trail requirements.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma; SAP S/4HANA MM Procurement

attributes.name: audience_segment_label
attributes.description: The audience segment or demographic targeting profile for which this rate applies (e.g., 'Adults 18-34', 'In-Market Auto Buyers', 'Lookalike High-Value'). Supports audience-based rate differentiation used in DMP/CDP-driven buying on The Trade Desk.
attributes.references: IAB Audience Taxonomy; The Trade Desk Audience Targeting

attributes.name: base_rate
attributes.description: The standard published rate for the pricing unit defined by the pricing_model (e.g., CPM rate in currency per 1,000 impressions, CPC rate per click, flat fee amount). This is the principal quantitative fact of the rate card before any volume discounts or negotiated adjustments.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Rate Card Management; IAB Standard Pricing

attributes.name: brand_safety_tier
attributes.description: The brand safety classification level associated with this rate card's inventory. TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group) certified inventory commands premium rates. Supports brand safety compliance and supply chain transparency requirements.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Certification; GARM Brand Safety Floor Standards

attributes.name: channel_category
attributes.description: Broad channel classification for the media inventory covered by this rate card (e.g., digital, traditional broadcast/print, programmatic, direct-sold, out-of-home). Supports media planning and channel mix analytics.
attributes.references: IAB Channel Taxonomy; 4As Media Planning Standards

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this rate card record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and incremental load processing.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); Databricks Lakehouse Audit Standards

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the rate card rates are denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multi-currency media buying operations and financial reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

attributes.name: discount_rate_pct
attributes.description: The percentage discount applied to the base_rate to arrive at the negotiated net rate (e.g., 15.00 for a 15% agency discount). Expressed as a percentage. Used in Mediaocean Prisma billing and SAP S/4HANA financial reconciliation.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma; 4As Agency Discount Standards; SAP S/4HANA CO Module

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: The date after which this rate card version is no longer valid for new bookings. Nullable for open-ended rate cards with no defined expiry.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma; IAB Insertion Order Standard Terms

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: The date from which this rate card version becomes binding and applicable for media buying, IO execution, and billing. Aligns with vendor IO terms.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma; IAB Insertion Order Standard Terms

attributes.name: geo_market
attributes.description: The geographic market or region for which this rate card applies (e.g., 'USA', 'GBR', 'New York DMA', 'EMEA'). Rates may vary by market due to inventory supply and demand dynamics.
attributes.references: IAB Geo Taxonomy; Nielsen DMA Definitions

attributes.name: guaranteed_viewability_pct
attributes.description: The minimum viewability percentage guaranteed by the vendor for inventory sold under this rate card (e.g., 70.00 for 70% viewability guarantee). Null if no viewability guarantee is offered. Supports campaign performance KPI tracking.
attributes.references: MRC Viewability Measurement Guidelines; IAB Viewability Standards

attributes.name: inventory_type
attributes.description: The category of media inventory or service covered by this rate card. Classifies the supply type (e.g., display, video, OOH/Out-of-Home, DOOH/Digital Out-of-Home, OTT/Over-the-Top, CTV/Connected TV). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: display|video|audio|OOH|DOOH|print|broadcast|OTT|CTV|search|social|native — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Tech Lab Inventory Classification; MRC Digital Measurement Standards

attributes.name: io_terms_reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier or URL to the vendor's standard Insertion Order (IO) terms and conditions document associated with this rate card. Links rate card pricing to contractual obligations in Mediaocean Prisma.
attributes.references: IAB Standard Terms and Conditions for Internet Advertising; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: is_negotiated_rate
attributes.description: Indicates whether this rate is a negotiated/contracted rate (True) as opposed to the vendor's published open-market rate (False). Negotiated rates are commercially sensitive and used in IO reconciliation and P&L reporting.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma; IAB Insertion Order Standard Terms

attributes.name: minimum_spend
attributes.description: The minimum monetary commitment required by the vendor to activate this rate card or rate tier. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code. Relevant for IO negotiation and budget planning.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma; IAB Insertion Order Standard Terms

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional context, special conditions, or annotations related to this rate card entry (e.g., seasonal restrictions, exclusivity clauses, make-good provisions). Sourced from Mediaocean Prisma rate card comments.

attributes.name: placement_position
attributes.description: The placement position or contextual location of the ad unit on the publisher's inventory (e.g., above_the_fold, pre_roll, homepage_takeover). Position premiums are a standard component of vendor rate cards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: above_the_fold|below_the_fold|pre_roll|mid_roll|post_roll|interstitial|homepage_takeover|run_of_site — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Viewability Standards; MRC Viewability Guidelines

attributes.name: pricing_model
attributes.description: The commercial pricing model under which rates are defined. CPM (Cost Per Mille) for impression-based; CPC (Cost Per Click) for click-based; CPA (Cost Per Acquisition) for conversion-based; flat_fee for fixed-cost placements; GRP (Gross Rating Point) for broadcast/linear TV; CTV for Connected TV; CPCV for Cost Per Completed View. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CPM|CPC|CPA|flat_fee|GRP|CTV|CPCV — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Standard Pricing Models; MRC Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: published_date
attributes.description: The date on which the vendor formally published or issued this rate card version. Distinct from effective_start_date; a rate card may be published in advance of its effective period.

attributes.name: rate_card_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name or title of the rate card as published by the vendor (e.g., 'Q3 2024 Display Standard Rate Card', 'Programmatic Open Market CPM Card').

attributes.name: rate_card_number
attributes.description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned by the vendor or Mediaocean Prisma to uniquely reference this rate card document. Used in Insertion Order (IO) negotiations and reconciliation.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Rate Card Management; IAB Standard Terms

attributes.name: rate_card_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the rate card. 'active' indicates the card is currently in use for media buying; 'superseded' means a newer version has replaced it; 'expired' means the effective period has lapsed.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Rate Card Management; IAB Standard Terms

attributes.name: rate_card_type
attributes.description: Indicates whether the rates are open-market published rates or negotiated/contracted rates. 'programmatic_guaranteed' and 'private_marketplace' (PMP) reflect programmatic deal structures. Drives reconciliation logic in Mediaocean Prisma.
attributes.references: IAB Programmatic Deal Types; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: rate_unit
attributes.description: The unit of measure against which the base_rate is applied (e.g., per_mille for CPM, per_click for CPC, per_grp for GRP-based broadcast buying, flat for fixed-fee placements). Ensures correct billing calculation in Mediaocean Prisma. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: per_mille|per_click|per_acquisition|per_view|per_grp|per_day|per_week|per_month|flat — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Standard Pricing Models; MRC Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this rate card record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail compliance.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); Databricks Lakehouse Audit Standards

attributes.name: version
attributes.description: Version identifier of the rate card (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Tracks revisions issued by the vendor over time to support historical rate lookups and audit trails.

attributes.name: viewability_standard
attributes.description: The viewability measurement standard applied to inventory covered by this rate card (e.g., MRC display standard: 50% pixels for 1 second; MRC video: 50% pixels for 2 seconds). Affects rate premiums and campaign KPI commitments.
attributes.references: MRC Viewability Measurement Guidelines; IAB Viewability Standards

attributes.name: volume_threshold_max
attributes.description: The upper bound of the volume or spend range that qualifies for this rate tier. Nullable for the highest open-ended tier. Used with volume_threshold_min to define tier eligibility.

attributes.name: volume_threshold_min
attributes.description: The lower bound of the volume or spend range (in impressions, clicks, or currency units per currency_code) that qualifies for this rate tier. Used in conjunction with volume_threshold_max to define tier eligibility.

attributes.name: volume_threshold_unit
attributes.description: The unit of measure for the volume thresholds (volume_threshold_min and volume_threshold_max). Specifies whether thresholds are in impressions, clicks, currency spend, GRP points, or other measurable units.
attributes.references: IAB Standard Pricing Models; MRC Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: volume_tier_label
attributes.description: Human-readable label identifying the volume or spend tier this rate applies to (e.g., 'Tier 1: $0–$50K', 'Tier 2: $50K–$250K', 'Enterprise'). Supports tiered pricing structures common in programmatic and direct media deals.

products.name: io_line
products.description: Individual line items within a vendor Insertion Order, each specifying a discrete media buy — placement, ad unit, targeting parameters, flight dates, contracted impressions or GRPs, CPM/CPC rate, and authorized spend. Captures delivery method (programmatic RTB, direct, guaranteed), ad format (display, video VAST, OOH, CTV), and reconciliation status against actuals. Core to Mediaocean Prisma billing and reconciliation.

attributes.name: io_line_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for each individual line item within a vendor Insertion Order. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_LINE — represents one discrete media buy line within a vendor IO header.

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: IO lines specify which client brand the media buy supports. Multi-brand advertisers require brand-level media spend tracking. Critical for brand budget management, pacing dashboards, and brand-level R

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertising campaign this IO line item is associated with, enabling campaign-level spend and delivery aggregation.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360; Mediaocean Prisma Campaign Linking

attributes.name: contract_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_insertion_order. Business justification: Vendor IO lines execute against a contracted insertion order. Linking io_line.contract_insertion_order_id enables line-level spend tracking against IO-authorized amounts, pacing reports, and delivery

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.flight. Business justification: IO lines often map to specific flights within campaigns for budget allocation and pacing. Required for flight-level budget tracking, delivery monitoring, and financial planning in advertising media bu

attributes.name: initiative_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.initiative. Business justification: Insertion order lines represent committed media buys executed within specific projects/initiatives. Linking IO lines to initiative enables project media planning tracking, budget monitoring, and deliv

attributes.name: line_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.line_item. Business justification: IO lines frequently correspond 1:1 with campaign line items for execution tracking. Essential for linking contractual commitments to delivery execution, reconciliation, and billing in advertising oper

attributes.name: media_placement_id
attributes.description: The placement identifier from Google Campaign Manager 360 (CM360) ad server corresponding to this IO line item. Enables trafficking, delivery tracking, and third-party ad serving reconciliation.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 Placement Trafficking; IAB Ad Serving Standards

attributes.name: programmatic_deal_id
attributes.description: The unique deal identifier assigned by the Supply-Side Platform (SSP) or publisher for Private Marketplace (PMP) or Programmatic Guaranteed deals. Used to activate and track specific inventory packages in The Trade Desk and other Demand-Side Platforms (DSPs).
attributes.references: IAB OpenRTB Deal ID Standard; The Trade Desk PMP Deal Management; OpenRTB 2.x Specification

attributes.name: spec_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.spec. Business justification: Ad ops IO management: each IO line specifies the creative spec (format, dimensions, file size limits) required for delivery. Linking io_line to spec enables automated creative-to-IO spec validation an

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the third-party media vendor, publisher, or technology partner supplying the media inventory for this line item. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE RESOURCE_REFERENCE category.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Vendor Master; TAG Vendor Registry

attributes.name: vendor_io_media_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key reference to the parent Insertion Order header that this line item belongs to. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE HEADER_REFERENCE category — mandatory link to the parent IO.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma IO Header; IAB Insertion Order Standard

attributes.name: vendor_rate_card_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_rate_card. Business justification: Each io_line represents a discrete media buy priced against a vendor's published rate card. The io_line carries rate fields (rate, net_rate, agency_commission_pct, ad_format, ad_unit_size) that are de

attributes.name: actual_spend
attributes.description: The actual amount spent against this line item based on delivered units and applicable rate, as reported by the vendor or ad server. Compared against contracted_spend during financial reconciliation in Mediaocean Prisma and SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Actuals; SAP S/4HANA FI Vendor Invoice; Google Campaign Manager 360 Spend Reporting

attributes.name: actual_units_delivered
attributes.description: The actual number of units (impressions, clicks, GRPs, etc.) delivered by the vendor for this line item as reported by the ad server or third-party measurement provider. Used in reconciliation against contracted_units to identify under- or over-delivery.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 Delivery Reporting; MRC Impression Counting Standards; Comscore Cross-Platform Measurement

attributes.name: added_value_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this line item represents added-value inventory (bonus impressions, editorial mentions, or complimentary placements) provided by the vendor at no additional cost as part of the negotiated IO package.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Added Value Tracking; 4As Media Buying Standards; ANA Transparency Guidelines

attributes.name: agency_commission_pct
attributes.description: The agency commission percentage applied to this line item's gross spend, typically 15% per industry standard. Used to calculate net vendor cost and agency revenue. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15%).
attributes.references: 4As Agency Commission Standard; ANA Media Transparency Guidelines; Mediaocean Prisma Commission Calculation

attributes.name: brand_safety_segments
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of brand safety and content suitability categories applied to this line item to prevent ad adjacency with inappropriate content. Aligned with TAG Brand Safety Standards and IAB Content Taxonomy.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Standards; IAB Content Taxonomy; GARM Brand Safety Floor

attributes.name: buying_type
attributes.description: The pricing and buying model for this line item. Specifies whether inventory is purchased on a Cost Per Mille (CPM), Cost Per Click (CPC), Cost Per Acquisition (CPA), Cost Per View (CPV), Cost Per Completed View (CPCV), flat rate, Gross Rating Point (GRP), or Target Rating Point (TRP) basis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CPM|CPC|CPA|CPV|CPCV|flat_rate|GRP|TRP — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Buying Model Taxonomy; MRC Measurement Standards; Nielsen GRP/TRP Standards

attributes.name: contracted_spend
attributes.description: The total authorized spend amount for this line item as agreed in the Insertion Order (rate × contracted_units for unit-based buys, or flat rate for flat buys). Represents the financial commitment to the vendor. Core to Mediaocean Prisma billing and budget management.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma IO Authorized Spend; SAP S/4HANA CO Budget Commitment; IAB IO Standard

attributes.name: contracted_units
attributes.description: The total number of units (impressions, clicks, acquisitions, GRPs, TRPs, or views) contracted with the vendor for this line item as specified in the Insertion Order. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_QUANTITY category.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma IO Line Quantity; IAB Impression Counting Guidelines; MRC Measurement Standards

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this IO line item record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; Mediaocean Prisma Record Management; GDPR Article 5 Accountability

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary values on this line item (rate, contracted spend, actual spend). Supports multi-currency media buying across international campaigns.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Configuration

attributes.name: delivery_method
attributes.description: The mechanism by which ad inventory is purchased and delivered for this line item. Distinguishes between Real-Time Bidding (RTB) programmatic, programmatic direct, guaranteed direct buys, non-guaranteed direct, and Private Marketplace (PMP) deals. Core to The Trade Desk and Mediaocean Prisma workflow classification.
attributes.references: IAB Programmatic Buying Taxonomy; The Trade Desk Deal Types; Mediaocean Prisma Delivery Classification

attributes.name: dsp_line_code
attributes.description: The external line item identifier assigned by the Demand-Side Platform (DSP), such as The Trade Desk, for programmatic buys. Enables cross-system reconciliation between Mediaocean Prisma and the DSP platform.
attributes.references: The Trade Desk Line Item ID; IAB Programmatic Identifier Standards

attributes.name: flight_end_date
attributes.description: The date on which ad delivery for this line item is scheduled to end. Defines the close of the contracted flight period. Used for campaign scheduling, pacing, and vendor reconciliation.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Flight Dates; Google Campaign Manager 360 Flight Scheduling; IAB IO Standard

attributes.name: flight_start_date
attributes.description: The date on which ad delivery for this line item is scheduled to begin. Defines the start of the contracted flight period. Used for campaign scheduling, pacing, and reconciliation.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Flight Dates; Google Campaign Manager 360 Flight Scheduling; IAB IO Standard

attributes.name: frequency_cap
attributes.description: Maximum number of times a unique user or household may be served an ad from this line item within the frequency_cap_period. Controls ad fatigue and audience experience as configured in Google Campaign Manager 360 or The Trade Desk.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 Frequency Capping; The Trade Desk Frequency Management; IAB Frequency Cap Standards

attributes.name: frequency_cap_period
attributes.description: The time window over which the frequency_cap applies. Defines whether the cap is enforced per hour, day, week, month, or over the lifetime of the campaign flight.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 Frequency Capping; The Trade Desk Frequency Management

attributes.name: geo_targeting
attributes.description: Geographic scope of ad delivery for this line item, specifying targeted countries, regions, DMAs, or cities. Expressed as a comma-separated list of location identifiers or descriptive text (e.g., 'USA - New York DMA, Los Angeles DMA').
attributes.references: IAB Geographic Targeting Standards; The Trade Desk Geo-Targeting; Google Campaign Manager 360 Geo

attributes.name: grp_contracted
attributes.description: The total Gross Rating Points (GRPs) contracted for this line item, applicable to broadcast, OOH, and audience-guaranteed buys. Represents reach × frequency for the target audience. Tracked via Nielsen Ad Intel.
attributes.references: Nielsen Ad Intel GRP Tracking; MRC Audience Measurement Standards; IAB GRP Definition

attributes.name: line_description
attributes.description: Human-readable description of the media buy represented by this line item, including placement name, ad unit, and targeting summary as specified in the Insertion Order.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Line Item Description; IAB IO Standard

attributes.name: line_number
attributes.description: Sequential line number identifying the position of this line item within the parent Insertion Order. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_SEQUENCE category. Used for ordering, referencing, and reconciliation against vendor invoices.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Line Sequencing; IAB IO Standard

attributes.name: line_status
attributes.description: Current workflow and lifecycle status of this IO line item within Mediaocean Prisma. Tracks progression from draft through approval, active delivery, completion, and financial reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|paused|completed|cancelled|reconciled — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Line Item Workflow; IAB IO Lifecycle Standard

attributes.name: makegood_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this IO line item is a make-good — compensatory inventory provided by the vendor to offset under-delivery or quality failures on a prior line item. True if this is a make-good line; False otherwise.
attributes.references: 4As Make-Good Standards; ANA Media Buying Guidelines; Mediaocean Prisma Make-Good Workflow

attributes.name: net_rate
attributes.description: The net unit rate payable to the vendor after deducting agency commission from the gross rate. Used for vendor invoice validation and SAP S/4HANA accounts payable processing.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Net Rate Calculation; SAP S/4HANA FI Vendor Payment; 4As Billing Standards

attributes.name: prisma_line_code
attributes.description: The native line item identifier from Mediaocean Prisma, the system of record for media planning, buying, billing, and reconciliation. Used for cross-system traceability and audit.

attributes.name: rate
attributes.description: The negotiated unit rate for this line item expressed in the currency of the IO. Interpretation depends on buying_type: CPM rate per 1,000 impressions, CPC rate per click, CPA rate per acquisition, flat rate for the full flight, or GRP/TRP rate per rating point. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT category.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Rate Card; IAB Pricing Standards; 4As Rate Negotiation Guidelines

attributes.name: reconciliation_status
attributes.description: Financial reconciliation status of this line item comparing contracted delivery against vendor-reported actuals. Tracks whether the line has been reconciled, is under dispute, or has been written off. Core to Mediaocean Prisma billing reconciliation workflow.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Reconciliation Workflow; SAP S/4HANA FI Vendor Invoice Reconciliation; 4As Billing Reconciliation Guidelines

attributes.name: targeting_description
attributes.description: Free-text description of the audience targeting parameters applied to this line item, including demographic, geographic, behavioral, contextual, or keyword targeting criteria as configured in The Trade Desk or Google Campaign Manager 360.
attributes.references: The Trade Desk Audience Targeting; Google Campaign Manager 360 Targeting; IAB Audience Taxonomy

attributes.name: trp_contracted
attributes.description: The total Target Rating Points (TRPs) contracted for this line item, representing GRPs delivered specifically to the defined target audience demographic. Used for audience-guaranteed buys tracked via Nielsen Ad Intel.
attributes.references: Nielsen Ad Intel TRP Tracking; MRC Audience Measurement Standards; IAB TRP Definition

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this IO line item record was last modified. Tracks changes to rate, units, flight dates, status, or targeting parameters for audit and reconciliation purposes.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; Mediaocean Prisma Change Management; GDPR Article 5 Accountability

attributes.name: viewability_target_pct
attributes.description: The contracted minimum viewability rate (percentage of served impressions that meet MRC viewability standards) for this line item. Used to enforce quality guarantees with vendors and measure delivery compliance.
attributes.references: MRC Viewability Standards; IAB Viewability Guidelines; TAG Inventory Quality

products.name: invoice
products.description: Invoice, reconciliation, and billing resolution record for media vendors, publishers, and production suppliers. Captures the full invoice-to-payment lifecycle: invoice receipt (number, vendor reference, billing period, gross/net amounts, currency, payment due date), reconciliation against IO-authorized spend and actual ad server delivery data (CM360, The Trade Desk) including variance amount, variance percentage, discrepancy reason code, reconciliation period, dispute management, resolution status, and final approved billing amount. Supports invoice statuses: received, reconciled, approved, disputed, paid. Feeds SAP S/4HANA Accounts Payable, Mediaocean Prisma post-buy reconciliation, and financial close processes.

attributes.name: invoice_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the invoice record in the vendor billing and reconciliation system. Primary key for the invoice data product within the vendor domain.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — Vendor Invoice Management

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Advertising invoices are typically allocated to specific client brands, not just advertiser level. Brand-level budget tracking and P&L reporting require invoice-to-brand attribution. Essential for bra

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertising campaign associated with the media spend or production services billed on this invoice. Enables campaign-level financial reconciliation and P&L reporting.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 (CM360) Campaign Object; Mediaocean Prisma Campaign

attributes.name: contract_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_insertion_order. Business justification: Vendor invoices in media buying are issued against specific insertion orders. Linking invoice.contract_insertion_order_id enables IO-to-invoice matching, authorized spend validation, and payment appro

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.flight. Business justification: Invoices often bill at flight level in multi-flight campaigns for granular financial tracking. Essential for flight-level budget reconciliation, variance analysis, and financial reporting in advertisi

attributes.name: initiative_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.initiative. Business justification: Vendor invoices must be allocated to projects/initiatives for accurate project cost accounting, budget tracking, and financial reporting. Essential for project P&L, variance analysis, and client billi

attributes.name: line_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.line_item. Business justification: Invoices frequently itemize by line item for granular billing and cost tracking. Critical for line-item-level cost reconciliation, variance analysis, and financial reporting in advertising media buyin

attributes.name: production_job_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.production_job. Business justification: Accounts payable / production cost reconciliation: vendor invoices for studio, post-production, and talent services must be matched to the production job they bill against. Finance teams reconcile act

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Vendor invoices often bill against SOW-level budgets and scope, not just insertion orders. Finance teams reconcile invoice amounts to SOW committed spend and track SOW budget burn rates across multipl

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the third-party media vendor, publisher, technology partner (DSP/SSP/DMP), or production supplier who issued this invoice. Links to the vendor master record.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP — Vendor Master; Mediaocean Prisma Vendor Registry

attributes.name: agency_discount_amount
attributes.description: The monetary value of agency commission or trade discount deducted from the gross invoice amount, as negotiated in the vendor contract or IO. Typically expressed as a percentage of gross media spend.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Agency Discount; 4As Agency Commission Standards; SAP S/4HANA FI-AP

attributes.name: approved_billing_amount
attributes.description: The final approved amount authorized for payment after reconciliation and dispute resolution. May differ from the original net_amount if adjustments were made during the reconciliation or dispute process. This is the amount posted to SAP S/4HANA FI-AP for payment.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Approved Payment; Mediaocean Prisma Final Approved Billing; GAAP

attributes.name: billing_period_end
attributes.description: The end date of the media flight, production period, or service period covered by this invoice. Together with billing_period_start, defines the reconciliation window for delivery data matching.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Billing Period; IAB Insertion Order Standard

attributes.name: billing_period_start
attributes.description: The start date of the media flight, production period, or service period covered by this invoice. Used to align invoice charges with campaign delivery windows and IO-authorized spend periods.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Billing Period; IAB Insertion Order Standard

attributes.name: brand_safety_compliant
attributes.description: Indicates whether the media inventory delivered by the vendor during the billing period was verified as brand-safe per TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group) and internal brand safety standards. A value of False may trigger invoice dispute or vendor review.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Standards; IAB Brand Safety Guidelines; ASA Advertising Standards

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the system, representing the audit trail creation event. Follows ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Audit Trail; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); ISO 8601

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this invoice (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multi-currency media buying operations and SAP S/4HANA foreign currency revaluation.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Management; GAAP

attributes.name: delivered_spend_amount
attributes.description: The actual ad spend amount as recorded by the ad server (Google CM360) or DSP (The Trade Desk) for the billing period. Represents the verified delivery data used in post-buy reconciliation against the vendor invoice.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360 Reporting; The Trade Desk Bid Management; MRC Measurement Standards

attributes.name: delivery_data_source
attributes.description: The ad server or measurement platform used as the authoritative source of delivery data for reconciliation (e.g., Google CM360, The Trade Desk). Determines which system's impression and spend data is used to validate the vendor invoice. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cm360|the_trade_desk|mediaocean_prisma|nielsen|comscore|vendor_reported|other — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: MRC Measurement Standards; Google CM360; The Trade Desk; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: discrepancy_reason_code
attributes.description: Standardized code identifying the root cause of the reconciliation variance. Delivery shortfall: vendor delivered less than invoiced. Overbilling: vendor charged above IO rate. Trafficking error: ad server setup error. Invalid traffic: IVT/fraud detected. Make good: compensatory delivery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: delivery_shortfall|overbilling|trafficking_error|data_discrepancy|invalid_traffic|make_good|other — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: TAG Invalid Traffic Standards; MRC Discrepancy Guidelines; IAB Ad Fraud Standards; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: dispute_raised_date
attributes.description: The date on which a formal billing dispute was raised with the vendor. Initiates the dispute resolution clock and triggers vendor notification per contractual SLA terms.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Dispute Management; IAB Billing Dispute Guidelines; 4As Billing Standards

attributes.name: dispute_resolution_date
attributes.description: The date on which the billing dispute was formally resolved and the approved billing amount was agreed upon with the vendor. Marks the transition from 'disputed' to 'approved' status.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Dispute Management; IAB Billing Dispute Guidelines; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: dispute_resolution_notes
attributes.description: Free-text narrative documenting the outcome of the dispute resolution process, including the agreed adjustment, vendor concessions, make-good arrangements, or credit note details.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Dispute Management; IAB Billing Dispute Guidelines

attributes.name: gross_amount
attributes.description: The total gross amount billed by the vendor before any agency discounts, rebates, or adjustments. Represents the full rate card value of media or services delivered as stated on the vendor invoice.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP — Gross Amount; GAAP; Mediaocean Prisma Gross Billing

attributes.name: invalid_traffic_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether invalid traffic (IVT) or ad fraud was detected in the delivery data for this invoice period, as identified by TAG-certified measurement tools. A True value triggers discrepancy_reason_code = 'invalid_traffic' and initiates dispute proceedings.
attributes.references: TAG Invalid Traffic (IVT) Standards; MRC IVT Guidelines; IAB Ad Fraud Standards

attributes.name: invoice_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the invoice within the invoice-to-payment workflow. Received: invoice has been received from vendor. Reconciled: matched against IO and delivery data. Approved: cleared for payment. Disputed: discrepancy identified requiring resolution. Paid: payment has been issued.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Invoice Lifecycle; Mediaocean Prisma Post-Buy Reconciliation

attributes.name: invoice_type
attributes.description: Classification of the invoice by the nature of services billed. Media covers ad placements and programmatic buys; production covers creative and content production; technology covers DSP/SSP/DMP platform fees; data covers audience data and analytics services; talent covers freelance and contractor services. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: media|production|technology|data|talent|other — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Taxonomy; Mediaocean Prisma Invoice Classification

attributes.name: io_authorized_amount
attributes.description: The total spend amount authorized under the associated Insertion Order (IO) for the billing period. Used as the baseline for reconciliation against the vendor invoice gross amount to identify overbilling or underbilling.
attributes.references: IAB Insertion Order Standard; Mediaocean Prisma IO Authorization; SAP S/4HANA PS

attributes.name: issue_date
attributes.description: The date the invoice was issued by the vendor, as stated on the invoice document. Used as the principal business event date for the invoice and for payment terms calculation.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP — Invoice Date; GAAP Revenue Recognition

attributes.name: net_amount
attributes.description: The net payable amount after deducting agency discounts and rebates from the gross amount. This is the amount recorded in SAP S/4HANA Accounts Payable for payment processing.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP — Net Amount; GAAP; Mediaocean Prisma Net Billing

attributes.name: number
attributes.description: Externally-assigned invoice number as provided by the vendor on the invoice document. Used as the primary business identifier for matching vendor invoices to internal purchase orders and IOs. Must be unique per vendor.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP — Vendor Invoice Number; IAB Invoice Standards

attributes.name: payment_date
attributes.description: The date on which payment was issued to the vendor for the approved billing amount. Populated when invoice status transitions to 'paid'. Used for cash flow reporting and vendor payment history.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Payment Run; GAAP Cash Disbursement

attributes.name: payment_due_date
attributes.description: The contractual date by which payment must be made to the vendor to comply with agreed payment terms (e.g., Net 30, Net 60). Used for cash flow planning and avoiding late payment penalties.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP — Due Date; Mediaocean Prisma Payment Terms

attributes.name: payment_reference
attributes.description: The payment transaction reference number generated by SAP S/4HANA FI-AP when the payment run is executed. Used to trace the payment in bank statements and vendor remittance advice.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Payment Document; GAAP Cash Disbursement

attributes.name: payment_terms
attributes.description: The contractual payment terms agreed with the vendor, defining the number of days from invoice receipt to payment due date (e.g., Net 30, Net 60). Sourced from the vendor contract in SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Payment Terms; Mediaocean Prisma Vendor Contract; 4As Payment Standards

attributes.name: received_date
attributes.description: The date the invoice was received and logged into the accounts payable system. May differ from the issue date due to postal or electronic delivery lag. Used for aging analysis and payment terms compliance.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP — Posting Date; GAAP Accounts Payable

attributes.name: reconciliation_date
attributes.description: The date on which the invoice reconciliation was completed and the variance amount and percentage were calculated. Marks the transition from 'received' to 'reconciled' status.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Post-Buy Reconciliation; SAP S/4HANA FI-AP

attributes.name: reconciliation_period_end
attributes.description: The end date of the reconciliation window used to pull delivery data from CM360 or The Trade Desk for matching against this invoice.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Post-Buy Reconciliation; Google CM360 Reporting; The Trade Desk Reporting

attributes.name: reconciliation_period_start
attributes.description: The start date of the reconciliation window used to pull delivery data from CM360 or The Trade Desk for matching against this invoice. May differ from billing_period_start if reconciliation covers a different date range.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Post-Buy Reconciliation; Google CM360 Reporting; The Trade Desk Reporting

attributes.name: tax_amount
attributes.description: The applicable tax amount (e.g., VAT, GST, sales tax) charged on the invoice as required by the vendor's jurisdiction. Recorded separately for tax compliance and SAP S/4HANA FI tax reporting.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Module; GAAP; IFRS; FTC Advertising Practices

attributes.name: total_payable_amount
attributes.description: The final total amount payable to the vendor, calculated as net_amount plus tax_amount. This is the amount that will be disbursed in the payment run in SAP S/4HANA FI-AP.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP — Total Amount; GAAP; Mediaocean Prisma Final Billing

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when the invoice record was last modified, capturing any status changes, reconciliation updates, dispute actions, or payment postings. Used for audit trail and change tracking in the Silver Layer.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Audit Trail; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); ISO 8601

attributes.name: variance_amount
attributes.description: The monetary difference between the vendor's invoiced gross amount and the delivered spend amount recorded by the ad server or DSP. A positive value indicates overbilling by the vendor; negative indicates underbilling.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Post-Buy Reconciliation; MRC Discrepancy Standards; IAB Ad Impression Measurement

attributes.name: variance_percentage
attributes.description: The variance amount expressed as a percentage of the delivered spend amount. Used to assess whether the discrepancy exceeds the industry-standard tolerance threshold (typically 10% per MRC guidelines) triggering a formal dispute.
attributes.references: MRC Discrepancy Standards; IAB Ad Impression Measurement Guidelines; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: vendor_reference_number
attributes.description: Vendor's own internal reference or purchase order number included on the invoice, used to cross-reference the vendor's billing system and facilitate dispute resolution.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP — Vendor Reference; Mediaocean Prisma Post-Buy Reconciliation

products.name: reconciliation
products.description: Media cost reconciliation record comparing IO-authorized spend against vendor-invoiced amounts and actual delivery data from ad servers (CM360, The Trade Desk). Captures reconciliation period, variance amount, variance percentage, discrepancy reason code, resolution status, and approved final billing amount. Supports Mediaocean Prisma post-buy reconciliation and SAP S/4HANA financial close processes.

attributes.name: reconciliation_id
attributes.description: Unique surrogate identifier for the media cost reconciliation record. Primary key for this entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Post-Buy Reconciliation; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Reconciliations track delivered spend by brand for budget management. Multi-brand advertisers reconcile vendor invoices at brand level to ensure accurate brand P&L. Essential for brand-level financial

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the advertising campaign associated with this reconciliation record, enabling campaign-level financial close and post-buy analysis.

attributes.name: contract_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_insertion_order. Business justification: Media reconciliation is performed at the IO level — comparing IO-authorized spend against delivered and invoiced amounts. Linking reconciliation.contract_insertion_order_id enables IO reconciliation r

attributes.name: flight_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.flight. Business justification: Reconciliations often occur at flight level for multi-flight campaigns to verify delivery and spend. Essential for flight-level discrepancy resolution, makegood tracking, and financial accuracy in adv

attributes.name: initiative_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.initiative. Business justification: Media reconciliations validate vendor billing against project budgets and deliverables. Linking reconciliation to initiative enables project-level financial close, variance reporting, and ensures proj

attributes.name: invoice_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.invoice. Business justification: Reconciliation is the process of comparing IO-authorized spend against vendor-invoiced amounts and ad-server-delivered spend. Each reconciliation record analyzes a specific invoice to identify varianc

attributes.name: io_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_io_line. Business justification: Reconciliations occur at IO line level to match contracted vs delivered units. Essential for billing disputes, makegood identification, under-delivery penalties, and vendor performance evaluation. Ena

attributes.name: line_item_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.line_item. Business justification: Reconciliations commonly reconcile at line-item level for granular delivery verification against contracted units. Critical for line-item-level discrepancy resolution, billing accuracy, and vendor per

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Reconciliation validates delivered media spend against SOW-level budgets and scope commitments. Finance teams track SOW budget utilization, flag overruns, and trigger SOW amendments when reconciled sp

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the third-party media vendor, publisher, or technology partner (DSP/SSP) whose invoice is being reconciled against authorized spend.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma; TAG Supply Chain Transparency Standard

attributes.name: adserver_delivered_amount
attributes.description: Actual media cost derived from ad server delivery data (Google Campaign Manager 360 or The Trade Desk) for the reconciliation period. Used as the authoritative delivery benchmark in post-buy reconciliation.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360; The Trade Desk; MRC Measurement Standards

attributes.name: adserver_source
attributes.description: Identifies the authoritative ad serving or DSP platform from which delivery data was sourced for this reconciliation (e.g., Google Campaign Manager 360, The Trade Desk). Ensures traceability of delivery benchmarks.
attributes.references: Google Campaign Manager 360; The Trade Desk; MRC Measurement Standards

attributes.name: approved_billing_amount
attributes.description: The mutually agreed and approved final billing amount after reconciliation resolution. This is the amount posted to SAP S/4HANA for financial close and vendor payment processing.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; Mediaocean Prisma; 4As Financial Reconciliation Guidelines

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the agency finance or media operations team member who formally approved the final billing amount and closed the reconciliation record.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI; 4As Financial Reconciliation Guidelines

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the reconciliation record was formally approved and the final billing amount was confirmed. Key lifecycle event for SAP S/4HANA financial period close.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: buying_method
attributes.description: Method by which the media was purchased, distinguishing between direct IO buys, programmatic guaranteed, open RTB (Real-Time Bidding), private marketplace (PMP), or preferred deals. Affects reconciliation workflow and tolerance thresholds.
attributes.references: IAB Programmatic Buying Standards; The Trade Desk; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: channel_type
attributes.description: The media channel category for the spend being reconciled (e.g., Display, Video, CTV/OTT, OOH/DOOH, Search). Enables channel-level reconciliation reporting and variance analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: display|video|search|social|ooh|dooh|ctv|ott|audio|print — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Digital Ad Impression Measurement Guidelines; MRC Standards

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this reconciliation record was first created in the system, establishing the audit trail start point for the post-buy reconciliation lifecycle.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI; Mediaocean Prisma; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

attributes.name: credit_note_amount
attributes.description: Monetary value of the vendor-issued credit note applied to resolve an overbilling discrepancy. Reduces the net payable amount in SAP S/4HANA Accounts Payable.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Accounts Payable; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: credit_note_number
attributes.description: Vendor-issued credit note reference number when a credit adjustment is issued to resolve an overbilling discrepancy. Linked to SAP S/4HANA Accounts Payable credit memo processing.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Accounts Payable; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary amounts on this reconciliation record (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency media buying operations.
attributes.references: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI

attributes.name: discrepancy_description
attributes.description: Free-text narrative explaining the specific nature of the billing discrepancy, including details of delivery shortfalls, trafficking errors, or vendor disputes not fully captured by the reason code.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma; IAB Post-Buy Audit Guidelines

attributes.name: discrepancy_reason_code
attributes.description: Standardized code classifying the root cause of the billing discrepancy between IO-authorized spend and vendor invoice. Drives resolution workflow and vendor performance tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: delivery_shortfall|trafficking_error|ad_fraud|brand_safety_block|viewability_adjustment|data_discrepancy|billing_error|makegood_offset — promote to reference product]
attributes.references: IAB Post-Buy Audit Guidelines; TAG Anti-Fraud Standards; MRC Measurement Standards

attributes.name: due_date
attributes.description: Date by which the approved billing amount must be paid to the vendor per contractual payment terms. Derived from vendor invoice date and agreed net payment terms (e.g., Net 30, Net 60).
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Accounts Payable; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: io_authorized_amount
attributes.description: Total media spend amount authorized under the Insertion Order (IO) for the reconciliation period. Represents the contractually committed budget against which vendor invoices are compared.
attributes.references: IAB Insertion Order Standard; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: makegood_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether a make-good placement has been agreed as part of the discrepancy resolution, where the vendor compensates for delivery shortfalls with additional inventory rather than a monetary credit.
attributes.references: IAB Post-Buy Audit Guidelines; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional commentary, escalation notes, or vendor communication summaries relevant to the reconciliation record. Used by media finance teams during dispute resolution.

attributes.name: period_end_date
attributes.description: End date of the media delivery period covered by this reconciliation record. Together with period_start_date defines the reconciliation billing window.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma; IAB Post-Buy Audit Guidelines

attributes.name: period_start_date
attributes.description: Start date of the media delivery period covered by this reconciliation record. Defines the billing window for comparing IO-authorized spend against vendor-invoiced amounts.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma; IAB Post-Buy Audit Guidelines

attributes.name: prisma_reconciliation_ref
attributes.description: Reference identifier from Mediaocean Prisma post-buy reconciliation module corresponding to this record. Enables traceability between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the source system of record.

attributes.name: reconciliation_number
attributes.description: Externally-known business identifier for this reconciliation record, used in Mediaocean Prisma post-buy workflows and SAP S/4HANA financial close correspondence with vendors.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Post-Buy Reconciliation; SAP S/4HANA FI

attributes.name: reconciliation_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the reconciliation record within the post-buy financial close workflow. Drives approval routing in Mediaocean Prisma and SAP S/4HANA.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Post-Buy Reconciliation; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

attributes.name: reconciliation_type
attributes.description: Classification of the reconciliation record indicating whether it covers standard media buys, programmatic (RTB/DSP) activity, make-good placements, credit adjustments, or final billing close.
attributes.references: IAB Post-Buy Audit Guidelines; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: resolution_date
attributes.description: Date on which the billing discrepancy was formally resolved and the approved billing amount was agreed between the agency and the vendor.

attributes.name: resolution_status
attributes.description: Current status of the discrepancy resolution process. Tracks whether the variance is unresolved, awaiting vendor response, under internal review, fully resolved, or written off as immaterial.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Post-Buy Reconciliation; 4As Financial Reconciliation Guidelines

attributes.name: sap_document_number
attributes.description: SAP S/4HANA FI accounting document number generated upon financial close posting of the approved billing amount. Provides the audit link between media reconciliation and the general ledger.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; ANA Media Transparency Guidelines

attributes.name: tolerance_threshold_pct
attributes.description: Agreed acceptable variance percentage threshold below which discrepancies are considered immaterial and do not require formal dispute resolution. Typically set per vendor contract or IAB industry standard (commonly 10%).
attributes.references: IAB Post-Buy Audit Guidelines; MRC Measurement Standards; ANA Media Transparency Guidelines

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this reconciliation record was most recently modified, supporting audit trail requirements and change tracking in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA FI; Mediaocean Prisma; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

attributes.name: variance_amount
attributes.description: Absolute monetary difference between the vendor-invoiced amount and the ad server delivered amount (vendor_invoiced_amount minus adserver_delivered_amount). Positive value indicates vendor overbilling; negative indicates underbilling.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma Post-Buy Reconciliation; IAB Post-Buy Audit Guidelines

attributes.name: variance_pct
attributes.description: Variance expressed as a percentage of the IO-authorized amount ((variance_amount / io_authorized_amount) * 100). Used to assess materiality of discrepancy against agency tolerance thresholds (typically ±10% per IAB guidelines).
attributes.references: IAB Post-Buy Audit Guidelines; MRC Measurement Standards; Mediaocean Prisma

attributes.name: vendor_invoiced_amount
attributes.description: Total gross amount billed by the vendor on their invoice for the reconciliation period. Primary input to variance calculation against IO-authorized spend.
attributes.references: Mediaocean Prisma; SAP S/4HANA FI Accounts Payable

attributes.name: within_tolerance_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the variance percentage falls within the agreed tolerance threshold, determining whether formal dispute escalation is required.
attributes.references: IAB Post-Buy Audit Guidelines; MRC Measurement Standards

products.name: performance_evaluation
products.description: Periodic vendor performance evaluation records capturing delivery fulfillment rate against IO commitments, make-good obligations, invoice accuracy rate, response time SLA adherence, brand safety incident count, and overall vendor scorecard rating. Evaluated quarterly (QBR cycle) and used to inform preferred vendor list tier assignments and contract renewal decisions. Operational records — not aggregated analytics.

attributes.name: performance_evaluation_id
attributes.description: Unique system-generated identifier for each vendor performance evaluation record. Primary key for the performance_evaluation product.

attributes.name: agency_relationship_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.agency_relationship. Business justification: Vendor performance evaluations are conducted in context of specific agency-client relationships. Vendor scorecards vary by client account due to different SLAs, rate cards, and service expectations. L

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Vendor scorecards and delivery fulfillment evaluations in advertising are conducted at the brand level — a supplier's performance against a Coca-Cola vs. a Nike campaign is tracked separately. Brand-l

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to campaign.campaign. Business justification: Post-campaign vendor scorecards are scoped to specific campaigns — delivery fulfillment rate, brand safety incidents, and invalid traffic rate are evaluated per campaign engagement. A domain expert ex

attributes.name: initiative_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.initiative. Business justification: Vendor performance evaluations are often scoped to specific projects they supported, assessing delivery, quality, and responsiveness within project context. Enables project-based vendor scorecarding a

attributes.name: production_job_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to creative.production_job. Business justification: Vendor performance management: evaluating a production studio or creative vendor requires referencing the specific production job(s) assessed. Scorecard ratings for delivery fulfillment, revision coun

attributes.name: sla_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sla. Business justification: Vendor performance evaluations directly measure SLA compliance (performance_evaluation has sla_breaches_count, sla_incidents_total, response_time_sla_adherence_rate). Linking to the specific SLA being

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Performance evaluations assess vendor delivery against SOW scope, deliverables, and SLA targets. Account teams score vendors on SOW fulfillment rates, link evaluation outcomes to SOW renewal decisions

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Reference to the vendor being evaluated. Links to the vendor master record in the vendor domain.

attributes.name: actual_delivered_units
attributes.description: Total number of impressions, clicks, or deliverable units actually delivered by the vendor during the evaluation period as recorded in the ad server (e.g., Google Campaign Manager 360). Numerator for delivery fulfillment rate calculation.
attributes.references: MRC Measurement Standards; IAB Impression Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the senior stakeholder (e.g., VP of Media, Chief Procurement Officer) who formally approved this vendor performance evaluation and its resulting tier assignment and renewal recommendation.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Internal Governance Standards; ANA Vendor Accountability Framework

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the vendor performance evaluation was formally approved by the designated approver. Distinct lifecycle event from evaluation completion.

attributes.name: brand_safety_incident_count
attributes.description: Number of brand safety incidents recorded against the vendor during the evaluation period, including ad placements adjacent to harmful, inappropriate, or non-brand-safe content. Critical metric for TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group) compliance and preferred vendor list eligibility.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Standards; IAB Brand Safety Guidelines; GARM Brand Safety Floor

attributes.name: brand_safety_incident_severity
attributes.description: Highest severity level of brand safety incidents recorded against the vendor during the evaluation period. Aligned to GARM (Global Alliance for Responsible Media) brand safety floor and suitability framework tiers.
attributes.references: TAG Brand Safety Standards; GARM Brand Safety Floor; IAB Brand Safety Guidelines

attributes.name: contract_renewal_recommendation
attributes.description: Formal recommendation for contract renewal action based on the outcome of this performance evaluation. Feeds directly into the contract management workflow and vendor relationship strategy decisions.
attributes.references: 4As Agency Contract Standards; ANA Vendor Accountability Framework

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this vendor performance evaluation record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and governance purposes.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); IAB Data Governance Standards

attributes.name: delivery_fulfillment_rate
attributes.description: Percentage of Insertion Order (IO) committed impressions, units, or deliverables that were actually fulfilled by the vendor during the evaluation period. Calculated as (actual_delivered / io_committed) * 100. Core KPI for media vendor accountability.
attributes.references: MRC Measurement Standards; IAB Impression Measurement Guidelines

attributes.name: disputed_invoices_count
attributes.description: Number of invoices submitted by the vendor during the evaluation period that required dispute resolution, credit notes, or correction due to billing inaccuracies. Feeds into invoice accuracy rate and financial risk assessment.
attributes.references: 4As Financial Reconciliation Standards; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

attributes.name: evaluation_cycle
attributes.description: The fiscal quarter in which this evaluation was conducted, aligned to the QBR (Quarterly Business Review) cycle. Used to track cadence and trend analysis across periods.

attributes.name: evaluation_date
attributes.description: The principal business date on which the formal vendor performance evaluation was conducted or finalized, typically the QBR session date.

attributes.name: evaluation_notes
attributes.description: Free-text qualitative commentary from the evaluator capturing context, mitigating factors, notable incidents, or strategic observations not captured in the quantitative scorecard metrics. Used in QBR presentations and vendor relationship management.
attributes.references: 4As QBR Standards; ANA Vendor Accountability Framework

attributes.name: evaluation_number
attributes.description: Externally-visible business identifier for this evaluation record, used in QBR documentation, vendor communications, and preferred vendor list reviews. Format: VPE-{YEAR}-{SEQUENCE}.

attributes.name: evaluation_period_end_date
attributes.description: End date of the performance measurement period covered by this evaluation, typically aligned to the quarterly QBR cycle (e.g., last day of the quarter).

attributes.name: evaluation_period_start_date
attributes.description: Start date of the performance measurement period covered by this evaluation, typically aligned to the quarterly QBR cycle (e.g., first day of the quarter).

attributes.name: evaluation_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state of the vendor performance evaluation record. Draft indicates data collection in progress; in_review indicates pending stakeholder sign-off; completed indicates finalized; approved indicates accepted by vendor management; disputed indicates vendor has raised a formal objection.

attributes.name: evaluation_year
attributes.description: The fiscal year in which this evaluation was conducted. Combined with evaluation_cycle to uniquely identify the QBR period for trend and year-over-year comparison.

attributes.name: evaluator_name
attributes.description: Name of the internal agency staff member or team lead responsible for conducting and submitting this vendor performance evaluation, typically the media buyer, account director, or vendor management lead.

attributes.name: invalid_traffic_rate
attributes.description: Percentage of impressions delivered by the vendor during the evaluation period classified as invalid traffic (IVT) including general invalid traffic (GIVT) and sophisticated invalid traffic (SIVT) per MRC and TAG standards. Critical for fraud prevention and supply chain transparency.
attributes.references: MRC Invalid Traffic Standards; TAG Certified Against Fraud Program; IAB Tech Lab IVT Guidelines

attributes.name: invoice_accuracy_rate
attributes.description: Percentage of vendor invoices submitted during the evaluation period that matched the reconciled billing amounts within the agreed tolerance threshold, without requiring correction or credit notes. Calculated as (accurate_invoices / total_invoices) * 100. Sourced from Mediaocean Prisma reconciliation data.
attributes.references: 4As Financial Reconciliation Standards; IAB Payment Standards

attributes.name: io_committed_units
attributes.description: Total number of impressions, clicks, or deliverable units committed by the vendor across all active Insertion Orders (IOs) during the evaluation period. Denominator for delivery fulfillment rate calculation.
attributes.references: IAB Insertion Order Standards; MRC Measurement Standards

attributes.name: makegood_fulfilled_count
attributes.description: Number of make-good obligations that were successfully fulfilled by the vendor during the evaluation period. Compared against makegood_obligation_count to assess vendor responsiveness to under-delivery.
attributes.references: IAB Make-Good Standards; 4As Media Buying Guidelines

attributes.name: makegood_obligation_count
attributes.description: Number of make-good obligations raised against the vendor during the evaluation period due to under-delivery against IO commitments. A make-good is compensatory inventory provided by the vendor to offset shortfalls.
attributes.references: IAB Make-Good Standards; 4As Media Buying Guidelines

attributes.name: mrc_accreditation_status
attributes.description: Current MRC (Media Rating Council) accreditation status of the vendor at the time of evaluation. MRC accreditation validates the vendor's measurement methodologies and is required for audience measurement and impression counting credibility.
attributes.references: MRC Accreditation Program; IAB Measurement Standards

attributes.name: overall_scorecard_rating
attributes.description: Composite weighted scorecard rating for the vendor for the evaluation period, typically on a scale of 0.00 to 10.00 or 0.00 to 100.00. Aggregates delivery fulfillment, invoice accuracy, SLA adherence, brand safety, and viewability dimensions into a single performance score used for preferred vendor list tier assignment.
attributes.references: ANA Vendor Accountability Framework; 4As Vendor Scorecard Standards

attributes.name: preferred_vendor_tier
attributes.description: Preferred vendor list tier assignment resulting from this evaluation.
