Tier 1 indicates top-performing strategic partners; Tier 2 indicates approved vendors; Tier 3 indicates conditional approval; Probation indicates performance improvement required; Disqualified indicates removal from preferred vendor list.
attributes.references: ANA Preferred Vendor List Standards; 4As Vendor Management Guidelines

attributes.name: prior_preferred_vendor_tier
attributes.business_glossary_term: Prior Period Preferred Vendor List (PVL) Tier
attributes.description: The vendor's preferred vendor list tier from the immediately preceding evaluation period. Used to track tier movement (promotion or demotion) resulting from this evaluation cycle.

attributes.name: prior_scorecard_rating
attributes.description: The vendor's overall scorecard rating from the immediately preceding evaluation period. Enables quarter-over-quarter trend analysis and identification of improving or declining vendor performance trajectories.
attributes.references: ANA Vendor Accountability Framework; 4As QBR Standards

attributes.name: response_time_sla_adherence_rate
attributes.business_glossary_term: Response Time Service Level Agreement (SLA) Adherence Rate
attributes.description: Percentage of vendor response obligations (e.g., campaign issue resolution, data discrepancy responses, creative trafficking requests) fulfilled within the contractually agreed SLA response time during the evaluation period.
attributes.references: 4As Agency-Vendor SLA Standards; ANA Vendor Accountability Framework

attributes.name: scorecard_rating_scale
attributes.description: The numeric scale used for the overall_scorecard_rating in this evaluation (e.g., 0-10, 0-100, 1-5). Ensures correct interpretation of the rating value across different evaluation frameworks or vendor categories.

attributes.name: sla_breaches_count
attributes.description: Number of SLA response obligations that were not fulfilled within the contractually agreed timeframe during the evaluation period. Used to identify chronic SLA non-compliance patterns.
attributes.references: 4As Agency-Vendor SLA Standards; ANA Vendor Accountability Framework

attributes.name: sla_incidents_total
attributes.description: Total number of SLA-governed response obligations raised against the vendor during the evaluation period. Denominator for response_time_sla_adherence_rate calculation.

attributes.name: tag_certification_status
attributes.description: Current TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group) certification status of the vendor at the time of evaluation. TAG certification is a prerequisite for preferred vendor list tier 1 eligibility and indicates compliance with anti-fraud, brand safety, and supply chain transparency standards.
attributes.references: TAG Certification Program; ANA Vendor Accountability Framework

attributes.name: total_invoices_submitted
attributes.description: Total number of invoices submitted by the vendor during the evaluation period. Denominator for invoice accuracy rate calculation. Sourced from Mediaocean Prisma billing records.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this vendor performance evaluation record was last modified. Audit trail field for change tracking and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.
attributes.references: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); IAB Data Governance Standards

attributes.name: vendor_category
attributes.description: Classification of the vendor type being evaluated. Determines which performance dimensions and benchmarks are applicable. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: media_publisher|dsp|ssp|dmp|cdp|production_supplier|technology_partner|research_vendor — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: vendor_dispute_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the vendor has formally disputed the findings of this performance evaluation. When True, the evaluation status should reflect 'disputed' and a resolution process must be initiated.
attributes.references: ANA Vendor Accountability Framework; 4As Dispute Resolution Guidelines

attributes.name: vendor_dispute_notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes capturing the vendor's formal objection or dispute rationale when vendor_dispute_flag is True. Documents the basis of the dispute for audit trail and resolution tracking purposes.
attributes.references: ANA Vendor Accountability Framework; 4As Dispute Resolution Guidelines

attributes.name: viewability_rate
attributes.description: Percentage of impressions delivered by the vendor during the evaluation period that met the MRC (Media Rating Council) viewability standard (50% of pixels in view for 1 continuous second for display; 50% for 2 seconds for video). Key quality metric for media vendor evaluation.
attributes.references: MRC Viewability Standards; IAB Viewability Guidelines

domains.name: project
domains.description: Manages agency project lifecycle, work breakdown structures, task assignments, timelines, milestones, and resource scheduling across campaigns and client engagements. Integrates with Workfront/Monday.com as system of record for project execution.

products.name: initiative
products.description: Master record for an agency project or engagement — the authoritative SSOT for project identity, scope, and lifecycle within the agency. Captures project name, type (campaign production, brand strategy, media planning, PR activation, etc.), status (briefing, planning, in-flight, on-hold, completed, cancelled), priority, start and end dates, planned vs. actual duration, client account reference, associated campaign reference, owning department, project manager, delivery methodology (agile, waterfall, hybrid), source system (Workfront/Monday.com) project ID, and overall health indicator. Serves as the top-level container for all work breakdown structures, tasks, milestones, and resource assignments.

attributes.name: initiative_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the agency project or engagement. Primary key.

attributes.name: advertiser_id
attributes.description: Reference to the client advertiser account for whom this project is being executed.

attributes.name: agency_relationship_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.agency_relationship. Business justification: Projects are delivered under specific agency relationship agreements (AOR, project-based retainer, pitch work). Tracking which relationship governs the work is essential for billing rules, scope bound

attributes.name: agreement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Master agreements govern multi-project client relationships. Agencies link initiatives to MSAs to apply correct rate cards, enforce compliance obligations, and manage multi-year retainer terms across

attributes.name: attribution_model_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to performance.attribution_model. Business justification: Strategic initiatives define attribution methodology for performance evaluation. Real business process: campaign planning phase where strategists select attribution model (last-click, linear, data-dri

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Initiatives are executed on behalf of a specific brand. Brand-level reporting — SOV tracking, brand health metrics, budget utilization by brand — requires linking initiatives to client_brand. initiati

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the associated marketing campaign if this project is part of a broader campaign initiative. Nullable for non-campaign projects.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Projects must be assigned to cost centers for financial reporting, budget allocation, and P&L tracking in agency operations. Essential for departmental profitability analysis and overhead allocation.

attributes.name: parent_initiative_id
attributes.description: Self-referencing FK on initiative (parent_initiative_id)

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Initiatives execute SOW-defined scope. Agencies track SOW-to-project mapping for revenue recognition, deliverable tracking, budget burn analysis, and client invoicing against contractual commitments.

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Projects/initiatives frequently engage vendors for media buying, tech platforms, creative production. Tracking primary supplier enables project vendor management, cost allocation, and vendor performan

attributes.name: actual_cost
attributes.description: Total actual cost incurred to date for labor, materials, and external expenses. Updated as costs are recorded.
attributes.references: Financial Accounting Standards Board (FASB) - Cost Accounting

attributes.name: actual_duration_days
attributes.description: Actual elapsed duration of the project in calendar days. Populated upon project completion.

attributes.name: billing_type
attributes.description: Commercial model under which the project is billed to the client.
attributes.references: Association of National Advertisers (ANA) - Compensation Models

attributes.name: budget_amount
attributes.description: Total approved budget allocated for this project in the agency's reporting currency.
attributes.references: Financial Accounting Standards Board (FASB) - Budget Management

attributes.name: budget_currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the project budget (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).

attributes.name: cancellation_date
attributes.description: Date when the project was formally cancelled. Populated only for cancelled projects.

attributes.name: cancellation_reason
attributes.description: Explanation for why the project was cancelled (e.g., client budget cuts, scope changes, strategic pivot).

attributes.name: client_satisfaction_score
attributes.description: Client-reported satisfaction rating for the project, typically on a scale of 1.00 to 5.00. Captured post-delivery or at milestones.
attributes.references: Net Promoter Score (NPS) and Client Feedback Standards

attributes.name: completion_date
attributes.description: Actual date when all project deliverables were completed and accepted by the client.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this project record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: deliverables_summary
attributes.description: High-level summary of key deliverables expected from this project (e.g., creative assets, media plans, campaign reports).

attributes.name: delivery_methodology
attributes.description: Project management methodology applied for planning and execution (e.g., agile sprints, waterfall phases, hybrid approach).

attributes.name: end_date
attributes.description: Planned or actual date when project work is scheduled to complete or was completed.

attributes.name: health_indicator
attributes.description: Overall health status of the project based on schedule, budget, scope, and quality metrics. Green indicates on-track, yellow indicates at-risk, red indicates critical issues.
attributes.references: Project Management Institute (PMI) - Project Health Monitoring

attributes.name: initiative_name
attributes.description: Human-readable name of the project or engagement, used for identification and reporting.

attributes.name: initiative_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the project indicating its stage in the execution workflow.
attributes.references: Project Management Institute (PMI) - Project Lifecycle Standards

attributes.name: is_billable
attributes.description: Indicates whether this project is billable to the client (true) or internal/non-billable (false).

attributes.name: is_confidential
attributes.description: Indicates whether this project is subject to confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements requiring restricted access.

attributes.name: kickoff_date
attributes.description: Date of the formal project kickoff meeting with the client and internal team.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this project record was last updated.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-form notes capturing additional context, special instructions, or important observations about the project.

attributes.name: owning_department
attributes.description: Primary agency department responsible for delivering this project. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: creative|media|strategy|digital|pr|production|account_services — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: planned_duration_days
attributes.description: Originally estimated duration of the project in calendar days from start to end.

attributes.name: priority
attributes.description: Business priority level assigned to the project for resource allocation and scheduling decisions.

attributes.name: project_code
attributes.description: Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the project for client communication and billing. Typically follows agency naming convention.
attributes.references: Project Management Institute (PMI) - Project Identifier Standards

attributes.name: project_type
attributes.description: Classification of the project based on the primary service offering or deliverable type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: campaign_production|brand_strategy|media_planning|creative_development|pr_activation|digital_marketing|content_creation|market_research|event_management|retainer_services — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: risk_level
attributes.description: Overall risk assessment for the project based on complexity, dependencies, client volatility, and resource constraints.

attributes.name: scope_description
attributes.description: Detailed narrative describing the project scope, objectives, deliverables, and boundaries.

attributes.name: source_system_code
attributes.description: Unique project identifier from the originating system of record (Workfront, Monday.com, or other project management platform).

attributes.name: source_system_name
attributes.description: Name of the project management system from which this project record was ingested.

attributes.name: start_date
attributes.description: Planned or actual date when project work officially begins.

products.name: work_package
products.description: Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) node representing a discrete, manageable unit of work within a project. Captures WBS code, package name, description, parent package reference (enabling hierarchical decomposition), level in the WBS hierarchy, owning discipline or department (creative, media, strategy, production, PR), planned effort hours, actual effort hours, planned cost, actual cost, status, and completion percentage. Enables multi-level decomposition of project scope into deliverable-oriented components aligned to Workfront/Monday.com task groups.

attributes.name: work_package_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the work package within the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS). Primary key.
attributes.references: Project Management Institute (PMI) PMBOK Guide - WBS Standards

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Work packages require cost center assignment for labor cost allocation and profitability analysis by discipline. Enables accurate project costing and departmental charge-back in agency financial syste

attributes.name: initiative_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent project or campaign engagement that this work package belongs to.

attributes.name: parent_work_package_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent work package in the hierarchical WBS structure. Null for top-level packages.
attributes.references: Project Management Institute (PMI) PMBOK Guide - WBS Hierarchy

attributes.name: segment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to audience.audience_segment. Business justification: Advertising work packages are scoped to specific audience segments (e.g., 'Build Retargeting Segment', 'Activate Prospecting Audience'). Project managers need this link to track segment-specific work

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Work packages decompose SOW scope. Agencies map WBS elements to SOW scope items for traceability, ensuring all contracted deliverables are planned and no out-of-scope work is executed without authoriz

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: In advertising operations, specific work packages (e.g., creative production, media buying execution) are assigned to external vendors. Linking supplier_id to work_package enables vendor accountabilit

attributes.name: actual_cost
attributes.description: Actual cost incurred for this work package to date. Used for cost variance analysis and Earned Value Management (EVM).
attributes.references: Project Management Institute (PMI) PMBOK Guide - Cost Management

attributes.name: actual_effort_hours
attributes.description: Actual labor hours expended on this work package to date. Tracked from time entry systems for variance analysis.
attributes.references: Project Management Institute (PMI) PMBOK Guide - Resource Management

attributes.name: actual_end_date
attributes.description: Actual date when work on this package was completed. Null if not yet completed.
attributes.references: Project Management Institute (PMI) PMBOK Guide - Schedule Management

attributes.name: actual_start_date
attributes.description: Actual date when work on this package commenced. Null if not yet started.
attributes.references: Project Management Institute (PMI) PMBOK Guide - Schedule Management

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Current approval state of the work package deliverable. Tracks client or internal stakeholder sign-off.
attributes.references: Project Management Institute (PMI) PMBOK Guide - Quality Management

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the person who approved this work package. Null if not yet approved.

attributes.name: approved_date
attributes.description: Date when this work package was formally approved. Null if not yet approved.

attributes.name: completion_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of work completed for this package (0.00 to 100.00). Used for progress tracking and Earned Value (EV) calculation.
attributes.references: Project Management Institute (PMI) PMBOK Guide - Earned Value Management

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this work package record was first created in the system.
attributes.references: Data Governance Framework - Audit Trail Standards

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for planned and actual cost amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

attributes.name: deliverable_type
attributes.description: Classification of the primary deliverable produced by this work package (e.g., 'Creative Brief', 'Media Plan', 'Campaign Report', 'Video Asset'). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: creative_brief|media_plan|campaign_report|video_asset|print_ad|digital_banner|social_content|presentation_deck|research_report|strategy_document|press_release — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: discipline
attributes.description: Primary agency discipline or department responsible for executing this work package (e.g., creative, media, strategy, production, PR). attributes.name: is_billable
attributes.description: Indicates whether this work package is billable to the client (true) or non-billable internal work (false).

attributes.name: is_critical_path
attributes.description: Indicates whether this work package is on the project critical path. Delays to critical path items directly impact project completion date.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this work package record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit requirements.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-form text field for additional context, instructions, risks, or issues related to this work package.

attributes.name: planned_cost
attributes.description: Budgeted cost for this work package including labor, materials, and other direct costs. Baseline for Earned Value Management (EVM).
attributes.references: Project Management Institute (PMI) PMBOK Guide - Cost Management

attributes.name: planned_effort_hours
attributes.description: Estimated total labor hours required to complete this work package. Used for resource capacity planning and baseline cost estimation.
attributes.references: Project Management Institute (PMI) PMBOK Guide - Resource Management

attributes.name: planned_end_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date when work on this package is planned to be completed. Used for baseline schedule tracking.
attributes.references: Project Management Institute (PMI) PMBOK Guide - Schedule Management

attributes.name: planned_start_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date when work on this package is planned to begin. Used for baseline schedule tracking.
attributes.references: Project Management Institute (PMI) PMBOK Guide - Schedule Management

attributes.name: priority
attributes.description: Business priority level assigned to this work package for resource allocation and scheduling decisions.

attributes.name: wbs_code
attributes.description: Hierarchical alphanumeric code representing the work package position in the WBS (e.g., 1.2.3.1). Enables structured decomposition and rollup reporting.
attributes.references: Project Management Institute (PMI) PMBOK Guide - WBS Dictionary

attributes.name: wbs_level
attributes.description: Numeric depth of this work package in the WBS hierarchy (1 = top level, 2 = second level, etc.). Enables level-based filtering and rollup.

attributes.name: work_package_description
attributes.description: Detailed narrative describing the scope, deliverables, and objectives of this work package. Provides context for resource planning and execution.

attributes.name: work_package_name
attributes.description: Short, descriptive name of the work package that clearly identifies the deliverable or work scope (e.g., 'Creative Concept Development', 'Media Buy Execution').

attributes.name: work_package_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the work package indicating progress and execution state.

attributes.name: work_package_type
attributes.description: Classification of the work package within the WBS structure (deliverable-oriented, milestone, phase, task group, or control account).
attributes.references: Project Management Institute (PMI) PMBOK Guide - WBS Types

products.name: task
products.description: Atomic unit of work assigned to an individual or team within a work package. Captures task name, description, task type (creative production, copy writing, media trafficking, client review, QA, approval, etc.), assigned worker reference, assigned team, planned start date, planned end date, actual start date, actual end date, estimated hours, logged hours, completion percentage, priority, status (not started, in progress, blocked, complete), dependency type, predecessor task reference, and source system task ID from Workfront or Monday.com. The operational heartbeat of project execution tracking.

attributes.name: task_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the task. Primary key.

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Tasks need cost center tracking for time-based costing and departmental charge-back. Critical for labor cost allocation when time entries roll up to tasks for financial posting.

attributes.name: initiative_id
attributes.description: Reference to the project this task belongs to.

attributes.name: parent_task_id
attributes.description: Self-referencing FK on task (parent_task_id)

attributes.name: primary_predecessor_task_id
attributes.description: Reference to the task that must be completed or started before this task can proceed, based on the dependency type.

attributes.name: work_package_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent work package that contains this task.

attributes.name: actual_end_date
attributes.description: The actual date when this task was completed.

attributes.name: actual_start_date
attributes.description: The actual date when work on this task began.

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: The current approval status of the task deliverable (not submitted, pending, approved, rejected, changes requested).

attributes.name: blocked_reason
attributes.description: Description of the reason why this task is blocked or cannot proceed.

attributes.name: completed_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this task was marked as complete.

attributes.description: The percentage of work completed for this task, expressed as a value between 0 and 100.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this task record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: deliverable_type
attributes.description: The type of deliverable or output expected from this task (e.g., creative asset, media plan, trafficking sheet, approval document).

attributes.name: dependency_type
attributes.description: The type of dependency relationship this task has with its predecessor (finish-to-start, start-to-start, finish-to-finish, start-to-finish, or none).

attributes.name: estimated_hours
attributes.description: The estimated number of hours required to complete this task.

attributes.name: is_billable
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the hours logged on this task are billable to the client.
attributes.references: American Association of Advertising Agencies (4As) Billing Standards

attributes.name: is_milestone
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this task represents a project milestone.

attributes.name: logged_hours
attributes.description: The actual number of hours logged or worked on this task.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this task record was last modified or updated.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional notes, comments, or instructions related to this task.

attributes.name: planned_end_date
attributes.description: The scheduled date when this task is planned to be completed.

attributes.name: planned_start_date
attributes.description: The scheduled date when work on this task is planned to begin.

attributes.name: priority
attributes.description: The priority level assigned to this task indicating its relative importance and urgency.

attributes.name: source_system_name
attributes.description: The name of the operational system of record from which this task was sourced (e.g., Workfront, Monday.com).

attributes.name: source_system_task_code
attributes.description: The unique identifier for this task in the source system (Workfront or Monday.com).

attributes.name: task_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the work to be performed in this task.

attributes.name: task_name
attributes.description: The name or title of the task.

attributes.name: task_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the task (not started, in progress, blocked, complete, cancelled, on hold).

attributes.name: task_type
attributes.description: The category or type of work this task represents (e.g., creative production, copywriting, media trafficking, client review, quality assurance, approval).

products.name: milestone
products.description: Significant scheduled checkpoint or deliverable gate within a project lifecycle. Captures milestone name, description, milestone type (kickoff, creative review, client approval, campaign launch, post-campaign report, billing trigger, contractual gate), planned date, actual date, status (upcoming, achieved, missed, deferred), owner, associated work package, linked SOW contractual milestone reference, and variance in days between planned and actual achievement. Used for client reporting, billing triggers, and executive project health dashboards.

attributes.name: milestone_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the project milestone. Primary key.

attributes.name: amendment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.amendment. Business justification: Contract amendments in advertising frequently revise milestone dates and deliverable scope. Linking project_milestone.amendment_id → amendment provides the audit trail showing which amendment triggere

attributes.name: initiative_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent project to which this milestone belongs.

attributes.name: replanned_from_project_milestone_id
attributes.description: Self-referencing FK on project_milestone (replanned_from_project_milestone_id)

attributes.name: sla_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sla. Business justification: Contractual milestone gates in advertising are governed by SLAs (e.g., creative delivery turnaround SLAs). project_milestone.is_contractual_gate flag signals this need. Linking to sla enables SLA brea

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Contractual campaign milestones (e.g., creative delivery gate, campaign launch) are often vendor-dependent in advertising. Linking supplier_id to project_milestone enables vendor-specific milestone tr

attributes.name: work_package_id
attributes.description: Reference to the associated work package or phase within the project structure.

attributes.name: acceptance_criteria
attributes.description: Specific, measurable criteria that must be met for the milestone to be considered successfully achieved and approved by stakeholders.

attributes.name: actual_date
attributes.description: Actual date the milestone was achieved or completed. Null if milestone has not yet been reached.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the milestone completion was formally approved by the designated approver. Null if not yet approved.

attributes.name: baseline_date
attributes.description: Baseline date captured at project approval or contract signature, used for variance analysis and performance measurement against original commitment.

attributes.name: completion_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of work completed toward achieving this milestone, expressed as a decimal (0.00 to 100.00). Used for progress tracking and earned value analysis.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this milestone record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: deliverable_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the tangible deliverable or output associated with this milestone (e.g., 'Final creative assets in all formats', 'Campaign performance dashboard', 'Client presentation deck').

attributes.name: dependency_count
attributes.description: Number of predecessor tasks or milestones that must be completed before this milestone can be achieved. Used for dependency analysis and scheduling.

attributes.name: forecast_date
attributes.description: Current forecasted date for milestone completion based on project progress and risk assessment. Updated regularly during project execution.

attributes.name: is_billing_trigger
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether achievement of this milestone triggers invoice generation or payment milestone per the client contract.

attributes.name: is_contractual_gate
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this milestone is a contractually binding checkpoint defined in the SOW, requiring formal client sign-off before proceeding.

attributes.name: is_critical_path
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this milestone lies on the project critical path. Delays to critical path milestones directly impact overall project completion date.

attributes.name: milestone_description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the milestone deliverable, acceptance criteria, and business significance.

attributes.name: milestone_name
attributes.description: Business-friendly name of the milestone (e.g., 'Creative Concept Approval', 'Campaign Launch', 'Post-Campaign Report Delivery').

attributes.name: milestone_type
attributes.description: Classification of the milestone by its business function: kickoff (project initiation), creative_review (internal creative approval), client_approval (client sign-off gate), campaign_launch (media go-live), post_campaign_report (campaign performance delivery), billing_trigger (invoice generation event), contractual_gate (SOW-defined checkpoint). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kickoff|creative_review|client_approval|campaign_launch|post_campaign_report|billing_trigger|contractual_gate — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this milestone record was last updated.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional context, issues, decisions, or stakeholder feedback related to the milestone.

attributes.name: planned_date
attributes.description: Originally scheduled date for milestone achievement as defined in the project plan or SOW.

attributes.name: project_milestone_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the milestone: upcoming (not yet started), in_progress (work underway toward milestone), achieved (successfully completed on or before planned date), missed (completed after planned date or not completed by deadline), deferred (postponed to future date), cancelled (no longer required).

attributes.name: risk_level
attributes.description: Current risk assessment for on-time milestone achievement: low (on track, no issues), medium (minor concerns, monitoring required), high (significant risk of delay), critical (delay imminent or already occurred).

attributes.name: sow_milestone_reference
attributes.description: External reference code linking this milestone to a contractual milestone defined in the client Statement of Work (SOW). Used for contractual compliance and billing trigger validation.

attributes.name: variance_days
attributes.description: Calculated variance in days between planned date and actual date. Positive values indicate delay (missed deadline), negative values indicate early completion. Null if milestone not yet achieved.

products.name: resource_plan
products.description: Forward-looking resource demand plan for a project, capturing the planned allocation of agency talent and capacity across the project timeline. Stores project reference, planning period (week, sprint, month), role or discipline required, planned FTE count, planned hours, skill requirements, seniority level, department, cost rate applied, total planned cost, and plan version. Distinct from talent.resource_allocation (which records confirmed individual assignments) — this is the demand-side capacity plan used for staffing decisions and capacity forecasting at the project level.

attributes.name: resource_plan_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the resource plan record. Primary key for the resource plan entity.

attributes.name: contract_rate_card_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_card. Business justification: Agency resource plans are costed against negotiated rate cards. resource_plan has cost_rate, role_code, seniority_level that must align with contracted rates. This link enables accurate cost planning

Bridges project execution with talent workforce data and enables utilization tracking at the task level.

products.name: deliverable_tracker
products.description: Operational tracking record for each project deliverable — monitoring production progress from brief through final delivery. Captures deliverable name, deliverable type (creative asset, media plan, strategy deck, report, campaign setup, trafficking sheet, etc.), associated work package, planned delivery date, actual delivery date, delivery status (not started, in production, in review, approved, delivered, rejected), review round count, client approval status, format specifications, file reference, and delivery channel (email, portal, FTP, DAM). Distinct from contract.deliverable (contractual commitment) — this is the operational production tracking record.

attributes.name: deliverable_tracker_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the deliverable tracking record. Primary key for operational tracking of project deliverables from brief through final delivery.

attributes.name: campaign_id
attributes.description: Reference to the campaign this deliverable supports, if applicable. Links deliverable to the broader campaign execution.

attributes.name: contract_deliverable_id
attributes.description: Reference to the contractual deliverable commitment in the Statement of Work (SOW) or Insertion Order (IO). Links operational tracking to contractual obligation.

attributes.name: initiative_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent project under which this deliverable is being produced.

attributes.name: milestone_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.project_milestone. Business justification: Deliverables are reviewed and approved at milestone gates (project_milestone has is_billing_trigger and is_contractual_gate flags). Linking deliverable_tracker to project_milestone enables the system

attributes.name: plan_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.media_plan. Business justification: Media plans are contractual deliverables tracked through project delivery workflows. Required for client approval gates, billing triggers tied to plan delivery, and SOW compliance verification in agen

attributes.name: project_brief_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.project_brief. Business justification: Deliverables are defined and scoped in the project_brief (key_deliverables, scope_description, success_criteria). Linking deliverable_tracker to project_brief provides direct traceability from the bri

attributes.name: revision_of_deliverable_tracker_id
attributes.description: Self-referencing FK on deliverable_tracker (revision_of_deliverable_tracker_id)

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Deliverables in advertising (video assets, display creatives, media placements) are produced by specific vendors. Linking supplier_id to deliverable_tracker enables vendor-level delivery performance r

attributes.name: task_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.task. Business justification: A deliverable is produced by executing specific tasks. deliverable_tracker already links to work_package_id (the container) but lacks a direct link to the specific task that produces the deliverable.

attributes.name: trafficking_instruction_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.trafficking_instruction. Business justification: Trafficking instructions are formal deliverables in ad agency project management — they must be authored, reviewed, approved, and sent to ad servers. Project teams track each instruction as a delivera

attributes.name: work_package_id
attributes.description: Reference to the work package or work breakdown structure element that this deliverable belongs to.

attributes.name: actual_delivery_date
attributes.description: Actual date on which the deliverable was delivered. Used to measure on-time delivery performance and project execution accuracy.

attributes.name: actual_hours
attributes.description: Actual effort in hours logged against this deliverable. Used to measure estimation accuracy and project profitability.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the deliverable received final client approval. Marks formal acceptance and completion of the deliverable lifecycle.

attributes.name: client_approval_status
attributes.description: Current approval state from the client perspective. Indicates whether the client has formally accepted the deliverable.
attributes.references: American Association of Advertising Agencies (4As) Client Service Standards

attributes.name: compliance_check_status
attributes.description: Status of regulatory and brand compliance review. Ensures deliverable meets legal, regulatory, and brand guideline requirements before delivery.
attributes.references: Advertising Standards Authority (ASA); Federal Trade Commission (FTC) Advertising Practices; Trustworthy Accountability Group (TAG)

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this deliverable tracking record was first created in the system. Marks the start of operational tracking.

attributes.name: deliverable_name
attributes.description: Business name or title of the deliverable being tracked (e.g., 'Q4 Brand Campaign Video', 'Media Plan Deck', 'Trafficking Sheet').

attributes.name: deliverable_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the deliverable in the production workflow. Tracks progression from initiation through final delivery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_started|in_production|in_review|approved|delivered|rejected|on_hold|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: deliverable_type
attributes.description: Classification of the deliverable by format and purpose. Distinguishes creative assets from strategic documents, operational setup files, and reporting outputs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: creative_asset|media_plan|strategy_deck|report|campaign_setup|trafficking_sheet|presentation|research_report|analytics_dashboard|social_content|video_asset|display_banner|landing_page|email_creative|print_collateral|ooh_creative|other — 17 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: delivered_timestamp
attributes.description: Precise timestamp when the deliverable was marked as delivered. Provides exact delivery time for Service Level Agreement (SLA) measurement.

attributes.name: delivery_channel
attributes.description: Method or platform through which the deliverable was or will be transmitted to the recipient (e.g., email, client portal, FTP, DAM system). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|client_portal|ftp|dam|cloud_storage|physical_delivery|api|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: delivery_notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes accompanying the deliverable delivery. May include usage instructions, context, or special handling requirements.

attributes.name: estimated_hours
attributes.description: Planned effort in hours required to complete this deliverable. Used for resource planning and capacity management.

attributes.name: file_reference
attributes.description: File path, URL, or Digital Asset Management (DAM) system reference pointing to the deliverable asset location.
attributes.references: Adobe Creative Cloud DAM Standard; Workfront DAM Integration

attributes.name: format_specification
attributes.description: Technical format requirements for the deliverable (e.g., 'MP4 1920x1080 30fps', 'PDF print-ready CMYK', 'HTML5 300x250'). Ensures deliverable meets technical specifications.
attributes.references: Interactive Advertising Bureau (IAB) Creative Standards; Video Ad Serving Template (VAST)

attributes.name: internal_approval_status
attributes.description: Internal agency approval status before client submission. Ensures quality control and brand compliance before external delivery.

attributes.name: is_billable
attributes.description: Indicates whether this deliverable is billable to the client or is part of non-billable agency overhead or pro-bono work.
attributes.references: SAP S/4HANA Project Systems (PS) Billing Standard

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this deliverable tracking record. Used for change tracking and audit trail.

attributes.name: planned_delivery_date
attributes.description: Target date by which the deliverable is scheduled to be completed and delivered to the client or internal stakeholder.

attributes.name: priority
attributes.description: Business priority level assigned to this deliverable. Drives resource allocation and scheduling decisions.

attributes.name: rejection_reason
attributes.description: Explanation provided when a deliverable is rejected by client or internal reviewer. Captures feedback for rework and process improvement.

attributes.name: review_round_count
attributes.description: Number of review cycles the deliverable has undergone. Tracks iteration intensity and helps identify scope creep or unclear requirements.

attributes.name: source_system_deliverable_code
attributes.description: Original identifier from the source project management or work management system (e.g., Workfront task ID, Monday.com item ID).

attributes.name: source_system_name
attributes.description: Name of the operational system from which this deliverable tracking record originated (e.g., 'Workfront', 'Monday.com', 'Asana').

attributes.name: version_number
attributes.description: Version identifier for the deliverable (e.g., 'v1.0', 'v2.3', 'Final'). Tracks iteration history and ensures version control.
attributes.references: Workfront Proofing and Version Control Standard

products.name: time_entry
products.description: Individual time entry record submitted by agency staff against a specific project task or work package — the granular time-tracking record at the project level. Captures worker reference, project reference, work package reference, task reference, entry date, hours logged, activity type (creative development, strategy, client management, production, media planning, QA, admin), billable flag, billing rate, billable amount, approval status (submitted, approved, rejected), approver, and source system (Workfront/Monday.com timelog ID). Distinct from talent.timesheet (the aggregated pay-period timesheet header) — this is the line-level project time allocation record.

attributes.name: time_entry_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the time entry record. Primary key for the time entry entity.

attributes.name: adjusted_time_entry_id
attributes.description: Self-referencing FK on time_entry (adjusted_time_entry_id)

attributes.name: assignment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.project_assignment. Business justification: Time entries are submitted by workers who operate under specific project assignments. Linking time_entry to project_assignment provides the assignment context (billing_rate, cost_rate, assignment_role

attributes.name: contract_rate_card_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_card. Business justification: time_entry has billing_rate and cost_rate that must reconcile against the contracted rate card. Advertising agencies bill time against client-negotiated rate cards. This link enables billing validatio

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Time entries must post to cost centers for labor cost allocation, utilization reporting, and departmental P&L. Core requirement for agency financial systems integrating time tracking with GL posting.

attributes.name: finance_budget_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Time entries (labor costs) must be charged against the approved finance budget for labor cost tracking and budget consumption reporting. This is core agency resource management — time entries drive bu

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Time entries require GL account assignment for payroll expense posting and revenue recognition. Essential for labor cost capitalization, WIP accounting, and financial statement preparation in agency o

attributes.name: initiative_id
attributes.description: Reference to the project against which this time was logged. Links to the project domain project entity.

attributes.name: rate_card_line_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.rate_card_line. Business justification: Time entries in advertising are billed at specific rate card line rates by role and service type. rate_card_line has unit_rate, role_title, service_type matching time_entry billing attributes. This li

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Time is billed against SOWs. Agencies track SOW-level time for client invoicing, utilization reporting, and validating labor charges against SOW rate cards and authorized budget caps.

attributes.name: task_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific task or deliverable against which time was logged. Links to the project.task entity.

attributes.name: work_package_id
attributes.description: Reference to the work package within the project. Represents a logical grouping of tasks for billing or resource planning purposes.

attributes.name: activity_type
attributes.description: The type of work activity performed during this time entry. Categorizes time for resource utilization analysis and billing purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: creative_development|strategy|client_management|production|media_planning|quality_assurance|account_services|research|copywriting|design|video_editing|project_management|administrative — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: approval_status
attributes.description: Current approval state of the time entry. Tracks the workflow from submission through manager approval for payroll and billing processing.

attributes.name: approval_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the time entry was approved or rejected by the approver. Null if not yet reviewed.

attributes.name: billable_amount
attributes.description: The total billable amount for this time entry, calculated as hours_logged multiplied by billing_rate. Null for non-billable entries. Used for revenue recognition and client invoicing.

attributes.name: billing_rate
attributes.description: The hourly billing rate applied to this time entry for client invoicing. May vary by worker role, project contract terms, or activity type. Null for non-billable entries.

attributes.name: cost_amount
attributes.description: The total internal cost for this time entry, calculated as hours_logged multiplied by cost_rate. Used for project margin analysis and financial reporting.

attributes.name: cost_rate
attributes.description: The internal cost rate per hour for this worker. Used for project profitability analysis and resource cost tracking. Distinct from billing_rate.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this time entry record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the billable amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency project billing.

attributes.name: entry_date
attributes.description: The calendar date on which the work was performed. Used for timesheet aggregation and project timeline tracking.

attributes.name: hours_logged
attributes.description: The number of hours worked on this task for the entry date. Supports fractional hours (e.g., 1.5 hours, 0.25 hours).

attributes.name: is_billable
attributes.description: Indicates whether this time entry is billable to the client. True for billable time, False for non-billable or internal time.

attributes.name: is_locked
attributes.description: Indicates whether this time entry is locked and cannot be edited. True when the entry has been processed for payroll or billing. False for editable entries.

attributes.name: is_overtime
attributes.description: Indicates whether this time entry represents overtime hours. True for overtime, False for regular hours. Used for payroll processing and labor cost analysis.

attributes.name: location
attributes.description: The physical or virtual location where the work was performed. Used for resource allocation analysis and compliance with remote work policies.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when this time entry record was last modified. Updated whenever any field changes. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Optional free-text notes or comments provided by the worker describing the work performed. Used for detailed activity tracking and client reporting.

attributes.name: rejection_reason
attributes.description: Free-text explanation provided by the approver when rejecting a time entry. Null for approved or pending entries.

attributes.name: source_system_code
attributes.description: The unique identifier for this time entry in the source system (Workfront or Monday.com). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.

attributes.name: source_system_name
attributes.description: The name of the system from which this time entry was ingested. Supports multi-system time tracking integration.

attributes.name: submitted_timestamp
attributes.description: The date and time when the worker submitted this time entry for approval. Null for draft entries.

products.name: project_budget
products.description: Project-level budget record defining the approved financial envelope for a project, distinct from finance.budget (which operates at cost center and campaign level). Captures project reference, budget version, budget type (original approved, revised, client-approved), total approved budget, budget by phase or work package, contingency reserve, management reserve, currency, approval date, approver, budget status (draft, approved, locked, over-budget), total committed spend, total actual spend, and remaining budget. Enables project financial control and client billing reconciliation.

attributes.name: project_budget_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the project budget record. Primary key.

attributes.name: amendment_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.amendment. Business justification: Contract amendments in advertising trigger project budget revisions (scope changes, budget increases). Linking project_budget.amendment_id → amendment provides the audit trail showing which amendment

attributes.name: brand_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Agency project budgets are allocated and tracked at brand level for brand-level P&L and budget utilization reporting. Finance teams require direct brand attribution on project budgets to produce brand

attributes.name: contract_insertion_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract_insertion_order. Business justification: In advertising agencies, project budgets are built directly against Insertion Orders — the IO is the spend authorization document. This link enables IO-to-budget reconciliation reporting, a core finan

attributes.name: cost_center_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Project budgets are posted to cost centers for management accounting and overhead allocation. project_budget has a denormalized cost_center_code plain attribute but no FK. Linking to cost_center enabl

attributes.name: finance_budget_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Agency project budgets must reconcile against the finance-controlled finance_budget for variance reporting, SOX compliance, and budget approval workflows. Finance teams need to trace project spend bac

attributes.name: gl_account_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Project budgets need GL account mapping for financial statement integration, accrual posting, and revenue/expense classification. Required for month-end close and financial reporting in agency ERP sys

attributes.name: initiative_id
attributes.description: Reference to the parent project for which this budget is defined. Links to the project master record.

attributes.name: plan_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to media.media_plan. Business justification: Project budgets allocate funds to specific media plans. Required for variance analysis between planned media spend and project budget, enabling financial forecasting, budget reallocation decisions, an

attributes.name: revised_from_project_budget_id
attributes.description: Self-referencing FK on project_budget (revised_from_project_budget_id)

attributes.name: sow_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Project budgets are allocated from SOW budgets. Finance tracks SOW-to-project budget mapping for billing reconciliation, variance analysis, and ensuring project spend does not exceed contractual autho

attributes.name: supplier_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: project_budget contains a `third_party_vendor_budget` attribute, confirming per-vendor budget allocation is tracked. Linking supplier_id enables budget-vs-actual spend reporting by vendor — a standard

attributes.name: vendor_rate_card_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_rate_card. Business justification: In advertising, project budgets for media and production are built from vendor rate cards. Linking vendor_rate_card_id to project_budget enables budget-to-rate-card traceability — auditors and finance

attributes.name: work_package_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to project.work_package. Business justification: project_budget currently carries work_breakdown_structure_code as a free-text string reference to the WBS. Adding a proper FK work_package_id to project.work_package normalizes this relationship, repl

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date on which this budget version was formally approved by the authorized approver or governance body.

attributes.name: approved_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this budget version was formally approved. Captures the precise moment of approval for audit and compliance purposes.

attributes.name: baseline_budget_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this budget version represents the original approved baseline for the project. True for the baseline version, False for revisions.

attributes.name: budget_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the budget: draft (in preparation), submitted (pending approval), approved (active), locked (no further changes), over-budget (exceeded threshold), or closed (project complete).

attributes.name: budget_type
attributes.description: Classification of the budget record: original approved (baseline), revised (internal adjustment), client-approved (client sign-off), internal forecast (planning estimate), or contingency (reserve allocation).

attributes.name: client_approval_date
attributes.description: Date on which the client formally approved this budget version. Null if not yet client-approved.

attributes.name: client_approved_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this budget version has been formally approved by the client. True if client-approved, False otherwise.

attributes.name: contingency_reserve
attributes.description: Budget reserve allocated for identified risks and known-unknowns. Typically controlled by the project manager and released as needed for scope changes or risk mitigation.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this budget record was first created in the source system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.

attributes.name: creative_production_budget
attributes.description: Portion of the total budget allocated to creative development and production activities, including design, video production, photography, and Digital Asset Management (DAM).

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this budget (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

attributes.name: effective_end_date
attributes.description: Date on which this budget version ceases to be effective. Null for the current active budget version.

attributes.name: effective_start_date
attributes.description: Date from which this budget version becomes effective and active for financial tracking and control.

attributes.name: fiscal_quarter
attributes.description: Fiscal quarter to which this project budget primarily applies (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4). Supports quarterly financial planning and Quarterly Business Review (QBR) processes.

attributes.name: fiscal_year
attributes.description: Fiscal year to which this project budget applies (e.g., 2024, 2025). Enables financial period alignment and year-over-year analysis.

attributes.name: labor_budget
attributes.description: Portion of the total budget allocated to labor costs, including salaries, contractor fees, and Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) resources.

attributes.name: locked_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this budget version was locked to prevent further modifications. Null if the budget is still open for changes.

attributes.name: management_reserve
attributes.description: Budget reserve allocated for unknown-unknowns and unplanned scope changes. Typically controlled by senior management and requires formal approval to access.

attributes.name: media_budget
attributes.description: Portion of the total budget allocated to media buying and placement costs across all channels (digital, broadcast, print, Out-of-Home (OOH), etc.).

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this budget record was last modified in the source system. Supports change tracking and audit trail.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional notes, assumptions, constraints, or context related to this budget version. Supports audit trail and knowledge transfer.

attributes.name: remaining_budget
attributes.description: Calculated remaining budget available for allocation. Typically total_approved_budget minus (total_committed_spend + total_actual_spend).

attributes.name: revision_reason
attributes.description: Textual explanation of why this budget version was revised from the previous version. Captures scope changes, client requests, cost overruns, or other justifications.

attributes.name: source_system_budget_code
attributes.description: Original budget identifier from the source system (e.g., Workfront project budget ID, SAP Project Systems (PS) budget ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation.

attributes.name: source_system_name
attributes.description: Name of the operational system from which this budget record originated (e.g., Workfront, SAP S/4HANA PS, Mediaocean Prisma). Supports data lineage and Extract Transform Load (ETL) auditing.

attributes.name: technology_budget
attributes.description: Portion of the total budget allocated to technology costs, including ad serving platforms, Demand-Side Platform (DSP), Data Management Platform (DMP), analytics tools, and Application Programming Interface (API) integrations.

attributes.name: third_party_vendor_budget
attributes.description: Portion of the total budget allocated to external vendor services, including research firms, data providers, freelancers, and specialized consultants.

attributes.name: total_actual_spend
attributes.description: Cumulative amount of budget that has been actually spent (invoiced and paid or accrued) against this project budget.

attributes.name: total_approved_budget
attributes.description: The total financial envelope approved for this project budget version. Represents the authorized spending limit.

attributes.name: total_committed_spend
attributes.description: Cumulative amount of budget that has been committed through Purchase Orders (PO), Insertion Orders (IO), contracts, or other binding commitments, but not yet invoiced or paid.

attributes.name: utilization_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of the total approved budget that has been utilized (committed or spent). Calculated as ((total_committed_spend + total_actual_spend) / total_approved_budget) * 100.
attributes.references: Project Management Institute (PMI) Earned Value Management

attributes.name: version
attributes.description: Version identifier for this budget iteration (e.g., v1.0, v2.1, 2024-Q1-Rev3). Enables tracking of budget revisions over the project lifecycle.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `advertising_ecm`.`_metrics`.`audience_activation`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Audience activation performance metrics tracking reach, match quality, cost efficiency, and delivery health across all activation events. Used by media and audience operations teams to evaluate activation effectiveness and optimize spend."
  source: "`advertising_ecm`.`audience`.`activation`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "activation_type"
      expr: activation_type
      comment: "Type of audience activation (e.g., prospecting, retargeting, suppression) used to segment performance by activation strategy."
    - name: "activation_method"
      expr: activation_method
      comment: "Method used to activate the audience (e.g., pixel, CRM upload, API sync) for diagnosing delivery and match rate differences."
    - name: "activation_status"
      expr: activation_status
      comment: "Current status of the activation (e.g., active, paused, completed, error) for operational monitoring."
    - name: "consent_framework"
      expr: consent_framework
      comment: "Privacy consent framework governing the activation (e.g., GDPR, CCPA, TCF 2.0) for compliance segmentation."
    - name: "consent_status"
      expr: consent_status
      comment: "Consent status of the audience at activation time, critical for privacy compliance reporting."
    - name: "data_source_type"
      expr: data_source_type
      comment: "Origin of the audience data (e.g., first-party, third-party, modeled) for data quality and cost analysis."
    - name: "currency_code"
      expr: currency_code
      comment: "Currency in which activation costs are denominated, enabling multi-currency cost analysis."
    - name: "activation_date"
      expr: activation_date
      comment: "Calendar date the activation was initiated, used for time-series trending of activation volume and performance."
    - name: "priority_level"
      expr: priority_level
      comment: "Priority tier assigned to the activation, used to assess whether high-priority activations achieve better match rates and reach."
    - name: "sync_frequency"
      expr: sync_frequency
      comment: "How frequently the audience is synced to the activation platform, relevant for freshness and match rate analysis."
  measures:
    - name: "total_activations"
      expr: COUNT(1)
      comment: "Total number of activation records. Baseline volume metric for tracking activation throughput and operational scale."
    - name: "total_estimated_reach"
      expr: SUM(CAST(estimated_reach AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Sum of estimated addressable users across all activations. Directly informs media planning capacity and audience scale decisions."
    - name: "total_matched_users"
      expr: SUM(CAST(matched_user_count AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total number of users successfully matched on the activation platform. Measures effective addressable audience delivered to media."
    - name: "total_unmatched_users"
      expr: SUM(CAST(unmatched_user_count AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total number of users that failed to match on the activation platform. High values indicate data quality or identity resolution issues requiring intervention."
    - name: "avg_match_rate_pct"
      expr: AVG(CAST(match_rate_percentage AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average match rate percentage across activations. A key quality KPI — declining match rates signal identity graph degradation or data supplier issues."
    - name: "avg_cost_cpm"
      expr: AVG(CAST(cost_cpm AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average CPM (cost per thousand impressions) across activations. Core efficiency metric for media cost benchmarking and supplier negotiation."
    - name: "total_cost_cpm_weighted"
      expr: SUM(CAST(cost_cpm AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Sum of CPM values across activations, used as a proxy for total activation cost exposure when combined with reach metrics."
    - name: "avg_bid_modifier"
      expr: AVG(CAST(bid_modifier AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average bid modifier applied across activations. Indicates how aggressively the team is bidding on audience segments relative to base bids."
    - name: "match_rate_vs_estimated_reach_ratio"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CAST(matched_user_count AS DOUBLE)) / NULLIF(SUM(CAST(estimated_reach AS DOUBLE)), 0), 2)
      comment: "Ratio of matched users to estimated reach expressed as a percentage. Measures how well the activation platform delivers against the projected audience size — a critical data quality and platform performance KPI."
    - name: "error_activation_count"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN activation_status = 'error' THEN 1 END)
      comment: "Count of activations currently in error state. Operational health KPI — elevated error counts require immediate investigation to prevent campaign delivery failures."
    - name: "active_activation_count"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN activation_status = 'active' THEN 1 END)
      comment: "Count of currently active activations. Tracks live audience delivery footprint across the portfolio."
$$
metric_views.description: Audience activation performance metrics tracking reach, match quality, cost efficiency, and delivery health across all activation events. Used by media and audience operations teams to evaluate activation effectiveness and optimize spend.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `advertising_ecm`.`_metrics`.`audience_behavioral_profile`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Behavioral profile quality and targeting eligibility metrics tracking profile reach, quality scores, lookalike similarity, and consent posture. Used by audience data science and targeting teams to evaluate behavioral signal quality and optimize audience modeling inputs."
  source: "`advertising_ecm`.`audience`.`behavioral_profile`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "profile_status"
      expr: profile_status
      comment: "Current operational status of the behavioral profile (e.g., active, expired, pending) for inventory health monitoring."
    - name: "profile_type"
      expr: profile_type
      comment: "Type of behavioral profile (e.g., intent, affinity, lifestyle) for portfolio composition and signal type analysis."
    - name: "engagement_level"
      expr: engagement_level
      comment: "Engagement tier of the behavioral profile (e.g., high, medium, low) for audience quality stratification."
    - name: "device_type_primary"
      expr: device_type_primary
      comment: "Primary device type associated with the behavioral profile (e.g., mobile, desktop, CTV) for cross-device reach analysis."
    - name: "consent_status"
      expr: consent_status
      comment: "Consent status of the behavioral profile, critical for privacy compliance and activation eligibility."
    - name: "targeting_eligibility_flag"
      expr: targeting_eligibility_flag
      comment: "Boolean flag indicating whether the profile is eligible for targeting. Used to measure the net usable behavioral audience pool."
    - name: "cross_device_activity_flag"
      expr: cross_device_activity_flag
      comment: "Boolean flag indicating cross-device behavioral signals are present, used to assess omnichannel audience quality."
    - name: "brand_affinity_category"
      expr: brand_affinity_category
      comment: "Primary brand affinity category of the profile, used for brand-aligned audience segmentation and targeting strategy."
    - name: "browsing_category_primary"
      expr: browsing_category_primary
      comment: "Primary browsing category of the profile for contextual and interest-based targeting alignment."
    - name: "last_observed_behavior_date"
      expr: last_observed_behavior_date
      comment: "Date of the most recent behavioral signal observed, used for recency analysis and data freshness monitoring."
  measures:
    - name: "total_behavioral_profiles"
      expr: COUNT(1)
      comment: "Total number of behavioral profiles in the library. Baseline inventory metric for behavioral data asset scale."
    - name: "targeting_eligible_profile_count"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN targeting_eligibility_flag = TRUE THEN 1 END)
      comment: "Number of behavioral profiles eligible for targeting. Measures the net usable behavioral audience pool after consent and compliance gating."
    - name: "total_profile_reach_estimate"
      expr: SUM(CAST(profile_reach_estimate AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total estimated reach across all behavioral profiles. Measures the aggregate addressable audience represented by the behavioral data asset."
    - name: "avg_profile_quality_score"
      expr: AVG(CAST(profile_quality_score AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average quality score across behavioral profiles. A data asset health KPI — declining scores indicate signal degradation requiring data supplier review or model refresh."
    - name: "avg_lookalike_similarity_score"
      expr: AVG(CAST(lookalike_similarity_score AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average lookalike similarity score across behavioral profiles. Measures how well behavioral profiles align with seed audiences — a key input to lookalike model quality."
    - name: "targeting_eligibility_rate_pct"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN targeting_eligibility_flag = TRUE THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(1), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of behavioral profiles eligible for targeting. A compliance and data quality KPI — low rates indicate consent or data quality issues reducing addressable reach."
    - name: "cross_device_profile_rate_pct"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN cross_device_activity_flag = TRUE THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(1), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of behavioral profiles with cross-device signals. Measures the omnichannel depth of the behavioral data asset — higher rates enable more effective cross-screen targeting."
$$
metric_views.description: Behavioral profile quality and targeting eligibility metrics tracking profile reach, quality scores, lookalike similarity, and consent posture. Used by audience data science and targeting teams to evaluate behavioral signal quality and optimize audience modeling inputs.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `advertising_ecm`.`_metrics`.`audience_demographic_profile`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Demographic profile coverage and quality metrics tracking population size, profile confidence, consent posture, and geographic distribution. Used by audience planning, research, and compliance teams to assess demographic data asset quality and regulatory readiness."
  source: "`advertising_ecm`.`audience`.`demographic_profile`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "profile_status"
      expr: profile_status
      comment: "Current operational status of the demographic profile (e.g., active, expired, pending) for inventory health monitoring."
    - name: "gender"
      expr: gender
      comment: "Gender attribute of the demographic profile for audience composition analysis."
    - name: "age_band"
      expr: age_band
      comment: "Age band of the demographic profile (e.g., 18-24, 25-34) for generational audience segmentation."
    - name: "household_income_tier"
      expr: household_income_tier
      comment: "Household income tier of the demographic profile for premium audience identification and value-based targeting."
    - name: "education_level"
      expr: education_level
      comment: "Education level of the demographic profile for audience quality and brand alignment analysis."
    - name: "parental_status"
      expr: parental_status
      comment: "Parental status of the demographic profile for family-oriented campaign targeting."
    - name: "urbanicity"
      expr: urbanicity
      comment: "Urbanicity classification (e.g., urban, suburban, rural) for geographic audience strategy."
    - name: "country_code"
      expr: country_code
      comment: "Country of the demographic profile for geographic market analysis and compliance jurisdiction determination."
    - name: "consent_status"
      expr: consent_status
      comment: "Consent status of the demographic profile, critical for privacy compliance and activation eligibility."
    - name: "psychographic_segment"
      expr: psychographic_segment
      comment: "Psychographic segment classification of the profile for values-based and lifestyle audience targeting."
  measures:
    - name: "total_demographic_profiles"
      expr: COUNT(1)
      comment: "Total number of demographic profiles in the library. Baseline inventory metric for demographic data asset scale."
    - name: "total_estimated_population"
      expr: SUM(CAST(estimated_population_size AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total estimated population represented by demographic profiles. Measures the aggregate addressable audience scale of the demographic data asset."
    - name: "avg_estimated_population"
      expr: AVG(CAST(estimated_population_size AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average estimated population size per demographic profile. Low values may indicate over-segmentation reducing targeting scale."
    - name: "avg_profile_confidence_score"
      expr: AVG(CAST(profile_confidence_score AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average confidence score of demographic profile assignments. A data quality KPI — low confidence scores indicate unreliable demographic signals that may degrade targeting precision."
    - name: "high_confidence_profile_rate_pct"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN profile_confidence_score >= 0.8 THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(1), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of demographic profiles with confidence score at or above 0.8. Tracks the proportion of high-quality demographic signals available for precision targeting."
    - name: "active_profile_count"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN profile_status = 'active' THEN 1 END)
      comment: "Number of currently active demographic profiles. Measures the usable demographic inventory available for campaign targeting."
    - name: "consented_profile_count"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN consent_status = 'consented' THEN 1 END)
      comment: "Number of demographic profiles with confirmed consent status. Measures the privacy-compliant addressable demographic audience pool."
    - name: "consent_coverage_rate_pct"
      expr: ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN consent_status = 'consented' THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(1), 0), 2)
      comment: "Percentage of demographic profiles with confirmed consent. A privacy governance KPI — low rates reduce the addressable audience in regulated markets and signal consent collection gaps."
$$
